target_output.data_model: formulations (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, active_ingredient VARCHAR, target_dose_mg NUMERIC, batch_size INTEGER, created_by VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). excipients (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, cas_number VARCHAR, regulatory_limit_mg NUMERIC, limit_source VARCHAR, last_updated TIMESTAMPTZ). dose_calculations (id UUID PK, formulation_id FK->formulations, excipient_ratios JSONB, calculated_dose_per_unit NUMERIC(10,4), overage_percent NUMERIC(5,2), limit_violations JSONB, scientist_id VARCHAR, calculated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, sync_status ENUM('pending','synced')). audit_trail_entries (id UUID PK, calculation_id UUID FK->dose_calculations, scientist_ad_dn VARCHAR NOT NULL, inputs_hash CHAR(64) NOT NULL, result_hash CHAR(64) NOT NULL, pkcs7_signature TEXT NOT NULL, signed_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL). materials (id UUID PK, material_code VARCHAR UNIQUE, description VARCHAR, supplier VARCHAR, last_synced_at TIMESTAMPTZ). SQLCipher key: PBKDF2-SHA256(AD_dn + AD_password_hash, salt=DPAPI_protected_registry_value, iterations=310000, dklen=32). Clinical use entities: patient_weight_profiles (id UUID PK, patient_id_hash VARCHAR, weight_kg NUMERIC, bsa_m2 NUMERIC, creatinine_clearance_ml_min NUMERIC, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ, recorded_by_pharmacist_id UUID), dose_calculations (id UUID PK, formulation_id FK, patient_weight_profile_id FK, calculated_dose_mg NUMERIC, adjusted_dose_mg NUMERIC, adjustment_reason TEXT, clinical_notes TEXT, approved_by_physician_id UUID, approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ), contraindication_alerts (id UUID PK, formulation_id FK, condition_icd10 VARCHAR, severity ENUM contraindicated/caution, alert_text TEXT).
target_output.api_design: FastAPI sidecar endpoints on localhost:18080 (not externally accessible). POST /calculate: body {formulation_id, excipient_ratios JSONB, scientist_id}; returns {dose_per_unit, overage_percent, limit_violations}. POST /sign-calculation: body {calculation_id, scientist_ad_dn}; loads cert from Windows Certificate Store, signs, writes audit_trail_entry; returns {audit_entry_id, signed_at}. GET /materials?q=search_term: returns paginated materials list from local SQLite. GET /audit-trail?scientist_id=&from=&to=: returns paginated audit entries with signature_valid flag. POST /sync/trigger: manually triggers SQL Server sync; returns {synced_count, errors}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: AD LDAP authentication via ldap3; Electron safeStorage for session token. SQLCipher AES-256 with PBKDF2-SHA256 derived key (310,000 iterations, per-user DPAPI-protected salt in Windows registry); key never stored in plaintext or exposed to renderer. PKCS#7 detached digital signature on each audit_trail_entry; scientist certificate loaded from Windows Certificate Store; signature verified on read and verification status surfaced in Audit Trail UI. REVOKE UPDATE, DELETE on audit_trail_entries from application DB user. 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature requirements met by PKCS#7 scheme. EU Annex 11 access controls met by RBAC (Scientist/Supervisor/Admin). Sentry crash reports contain no dose calculation data.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Electron app packaged via electron-builder as MSI (Windows) and PKG (macOS). Python sidecar frozen via PyInstaller and bundled in installer. SCCM pushes installer to workstation fleet. Update check on startup against internal release server; new installer version triggers download and silent install prompt. GitHub Actions CI builds MSI/PKG artifacts and runs pytest parity tests. NOTE: This plan does not detail the IQ/OQ/PQ validation phases or provide a validation test protocol structure, which is an omission given the compliance_context contract requirement for computer system validation documentation.
target_output.observability: On-premise Sentry for crash reporting; Sentry SDK in Electron main process and Python sidecar. Structured JSON log file on workstation at %APPDATA%/DoseCalc/logs/ rotated daily, retained 90 days. Log entries include: auth events, calculation IDs, sync status, signature verification results. No external telemetry.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: No cloud hosting costs. On-premise Sentry instance is shared infrastructure. Installer distribution via SCCM at no additional cost. SQLite local storage ~50MB per active year per scientist. SQL Server central DB estimated 10GB after 5 years across 50 scientists.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (weeks 1-6): Electron shell with AD LDAP auth, FastAPI sidecar with DoseCalculationEngine (numerical parity verified against VB6 golden dataset of 200 reference calculations), SQLite schema via Alembic, SQLCipher with PBKDF2 key derivation. Phase 2 (weeks 7-10): Audit trail with PKCS#7 signatures, excipient limit checker, materials lookup. Phase 3 (weeks 11-14): SQL Server nightly sync, Admin panel, Supervisor review flow. Phase 4: IQ/OQ/PQ validation execution.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python: pytest with 90% coverage gate on DoseCalculationEngine and AuditTrailWriter. Numerical parity tests load VB6 golden dataset fixtures and assert output within 0.001% tolerance. TypeScript: Vitest for React components, ESLint + Prettier. contextBridge API surface reviewed in security PR checklist. electron-builder config pinned to specific Electron and Node versions for reproducible installers. Alembic migrations required for all schema changes with down migrations.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: PBKDF2 key derivation means AD password change invalidates local SQLite access; mitigated by re-keying workflow triggered on next login after AD password change detection. Risk: PyInstaller-frozen sidecar may trigger AV false positives on workstations; mitigated by pre-submission to IT security team for AV whitelist before SCCM deployment. Tradeoff: PKCS#7 using Windows Certificate Store limits macOS compatibility to macOS Keychain integration requiring separate implementation path.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: SQLCipher key derivation: PBKDF2-SHA256 from AD credentials with DPAPI-protected salt resolves ISS-001. Audit trail electronic signature: PKCS#7 from Windows Certificate Store resolves ISS-002. VB6 parity: golden dataset of 200 reference calculations used for OQ numerical parity tests.

metadata.notes: Round 2 resolves ISS-001 and ISS-002 but intentionally omits IQ/OQ/PQ validation detail required by compliance_context contract — deliberate flaw for training data.

project_goal.value: Real-time lab instrument monitoring dashboard streaming live sensor readings from bioreactors and chromatography systems, alerting process development scientists when parameters drift outside acceptable ranges
project_goal.rationale: Manual monitoring of instrument parameters is error-prone; automated alerting reduces out-of-spec batch risk

target_users.value: Process development scientists and lab supervisors monitoring bioreactor and chromatography runs
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in stakeholder interviews

project_class.rationale: Requires both real-time dashboard UI and backend ingest/alert processing

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, realtime, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Live sensor streaming, WebSocket push, time-series storage, alert analytics

complexity_level.rationale: MQTT ingest at 5s cadence across multiple instruments, WebSocket fan-out, InfluxDB time-series, alert escalation logic

risk_level.rationale: Missed alerts could allow out-of-spec batch parameters to go unnoticed, impacting product quality and 21 CFR Part 11 process records

data_sensitivity.rationale: Sensor data and process parameters are proprietary manufacturing IP

external_exposure.rationale: Dashboard accessible only on corporate LAN; instruments on isolated instrument network segment

access_model.value: Okta OIDC SSO; role-based access for Scientist (configure alert rules for own batches) and Supervisor (view all batches, acknowledge escalated alerts)
access_model.rationale: Corporate Okta is the identity provider; role separation required for alert acknowledgment authority

feature_scope.value: Live instrument parameter charts, per-batch alert rule configuration, real-time alert notifications via WebSocket, alert escalation to supervisor after timeout, historical trend analysis, batch run summary reports
feature_scope.rationale: Core monitoring and alerting features identified in requirements workshop

mvp_scope.value: Live sensor data display from bioreactors, configurable alert rules per batch, real-time WebSocket alerts to scientist dashboard
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum for scientists to replace manual log-sheet monitoring

security_baseline.value: Okta OIDC authentication, TLS 1.3 for all HTTP and WebSocket connections, InfluxDB access via service token only, no direct DB access from browser
security_baseline.rationale: Corporate security baseline; InfluxDB must not be directly reachable from frontend

frontend_stack.rationale: Team familiarity with Next.js; SSR for initial dashboard load, CSR for live chart updates

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI
backend_stack.rationale: Python ecosystem for MQTT integration and scientific data processing

data_platform.value: InfluxDB 2 for time-series sensor data; Redis 7 for alert state and active WebSocket session tracking
data_platform.rationale: InfluxDB purpose-built for high-frequency sensor time-series; Redis for ephemeral alert coordination

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1
hosting_target.rationale: Corporate AWS standard; Fargate avoids node management overhead

privacy_retention_policy.value: Sensor readings retained in InfluxDB 90 days hot; archived to S3 Parquet indefinitely for historical analysis; alert events retained 2 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Process analytical technology records needed for batch release support; 90-day hot window covers active development campaigns

future_scope.value: ML-based anomaly detection on sensor streams, integration with LIMS batch release workflow, mobile push notifications for on-call supervisors
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap items after core monitoring is stable

constraints.value: Instruments publish MQTT on isolated instrument network segment; backend must bridge MQTT to cloud without exposing instrument network; latency from sensor reading to dashboard alert must be under 10 seconds
constraints.rationale: Instrument network isolation is a security requirement; 10s latency ceiling set by process team

observability_baseline.value: Grafana dashboards on Prometheus metrics; alert on MQTT message gap >15s per instrument
observability_baseline.rationale: Existing Grafana/Prometheus stack; MQTT gap alert detects instrument connectivity failures

execution_preference.value: Agile sprints with instrument team co-development for MQTT integration testing
execution_preference.rationale: Instrument MQTT protocol requires close collaboration with lab automation team

compliance_context.value: 21 CFR Part 11 process analytical technology (PAT) guidance; FDA PAT framework for real-time monitoring of critical process parameters
compliance_context.rationale: Real-time process parameter monitoring in regulated biopharmaceutical manufacturing

project.goal: Real-time lab instrument monitoring with configurable alerting
project.target_users: Process development scientists and supervisors

frontend.charting: Apache ECharts or Recharts for live sensor charts
frontend.realtime: WebSocket client for live alert push

backend.ingest: MQTT subscriber (paho-mqtt) bridging instrument network to InfluxDB

security.auth: Okta OIDC
security.db_access: InfluxDB via service token, no direct browser access

data.cache: Redis 7 for alert state and WebSocket session registry

constraints.latency: Sensor to dashboard alert under 10 seconds
constraints.network: MQTT bridge from isolated instrument network

open_questions.charting_library: Apache ECharts vs Recharts for live sensor charts not yet decided

reasoner_reviews.product: The core value proposition is clear: replace manual log-sheet monitoring with automated real-time alerting. The 10-second latency ceiling is achievable with the proposed MQTT-to-WebSocket path. Alert escalation to supervisor after configurable timeout is essential for on-call coverage.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The MQTT bridge architecture is correct for isolating the instrument network from the cloud backend. InfluxDB 2 is well-suited for 5-second cadence multi-instrument sensor data. Redis pub/sub should be used to fan out alert events to WebSocket sessions rather than polling InfluxDB from the frontend.
reasoner_reviews.security: Okta OIDC with TLS 1.3 is sufficient for the internal-only exposure. InfluxDB should never be directly accessible from the browser; all queries must proxy through the FastAPI backend. Alert rule configuration must be scoped to the batch owner to prevent cross-scientist interference.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 10-second end-to-end latency budget should be broken down: MQTT delivery ~1s, InfluxDB write ~0.5s, alert evaluation ~0.5s, WebSocket push ~0.5s. This leaves 7.5s headroom. The MQTT broker must be deployed on a network segment with both instrument VLAN and ECS VPC access.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The open question on charting library should be resolved: Apache ECharts has better performance for high-frequency streaming updates (100+ data points/second per chart) compared to Recharts. Recommend ECharts.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 with App Router. Server components for initial batch list and static dashboard chrome. Client components for live sensor charts and alert notification panel. WebSocket connection managed in a React Context provider that subscribes on mount and reconnects with exponential backoff.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Dashboard: live sensor charts per active batch with parameter charts (pH, DO, temperature, pressure for bioreactors; UV absorbance, conductivity for chromatography). Alert Panel: real-time list of active alerts with acknowledge button. Batch Configuration: alert rule setup per batch run with min/max thresholds per parameter. Historical View: trend charts over selectable time range queried from InfluxDB via backend proxy.
frontend.performance_notes: Apache ECharts selected for live streaming charts; supports incremental data append without full re-render. Charts limited to 500 data points sliding window to prevent memory growth on long-running batch sessions.
frontend.frontend_risks: WebSocket reconnection during network hiccups must not lose in-flight alerts; alerts buffered in Redis and replayed to reconnecting clients.

backend.service_design: Two ECS Fargate services: IngestService (MQTT subscriber + InfluxDB writer + alert evaluator) and APIService (FastAPI HTTP/WebSocket serving the Next.js frontend). Both services read from Redis for alert state coordination.
backend.business_modules: MQTTBridge: paho-mqtt subscriber connecting to EMQX broker on instrument network bridge, parses instrument JSON payloads, writes to InfluxDB measurements per instrument type. AlertEvaluator: on each write, checks reading against alert_rules for the active batch run; publishes to Redis pub/sub channel on threshold breach. WebSocketManager: subscribes to Redis channel, fans out alert events to connected scientist sessions.
backend.scaling_notes: IngestService single instance sufficient for current 20-instrument fleet; horizontal scaling requires MQTT topic partitioning strategy. APIService scales to 3 instances behind ALB with WebSocket sticky sessions.

security.auth_design: Okta OIDC: Next.js frontend uses next-auth with Okta provider. FastAPI backend validates Okta JWT on protected endpoints and WebSocket upgrade handshake.
security.authorization_model: Scientist: configure and view alert rules for batches they own. Supervisor: view all batches and acknowledge escalated alerts. Role claims from Okta group membership.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Alert events written to PostgreSQL alert_events table with scientist_id, batch_id, parameter, threshold, reading_value, timestamp. Acknowledgment actions logged with acknowledger_id and timestamp.

data.entities: instruments (id UUID PK, name, type ENUM bioreactor/chromatography, mqtt_topic, lab_location, status). batches (id UUID PK, instrument_id FK, scientist_id, start_time, end_time, status ENUM active/completed/aborted). alert_rules (id UUID PK, batch_id FK, parameter_name, min_threshold, max_threshold, escalation_timeout_minutes). alert_events (id UUID PK, batch_id FK, instrument_id FK, parameter_name, reading_value, threshold_violated, acknowledged_by, acknowledged_at, created_at). sensor_readings stored in InfluxDB measurement per instrument_type tagged by instrument_id and batch_id.
data.storage_design: InfluxDB 2 for sensor_readings (high-frequency writes, 90-day retention policy, downsampling task to 1-minute aggregates after 7 days). PostgreSQL 16 for instruments, batches, alert_rules, alert_events relational data. Redis 7 for alert state cache and WebSocket session registry.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate in us-east-1. Two services: ingest-service (1 task, 1 vCPU / 2GB RAM) and api-service (1-3 tasks, ALB with WebSocket sticky sessions). InfluxDB on ECS with EBS volume or InfluxDB Cloud for managed option. Redis on ElastiCache.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Python pytest, Next.js build, Docker push to ECR, ECS rolling update. Environments: dev and production.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scrapes FastAPI /metrics. Grafana dashboards: MQTT message rate per instrument, alert evaluation latency, WebSocket connected session count, InfluxDB write throughput. Alert: MQTT gap >15s per instrument_id triggers PagerDuty.

target_output.thinking_summary: First-pass plan for real-time bioreactor and chromatography monitoring. The architecture is sound: MQTT bridge to InfluxDB, FastAPI WebSocket fan-out via Redis pub/sub, Next.js live charts. Per weak quality target, plan sections are present but several are thinner than ideal — phased_implementation lacks specifics on instrument onboarding, observability lacks specific metric names, and development_guidelines are brief.
target_output.title: Lab Instrument Real-Time Monitoring Dashboard Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A fullstack application streaming live sensor readings from bioreactors and chromatography systems to process development scientists via WebSocket-pushed dashboards. An MQTT bridge service subscribes to instrument topics on the isolated instrument network, writes readings to InfluxDB 2, evaluates alert rules per batch, and publishes breach events to Redis pub/sub. A FastAPI APIService fans alert events to connected Next.js dashboard sessions over WebSocket. Scientists configure per-batch alert thresholds; unacknowledged alerts escalate to supervisors after a configurable timeout. Deployed on AWS ECS Fargate with Okta OIDC authentication.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two ECS Fargate services: IngestService handles MQTT subscription from the instrument network EMQX broker, writes sensor readings to InfluxDB 2, and evaluates alert_rules on each incoming reading, publishing breach events to a Redis pub/sub channel. APIService serves the Next.js frontend via HTTP and WebSocket, subscribing to the Redis breach channel to fan out alert events to connected scientist sessions with sticky session routing at the ALB. PostgreSQL 16 stores relational data: instruments, batches, alert_rules, and alert_events. Next.js renders server-side batch lists and client-side live sensor charts using Apache ECharts with a 500-point sliding window. Okta OIDC secures all endpoints; JWT validated on both HTTP and WebSocket upgrade handshake.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14, TypeScript, Apache ECharts for live streaming charts, next-auth with Okta provider, React Context WebSocket manager. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, paho-mqtt, influxdb-client, asyncio WebSocket manager. Data: InfluxDB 2 (sensor time-series, 90-day retention), PostgreSQL 16 (relational), Redis 7 (alert state, WebSocket sessions). Infrastructure: AWS ECS Fargate, ALB, ElastiCache Redis, ECR, GitHub Actions. Observability: Prometheus, Grafana.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Live Sensor Charts: per-batch real-time charts for pH, DO, temperature, pressure (bioreactor) and UV absorbance, conductivity (chromatography) updating every 5 seconds. Alert Rule Config: scientists set min/max thresholds per parameter per batch run with escalation timeout. Real-Time Alerts: WebSocket-pushed notifications when readings breach thresholds. Alert Escalation: unacknowledged alerts forwarded to supervisor after timeout. Historical Trends: selectable time-range charts from InfluxDB via backend proxy. Batch Management: start/stop batch runs, link to instrument.
target_output.system_components: EMQX MQTT broker (on instrument-network bridge). IngestService (ECS Fargate: paho-mqtt subscriber, InfluxDB writer, alert evaluator, Redis publisher). APIService (ECS Fargate 1-3 tasks: FastAPI HTTP + WebSocket, Redis subscriber). ALB with WebSocket sticky sessions. InfluxDB 2 (ECS + EBS or InfluxDB Cloud). PostgreSQL 16 (RDS). ElastiCache Redis 7. Next.js frontend served from APIService. Prometheus + Grafana for observability.
target_output.workflows: Sensor Ingest and Alert: Instrument publishes JSON reading {instrument_id, parameter, value, timestamp} to MQTT topic instruments/{id}/readings every 5 seconds. IngestService MQTTBridge receives message, writes to InfluxDB measurement instrument_readings tagged by instrument_id and batch_id. AlertEvaluator queries alert_rules for the active batch; if value outside [min_threshold, max_threshold], inserts alert_events row and publishes {alert_event_id, batch_id, scientist_id, parameter, value} to Redis channel batch-alerts. APIService WebSocketManager receives Redis message, looks up scientist WebSocket sessions for the batch, pushes alert JSON to each connected session. Alert Escalation: ECS scheduled task runs every minute, queries alert_events where acknowledged_at IS NULL AND created_at < NOW() - escalation_timeout; for each, pushes escalation alert to supervisor sessions.
target_output.data_model: instruments (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, type ENUM('bioreactor','chromatography'), mqtt_topic VARCHAR, lab_location VARCHAR, status ENUM('active','offline')). batches (id UUID PK, instrument_id FK, scientist_id VARCHAR, start_time TIMESTAMPTZ, end_time TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM('active','completed','aborted')). alert_rules (id UUID PK, batch_id FK, parameter_name VARCHAR, min_threshold NUMERIC, max_threshold NUMERIC, escalation_timeout_minutes INTEGER DEFAULT 15). alert_events (id UUID PK, batch_id FK, instrument_id FK, parameter_name VARCHAR, reading_value NUMERIC, threshold_violated VARCHAR, acknowledged_by VARCHAR, acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). InfluxDB measurement instrument_readings: fields value FLOAT, tags instrument_id, batch_id, parameter; 90-day retention, 1-min downsample after 7 days.
target_output.api_design: FastAPI on /api/v1. Auth: Okta JWT Bearer on all endpoints. GET /batches: list active batches for scientist. POST /batches: start batch run. GET /batches/:id/readings?param=&from=&to=: proxy InfluxDB query for historical chart data. POST /batches/:id/alert-rules: create alert rule. GET /batches/:id/alert-events: list alerts for batch. PATCH /alert-events/:id/acknowledge: acknowledge alert. WebSocket /ws/batches/:id/alerts: push channel for live alerts, JWT validated on upgrade.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Okta OIDC via next-auth; Okta JWT validated on FastAPI middleware and WebSocket upgrade. TLS 1.3 on ALB for all HTTP and WebSocket traffic. InfluxDB accessed only via service token from IngestService and APIService; not directly reachable from browser. Alert rule write scoped to batch owner (scientist_id match check). Alert acknowledgment restricted to Supervisor role. 21 CFR Part 11 PAT: alert_events written to append-only PostgreSQL table; alert acknowledgment audit logged with acknowledger identity and timestamp.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate us-east-1. IngestService: 1 task, 1 vCPU / 2GB, no autoscaling needed for 20-instrument fleet. APIService: 1-3 tasks, ALB sticky sessions for WebSocket affinity, HPA on CPU 60%. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ. ElastiCache Redis cluster mode disabled (single primary). GitHub Actions CI/CD: pytest, Next.js build, Docker push ECR, ECS rolling update. Environments: dev and production only.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes FastAPI /metrics endpoint. Grafana: MQTT message rate, alert evaluation latency, WebSocket session count. Alert: MQTT message gap >15s per instrument triggers PagerDuty notification. Loki log aggregation collects structured JSON logs from the FastAPI ingest service with instrument_id and sensor_type fields for per-instrument drill-down. Uptime Kuma monitors the MQTT broker health endpoint and sends Slack webhook alerts on broker restart or certificate expiry.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ~$400/month estimated. ECS Fargate 2 services ~$150, RDS PostgreSQL ~$120, ElastiCache ~$60, InfluxDB Cloud Starter ~$70. Scales horizontally for additional instruments by adding MQTT topic partitions.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (weeks 1-5): MQTT bridge and InfluxDB ingest, live sensor chart in Next.js. Phase 2 (weeks 6-9): Alert rule engine, WebSocket push, Redis pub/sub integration. Phase 3 (weeks 10-12): Alert escalation, Supervisor dashboard, historical trend views.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python: pytest for alert evaluation logic. TypeScript: ESLint + Next.js lint rules. WebSocket reconnection with exponential backoff required. InfluxDB queries must use batch_id tag filter to prevent cross-batch data leakage.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: MQTT broker connectivity loss causes alert gap; mitigation is IngestService reconnect loop with Prometheus gap alert. Risk: InfluxDB disk exhaustion if retention policy task fails; mitigation is CloudWatch EBS utilization alert. Tradeoff: WebSocket sticky sessions limit APIService horizontal scaling; acceptable for 20-instrument current fleet.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Charting library resolved: Apache ECharts selected over Recharts for better performance on high-frequency streaming updates. InfluxDB deployment: InfluxDB Cloud Starter selected over self-managed ECS to reduce operational overhead.

metadata.notes: First-pass realtime bioreactor/chromatography monitoring on AWS; plan quality weak with several under-developed sections.

profile.domain: Medicinal Chemistry SMILES Compliance Validator CLI

project_goal.value: Command-line tool for medicinal chemists to validate SMILES structures against internal prohibited substructure lists and export compliance reports flagging compounds with PAINS filters or restricted reagents
project_goal.rationale: Manual PAINS filtering is time-consuming and inconsistently applied across the chemistry team

target_users.value: Medicinal chemists running structure triage on developer workstations
target_users.rationale: CLI users with Python environments; no web browser interface needed

project_class.rationale: Local command-line execution with no server or browser component required

capabilities.rationale: Python CLI with local SQLite filter database; no frontend or auth needed

complexity_level.rationale: Deterministic substructure matching against local filter library; no distributed components

risk_level.rationale: Local-only tool with internal data; no external network exposure during normal use

data_sensitivity.rationale: Input SMILES are internal compound structures; filter libraries are curated internal policy data

external_exposure.rationale: CLI runs on developer workstation; only outbound network call is optional filter database update from internal registry URL

access_model.value: No authentication required; local execution by any workstation user with the tool installed
access_model.rationale: CLI is a local utility; no multi-user access control needed

feature_scope.value: Accept CSV of SMILES strings, run RDKit substructure matching against PAINS and restricted reagent filters, output per-compound pass/fail report in CSV or JSON, update local filter database from internal registry on demand
feature_scope.rationale: Core validation and reporting features identified in chemistry team requirements

mvp_scope.value: SMILES CSV input, PAINS substructure matching, pass/fail CSV report output
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum to replace manual PAINS triage workflow

security_baseline.value: No network calls during validation; optional update command fetches filter database over HTTPS from internal registry only; input SMILES never transmitted externally
security_baseline.rationale: Compound structures are confidential IP and must not leave the workstation during validation

frontend_stack.rationale: CLI tool with no browser or GUI component

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Click for CLI framework and RDKit for cheminformatics
backend_stack.rationale: RDKit is the standard open-source cheminformatics library; Click provides ergonomic CLI argument parsing

data_platform.value: SQLite for local filter database storing PAINS rules and restricted reagent substructures
data_platform.rationale: Embedded SQLite requires no server; filter library is read-only during validation runs

hosting_target.value: Developer workstations; distributed as a Python package installable via pip from internal PyPI registry
hosting_target.rationale: pip install from internal registry is the standard distribution path for internal Python tools

privacy_retention_policy.value: Input SMILES not persisted by the tool; validation reports written to user-specified output path only; filter database stored in user home directory (~/.chemval/filters.db)
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Tool processes transient data; no server-side retention required

future_scope.value: SDF file input support, integration with corporate compound registration API for batch triage, web-based report viewer
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 extensions after CLI is adopted by chemistry team

constraints.value: RDKit must be available in the Python environment; SQLite filter database bundled with package for offline use; filter update requires network access to internal registry only
constraints.rationale: Offline-capable by default; update is an explicit opt-in action

observability_baseline.value: none; validation runs are transient CLI invocations with no persistent telemetry
observability_baseline.rationale: Local CLI tool does not require server-side observability

execution_preference.value: Ship as a versioned Python package with automated pytest and GitHub Actions CI on every pull request
execution_preference.rationale: Standard Python package development workflow

project.goal: CLI SMILES validator with PAINS and restricted reagent filter matching
project.target_users: Medicinal chemists on workstations

security.network: No network calls during validation; HTTPS only for optional filter update from internal registry

data.bundled: Initial filter library bundled with package

constraints.rdkit: RDKit must be installed in Python environment

open_questions.output_formats: CSV and JSON output; SDF to be added in phase 2

reasoner_reviews.product: The SMILES CSV input and per-compound pass/fail report covers the core use case. The filter database update command is an important feature for keeping the tool current with evolving PAINS and restricted reagent lists without requiring a new package release.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Click is the right framework for this CLI: group commands (validate, update-filters, list-filters) provide a clean interface. RDKit substructure matching via HasSubstructMatch against MolFromSmarts is deterministic and does not require network calls. SQLite is sufficient for the read-mostly filter database; no concurrent write scenarios. The filter database schema should store SMARTS strings alongside human-readable names and rule sources to make report output informative.
reasoner_reviews.security: The most important security property for this tool is that input SMILES are never transmitted externally during validation. The optional update command must enforce HTTPS to the internal registry URL and validate the downloaded filter set against a SHA-256 checksum before replacing the local database. No credentials should be required or stored.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: RDKit is a compiled dependency; the internal PyPI package should include pre-built wheels for supported platforms (Linux x86_64, macOS arm64, Windows x86_64). The initial bundled filter database must cover the standard PAINS A/B/C filter sets (480 rules) and the internal restricted reagent list.

backend.service_design: Single Python package 'chemval' with Click command group. Commands: 'validate' (core validation), 'update-filters' (fetch latest filter database from internal registry), 'list-filters' (display available filter categories and rule counts).
backend.api_patterns: CLI interface: chemval validate --input compounds.csv --output report.csv --format csv|json [--filters pains,restricted]. chemval update-filters [--registry-url https://internal-registry/chemval/filters]. chemval list-filters [--category pains|restricted].
backend.business_modules: SMILESParser: read input CSV, extract smiles and compound_id columns, call RDKit MolFromSmiles per row, collect parse errors. SubstructureValidator: for each valid mol, run HasSubstructMatch against each SMARTS in filter_rules table; collect matching rules. ReportWriter: aggregate per-compound results (compound_id, smiles, status=pass/fail, matched_rules list) and write CSV or JSON. FilterUpdater: fetch filters.db from internal registry over HTTPS, verify SHA-256 checksum from registry manifest, replace ~/.chemval/filters.db atomically.
backend.failure_handling: Invalid SMILES rows logged to stderr with row number and smiles value; written to report as status=parse_error. RDKit import failure exits with error message and pip install guidance. Filter database missing triggers download prompt on first run.

security.auth_design: No authentication. update-filters command uses HTTPS to internal registry URL; no credentials stored or required.
security.secrets_management: No secrets. Filter database checksum fetched from registry manifest over HTTPS and verified before replacing local DB.
security.security_risks: Input SMILES processing via RDKit MolFromSmiles; malformed SMILES handled gracefully by RDKit without shell execution. No external network calls during validate command.

data.entities: filter_rules (id INTEGER PK, category ENUM('pains_a','pains_b','pains_c','restricted_reagent'), name VARCHAR, smarts TEXT NOT NULL, description VARCHAR, source VARCHAR, added_at TEXT). filter_metadata (key TEXT PK, value TEXT) — stores version and last_updated_at.
data.storage_design: SQLite database at ~/.chemval/filters.db. Read-only during validate command; written only by update-filters command. Initial database bundled in package at chemval/data/filters_baseline.db and copied to ~/.chemval/ on first run.
data.schema_notes: SMARTS strings pre-validated with RDKit MolFromSmarts at database build time to prevent runtime parse failures. filter_metadata stores schema_version for migration detection on future updates.
data.data_risks: Stale filter database (no update run) may miss newly restricted substructures; mitigated by update-filters command printing last_updated_at at start of validate run if database is older than 30 days.

devops.deployment_topology: Python package published to internal PyPI registry (Artifactory or Nexus). Package includes pre-built RDKit wheels for Linux x86_64, macOS arm64, Windows x86_64 as extras_require per platform. No server deployment.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: python -m pytest (unit tests for SMILESParser, SubstructureValidator, ReportWriter with fixture SMILES sets including known PAINS positives), black + ruff lint, python -m build, twine upload to internal PyPI on version tag push.
devops.rollback_strategy: pip install chemval==<previous_version> from internal PyPI. Filter database rollback via ~/.chemval/filters.db.bak created by update-filters before replacement.
devops.devops_risks: RDKit compiled wheels increase package size (~30MB per platform); distribute via internal PyPI to avoid public registry for confidential tool.

target_output.thinking_summary: Simple local CLI tool with clear architecture: Click command group, RDKit substructure matching, SQLite filter database. No network calls during validation protects confidential SMILES data. The update-filters command with checksum verification is the key security control. Moderate quality target means most sections are solid but observability and deployment sections are intentionally brief given no server component.
target_output.title: SMILES Compliance Validator CLI Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 command-line tool that enables medicinal chemists to validate compound SMILES strings against the curated PAINS A/B/C filter set and internal restricted reagent substructure library without any network connectivity during validation. RDKit substructure matching against SMARTS rules stored in a local SQLite database produces per-compound pass/fail reports in CSV or JSON. A separate update-filters command fetches the latest filter library from an internal registry over HTTPS with SHA-256 checksum verification. Distributed as a pip-installable package from the internal PyPI registry with bundled pre-built RDKit wheels. No server deployment, no authentication, no persistent storage of input SMILES.
target_output.architecture_overview: Local command-line application with no network dependencies during core validation workflow. The 'chemval' Click command group exposes three subcommands: 'validate' reads a SMILES CSV, runs RDKit HasSubstructMatch against all SMARTS in the local SQLite filter_rules table, and writes a per-compound compliance report. 'update-filters' fetches a new filters.db from the internal registry URL over HTTPS, verifies the SHA-256 checksum from the registry manifest, and atomically replaces ~/.chemval/filters.db. 'list-filters' queries filter_metadata and filter_rules for a human-readable summary of installed rules by category. The filter database is bundled with the package at chemval/data/filters_baseline.db and copied to the user home directory on first invocation. RDKit is a required dependency with pre-built wheels for Linux, macOS, and Windows included in the internal PyPI package.
target_output.technology_stack: Python 3.12, Click 8.x (CLI framework), RDKit 2024.x (SMILES parsing, SMARTS substructure matching via HasSubstructMatch/MolFromSmarts/MolFromSmiles), SQLite 3 via Python stdlib sqlite3, requests library for HTTPS filter updates, hashlib for SHA-256 checksum verification. Packaging: pyproject.toml with setuptools, pre-built RDKit wheels per platform. CI: GitHub Actions with pytest, ruff, black, twine.
target_output.functional_feature_map: validate command: accepts --input CSV (compound_id, smiles columns), --output path, --format csv|json, --filters category selector (default: pains,restricted). Processes each SMILES row: MolFromSmiles parse (parse_error on failure), HasSubstructMatch against all SMARTS for selected categories, collects matching rule names. Outputs report with columns: compound_id, smiles, status (pass/fail/parse_error), matched_rules (comma-separated rule names). Prints summary: N compounds checked, M failed, K parse errors. update-filters command: fetches filters.db and manifest.json from --registry-url, verifies SHA-256, backs up existing db to filters.db.bak, replaces atomically. Prints last_updated_at and rule count delta. list-filters command: prints table of category, rule_count, last_updated for each filter category.
target_output.system_components: chemval CLI package (Python 3.12). Click command group with three subcommands. SMILESParser module (CSV reading, RDKit MolFromSmiles, parse error collection). SubstructureValidator module (SQLite SMARTS query, RDKit batch HasSubstructMatch). ReportWriter module (CSV and JSON output formatters). FilterUpdater module (HTTPS fetch, SHA-256 verify, atomic DB replace). SQLite filter database at ~/.chemval/filters.db (filter_rules, filter_metadata tables). Internal PyPI registry (Artifactory) for package distribution. GitHub Actions CI for test, lint, build, publish.
target_output.workflows: Validation Workflow: chemist runs 'chemval validate --input library.csv --output report.csv'. SMILESParser reads library.csv row by row, calls RDKit MolFromSmiles; rows with None mol object written to report as status=parse_error with error detail. For each valid mol, SubstructureValidator queries filter_rules WHERE category IN selected_categories, calls Chem.MolFromSmarts(smarts) per rule (cached in-memory for run), calls mol.HasSubstructMatch(query); collects matching rule names. ReportWriter aggregates per compound and writes report.csv. If filter database older than 30 days, prints warning: 'Filter database last updated {date}; run update-filters to get latest rules.' Filter Update Workflow: chemist runs 'chemval update-filters'. FilterUpdater sends GET to {registry_url}/manifest.json over HTTPS, parses {url, sha256, version, updated_at}. Downloads filters.db to temp file, computes SHA-256, compares against manifest; on mismatch aborts with error. Copies existing filters.db to filters.db.bak, moves temp file to filters.db. Prints 'Updated to version {version}, {N} rules ({+M} new)'.
target_output.data_model: SQLite database ~/.chemval/filters.db. filter_rules (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, category TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(category IN ('pains_a','pains_b','pains_c','restricted_reagent')), name TEXT NOT NULL, smarts TEXT NOT NULL, description TEXT, source TEXT, added_at TEXT). All SMARTS pre-validated with RDKit MolFromSmarts at database build time. filter_metadata (key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT). Keys: 'schema_version' (integer), 'last_updated_at' (ISO 8601), 'rule_count' (integer), 'pains_source' (e.g. 'Baell & Holloway 2010 JMedChem + internal additions'). Baseline database bundled at chemval/data/filters_baseline.db includes 480 PAINS A/B/C rules and the internal restricted reagent list.
target_output.api_design: CLI interface (no HTTP API). Commands and flags: 'chemval validate --input FILE --output FILE [--format csv|json] [--filters CATEGORIES]' where CATEGORIES is a comma-separated list of filter categories (default: all). 'chemval update-filters [--registry-url URL]' (URL defaults to hardcoded internal registry constant). 'chemval list-filters [--category CATEGORY]'. Exit codes: 0 = all compounds pass, 1 = one or more compounds failed validation, 2 = parse errors encountered, 3 = tool error (missing dependency, DB not found).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Input SMILES never transmitted externally during validate command; all processing is local and in-memory. update-filters enforces HTTPS to the internal registry URL; no credentials required or stored. SHA-256 checksum verification prevents tampered filter database installation. RDKit MolFromSmiles input handling is safe against malformed SMILES without shell execution risk. chemval is distributed from internal PyPI only; not published to PyPI.org. Confidential compound SMILES in input files are the user's responsibility to protect on their workstation; tool writes only to the user-specified output path.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Package published to internal PyPI (Artifactory). Installation: 'pip install chemval' from internal registry. Package includes optional extras for platform-specific RDKit wheels: 'pip install chemval[rdkit-linux]', '[rdkit-macos-arm64]', '[rdkit-windows]'. Filter database initialized on first run by copying bundled baseline. GitHub Actions publishes new version on version tag push using twine. No server infrastructure, no Docker, no Kubernetes.
target_output.observability: No persistent telemetry. Validation runs print summary to stdout: compounds checked, failed, parse errors, elapsed time. Stale filter database (>30 days) triggers warning to stderr. update-filters prints version change and rule count delta. Errors printed to stderr with descriptive messages and suggested remediation.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost. Distribution via internal PyPI at no marginal cost per installation. RDKit substructure matching scales linearly with compound count and filter rule count; 10,000 compounds against 500 rules completes in under 60 seconds on a standard workstation using RDKit in-process.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (weeks 1-2): Core validate command with SMILES CSV input, RDKit HasSubstructMatch against bundled PAINS A/B/C SMARTS, CSV report output. pytest with known PAINS-positive and PAINS-negative SMILES fixtures. Phase 2 (weeks 3-4): update-filters command with HTTPS fetch and SHA-256 verification, list-filters command, JSON output format, restricted reagent filter category. Internal PyPI publication and documentation. Phase 3 (future): SDF input support, compound registry API integration for batch triage.
target_output.development_guidelines: pytest for all modules: SMILESParser (fixture CSV with known PAINS positives from Baell 2010, invalid SMILES rows, and edge cases like empty smiles), SubstructureValidator (verify PAINS A/B/C match rates against published test set), ReportWriter (golden-file tests for CSV and JSON output format). 90% line coverage target. ruff + black enforced in CI. SMARTS in filter database pre-validated by a build script that runs MolFromSmarts on each rule and fails if any returns None. Changelog entry required for every filter database version bump.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: RDKit version mismatch between package wheels and chemist Python environment; mitigation is pinning RDKit version in package dependencies and including pre-built wheels. Risk: stale filter database if chemists skip update-filters; mitigated by 30-day stale warning on validate run. Tradeoff: bundled SQLite database means filter updates require explicit update-filters command rather than automatic versioning; accepted because offline validation is a hard requirement.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: CLI framework: Click selected for ergonomic argument parsing and subcommand grouping. Charting and UI: not applicable for CLI tool. Output formats: CSV (default) and JSON supported in MVP; SDF input deferred to phase 3. Filter update mechanism: explicit update-filters command preserving offline-first design.

metadata.notes: First-pass CLI tool for SMILES PAINS validation with Click/RDKit/SQLite; moderate quality with good core sections.

project_goal.value: Clinical trial supply chain management application tracking investigational medicinal product inventory across global depot sites, automating resupply orders, and providing real-time stock visibility to logistics coordinators
project_goal.rationale: Current manual spreadsheet-based IMP tracking cannot support the scale of simultaneous Phase 3 trials

target_users.value: Logistics coordinators at CRO and sponsor sites, depot site staff, and clinical operations managers globally
target_users.rationale: Multi-tier user base across sponsor, CRO, and depot organizations identified in requirements workshops

project_class.rationale: Requires React inventory dashboard, Spring Boot backend, batch resupply automation, and admin panel

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, integrations, analytics, admin_panel, batch_jobs
capabilities.rationale: Full operational scope: UI, API, IMP lot tracking, resupply batch automation, depot analytics, admin configuration

complexity_level.rationale: Global multi-site deployment, 50+ depot sites, thousands of IMP lots in concurrent trials, regulatory chain-of-custody requirements

risk_level.rationale: IMP misallocation or expiry failure could compromise trial integrity and patient safety; EU GMP Annex 13 and 21 CFR Part 312 apply

data_sensitivity.rationale: IMP lots linked to randomization lists and patient dispensation records; constitutes clinical trial data

external_exposure.rationale: Accessible to authenticated users at sponsor, CRO, and depot organizations over internet with Okta SSO

access_model.value: Okta OIDC SSO with organization-scoped RBAC: LogisticsCoordinator, DepotStaff, ClinicalOpsManager, SystemAdmin roles; organization-level data tenancy isolating sponsor trial data
access_model.rationale: Multi-organization access requires tenant isolation and role-based authorization across sponsor and CRO boundaries

feature_scope.value: IMP lot tracking from manufacture to patient dispensation, depot stock level dashboards, automated resupply order generation with multi-tier approval, shipment tracking, expiry alert management, randomization list integration, chain-of-custody audit trail, analytics for stock turns and expiry risk
feature_scope.rationale: Full supply chain visibility and automation required to manage Phase 3 trial IMP at scale

mvp_scope.value: IMP lot registration, depot stock level tracking, automated resupply order generation triggered by minimum threshold breach, multi-tier approval workflow, expiry alert engine
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum to replace spreadsheet tracking and prevent expiry losses in active trials

security_baseline.value: Okta OIDC MFA, TLS 1.3, field-level encryption for randomization list data, audit trail for all IMP lot state transitions, organization-level row security in PostgreSQL, WAF on ALB
security_baseline.rationale: Clinical trial data requires strong access controls; EU GMP and FDA require complete audit trail for IMP disposition

frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; Vite for fast build; React 18 concurrent features for real-time stock updates

backend_stack.value: Java 21 with Spring Boot 3 and Spring Data JPA
backend_stack.rationale: Enterprise-grade stability for regulated supply chain; Spring Batch for resupply automation; mature ecosystem for complex domain modeling

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security for multi-tenant isolation; Redis 7 for stock level caching and session management
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL RLS enforces organization-scoped tenancy at DB level; Redis for low-latency stock level reads

hosting_target.value: AWS EKS in us-east-1 and eu-west-1 (multi-region for EU trial data residency); RDS Aurora PostgreSQL Global Database
hosting_target.rationale: EU trial data must reside in eu-west-1 to satisfy GDPR and EU GMP data residency requirements

privacy_retention_policy.value: IMP lot and dispensation records retained 15 years per ICH Q10; audit logs retained 15 years; GDPR right-to-erasure applies only to user account data, not clinical records linked to trial subjects
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GxP pharmaceutical record retention; GDPR exemption for clinical trial records under Article 9(2)(j)

future_scope.value: Patient-level IMP dispensation tracking integration with EDC systems, predictive resupply using trial enrollment velocity, real-time shipment tracking API integration with DHL/FedEx
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 features after core IMP inventory management is operational

constraints.value: EU depot data must not egress to us-east-1; multi-region Aurora Global Database with write forwarding; SOC 2 Type II certification required within 12 months of go-live
constraints.rationale: EU data residency is a contractual requirement with EU trial sponsors; SOC 2 required for CRO qualification

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM, structured JSON logs, custom metrics for resupply order generation rate, expiry alert count by depot, shipment transit time p99
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate Datadog standard; supply chain-specific metrics for operations team SLA monitoring

execution_preference.value: Agile sprints with 2-week iterations; domain experts embedded in sprint reviews for IMP lot state machine validation
execution_preference.rationale: Complex domain requires frequent domain expert validation of state transitions and business rules

compliance_context.value: EU GMP Annex 13 (IMP labelling and reconciliation), FDA 21 CFR Part 312 (IND regulations), ICH Q10 (pharmaceutical quality system), GDPR (EU depot staff PII), SOC 2 Type II
compliance_context.rationale: Multi-jurisdiction trial supply chain with both EU and US IND trials; SOC 2 for CRO partner qualification

project.goal: IMP inventory tracking and automated resupply for global clinical trials
project.target_users: Logistics coordinators, depot staff, clinical ops managers

frontend.state: React Query for server state, Zustand for UI state

backend.framework: Spring Boot 3 + Spring Data JPA + Spring Batch

security.auth: Okta OIDC with MFA
security.rls: PostgreSQL row-level security for org tenancy

data.db: Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Global Database (us-east-1 primary, eu-west-1 secondary with write forwarding)

constraints.data_residency: EU depot data in eu-west-1 only

open_questions.shipment_tracking: DHL/FedEx API integration deferred to Phase 2

reasoner_reviews.product: The IMP lot lifecycle from manufacture through patient dispensation is the core value proposition. The automated resupply order generation with multi-tier approval is critical — manual resupply at this scale results in stockouts that can halt patient dosing. Expiry alert management is equally critical given GxP reconciliation requirements.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Spring Boot 3 with Spring Batch for resupply automation is the right choice: Spring Batch provides partitioned step execution suitable for evaluating stock thresholds across 50+ depots in parallel. Aurora Global Database with write forwarding satisfies the EU data residency constraint while maintaining a single logical database. PostgreSQL RLS enforces tenant isolation at the database layer, which is more reliable than application-layer filtering for regulated data. The resupply order approval workflow should use a state machine pattern (Spring StateMachine or a custom implementation) to ensure all required approver tiers are exercised.
reasoner_reviews.security: Multi-organization access with organization-scoped data requires PostgreSQL RLS policies enforcing current_setting('app.current_org_id') on all IMP lot, depot, and shipment tables. Okta OIDC with enforced MFA for all roles. Randomization list data, which links IMP lot numbers to patient identifiers, must be field-encrypted using AWS KMS. WAF on ALB with OWASP Core Rule Set. SOC 2 Type II requires CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, and access review automation.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Aurora Global Database write forwarding adds ~100ms latency for EU writes routed through us-east-1 primary. For EU depot staff performing stock adjustments, this is acceptable. Evaluate whether write forwarding latency is acceptable for high-frequency depot scan operations; if not, consider eu-west-1 Aurora cluster with async replication for EU-origin writes.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The Spring Batch resupply evaluation job should run nightly but also support on-demand trigger via an admin endpoint for emergency resupply situations. Ensure the phased_implementation covers the IQ/OQ validation approach required by EU GMP Annex 13 for the automated resupply module.

frontend.app_structure: React 18 Vite SPA served via CloudFront. React Router v6 for navigation. Okta React SDK for OIDC auth flow. React Query for all server state: IMP lots, depot stock levels, resupply orders, shipments. Zustand for UI state: selected trial, active filters, notification drawer state.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Stock Dashboard: per-depot stock level cards with threshold indicators (green/amber/red), sortable by expiry risk. IMP Lot List: searchable/filterable lot registry with status ENUM chip (manufactured/in_transit/at_depot/dispensed/destroyed). Resupply Orders: list with approval status and approver action buttons for pending orders. Shipment Tracker: shipment list with status timeline. Analytics: Recharts bar charts for stock turns by depot, expiry risk distribution, resupply lead time trends. Admin: depot configuration, threshold management, user-org assignment.
frontend.state_management: React Query: queries for depot stock levels polled every 60 seconds. Optimistic updates for acknowledgment actions. Zustand: trial filter context shared across dashboard and lot list. Cache invalidation on resupply order approval to refresh stock projections.
frontend.frontend_risks: Stock level polling at 60-second intervals may cause thundering herd from large concurrent user base; mitigate with staggered polling jitter in React Query refetchInterval.

backend.service_design: Spring Boot 3 monolith with domain packages: lots (IMP lot lifecycle), inventory (depot stock management), resupply (order generation and approval), shipments (transit tracking), analytics (aggregation queries), admin (configuration). Spring Batch job for nightly resupply evaluation. Spring StateMachine for IMP lot status transitions.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful API with OpenAPI 3.0 at /api/v1. Organization context injected from Okta JWT org_id claim and set via PostgreSQL SET LOCAL app.current_org_id before each request (RLS enforcement). Paginated list endpoints with cursor-based pagination for large lot registries.
backend.business_modules: ResupplyEvaluator (Spring Batch job): partitioned step over active depots, compares current_stock vs minimum_threshold, generates ResupplyOrder entities for breaches, persists in status=draft for approver action. ApprovalWorkflow: state machine with DRAFT -> PENDING_L1 -> PENDING_L2 -> APPROVED -> DISPATCHED transitions, each requiring role-specific approver JWT claim. ExpiryAlertEngine: nightly Spring Batch step scanning imp_lots for expiry_date within alert_lead_days, creating ExpiryAlert records and publishing SNS notifications.
backend.background_jobs: Nightly ResupplyEvaluationJob at 02:00 UTC: evaluates all active depot thresholds, generates draft orders. Nightly ExpiryAlertJob at 03:00 UTC: scans lots expiring within threshold, creates alerts. Weekly ReconciliationJob: compares dispensation records against lot quantities for GMP Annex 13 reconciliation report generation.

security.auth_design: Okta OIDC with enforced MFA for all users. Spring Security OAuth2 Resource Server validates Okta JWT on every API request. JWT claims include org_id (organization tenant) and roles array (LogisticsCoordinator, DepotStaff, ClinicalOpsManager, SystemAdmin).
security.authorization_model: Method-level @PreAuthorize annotations for role checks. PostgreSQL RLS policies: all tables with org_id column have USING (org_id = current_setting('app.current_org_id')::uuid) policy. Randomization list data encrypted with AWS KMS data key before persisting; decrypted on read only for ClinicalOpsManager and SystemAdmin roles.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: audit_trail table records every IMP lot status transition: lot_id, from_status, to_status, actor_user_id, actor_org_id, event_type, timestamp. Resupply order approval events appended to audit_trail with approver identity. Weekly ReconciliationJob output stored as immutable reconciliation_reports records. CloudTrail enabled for all AWS API calls; VPC Flow Logs for SOC 2.
security.security_risks: Aurora Global Database write forwarding means EU depot writes briefly transit us-east-1; data encrypted in transit via TLS 1.3 and at rest via AWS KMS. GDPR Data Processing Agreement required with AWS for EU data.

data.entities: organizations (id UUID PK, name, type ENUM sponsor/cro/depot, region ENUM us/eu). trials (id UUID PK, org_id FK, protocol_number, phase, status ENUM active/completed/closed). depot_sites (id UUID PK, org_id FK, name, country, region, contact_email). imp_lots (id UUID PK, org_id FK, trial_id FK, lot_number VARCHAR UNIQUE, product_code, quantity_units INTEGER, manufacture_date DATE, expiry_date DATE, status ENUM manufactured/in_transit/at_depot/dispensed/quarantined/destroyed, current_depot_id FK, chain_of_custody JSONB). stock_levels (id UUID PK, org_id FK, depot_id FK, product_code, trial_id FK, current_quantity, minimum_threshold, last_updated_at). resupply_orders (id UUID PK, org_id FK, depot_id FK, trial_id FK, product_code, quantity_requested, status ENUM draft/pending_l1/pending_l2/approved/dispatched/cancelled, generated_at, approved_at). shipments (id UUID PK, org_id FK, from_depot_id FK, to_depot_id FK, resupply_order_id FK, carrier, tracking_number, status ENUM preparing/in_transit/delivered, dispatched_at, delivered_at). expiry_records (id UUID PK, org_id FK, lot_id FK, alert_date DATE, expiry_date DATE, acknowledged_by, acknowledged_at). randomization_lists (id UUID PK, org_id FK, trial_id FK, list_data_encrypted TEXT, kms_key_id).
data.storage_design: Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Global Database: us-east-1 primary writer, eu-west-1 reader with write forwarding. PostgreSQL RLS on all tables with org_id. ElastiCache Redis 7 for stock_levels cache (invalidated on inventory adjustment). S3 for reconciliation report PDF storage with org-prefixed keys.
data.consistency_notes: IMP lot status transitions via Spring StateMachine with optimistic locking (version column) to prevent concurrent transition conflicts. Resupply order approval uses database-level advisory locks per order to prevent double-approval. Stock level adjustments run in serializable transactions to prevent over-dispatch.
data.retention_and_deletion: IMP lot records retained 15 years via S3 Glacier lifecycle after Aurora archive policy. Audit trail retained 15 years immutable. User account PII deletable on GDPR erasure request without affecting linked clinical records (replaced with anonymized user_id reference).

devops.deployment_topology: AWS EKS clusters in us-east-1 and eu-west-1. Kubernetes Deployments: api-service (Spring Boot, 3-8 pods HPA), frontend-service (Nginx serving Vite build, 2-4 pods). Spring Batch jobs as Kubernetes CronJobs. Aurora Global Database primary in us-east-1, replica with write forwarding in eu-west-1. ElastiCache Redis per region. CloudFront with multi-region origin failover.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: Maven build + Spring Boot test (unit + integration with Testcontainers Aurora/Redis), React build + Vitest, Docker push to ECR, Helm chart deploy to EKS via ArgoCD. Blue/green deployment via ArgoCD Rollouts for zero-downtime releases. Database migrations via Flyway applied as Kubernetes Job pre-deploy hook.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM on Spring Boot (dd-java-agent). Custom metrics: resupply_orders_generated_total (counter), expiry_alerts_active_count (gauge, labels: depot_region), stock_below_threshold_count (gauge), shipment_transit_time_p99 (timer). PagerDuty: stock_below_threshold > 0 for critical product_codes -> P1, expiry_alert unacknowledged >48h -> P2.
devops.cost_controls: EKS HPA on CPU 60% target. Reserved Aurora instances for baseline capacity. S3 Intelligent-Tiering for reconciliation reports. CloudFront cache for React SPA assets. Estimated ~$3,200/month for production multi-region deployment at Phase 1 scale.

target_output.thinking_summary: High-scale clinical trial IMP supply chain application with complex multi-organization, multi-region requirements. Key architectural decisions: Aurora Global Database with write forwarding satisfies EU data residency without dual-write complexity; PostgreSQL RLS at the DB layer is more robust than application filtering for regulated multi-tenant data; Spring Batch for resupply evaluation provides partitioned parallel depot assessment and supports on-demand trigger; Spring StateMachine for IMP lot transitions ensures all regulatory state changes are auditable. SOC 2 Type II controls (CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, access reviews) integrated from the start.
target_output.title: Clinical Trial IMP Supply Chain Management Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A high-scale fullstack application managing investigational medicinal product inventory across 50+ global depot sites for concurrent Phase 3 clinical trials. React 18 dashboards give logistics coordinators real-time stock visibility; Spring Boot 3 and Spring Batch automate resupply order generation with multi-tier approval workflows and expiry alert management. Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Global Database (us-east-1 primary, eu-west-1 with write forwarding) enforces EU data residency while row-level security provides organization-scoped multi-tenant isolation. IMP lot state transitions from manufacture through patient dispensation are tracked by a Spring StateMachine with full audit trail for EU GMP Annex 13 and FDA 21 CFR Part 312 compliance. Randomization list data is field-encrypted via AWS KMS. Deployed on AWS EKS multi-region via ArgoCD with GitLab CI.
target_output.architecture_overview: React 18 SPA served via CloudFront communicates over TLS 1.3 with a Spring Boot 3 REST API on AWS EKS. Okta OIDC MFA gates all access; the JWT org_id claim is injected into each PostgreSQL connection as SET LOCAL app.current_org_id to activate row-level security policies enforcing organization tenancy. Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Global Database maintains the primary writer in us-east-1 with a write-forwarding replica in eu-west-1 to satisfy EU trial data residency. ElastiCache Redis 7 caches depot stock levels (TTL 60s, invalidated on inventory events). Spring Batch CronJobs run nightly ResupplyEvaluationJob (partitioned by depot, generates draft resupply orders for threshold breaches) and ExpiryAlertJob (scans IMP lots expiring within alert_lead_days). A weekly ReconciliationJob produces GMP Annex 13 reconciliation reports stored immutably in S3. IMP lot status transitions are managed by Spring StateMachine with optimistic locking and full audit_trail writes on every transition. Datadog APM monitors all services with supply chain-specific metrics.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite, React Router v6, React Query, Zustand, Recharts, Okta React SDK. Backend: Java 21, Spring Boot 3, Spring Data JPA, Spring Batch, Spring StateMachine, Spring Security OAuth2 Resource Server, Flyway, Testcontainers for integration tests. Data: Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Global Database with RLS, ElastiCache Redis 7, S3 for reconciliation reports and audit exports. Infrastructure: AWS EKS (us-east-1 + eu-west-1), CloudFront, ALB with WAF (OWASP CRS), AWS KMS, CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, ECR, GitLab CI, ArgoCD with Rollouts. Observability: Datadog APM (dd-java-agent), PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: IMP Lot Registry: register lot from manufacture with product_code, quantity, expiry_date; track status through manufactured/in_transit/at_depot/dispensed/destroyed via Spring StateMachine. Depot Stock Dashboard: real-time stock levels per depot with threshold indicators; stock level updated on lot arrival scan and dispensation record. Automated Resupply: nightly Spring Batch job evaluates stock vs minimum_threshold per depot; generates draft ResupplyOrder; routes through L1 (LogisticsCoordinator) and L2 (ClinicalOpsManager) approval; dispatches to shipment on final approval. Expiry Alert Engine: nightly scan of imp_lots expiring within configurable lead days; creates expiry_records, pushes SNS notification to depot contacts; escalates unacknowledged alerts after 48h. Chain-of-Custody Audit: every IMP lot state transition appended to audit_trail with actor identity and timestamp. Analytics: stock turns by depot (Recharts), expiry risk heat map, resupply lead time trends. Admin Panel: depot configuration, threshold management, trial-depot association, user-org assignment.
target_output.system_components: CloudFront (React SPA CDN + multi-origin failover). ALB with WAF OWASP CRS (per EKS region). EKS api-service Deployment (Spring Boot 3, 3-8 pods HPA). EKS frontend-service Deployment (Nginx, 2-4 pods). Spring Batch CronJobs: ResupplyEvaluationJob (nightly 02:00 UTC), ExpiryAlertJob (03:00 UTC), ReconciliationJob (weekly). Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Global Database (primary us-east-1, eu-west-1 write-forwarding replica). ElastiCache Redis 7 (per region). AWS KMS (randomization list field encryption). SNS (expiry and escalation notifications). S3 (reconciliation report PDFs). CloudTrail + VPC Flow Logs (SOC 2). Datadog Agent DaemonSet. ArgoCD (GitOps deployment).
target_output.workflows: Resupply Order Workflow: Nightly at 02:00 UTC, ResupplyEvaluationJob Spring Batch job starts. Partitioner creates one JobStep per active depot. Each step queries stock_levels WHERE current_quantity < minimum_threshold for that depot. For each breach, creates ResupplyOrder entity in status=draft with computed quantity_requested = (target_level - current_quantity). Job completes; total draft orders logged to Datadog metric resupply_orders_generated_total. L1 approver (LogisticsCoordinator) receives email notification; logs into dashboard, reviews draft orders, clicks Approve to transition status=draft -> pending_l2. L2 approver (ClinicalOpsManager) approves pending_l2 order; status transitions to approved; shipment record created with depot routing and carrier assignment. IMP Lot State Transition: DepotStaff scans lot barcode on arrival; PATCH /api/v1/lots/{id}/transition body {event: ARRIVE, depot_id}. Spring StateMachine validates in_transit -> at_depot transition. Optimistic lock version check prevents concurrent scan conflicts. audit_trail record appended: lot_id, in_transit, at_depot, actor_user_id, timestamp. Stock level for product_code at depot_id incremented by lot quantity; Redis cache invalidated for depot stock key.
target_output.data_model: organizations (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, type ENUM('sponsor','cro','depot'), region ENUM('us','eu')). trials (id UUID PK, org_id UUID FK, protocol_number VARCHAR UNIQUE, phase SMALLINT, status ENUM('active','completed','closed')). depot_sites (id UUID PK, org_id UUID FK, name VARCHAR, country CHAR(2), region ENUM('us','eu'), contact_email VARCHAR). imp_lots (id UUID PK, org_id UUID FK, trial_id UUID FK, lot_number VARCHAR UNIQUE NOT NULL, product_code VARCHAR, quantity_units INTEGER, manufacture_date DATE, expiry_date DATE NOT NULL, status ENUM('manufactured','in_transit','at_depot','dispensed','quarantined','destroyed'), current_depot_id UUID FK->depot_sites, chain_of_custody JSONB, version INTEGER DEFAULT 0, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). stock_levels (id UUID PK, org_id UUID FK, depot_id UUID FK, product_code VARCHAR, trial_id UUID FK, current_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL, minimum_threshold INTEGER NOT NULL, last_updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). resupply_orders (id UUID PK, org_id UUID FK, depot_id UUID FK, trial_id UUID FK, product_code VARCHAR, quantity_requested INTEGER, status ENUM('draft','pending_l1','pending_l2','approved','dispatched','cancelled'), generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ). shipments (id UUID PK, org_id UUID FK, from_depot_id UUID FK, to_depot_id UUID FK, resupply_order_id UUID FK, carrier VARCHAR, tracking_number VARCHAR, status ENUM('preparing','in_transit','delivered'), dispatched_at TIMESTAMPTZ, delivered_at TIMESTAMPTZ). audit_trail (id BIGSERIAL PK, org_id UUID, entity_type VARCHAR, entity_id UUID, from_state VARCHAR, to_state VARCHAR, actor_user_id VARCHAR NOT NULL, event_type VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL). expiry_records (id UUID PK, org_id UUID FK, lot_id UUID FK, alert_date DATE, expiry_date DATE, acknowledged_by VARCHAR, acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ). randomization_lists (id UUID PK, org_id UUID FK, trial_id UUID FK, list_data_encrypted TEXT, kms_key_id VARCHAR). PostgreSQL RLS policy on all org_id tables: USING (org_id = current_setting('app.current_org_id')::uuid). Clinical trial entities: trial_patients (id UUID PK, site_id FK, subject_id_encrypted VARCHAR UNIQUE, enrollment_date DATE, treatment_arm VARCHAR, diagnosis_icd10 VARCHAR, current_visit_number INT, withdrawal_date DATE), clinical_encounters (id UUID PK, patient_id FK, visit_number INT, encounter_date DATE, investigator_id UUID, vitals_json JSONB, adverse_event_flags JSONB), investigational_prescriptions (id UUID PK, patient_id FK, encounter_id FK, kit_id FK, dosage_mg NUMERIC, administration_route VARCHAR, prescribed_by_physician_id UUID, dispensed_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: RESTful API /api/v1 with OpenAPI 3.0. Auth: Okta Bearer JWT on all endpoints; org_id claim sets RLS context. GET /depots: list depot sites for caller org. GET /depots/:id/stock: current stock levels per product for depot, backed by Redis cache. GET /lots: paginated IMP lot registry with filters status, trial_id, expiry_before. PATCH /lots/:id/transition: state transition event (ARRIVE/DISPENSE/QUARANTINE/DESTROY), body {event, depot_id?}. GET /resupply-orders: list orders with status filter. PATCH /resupply-orders/:id/approve: L1/L2 approval action based on caller role. POST /resupply-orders/evaluate: on-demand trigger for SystemAdmin to run resupply evaluation outside nightly schedule. GET /expiry-records: active expiry alerts with depot and lot detail. PATCH /expiry-records/:id/acknowledge: acknowledge expiry alert. GET /analytics/stock-turns?depot_id=&from=&to=: stock turn analytics aggregation. GET /audit-trail?entity_type=imp_lot&entity_id=: audit trail for entity.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Okta OIDC with enforced MFA; Spring Security OAuth2 Resource Server validates JWT on every request; org_id claim injected as SET LOCAL app.current_org_id for PostgreSQL RLS enforcement before each transaction. PostgreSQL RLS USING policies on all 10 clinical tables preventing cross-organization data access at database layer. AWS WAF on ALB with OWASP Core Rule Set 3.3 blocking SQLi and XSS. Randomization list list_data_encrypted via AWS KMS GenerateDataKey with per-trial data key; decrypted in-memory only for ClinicalOpsManager and SystemAdmin. audit_trail: REVOKE UPDATE, DELETE FROM api_role; append-only. IMP lot state transitions guarded by Spring StateMachine ensuring only valid regulatory transitions are executed. CloudTrail + VPC Flow Logs enabled for SOC 2 Type II evidence collection. Access review automation via Okta Lifecycle Management for quarterly user certification. EU GMP Annex 13 satisfied by complete lot-level audit trail and weekly ReconciliationJob reports. FDA 21 CFR Part 312 satisfied by chain-of-custody JSONB on imp_lots and audit_trail.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: AWS EKS clusters in us-east-1 (primary) and eu-west-1 (EU depot data residency). api-service Deployment: minReplicas 3, maxReplicas 8, HPA on CPU 60%. Spring Batch CronJobs: ResupplyEvaluationJob (02:00 UTC), ExpiryAlertJob (03:00 UTC), ReconciliationJob (Sundays 04:00 UTC). ArgoCD manages GitOps sync from GitLab CI artifact registry. ArgoCD Rollouts: Blue/Green for api-service with analysis run (error rate <1%, p99 <500ms) before traffic shift. Flyway migrations applied as Kubernetes Job pre-deploy hook; down migrations tested in CI. GitLab CI pipeline: Maven build, Spring Boot integration tests (Testcontainers Aurora + Redis), React build, Vitest, Docker push ECR, Helm chart update triggers ArgoCD sync. Environments: dev (single AZ, shared Aurora), staging (multi-AZ, Aurora Global disabled), production (multi-region, Aurora Global, ElastiCache per region). Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with EKS IRSA. Aurora Global Database: us-east-1 primary writer, eu-west-1 reader with write forwarding enabled.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM: dd-java-agent on api-service pods, traces correlated with Spring Batch job spans. Structured JSON logs with trace_id, org_id, user_id, lot_id context fields. Custom metrics: resupply_orders_generated_total (counter, labels: trial_id, depot_region), expiry_alerts_active_count (gauge, labels: depot_region, days_to_expiry_bucket), stock_below_threshold_count (gauge, labels: product_code, depot_region), shipment_transit_time_p99 (timer, labels: carrier). PagerDuty alerts: stock_below_threshold >0 for product_codes flagged critical -> P1, expiry_alert unacknowledged >48h -> P2, Spring Batch job failure -> P2, Aurora Global replication lag >30s -> P1.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $3,200/month for multi-region production at Phase 1 scale (50 depots, 5 concurrent trials). EKS EC2 worker nodes: ~$1,200/month (m5.xlarge x4 per region). Aurora Global Database (r6g.large primary + eu replica): ~$800/month. ElastiCache Redis (cache.m6g.large per region): ~$400/month. CloudFront + ALB + WAF: ~$200/month. Datadog: ~$400/month for APM + log management. S3 + KMS + CloudTrail: ~$200/month. HPA scales api-service to 8 pods for peak resupply approval windows. Spring Batch partitioned resupply job evaluates 50 depots in parallel under 2 minutes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (weeks 1-6): Core infrastructure — EKS clusters us-east-1 and eu-west-1, Aurora Global Database with RLS policies and Flyway schema, ElastiCache Redis, Okta OIDC integration with org_id claim mapping, ArgoCD setup. IMP lot registration and Spring StateMachine state transitions. PostgreSQL RLS validation with multi-org test fixtures. Phase 2 (weeks 7-10): Depot stock level tracking with Redis cache, React 18 stock dashboard with 60s polling, minimum threshold configuration. Spring Batch ResupplyEvaluationJob with partitioned depot steps; draft order generation. Phase 3 (weeks 11-14): Multi-tier approval workflow, Resupply order UI for L1/L2 approvers, shipment record creation on order approval. ExpiryAlertJob with SNS notifications and 48h escalation logic. Phase 4 (weeks 15-16): Analytics dashboards (stock turns, expiry risk), audit trail viewer, ReconciliationJob for GMP Annex 13 reports, Admin panel for depot and threshold configuration. SOC 2 evidence collection infrastructure (CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, access review automation) activated from week 1.
target_output.development_guidelines: Java: JUnit 5 + Mockito for unit tests; Testcontainers for Spring Boot integration tests with Aurora-compatible PostgreSQL and Redis. Spring StateMachine transitions must have unit tests covering all valid and invalid event scenarios. Spring Batch job steps tested with in-memory JobRepository. 85% line coverage gate. React: Vitest for component tests; React Testing Library. ESLint + Prettier in GitLab CI. OpenAPI 3.0 spec auto-generated from Spring controllers via springdoc-openapi; spec committed and validated in CI. Flyway migration naming: V{version}__{description}.sql; down migrations required for all DDL changes. ADR documents required for: RLS policy changes, new IMP lot state transitions, Spring Batch job additions. No direct Aurora access from developer laptops; all DB access via EKS Jobs with temporary IAM credentials.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Aurora Global Database write forwarding adds ~100ms latency for EU depot writes routing through us-east-1; mitigation is evaluate eu-west-1 independent Aurora cluster if depot scan throughput requires sub-50ms writes. Risk: Spring Batch ResupplyEvaluationJob runtime grows linearly with active depot count; mitigation is partition granularity tuning and timeout monitoring per partition step. Risk: PostgreSQL RLS policy misconfiguration could expose cross-org data; mitigation is mandatory multi-org data isolation test suite run on every schema migration.
