Tradeoff: Spring StateMachine adds framework complexity vs custom state validation; accepted because regulated IMP lot transitions require enforceable state machine with clear audit semantics. Risk: SOC 2 Type II timeline requires 6-month observation period starting at go-live; CloudTrail and access review automation must be operational from day one.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Aurora Global Database write forwarding selected over dual Aurora clusters for EU data residency — simpler operational model with acceptable latency. Spring StateMachine selected for IMP lot transitions over custom state validation — provides auditable transition history and enforced valid-transition rules. DHL/FedEx shipment tracking API integration deferred to Phase 2 — manual tracking_number entry sufficient for Phase 1. On-demand resupply evaluation via POST /resupply-orders/evaluate admin endpoint added to support emergency restocking outside nightly batch schedule.

metadata.notes: First-pass strong plan for high-scale clinical trial IMP supply chain on AWS EKS multi-region with EU data residency, Spring Batch automation, and multi-tier GMP compliance.

project_goal.value: Replace shared SSH keys and flat VPC networking with Vault-based dynamic credentials and micro-segmented security groups across the preclinical research compute environment
project_goal.rationale: Eliminate long-lived credentials and lateral movement risk in regulated research compute

target_users.value: Preclinical research engineers, IT security team, automated compute workloads
target_users.rationale: Both human operators and automated jobs require zero-trust credential access

project_class.rationale: Purely platform and environment architecture with no application UI

capabilities.rationale: Infrastructure automation and Vault backend service only; no frontend required

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-layer security architecture spanning Vault, Terraform, AWS networking, and IAM

risk_level.rationale: Regulated research environment with confidential compound data; misconfiguration could expose IP

data_sensitivity.rationale: Preclinical compound and assay data classified as confidential IP

external_exposure.rationale: Vault and compute clusters are internal; no external ingress

access_model.value: Vault AppRole for workloads, OIDC federation for human operators via AWS IAM Identity Center
access_model.rationale: Machine identity and human identity handled separately with different TTLs

feature_scope.value: Vault cluster with dynamic AWS/database secrets engines, Terraform micro-segmentation modules, Transit Gateway routing, automated credential rotation
feature_scope.rationale: Core zero-trust capabilities required for the overhaul

mvp_scope.value: Vault AppRole dynamic credentials for top-5 high-risk compute clusters, security group micro-segmentation blocking cross-cluster lateral movement
mvp_scope.rationale: Highest-risk attack surface addressed first

security_baseline.value: mTLS between Vault agents and Vault server, credential TTL max 1 hour, audit logging to S3, no shared static secrets
security_baseline.rationale: SOC 2 and NIST SP 800-53 AC-2 and SC-8 controls

frontend_stack.rationale: No user-facing frontend; Vault UI accessible internally for operators only

backend_stack.value: HashiCorp Vault 1.16, Terraform 1.8, AWS provider
backend_stack.rationale: Standard Vault and IaC tooling for secrets management

data_platform.value: Vault integrated storage (Raft) for secrets; Vault audit log streamed to S3
data_platform.rationale: Raft HA avoids external Consul dependency

hosting_target.value: 3-node Vault cluster on EC2 m6i.large across 3 AZs, VPC with Transit Gateway for cluster interconnect
hosting_target.rationale: HA Raft requires odd-node quorum across AZs

privacy_retention_policy.value: Vault audit logs retained 1 year in S3 with Object Lock, access logs 90 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: SOC 2 audit evidence retention requirement

future_scope.value: Vault PKI engine for internal certificate authority, dynamic cloud credentials for GCP satellite environments
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 PKI and multi-cloud expansion

constraints.value: Must not disrupt active preclinical compute jobs during migration; Vault rollout phased per cluster; budget under $3000/month for Vault infrastructure
constraints.rationale: Research continuity and cost ceiling

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for Vault metrics (token lease rate, credential request latency, seal status), PagerDuty for seal events
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate monitoring standard for infrastructure

execution_preference.value: Terraform modules applied per-cluster via GitHub Actions, reviewed and approved before plan execution
execution_preference.rationale: Infrastructure changes require peer review and audit trail

compliance_context.value: SOC 2 Type II, NIST SP 800-53 (AC-2, AC-6, SC-8, SI-7), internal GxP IT infrastructure policy
compliance_context.rationale: Research compute environment subject to SOC 2 and internal GxP IT controls

project.goal: Zero-trust secrets and network segmentation overhaul for preclinical compute
project.target_users: Research engineers and automated compute workloads
project.timeline: Phased over 12 weeks, one cluster per 2-week sprint

security.auth: Vault AppRole for workloads, IAM Identity Center OIDC for humans
security.transport: mTLS Vault agent to Vault server, TLS 1.3 for all traffic

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with Terraform plan/apply workflow

constraints.disruption: zero downtime for active compute jobs

open_questions.pki_engine: Vault PKI for internal CA deferred to phase 2

confirmed_decisions.vault_version: 1.16 with KV v2 and AWS secrets engine

reasoner_reviews.product: The zero-trust scope is well-scoped to address the highest-risk attack surface: long-lived SSH keys and flat networking. Phasing by cluster reduces blast radius during migration and allows the security team to validate credential behavior before rolling forward.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Vault Raft HA on 3 EC2 nodes across AZs is the right call to eliminate Consul dependency. AppRole with response-wrapped secret IDs prevents secret ID exfiltration. Terraform modules for security groups must enforce deny-all default with explicit allow rules per cluster-pair to achieve true micro-segmentation.
reasoner_reviews.security: The previous plan lacked explicit TTL enforcement and token renewal policy. Dynamic credentials must have max_ttl=1h with no renewal on AWS secrets engine roles. Vault audit device must be enabled on all nodes with fallback to syslog if S3 write fails to prevent audit bypass.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Budget of $3000/month is feasible for 3x m6i.large plus S3 audit storage. Migration phasing over 12 weeks is realistic but requires a parallel-run window per cluster to allow old SSH keys to coexist during cutover.

backend.service_design: Vault Raft cluster with AWS secrets engine (dynamic IAM credentials per workload role), KV v2 for static config secrets, Transit engine for envelope encryption of sensitive pipeline outputs
backend.api_patterns: Vault HTTP API consumed by workloads via Vault Agent sidecar with auto-auth AppRole; humans use Vault CLI via IAM Identity Center OIDC login
backend.failure_handling: Vault Agent caches credentials locally; on Vault unreachable, agent serves cached lease until TTL expiry; PagerDuty alert on seal event within 60 seconds
backend.scaling_notes: Raft performance standby nodes serve read requests; write quorum requires leader; scale read capacity by adding non-voter nodes

security.auth_design: AppRole with response-wrapped secret IDs injected via GitHub Actions OIDC token exchange; human operators authenticate via IAM Identity Center OIDC bound to Vault OIDC auth method
security.authorization_model: Vault policies scoped per research cluster (cluster-A-policy, cluster-B-policy etc); policies grant only aws/creds/cluster-A-role path; no cross-cluster credential access
security.secrets_management: AWS secrets engine issues dynamic IAM credentials with cluster-scoped permissions; max_ttl=1h, no manual renewal; PostgreSQL secrets engine for bioinformatics DB access with 30-min TTL
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Vault audit log (JSON format) streamed to S3 bucket with Object Lock (WORM) and server-side encryption; syslog secondary; file tertiary; CloudTrail captures IAM credential usage per dynamic lease

data.entities: vault_policies (name, rules_hcl, cluster_scope), dynamic_credentials (lease_id, role, issued_at, expires_at, revoked), security_groups (sg_id, cluster_name, ingress_rules_json, egress_rules_json), access_logs (timestamp, accessor, operation, path, remote_addr)
data.storage_design: Vault Raft integrated storage with automatic snapshots every 6 hours to S3; no external database for Vault state
data.retention_and_deletion: Audit logs in S3 with Object Lock 1-year retention; Vault lease records purged after TTL expiry; snapshots retained 30 days

devops.deployment_topology: 3-node Vault EC2 cluster (m6i.large) across us-east-1a/1b/1c behind an internal NLB; Vault Agent DaemonSet on each compute cluster node
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions workflow: terraform fmt/validate on PR, terraform plan output posted as PR comment, terraform apply on merge to main with required approval; Vault policy changes peer-reviewed by security team
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scrapes Vault /sys/metrics endpoint; Grafana dashboards for token lease count, secret engine request rate, seal status, Raft replication lag; PagerDuty alert on vault.core.unsealed=0
devops.rollback_strategy: Vault Raft snapshot restore procedure documented and tested quarterly; Terraform state stored in S3 with versioning; security group rollback via Terraform destroy+apply of previous module version

ISS-001.title: Vault audit device fallback not configured
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan did not specify audit device fallback behavior; if S3 write fails, Vault may block all operations rather than degrade gracefully, causing denial-of-service on the compute environment.

history.note: Identified by security reviewer; no fallback syslog device specified

ISS-002.title: TTL enforcement absent from AWS secrets engine role definitions
ISS-002.detail: The previous plan described dynamic credentials but did not specify max_ttl values for AWS secrets engine roles, leaving open the possibility of multi-day credential lifetimes.

history.note: Architect reviewer flagged missing max_ttl=1h constraint

focus_issues.title: Vault audit device fallback not configured
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 plan did not specify audit device fallback behavior; if S3 write fails, Vault may block all operations rather than degrade gracefully.

focus_issues.title: TTL enforcement absent from AWS secrets engine role definitions
focus_issues.detail: The previous plan described dynamic credentials but did not specify max_ttl values for AWS secrets engine roles.

latest_recommendations: Add syslog as secondary audit device with file device as tertiary so Vault degrades gracefully if S3 is unreachable
Specify max_ttl=1h and default_ttl=30m on all AWS secrets engine role definitions in Terraform HCL

previous_audits.summary: Solid Vault architecture foundation but two high-severity gaps in audit device resilience and credential TTL enforcement leave the plan non-compliant with SOC 2 AC-2 controls.
strengths: Raft HA topology across 3 AZs correctly specified
AppRole with response-wrapped secret IDs is best practice
Terraform module-per-cluster approach enables safe phased rollout

AWS secrets engine roles have no max_ttl defined

TTL enforcement missing - credential lifetime unbounded

Define max_ttl=1h default_ttl=30m in all Terraform aws_secretsengine_role resources

previous_plan.title: Zero-Trust Preclinical Compute Infrastructure Plan v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Initial plan establishing Vault Raft HA cluster with AppRole dynamic credentials and Terraform-managed security group micro-segmentation for preclinical compute environment.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: 3-node Vault Raft cluster on EC2 behind internal NLB; Vault Agent sidecars on compute nodes; Terraform modules enforcing per-cluster security groups via Transit Gateway routing.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: AppRole authentication with response-wrapped secret IDs; Vault audit log to S3; mTLS between agents and Vault server. Audit fallback not addressed.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions Terraform workflow with plan/apply separation; phased rollout one cluster per sprint. TTL values for secrets engine roles not specified in IaC.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 focuses on two blocking security gaps from the prior audit: Vault audit device fallback configuration and TTL enforcement on AWS secrets engine roles. Both are SOC 2 AC-2 and NIST SI-7 compliance requirements. The plan adds syslog secondary and file tertiary audit devices, and explicitly specifies max_ttl=1h and default_ttl=30m in all secrets engine role Terraform definitions. All other architecture elements from round 1 are preserved and refined.

fix_report.action_taken: Added syslog audit device as secondary and local file device as tertiary in Vault configuration; documented fallback escalation: S3 primary -> syslog secondary -> file tertiary; configured audit_non_hmac_request_keys to prevent sensitive data leakage in audit log
fix_report.expected_outcome: Vault audit logging remains operational even if S3 is unreachable; SOC 2 audit continuity requirement satisfied

fix_report.action_taken: Added explicit max_ttl=1h and default_ttl=30m to all AWS secrets engine role Terraform resources; documented lease TTL policy in development guidelines; added Prometheus alert for leases approaching max_ttl as operational check
fix_report.expected_outcome: Dynamic IAM credentials cannot exceed 1-hour lifetime regardless of renewal attempts; NIST AC-2 credential lifecycle control satisfied

target_output.title: Zero-Trust Preclinical Compute Infrastructure - Security Hardening v2
target_output.executive_summary: A zero-trust infrastructure overhaul for a preclinical research compute environment on AWS, replacing flat VPC networking and shared SSH keys with HashiCorp Vault 1.16 Raft HA dynamic credentials and Terraform-managed micro-segmented security groups. Round 2 resolves audit device fallback and TTL enforcement gaps identified in the prior audit, achieving SOC 2 Type II and NIST SP 800-53 compliance posture.
target_output.architecture_overview: A 3-node HashiCorp Vault Raft cluster deployed across us-east-1a/1b/1c on EC2 m6i.large instances behind an internal NLB handles all secrets issuance for the preclinical compute environment. Vault Agent sidecars run as a DaemonSet on each compute cluster node, authenticating via AppRole with response-wrapped secret IDs. The AWS secrets engine issues dynamic IAM credentials scoped per research cluster with max_ttl=1h enforced in Terraform HCL. Terraform modules manage per-cluster security groups via Transit Gateway, enforcing deny-all default with explicit allow rules between approved cluster pairs. Audit events flow to S3 WORM bucket (primary), syslog aggregator (secondary), and local file (tertiary) for uninterrupted SOC 2-compliant logging.
target_output.technology_stack: Infrastructure: HashiCorp Vault 1.16 (Raft HA, KV v2, AWS secrets engine, Transit engine), Terraform 1.8 with AWS provider 5.x, Vault Agent 1.16. Compute: EC2 m6i.large (Vault nodes), EC2 Auto Scaling Groups (research clusters). Networking: AWS VPC, Transit Gateway, micro-segmented security groups per cluster. Auth: Vault AppRole with response-wrapped secret IDs (workloads), IAM Identity Center OIDC (human operators). Observability: Prometheus, Grafana, PagerDuty. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with OIDC token exchange for Terraform apply. Audit: S3 Object Lock, rsyslog, CloudWatch Logs.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Dynamic Credential Issuance: Vault AWS secrets engine generates short-lived IAM credentials (max_ttl=1h, default_ttl=30m) per cluster-scoped role on demand, replacing static IAM access keys. AppRole Authentication: Compute workloads authenticate via AppRole with response-wrapped secret IDs injected through GitHub Actions OIDC exchange during job launch. Micro-Segmentation: Terraform modules create per-cluster security groups with deny-all default, blocking all cross-cluster traffic except explicitly whitelisted service ports. Triple Audit Logging: S3 primary with WORM retention, syslog secondary, file tertiary ensuring continuity if any device fails. Operator Access: Human engineers authenticate via IAM Identity Center OIDC bound to Vault OIDC auth method with MFA enforced.
target_output.system_components: Vault Raft cluster (3 EC2 m6i.large nodes, 3 AZs), internal NLB (Vault API routing), Vault Agent DaemonSet (per compute node, auto-auth + credential caching), AWS Transit Gateway (inter-cluster routing), per-cluster VPC security groups (Terraform-managed deny-all default), S3 audit bucket (Object Lock WORM, SSE-S3, 1-year retention), rsyslog aggregator (secondary audit device), CloudWatch Logs (tertiary file audit forwarded), IAM Identity Center (operator SSO with MFA), Prometheus + Grafana (Vault metrics dashboards), PagerDuty (seal event and audit failure alerting).
target_output.workflows: Dynamic Credential Request: Compute job starts on cluster-A, Vault Agent auto-auths via AppRole using response-wrapped secret ID injected at launch (60-second wrap TTL), requests AWS credentials from path aws/creds/cluster-A-role, receives temporary IAM key pair (max_ttl=1h); credentials used for job duration; on completion agent explicitly revokes lease via POST /v1/sys/leases/revoke/{lease_id}. Human Operator Vault Access: Engineer authenticates to IAM Identity Center with MFA, receives OIDC token, Vault OIDC auth method validates token audience and bound_claims, issues Vault token with cluster-admin policy (8-hour TTL, no renewal); operator manages Vault via CLI or internal Vault UI at https://vault.internal:8200. Terraform Drift Detection: GitHub Actions nightly workflow runs terraform plan against all cluster modules; diff output posted to security Slack channel and GitHub issue if any state drift detected between Git source and AWS actual state.
target_output.data_model: vault_policies (name VARCHAR PK, cluster_scope VARCHAR, rules_hcl TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMP, updated_at TIMESTAMP) stored in Vault KV v2 at secret/policies/{cluster}. dynamic_credential_leases (lease_id VARCHAR PK, role_name VARCHAR, issued_at TIMESTAMP, max_ttl_seconds INT DEFAULT 3600, revoked BOOLEAN, revoked_at TIMESTAMP) derived from Vault audit log parsed to PostgreSQL for compliance reporting. security_groups (sg_id VARCHAR PK, cluster_name VARCHAR, ingress_rules_json JSONB, egress_rules_json JSONB, last_applied_at TIMESTAMP) sourced from Terraform state. network_segments (segment_id VARCHAR PK, cidr_block CIDR, cluster_name VARCHAR, transit_gateway_attachment_id VARCHAR) from Terraform state. access_logs (id UUID PK, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ, accessor VARCHAR, operation VARCHAR, vault_path VARCHAR, remote_addr INET, audit_device VARCHAR DEFAULT 's3') parsed from S3 JSON audit stream.
target_output.api_design: Vault HTTP API endpoints consumed by workloads and operators: POST /v1/auth/approle/login (AppRole login, returns client_token with cluster-scoped policy), GET /v1/aws/creds/cluster-{name}-role (dynamic IAM credential issuance, returns access_key/secret_key/lease_id), POST /v1/sys/leases/revoke/{lease_id} (explicit credential revocation post-job), GET /v1/sys/health (cluster health, returns standby/sealed status for load balancer checks), PUT /v1/secret/data/policies/{cluster} (KV v2 policy storage by security team), GET /v1/sys/metrics (Prometheus scrape endpoint, returns token_creation_total, request_count, seal_status).
target_output.security_and_compliance: SOC 2 Type II and NIST SP 800-53 controls implemented: AC-2 (dynamic credentials max_ttl=1h enforced in Terraform vault_aws_secret_backend_role resources, no static IAM keys), AC-6 (Vault policies grant only cluster-specific aws/creds path, cross-cluster access forbidden), SC-8 (mTLS between Vault Agent and Vault server via mutual TLS certificates, TLS 1.3 minimum), SI-7 (Terraform drift detection nightly, any deviation triggers alert). Audit device configuration: S3 primary (Object Lock WORM, SSE-S3, 1-year retention, audit_non_hmac_request_keys configured), syslog secondary (rsyslog forwarding to SIEM aggregator), file tertiary (/var/log/vault/audit.log with logrotate 90-day). Vault auto-unseals via AWS KMS CMK; seal events trigger PagerDuty P1 within 60 seconds. Response-wrapped AppRole secret IDs with 60-second wrap TTL prevent exfiltration of long-lived secret IDs.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Vault cluster provisioned via Terraform user_data with Vault config including raft storage block, listener with tls_cert_file, and three audit device stanzas (file s3-audit, syslog rsyslog, file local-file). GitHub Actions CI/CD: on PR open - terraform fmt validate plan, plan diff posted as PR comment requiring security-team approval; on merge to main - terraform apply with manual approval gate. Phased cluster rollout over 12 weeks: 2-week sprint per cluster group, parallel SSH+AppRole window during cutover, SSH key revocation after 1-week stability validation. Vault Agent deployed via Ansible playbook on compute nodes: agent config with auto_auth AppRole block, cache stanza with use_auto_auth_token, and 6-hour snapshot schedule to S3 via cron. Vault binary updates via Terraform taint+replace on one node at a time to maintain quorum.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes Vault /sys/metrics every 30 seconds: vault_token_creation_total (rate of new credentials issued), vault_core_request_count (API throughput per secrets engine), vault_aws_secret_path_roll_seconds (credential issuance latency), vault_raft_replication_dataBytes (replication lag between Raft nodes), vault_core_unsealed (seal status gauge). Grafana dashboards: Vault cluster health (seal/unseal timeline, leader election events), credential request rate per cluster role (identifies unusual spikes), lease age distribution (validates max_ttl=1h compliance), audit write latency per device (detects S3 degradation before failover). PagerDuty alerts: vault_core_unsealed=0 (P1 immediate page), aws_secret_error_rate >5% over 5 minutes (P2 15-min), S3 audit write latency >2s (P3, triggers syslog fallback notification to security team).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $1,800/month: 3x EC2 m6i.large at $140/node ($420 total), S3 audit storage at $50/month for approximately 1TB/year at 5000 requests/day, Transit Gateway at $200/month, internal NLB at $50/month, Prometheus/Grafana EC2 at $80/month. Reserved instance pricing for Vault nodes reduces to $1,500/month after 12-month commitment. Raft read scaling by adding performance standby nodes at $140/node/month without disrupting write quorum. Vault Enterprise not required for single-namespace deployment; cost justified if multi-namespace or HSM auto-unseal needed.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (weeks 1-4): Vault Raft cluster provisioned with triple audit device configuration, AppRole and OIDC auth methods configured, AWS secrets engine with cluster-A-role (max_ttl=1h, default_ttl=30m), security group micro-segmentation for cluster-A, GitHub Actions Terraform workflow with approval gates, Prometheus/Grafana dashboards live. Phase 2 (weeks 5-8): Roll out dynamic credentials and micro-segmentation to compute clusters B through D; decommission SSH key access on migrated clusters after 1-week parallel-run validation; SOC 2 audit evidence collection begins. Phase 3 (weeks 9-12): Complete remaining clusters E and F, full SSH key retirement org-wide, Vault Transit engine for pipeline output envelope encryption, documentation of Vault PKI engine design for Phase 4.
target_output.development_guidelines: All Vault policy HCL and Terraform module code version-controlled in Git; no manual Vault CLI edits to production policies. Terraform resources must explicitly set max_ttl=3600 and default_ttl=1800 on all vault_aws_secret_backend_role resources - CI linting rule enforces this. Security group modules must include deny-all default ingress/egress rules; wildcard 0.0.0.0/0 CIDR blocks fail PR validation. Terraform plan reviewed and approved by at least one security team member before apply. All Vault configuration changes tested in staging-infra environment before production rollout. Vault Raft snapshot restore procedure tested quarterly in staging.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Vault cluster unavailability blocks all dynamic credential issuance, halting compute jobs. Mitigation: 3-node Raft HA with KMS auto-unseal; Vault Agent local cache serves existing credentials through TTL expiry (up to 1 hour) during brief cluster outages. Risk: AppRole secret ID exfiltration via GitHub Actions environment compromise. Mitigation: response-wrapped secret IDs with 60-second wrap TTL; GitHub Actions OIDC token exchange eliminates storage of static secret IDs in CI secrets. Tradeoff: Raft integrated storage over Consul reduces operational complexity but limits Vault to single-region primary cluster; multi-region active-active requires Vault Enterprise replication.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Vault PKI engine for internal certificate authority deferred to Phase 4 after AppRole rollout stabilizes - no current PKI use case justifies added complexity. Consul rejected in favor of Raft integrated storage to eliminate a second stateful cluster to manage. GCP multi-cloud extension planned for Phase 5 using Vault GCP secrets engine. Response-wrapped AppRole chosen over Trusted Orchestrator pattern because GitHub Actions OIDC integration already covers the orchestrator trust requirement.

metadata.notes: Zero-trust infra hardening round 2; resolves Vault audit fallback and TTL enforcement gaps for SOC 2 compliance.

project_goal.value: Migrate legacy on-premise pharmacokinetics modeling jobs from manual R scripts to a Kubernetes-orchestrated pipeline enabling reproducible execution and versioned model artifacts
project_goal.rationale: Manual R script execution is error-prone, non-reproducible, and lacks audit trails required for IND submissions

target_users.rationale: Scientists submit studies and consume model outputs; regulatory team requires artifact traceability

risk_level.rationale: Confidential PK data and IND submission artifacts; no direct patient data in this pipeline

feature_scope.rationale: Full PK workflow from dataset submission to versioned artifact archival

mvp_scope.rationale: End-to-end flow for one study validates the core architecture before multi-study scaling

security_baseline.value: OIDC authentication for all human access, Kubernetes network policies restricting pod-to-pod traffic, MinIO bucket policies limiting read to run-owner and reviewers, TLS 1.3 for all internal service traffic
security_baseline.rationale: On-premise environment requires explicit network isolation; no public internet exposure

backend_stack.rationale: targets provides dependency-aware pipeline execution with content-addressed caching

constraints.rationale: Hardware and licensing constraints from existing on-premise infrastructure

observability_baseline.rationale: On-premise monitoring stack already deployed on the Kubernetes cluster

execution_preference.value: GitLab CI pipeline with scientist-triggered runs via Git push to study branch; manual approval gate for production IND submission runs
execution_preference.rationale: Scientists own study execution; regulatory submissions require human approval

compliance_context.value: 21 CFR Part 11 for electronic records in PK modeling used in IND submissions; tamper-evident audit trail for all model execution events
compliance_context.rationale: FDA requires electronic records used in regulatory submissions to meet Part 11 controls

project.goal: Containerize PK modeling pipeline on Kubernetes for reproducible IND-grade execution
project.target_users: PK scientists, regulatory affairs team

backend.runtime: R 4.4 with targets framework in Docker containers
backend.pipeline: NONMEM parallel estimation on GPU nodes, nlmixr on CPU

security.auth: Keycloak OIDC for scientists, K8s service account RBAC for pipeline pods

data.storage: MinIO for artifacts with S3 versioning
data.cache: none - targets uses content-addressed file cache

constraints.hardware: NONMEM license on specific GPU node
constraints.reproducibility: R packages pinned per study via renv.lock

open_questions.nonmem_parallelism: NONMEM parallel threads vs Kubernetes Job resource limits to be calibrated

confirmed_decisions.pipeline_framework: targets over drake for active maintenance
confirmed_decisions.artifact_store: MinIO over NFS for S3 API compatibility

reasoner_reviews.product: The containerized targets pipeline directly solves the reproducibility problem by pinning R package versions via renv.lock per study container build. Slack completion notifications are essential for scientist workflow since they do not monitor a dashboard. The LIMS integration is correctly deferred to phase 2 as it adds significant scope.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Using targets framework is well-suited because its content-addressed caching (based on file hashes) means re-running a study after a minor script change only re-executes changed pipeline nodes, not the full NONMEM estimation which can take hours. The Kubernetes Job-per-study approach is clean but requires careful resource limit calibration for NONMEM parallel threads to avoid OOM evictions on shared GPU nodes.
reasoner_reviews.security: The 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail requirement means every pipeline run event must be written to an append-only audit log with the scientist identity, dataset hash, software version, and output artifact hash. MinIO bucket policies must prevent overwrite of finalized artifacts. Keycloak OIDC must be integrated into GitLab so that the scientist identity propagating through GitLab CI is the same identity recorded in the Part 11 audit log.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: NONMEM license node affinity in Kubernetes requires a NodeSelector or taint/toleration on the licensed GPU node. R package renv.lock pinning per study means Docker images will diverge per study version - a container registry cleanup policy is needed to manage image sprawl over time.

backend.service_design: Each PK study is a separate Git repository with a _targets.R pipeline definition; Docker image built per study using renv.lock snapshot for exact package reproducibility; Kubernetes Job spec generated by GitLab CI from study metadata YAML
backend.business_modules: targets pipeline nodes: load_data (read CSV study dataset from MinIO), validate_schema (dose, concentration, time, subject_id columns), run_nonmem (submit NONMEM control file to licensed node), run_nlmixr (alternative nlmixr model on CPU), compute_parameters (extract AUC, Cmax, t_half from model output), generate_diagnostics (VPC plots, NPDE residuals via ggPMx), archive_artifacts (push to MinIO with run_id prefix)
backend.background_jobs: GitLab CI runner with Kubernetes executor spawns Kubernetes Job per study run; Job mounts MinIO credentials from K8s secret; targets _targets.R uses aws.s3 package to read/write artifacts
backend.failure_handling: NONMEM non-convergence flagged as pipeline warning (not error) to allow diagnostic review; Kubernetes Job restart policy OnFailure with max 2 retries for transient OOM; failed run status written to PostgreSQL audit table with error log artifact

security.auth_design: Keycloak OIDC integrated with GitLab authentication; GitLab CI job context carries scientist identity token; pipeline metadata records Keycloak subject ID in run audit record
security.authorization_model: Kubernetes RBAC: pipeline service account has create Jobs, read Secrets; scientists read MinIO study bucket (study/{study_id}/*), no cross-study bucket access; regulatory viewer role has read-only access to finalized artifact buckets
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Part 11 audit log in PostgreSQL: run_audit (run_id, study_id, scientist_keycloak_id, dataset_s3_key, dataset_sha256, r_version, renv_lockfile_hash, started_at, completed_at, status, output_artifact_hashes_json); append-only enforced via table triggers rejecting UPDATE/DELETE
security.privacy_controls: MinIO Object Lock (Compliance mode) on finalized artifact buckets prevents deletion or overwrite for 15-year retention period

data.entities: pk_studies (study_id, compound_id, protocol_version, created_by, created_at), pipeline_runs (run_id, study_id, git_commit_sha, renv_lockfile_hash, status, started_at, completed_at), parameter_estimates (run_id, parameter_name, estimate, se, rse_pct, shrinkage), diagnostic_plots (run_id, plot_type ENUM vpc/npde/gof, s3_key, generated_at), run_audit (run_id, scientist_id, dataset_sha256, artifact_hashes_json, event_type, timestamp)
data.storage_design: MinIO buckets: studies/{study_id}/datasets/ (input CSVs), runs/{run_id}/outputs/ (parameter estimates, diagnostic plots, NONMEM tables), runs/{run_id}/logs/ (NONMEM output files). PostgreSQL 16 for structured metadata and Part 11 audit table
data.migration_notes: Existing manual R script outputs archived to MinIO cold storage bucket as baseline before pipeline migration; scientists re-run one historical study to validate output equivalence

devops.deployment_topology: Bare-metal Kubernetes cluster: 3 control plane nodes, 6 CPU worker nodes (16 cores), 2 GPU worker nodes (NVIDIA A100 for NONMEM), MinIO 4-node distributed deployment on dedicated storage nodes
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI .gitlab-ci.yml: build stage (docker build with renv.lock, push to internal Harbor registry), validate stage (targets dry-run to check pipeline graph), run stage (kubectl apply Job spec with study metadata, monitor Job completion), notify stage (Slack webhook with run summary and MinIO artifact links)
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scrapes Kubernetes metrics (Job success/failure rate, pod CPU/memory), custom metrics from pipeline: pk_run_duration_minutes (histogram per study type), nonmem_convergence_rate (gauge). Grafana dashboards per study type. Loki aggregates pod logs for debugging failed runs.
devops.rollback_strategy: Each run is immutable; re-run from any prior Git commit with same renv.lock produces identical output via targets caching; no rollback needed as prior artifacts remain in MinIO with run_id versioning

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a greenfield pipeline modernization for PK modeling on Kubernetes. The key architectural decisions are: targets framework for dependency-aware reproducible execution, renv.lock-pinned Docker images per study for exact reproducibility, Kubernetes Job-per-study with NONMEM node affinity for licensed compute, and MinIO Object Lock for 21 CFR Part 11 artifact immutability. The Part 11 audit trail requires scientist identity traceability through GitLab CI into the PostgreSQL audit table.
target_output.title: Pharmacokinetics Modeling Pipeline - Kubernetes Containerization Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Kubernetes-orchestrated data pipeline that migrates pharmacokinetics modeling from manual R scripts to containerized targets-framework workflows with full 21 CFR Part 11 compliance. Scientists push PK study datasets to GitLab, triggering containerized pipeline execution with NONMEM and nlmixr model estimation on on-premise GPU nodes. Versioned model artifacts, parameter estimates, and diagnostic plots are archived to MinIO with tamper-evident audit logging for IND submission traceability.
target_output.architecture_overview: A GitLab CI-triggered Kubernetes Job pipeline executes each PK study in an isolated Docker container built from R 4.4 with an renv.lock-pinned package snapshot. The targets framework manages pipeline node execution order and content-addressed caching, ensuring only changed pipeline steps re-execute on re-runs. NONMEM estimation jobs run via Kubernetes Job with NodeSelector targeting the NONMEM-licensed GPU nodes; nlmixr models run on standard CPU worker nodes. Model outputs are streamed to MinIO S3-compatible object store with Object Lock Compliance mode for 15-year artifact retention. A PostgreSQL audit table records every run event with scientist identity, dataset hash, and output artifact hashes to satisfy 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.
target_output.technology_stack: Pipeline: R 4.4 with targets 1.7, renv 1.0 for package pinning, ggPMx 1.3 for diagnostic plots. Modeling: NONMEM 7.5 (licensed GPU node), nlmixr2 2.1 (CPU). Containers: Docker, Harbor registry (internal). Orchestration: Kubernetes 1.29 (bare-metal), GitLab CI with Kubernetes executor. Storage: MinIO RELEASE.2024-03 (4-node distributed), PostgreSQL 16. Auth: Keycloak 24 OIDC. Observability: Prometheus, Grafana, Loki. Notifications: Slack webhook via GitLab CI.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Study Submission: scientist pushes dataset CSV and study metadata YAML to GitLab study repository, triggering CI pipeline. Container Build: Docker image built per study using renv.lock snapshot, tagged with Git commit SHA for exact reproducibility. Pipeline Execution: Kubernetes Job runs targets pipeline - data loading, schema validation, NONMEM/nlmixr estimation, parameter extraction, diagnostic plot generation. Artifact Archival: parameter estimates, NONMEM tables, VPC/NPDE/GOF plots, renv.lock archived to MinIO under run_id namespace. Part 11 Audit: every run event written to append-only PostgreSQL table with scientist identity and artifact hashes. Slack Notification: run completion summary with artifact MinIO links posted to study Slack channel.
target_output.system_components: GitLab CI server (Kubernetes executor for Job dispatch), Harbor container registry (study Docker images, internal), Kubernetes cluster (3 control plane, 6 CPU workers, 2 GPU workers with NONMEM node taint), MinIO 4-node distributed cluster (studies and runs buckets with Object Lock), PostgreSQL 16 (run metadata and Part 11 audit table), Keycloak 24 (OIDC for GitLab and scientist authentication), Prometheus + Loki (cluster and pipeline observability), Grafana (pipeline duration and convergence dashboards), Slack (completion notifications).
target_output.workflows: PK Study Run: Scientist creates study branch in GitLab, commits dataset CSV to studies/{study_id}/datasets/, pushes branch to trigger CI. GitLab CI builds Docker image with renv.lock, pushes to Harbor, applies Kubernetes Job spec with study_id and run_id annotations. Job pod mounts MinIO credentials from K8s secret, runs _targets.R: loads dataset from MinIO, validates dose/concentration/time/subject_id schema, submits NONMEM control file to licensed GPU node, extracts AUC/Cmax/t_half from NONMEM output tables, generates VPC and NPDE diagnostic plots via ggPMx, archives all outputs to MinIO runs/{run_id}/, writes run completion to PostgreSQL with artifact SHA-256 hashes. GitLab CI posts Slack message with run summary. NONMEM Re-run After Script Change: Scientist modifies R diagnostic script and re-pushes. targets detects only the diagnostic node hash changed; re-runs only generate_diagnostics and archive_artifacts nodes - NONMEM estimation result is served from targets cache. New Docker image built to reflect updated renv if packages changed.
target_output.data_model: pk_studies (study_id UUID PK, compound_id VARCHAR, protocol_version VARCHAR, sponsor_study_number VARCHAR, created_by_keycloak_id VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). pipeline_runs (run_id UUID PK, study_id UUID FK, git_commit_sha VARCHAR(40), renv_lockfile_hash VARCHAR(64), docker_image_tag VARCHAR, status ENUM submitted/running/completed/failed/converged_with_warnings, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, kubernetes_job_name VARCHAR). parameter_estimates (id UUID PK, run_id UUID FK, parameter_name VARCHAR, estimate NUMERIC, standard_error NUMERIC, rse_pct NUMERIC, shrinkage_pct NUMERIC, model_type ENUM nonmem/nlmixr). diagnostic_plots (id UUID PK, run_id UUID FK, plot_type ENUM vpc/npde/gof/eta_distribution, s3_key VARCHAR, file_sha256 VARCHAR(64), generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). run_audit (id UUID PK, run_id UUID FK, scientist_keycloak_id VARCHAR, event_type ENUM run_started/run_completed/run_failed/artifact_archived, dataset_s3_key VARCHAR, dataset_sha256 VARCHAR(64), artifact_hashes_json JSONB, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ) - UPDATE and DELETE forbidden via trigger.
target_output.api_design: No external HTTP API; pipeline integrations use the following interfaces: GitLab CI REST API for Job status polling (GET /projects/{id}/jobs/{job_id}), MinIO S3 API (PUT /runs/{run_id}/{artifact}, GET /runs/{run_id}/{artifact} with presigned URL for regulatory viewer access), Kubernetes Jobs API (kubectl apply -f job-spec.yaml, kubectl wait job/{run_id} --for=condition=complete), PostgreSQL psql for audit log queries (SELECT * FROM run_audit WHERE run_id=? ORDER BY timestamp), Slack Incoming Webhook (POST JSON payload with run_id, study_id, status, artifact_links). Internal R script reads MinIO via aws.s3::get_object() with service account credentials.
target_output.security_and_compliance: 21 CFR Part 11 controls: append-only PostgreSQL run_audit table with trigger blocking UPDATE/DELETE; scientist identity from Keycloak OIDC token propagated to CI environment variable and written to audit record; dataset SHA-256 and output artifact hashes stored in audit record to detect tampering; MinIO Object Lock Compliance mode on finalized-artifacts bucket (15-year WORM). Kubernetes security: network policies restricting pipeline pod egress to MinIO and PostgreSQL only; service account tokens with minimum-required RBAC (create Jobs, read Secrets); no pipeline pod runs as root (runAsNonRoot: true, readOnlyRootFilesystem: true). Secrets management: MinIO credentials and PostgreSQL DSN in Kubernetes Secrets (base64-encoded, namespace-scoped); Keycloak client secrets rotated every 90 days.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Bare-metal Kubernetes cluster provisioned via Ansible: 3 control plane (kubeadm), 6 CPU workers (16-core), 2 GPU workers (NVIDIA A100 with NONMEM license, tainted with dedicated=nonmem:NoSchedule). MinIO deployed as StatefulSet with 4 storage nodes using local NVMe PersistentVolumes. GitLab CI Kubernetes executor configured as a Helm-deployed GitLab Runner with autoscaling. GitLab CI pipeline stages: build (docker build, harbor push, tagged with commit SHA), validate (targets dry-run in lightweight container), run (kubectl apply Job, monitor with kubectl wait --timeout=480m), notify (Slack webhook). Environments: dev-k8s (single GPU node, no Object Lock), staging-k8s (2 GPU nodes, Object Lock in Governance mode), production-k8s (2 GPU nodes, Object Lock in Compliance mode, audit log enabled). Kubernetes upgrades via rolling drain/cordon per node.
target_output.observability: Prometheus metrics: kubernetes_job_duration_seconds (histogram by study_type label), kubernetes_job_failed_total (counter by failure_reason), minio_s3_requests_total (rate by bucket), postgresql_table_row_count (audit table growth rate). Grafana dashboards: Pipeline Health (job success rate, median run duration by study type, NONMEM convergence rate), MinIO Storage (artifact growth per study, bucket size trends), Audit Activity (run frequency per scientist, failed run reasons). Loki log aggregation for pipeline pod stdout: ERROR lines surface NONMEM non-convergence messages, schema validation failures, and MinIO write errors. Grafana alert: kubernetes_job_failed_total increase >3 in 1 hour triggers Slack #pk-pipeline-ops notification.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: On-premise infrastructure; primary costs are hardware amortization and licensing. NONMEM license: 2 parallel estimation slots on GPU nodes; NONMEM parallelism configured via $PRIOR NWPRI and $SIZES PD= parameters in control files. Kubernetes node scaling: additional CPU worker nodes added as study volume grows beyond 10 concurrent runs. MinIO capacity: 4x8TB NVMe = 32TB raw; with distributed erasure coding (4+2) provides approximately 21TB usable; estimated 500GB/year artifact growth. Harbor registry cleanup policy: retain last 5 image tags per study to prevent disk exhaustion.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (weeks 1-4): GitLab CI pipeline for single study type (2-compartment oral absorption), Docker image build with renv.lock, Kubernetes Job execution on GPU node, MinIO artifact archival, PostgreSQL Part 11 audit log, Slack notification, Keycloak OIDC integration. Phase 2 (weeks 5-8): Multi-study support with study metadata YAML schema, targets caching for re-run efficiency, NONMEM parallelism tuning, regulatory viewer MinIO presigned URL access, staging environment with Governance mode Object Lock. Phase 3 (weeks 9-12): Full study library onboarding (population PK models, PK/PD), Grafana pipeline dashboards, ggPMx diagnostic plot library expansion, renv.lock drift alerting, IND submission artifact packaging workflow.
target_output.development_guidelines: renv.lock must be committed to study Git repository and Docker image built deterministically from it - no ad-hoc package installs in pipeline scripts. targets pipeline nodes must be pure functions with explicit file inputs/outputs listed in tar_target() call to ensure correct dependency graph. NONMEM control files (.ctl) versioned in study Git repository alongside R pipeline. Kubernetes Job resource requests (CPU, memory) must be specified per study type; no resource-unconstrained Jobs. PostgreSQL run_audit table has a CI migration test verifying UPDATE/DELETE trigger rejection. MinIO Object Lock mode must be Compliance (not Governance) in production buckets - enforced via MinIO mc admin policy check in CI.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: NONMEM license node failure blocks all NONMEM-dependent study runs. Mitigation: nlmixr as fallback estimator for exploratory analyses; NONMEM vendor contacted about floating license for second GPU node. Risk: targets content-addressed cache invalidation on renv.lock update causes full re-estimation of all studies. Mitigation: renv.lock changes trigger a warning annotation in CI, not automatic cache invalidation; scientist explicitly marks cache as dirty. Tradeoff: per-study Docker images ensure exact reproducibility but create Harbor registry sprawl - mitigated by 5-tag retention policy. Tradeoff: bare-metal Kubernetes over managed cloud K8s trades operational burden for data residency compliance and NONMEM node control.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: targets framework chosen over drake due to active CRAN maintenance and better targets::tar_make_clustermq() parallelism support. MinIO chosen over NFS for S3 API compatibility enabling standard aws.s3 R package usage. NONMEM parallelism configured at maximum 8 threads per Job via $SIZES PD=8 to avoid exceeding CPU worker node capacity. Keycloak integrated with GitLab SSO so scientist identity from Keycloak propagates through CI pipeline to Part 11 audit log without manual identity mapping.

metadata.notes: PK modeling pipeline containerization; targets framework on Kubernetes with 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail.

project_goal.value: Evaluate differential privacy techniques for sharing aggregated ADME screening results with external academic collaborators without exposing individual compound identities or proprietary structural data
project_goal.rationale: Enable collaborative research while protecting compound IP that cannot be disclosed under NDAs

target_users.value: Internal computational chemistry team and external academic collaborators at partner universities
target_users.rationale: Internal team configures releases; external collaborators receive noise-protected aggregates

project_class.rationale: Prototype to evaluate DP technique effectiveness before production commitment

capabilities.rationale: API backend for data release, privacy-preserving analytics engine, no interactive frontend

complexity_level.rationale: Differential privacy budget management, Laplace/Gaussian noise mechanisms, and partner-facing API security

risk_level.rationale: Compound IP exposure risk if privacy budget is exhausted or epsilon configured too high

data_sensitivity.rationale: ADME screening results and compound profiles constitute proprietary drug discovery IP

external_exposure.rationale: Academic collaborators access the API with partner-specific JWT tokens; no public internet access

access_model.value: Custom JWT tokens per academic partner with configurable epsilon budget; internal team authenticates via service account; no self-registration
access_model.rationale: Partner identity and budget must be administratively controlled to prevent budget exhaustion attacks

feature_scope.value: DP epsilon budget configuration per partner, Laplace and Gaussian noise mechanism APIs, aggregated ADME statistics release (mean, variance, percentiles), privacy budget tracking and lock-out, release log with epsilon consumed per query
feature_scope.rationale: Core DP experiment features to evaluate technique effectiveness

mvp_scope.value: Single academic partner pilot: epsilon budget assignment, Laplace mechanism for solubility and permeability aggregates, budget tracking, automatic lock-out on exhaustion
mvp_scope.rationale: Single-partner pilot validates DP budget management before multi-partner expansion

security_baseline.value: mTLS between partner API clients and Cloud Run endpoint, JWT tokens signed with RSA-2048, partner tokens scoped to specific ADME assay panels, release log immutable
security_baseline.rationale: Partner-facing API requires mutual authentication and query-level audit trail

frontend_stack.value: none - REST API only; internal team uses Jupyter notebooks for prototype analysis
frontend_stack.rationale: Prototype does not require a UI; results consumed programmatically by collaborators

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI; diffprivlib 0.6 for DP mechanisms
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI for performant async API; IBM diffprivlib for validated DP algorithm implementations

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for ADME results, compound profiles, and privacy budget ledger
data_platform.rationale: Relational model suits compound-to-assay result joins and budget accounting queries

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run (serverless, auto-scaling to zero between partner queries)
hosting_target.rationale: Cloud Run suitable for prototype-scale intermittent partner queries; Cloud SQL manages PostgreSQL

privacy_retention_policy.value: Release request logs retained indefinitely for IP audit purposes; ADME raw data retained per compound lifecycle policy (10 years post-compound retirement)
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: IP protection requires permanent record of what was shared with each partner

future_scope.value: Local differential privacy for individual query responses, Renyi DP accountant for tighter budget tracking, multi-partner federated analytics
future_scope.rationale: Production-scale DP system roadmap after prototype validation

constraints.value: Epsilon total budget per partner must not exceed epsilon=5.0 (prototype policy); no raw compound identifiers or SMILES in any released data; prototype timeline 8 weeks
constraints.rationale: IP protection policy sets conservative epsilon ceiling for prototype evaluation

observability_baseline.value: Google Cloud Monitoring for Cloud Run metrics; structured logging of every data release request with epsilon consumed
observability_baseline.rationale: Every release must be auditable; Cloud Monitoring native for GCP

execution_preference.value: Cloud Build CI/CD with automated tests on DP mechanism accuracy and budget enforcement; deploy to Cloud Run on merge
execution_preference.rationale: Rapid iteration for prototype with automated regression testing of DP guarantees

compliance_context.value: GDPR data minimization principles for EU academic collaborator data; internal IP protection policy mandating epsilon budget caps and release audit logs
compliance_context.rationale: EU partner institutions fall under GDPR; compound IP requires contractual and technical access controls

project.goal: Build prototype DP API to evaluate ADME data sharing with academic partners under privacy budget
project.target_users: Internal chem team + academic collaborators

security.auth: RSA-2048 signed JWT per partner, mTLS on Cloud Run

constraints.no_raw_ids: no compound SMILES or registry IDs in releases

open_questions.renyi_dp: Renyi DP accountant for tighter budget tracking deferred to production

confirmed_decisions.dp_library: diffprivlib over Google DP library for Python 3.12 compatibility
confirmed_decisions.mechanism: Laplace for mean/variance, Gaussian for percentile queries

reasoner_reviews.product: The epsilon=5.0 total budget per partner is a reasonable conservative ceiling for a prototype with aggregated ADME statistics (solubility, permeability, microsomal stability means and variances). Academic collaborators primarily need trend data, not individual compound values, so aggregated DP releases should be scientifically useful at this epsilon.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The sequential composition theorem means each query consumes epsilon from the total budget; the system must track cumulative epsilon accurately before responding to prevent overspend. Using diffprivlib BudgetAccountant with a strict=True flag blocks queries that would exceed budget. Cloud SQL with private IP eliminates public network exposure of ADME raw data.
reasoner_reviews.security: The mTLS requirement for partner-facing Cloud Run endpoints needs a custom domain with managed certificate and Cloud Armor to enforce client certificate validation at the load balancer layer. JWT token signing keys must be stored in Cloud KMS, not in environment variables. The release log must be append-only; Cloud SQL row-level triggers should block UPDATE/DELETE on the release_requests table.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 8-week timeline is achievable for single-partner pilot. The epsilon=5.0 cap per partner needs to be parametric (stored per partner in PostgreSQL) not hardcoded, so different partners can have different budgets in future iterations.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application on Cloud Run with three route groups: /releases (partner query endpoints), /admin (internal budget configuration), /health. DP logic isolated in a DPEngine service class wrapping diffprivlib mechanisms
backend.api_patterns: Partner endpoints accept aggregation queries (assay_panel, statistic_type, noise_mechanism); DPEngine computes noisy statistic and deducts epsilon from BudgetAccountant; result returned with noise_scale and epsilon_consumed metadata
backend.business_modules: DPEngine: wraps diffprivlib LaplaceMechanism and GaussianMechanism, reads sensitivity from assay panel metadata, calls BudgetAccountant.check() before computation, writes release_requests record after successful release. BudgetManager: queries partner budget remaining, enforces epsilon_max, returns 402 Budget Exhausted when depleted.
backend.failure_handling: diffprivlib BudgetAccountant raises PrivacyLeakWarning on strict mode violation; FastAPI exception handler returns 400 Budget Exceeded with remaining epsilon; no partial releases on error

security.auth_design: Per-partner RSA-2048 JWT signed by Cloud KMS asymmetric key; partner token includes partner_id, assay_panel_scope, issued_at, expires_at (30-day tokens); FastAPI dependency validates signature against Cloud KMS public key
security.authorization_model: Partner JWT scope restricts to specific assay panels (e.g., scope: solubility,permeability); backend enforces panel scope on every query; internal admin endpoints require service account token
security.secrets_management: Cloud KMS for JWT signing keys; PostgreSQL DSN in Secret Manager injected via Cloud Run environment at startup; no secrets in container images or environment variables in VCS
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every release request logged to release_requests table (append-only via trigger); Cloud Logging structured log entry per request with partner_id, assay_panel, statistic_type, epsilon_consumed, noisy_result_metadata (no raw values)

data.entities: adme_results (compound_internal_id, assay_panel, parameter_name, value, unit, measured_at), compound_profiles (compound_internal_id, series_code, target_class, NOT smiles or registry_id), privacy_budgets (partner_id, epsilon_total, epsilon_consumed, locked, updated_at), release_requests (id, partner_id, assay_panel, statistic_type, epsilon_consumed, noise_mechanism, noisy_result, noise_scale, requested_at)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL with private IP; adme_results and compound_profiles never exposed via API; release_requests append-only; compound_internal_id is an opaque UUID not derivable from compound structure
data.retention_and_deletion: release_requests retained indefinitely; adme_results retained 10 years; compound_profiles deleted on compound retirement with CASCADE to adme_results

devops.deployment_topology: Cloud Run service with min-instances=0 (scale to zero), max-instances=10, ingress=all with Cloud Armor enforcing mTLS client cert; Cloud SQL Proxy sidecar for PostgreSQL access
devops.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build: unit tests (DPEngine accuracy assertions, budget enforcement tests), integration tests (mock partner JWT, epsilon deduction), docker build, push to Artifact Registry, deploy Cloud Run revision with traffic split 10% canary
devops.observability_stack: Cloud Monitoring: Cloud Run request count, latency, instance count. Custom metrics via structured logs: epsilon_consumed_per_request (histogram), budget_exhaustion_events_total (counter). Log-based alerts on budget_exhaustion_events.
devops.rollback_strategy: Cloud Run traffic splitting allows instant rollback to prior revision; Cloud SQL point-in-time recovery for data rollback

target_output.thinking_summary: This prototype focuses on differential privacy budget management for ADME data sharing with academic partners. The critical design decisions are: diffprivlib BudgetAccountant with strict=True to prevent epsilon overspend, compound_internal_id as opaque UUID to prevent re-identification, append-only release_requests table for IP audit trail, and per-partner epsilon budget stored in PostgreSQL (not hardcoded) for flexibility. mTLS enforcement on Cloud Run via Cloud Armor is the primary partner authentication control.
target_output.title: Differential Privacy ADME Data Release Prototype - Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A research prototype FastAPI service on GCP Cloud Run that evaluates differential privacy techniques for sharing aggregated ADME screening statistics with external academic collaborators. The system manages per-partner epsilon budgets using diffprivlib BudgetAccountant, applies Laplace and Gaussian noise mechanisms to solubility, permeability, and microsomal stability aggregates, and maintains an append-only release log for IP audit purposes. No compound identifiers, SMILES strings, or individual assay values are ever transmitted.
target_output.architecture_overview: A stateless FastAPI application deployed on GCP Cloud Run handles partner aggregation queries. The DPEngine service class wraps diffprivlib Laplace and Gaussian mechanisms, reading sensitivity parameters from an assay panel metadata table in Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16. Before each release, the BudgetAccountant checks remaining epsilon against the per-partner budget stored in the privacy_budgets table. On successful computation, the noisy aggregate is returned and a release_requests record written atomically in the same database transaction. Partners authenticate via RSA-2048 JWTs signed by a Cloud KMS asymmetric key. Cloud Armor enforces mTLS client certificate validation at the load balancer. Compound data is accessible only to the FastAPI service via Cloud SQL private IP; no compound identifiers ever appear in release outputs.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Python 3.12, FastAPI 0.111, uvicorn. DP Library: IBM diffprivlib 0.6 (LaplaceMechanism, GaussianMechanism, BudgetAccountant). Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL (private IP, auto-backups). Hosting: GCP Cloud Run (serverless, scale to zero). Auth: RSA-2048 JWT via python-jose, Cloud KMS asymmetric signing key. Security: Cloud Armor with mTLS, Secret Manager for credentials. CI/CD: Cloud Build, Artifact Registry. Observability: Cloud Logging structured JSON, Cloud Monitoring custom metrics.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Partner Query API: academic collaborator sends aggregation request (assay_panel=solubility, statistic=mean, noise_mechanism=laplace); DPEngine computes Laplace-noisy mean of internal solubility values; epsilon consumed deducted from partner budget; noisy result with noise_scale metadata returned. Budget Management: admin endpoint configures per-partner epsilon_total; read endpoint returns epsilon_remaining; lock endpoint manually freezes over-budget partners. Budget Enforcement: every query checks BudgetAccountant before computation; queries exceeding remaining budget return 402 with epsilon_remaining field. Release Logging: every successful release written to append-only release_requests table with partner_id, assay_panel, epsilon_consumed, noisy_result, noise_scale. IP Audit: internal team queries release_requests to produce partner data share summaries for legal review.
target_output.system_components: Cloud Run service (FastAPI, DPEngine, BudgetManager, JWT validator), Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 instance (private IP, adme_results, privacy_budgets, release_requests), Cloud KMS asymmetric key ring (JWT signing key for partner tokens), Cloud Armor security policy (mTLS client cert enforcement), Secret Manager (PostgreSQL DSN, Cloud KMS key path), Artifact Registry (Docker images), Cloud Build (CI/CD pipeline), Cloud Logging + Cloud Monitoring (request telemetry and epsilon metrics).
target_output.workflows: Partner Data Release: Academic collaborator sends POST /releases/query with JWT in Authorization header and payload {assay_panel: 'solubility', statistic_type: 'mean', noise_mechanism: 'laplace'}. FastAPI JWT dependency validates RSA signature against Cloud KMS public key, extracts partner_id and panel scope. BudgetManager queries privacy_budgets for partner epsilon_remaining. DPEngine loads raw solubility values for in-scope compounds from adme_results (filtered to non-retired compounds in partner assay panel). LaplaceMechanism computes sensitivity as (max_value - min_value), adds Laplace noise scaled to sensitivity/epsilon_query. BudgetAccountant deducts epsilon_query from budget. Transaction: insert release_requests record, update privacy_budgets.epsilon_consumed. Return noisy mean with noise_scale and epsilon_consumed. Budget Exhaustion: Partner attempts query when epsilon_remaining=0.02 but query requires epsilon=0.1. BudgetAccountant.check() raises PrivacyLeakWarning in strict mode. FastAPI exception handler returns HTTP 402 {error: 'budget_exhausted', epsilon_remaining: 0.02, message: 'Total epsilon budget of 5.0 consumed'}. privacy_budgets.locked set to True. Admin notified via Cloud Monitoring alert.
target_output.data_model: adme_results (compound_internal_id UUID, assay_panel VARCHAR, parameter_name VARCHAR, value NUMERIC, unit VARCHAR, measured_at DATE) - compound_internal_id is opaque UUID with no structural derivability. compound_profiles (compound_internal_id UUID PK, series_code VARCHAR, target_class VARCHAR, retired_at DATE) - no SMILES, no external registry ID. privacy_budgets (partner_id VARCHAR PK, epsilon_total NUMERIC, epsilon_consumed NUMERIC DEFAULT 0, locked BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ) - epsilon_consumed updated atomically with each release. noise_parameters (assay_panel VARCHAR PK, parameter_name VARCHAR, sensitivity NUMERIC, default_mechanism ENUM laplace/gaussian, max_value NUMERIC, min_value NUMERIC) - drives DPEngine sensitivity calculation. release_requests (id UUID PK, partner_id VARCHAR, assay_panel VARCHAR, statistic_type VARCHAR, noise_mechanism VARCHAR, epsilon_consumed NUMERIC, noise_scale NUMERIC, noisy_result NUMERIC, requested_at TIMESTAMPTZ) - UPDATE and DELETE blocked by PostgreSQL trigger for IP audit immutability.
target_output.api_design: Partner-facing REST API (requires valid partner JWT): POST /releases/query {assay_panel, statistic_type, noise_mechanism, epsilon_request} -> {noisy_result, noise_scale, epsilon_consumed, epsilon_remaining, release_id}. GET /releases/history -> [{release_id, assay_panel, statistic_type, epsilon_consumed, requested_at}] (partner's own history). GET /releases/budget -> {epsilon_total, epsilon_consumed, epsilon_remaining, locked}. Admin-only endpoints (service account token): POST /admin/partners {partner_id, epsilon_total, assay_panel_scope} -> creates partner JWT signed via KMS. PATCH /admin/partners/{partner_id}/budget {epsilon_total}. GET /admin/partners/{partner_id}/releases -> full release history. GET /health -> {status: ok, db: connected}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: GDPR data minimization: only noisy aggregates transmitted to external partners; no individual compound values, identifiers, or SMILES in any API response; compound_internal_id is opaque UUID. IP protection: release_requests append-only via PostgreSQL trigger (UPDATE/DELETE raise exception); epsilon_max=5.0 enforced per partner with BudgetAccountant strict=True; admin lock endpoint freezes over-budget partners. Authentication: RSA-2048 JWT signed by Cloud KMS asymmetric key (key rotation every 365 days); partner tokens include partner_id and assay_panel_scope claims; tokens expire after 30 days. Transport: mTLS enforced via Cloud Armor on all partner API calls; TLS 1.3 minimum; Cloud SQL private IP prevents database network exposure. Secrets: JWT signing key path and PostgreSQL DSN in Secret Manager; no secrets in container environment variables or Dockerfile.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Run service deployed via Cloud Build: unit test stage (DPEngine mechanism accuracy assertions with known epsilon/sensitivity values, BudgetAccountant strict mode rejection test, append-only trigger test), integration test stage (mock partner JWT, end-to-end epsilon deduction and lock-out), docker build stage (push to Artifact Registry), deploy stage (gcloud run deploy with traffic split 10% canary on new revision). Cloud SQL: automated daily backups to GCS with 7-day retention, point-in-time recovery enabled, private IP with Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar in Cloud Run. Environments: dev (Cloud Run, Cloud SQL dev instance, epsilon_max=10 for testing), production (Cloud Run min=0 max=10, Cloud SQL production instance, Cloud Armor mTLS). Secret Manager secrets versioned; rotation requires Cloud Build redeploy.
target_output.observability: Cloud Logging: structured JSON log per release request with fields partner_id, assay_panel, epsilon_consumed, epsilon_remaining, noise_mechanism, release_id. Cloud Monitoring metrics from log-based metrics: epsilon_consumed_per_query (histogram by partner_id and assay_panel), budget_exhaustion_events_total (counter by partner_id), release_request_latency_ms (histogram). Dashboards: partner budget consumption over time (cumulative epsilon per partner), release frequency by assay panel, budget lock events. Alerts: budget_exhaustion_event triggers email to IP legal team; Cloud Run error_rate >5% over 10 minutes triggers PagerDuty.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Prototype scale: ~$150/month. Cloud Run at zero-cost idle (min-instances=0), approximately $30/month for 10,000 monthly partner queries at average 100ms each. Cloud SQL db-f1-micro at $10/month for prototype data volume. Secret Manager $0.06/month per secret. Cloud Armor $5/month minimum. Cloud Build $0.003/build minute. Scale to production: Cloud Run auto-scales to 10 instances to handle parallel partner queries; Cloud SQL upgrade to db-n2-standard-2 at $100/month if query volume exceeds 100k/month.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (weeks 1-4): FastAPI with DPEngine wrapping diffprivlib Laplace mechanism, PostgreSQL schema with privacy_budgets and release_requests, append-only trigger, single partner JWT issuance via admin endpoint, epsilon budget tracking and lock-out, Cloud Run deployment with Cloud SQL private IP. Phase 2 (weeks 5-6): Gaussian mechanism for percentile statistics, assay panel scope enforcement in JWT, Cloud Armor mTLS configuration for partner-facing endpoint, release history endpoint. Phase 3 (weeks 7-8): Cloud Monitoring epsilon consumption dashboards, automated IP legal review report from release_requests, accuracy evaluation Jupyter notebook comparing DP release quality at epsilon=[0.5, 1.0, 2.0, 5.0], prototype findings documented for production DP system design.
target_output.development_guidelines: DPEngine must never log or return raw adme_results values - only noisy aggregates; CI test asserts no raw compound values in any API response fixture. BudgetAccountant must be instantiated with strict=True; any change to strict=False requires security lead approval. Sensitivity values in noise_parameters table must be computed from actual adme_results min/max ranges and reviewed by a computational chemistry lead - no hardcoded defaults. Partner JWT generation is admin-only and requires dual approval before issuance. All database migrations must include a test verifying the append-only trigger blocks UPDATE/DELETE on release_requests.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Epsilon calibration error where sensitivity is underestimated allows privacy budget to be spent faster than anticipated, enabling composition attacks at lower total epsilon. Mitigation: sensitivity values in noise_parameters reviewed by computational chemistry lead and conservative clamping applied. Risk: Cloud Run cold start latency (up to 2 seconds at min=0) during burst partner queries causes timeouts. Mitigation: min-instances=1 in production if latency SLA is required; acceptable in prototype. Tradeoff: global epsilon budget per partner (sequential composition) over per-query local DP provides stronger privacy guarantees but limits query volume; appropriate for prototype where partner query volume is low.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: diffprivlib chosen over Google differential-privacy library for Python 3.12 compatibility and active PyPI maintenance. Laplace mechanism chosen for mean/variance aggregates; Gaussian mechanism selected for percentile queries where L2 sensitivity is more natural. epsilon=5.0 total per partner set as prototype policy ceiling, not a scientific optimum - prototype will measure utility degradation at varying epsilon to inform production policy. Renyi DP accountant for tighter budget tracking deferred to production system after prototype validates basic mechanism behavior.

metadata.notes: Differential privacy ADME data sharing prototype; epsilon budget management with IP audit trail for academic partner collaboration.

project_goal.value: Replace disconnected Python scripts for protein-ligand docking with a unified desktop application for job submission, result visualization, and interaction fingerprint analysis
project_goal.rationale: Structural biologists waste time managing ad-hoc scripts; unified tool improves workflow consistency and result traceability

target_users.value: Structural biologists and computational chemists at researcher workstations
target_users.rationale: Primary users run docking jobs locally against crystal structures and analyze binding poses

project_class.rationale: Single-user local application running on researcher workstations; no server component

capabilities.rationale: Tauri React UI, Rust Axum local backend for job execution, SQLite for result persistence

complexity_level.rationale: 3D molecular visualization, AutoDock Vina subprocess management, SQLite persistence

risk_level.rationale: Local-only application; no network exposure; data is internal research, not PHI or financial

data_sensitivity.rationale: Protein structures and ligand docking results are internal research data, not regulated

external_exposure.rationale: Desktop app with local Axum server binding to 127.0.0.1 only; no external ports

access_model.value: Single-user local app; no authentication required; OS file system permissions protect data directory
access_model.rationale: Local workstation application does not require user authentication

feature_scope.value: Protein PDB file loading, ligand SDF/MOL2 file import, AutoDock Vina job submission with configurable search space, real-time job status polling, 3D binding pose viewer, interaction fingerprint analysis (H-bonds, hydrophobic contacts), multi-ligand comparison, SQLite result persistence
feature_scope.rationale: Complete docking workflow from structure loading through fingerprint analysis

mvp_scope.value: Protein/ligand file loading, AutoDock Vina job submission and result retrieval, top-10 pose display in 3D viewer, basic H-bond interaction annotation
mvp_scope.rationale: Core docking workflow validates the architecture before advanced fingerprint analysis

security_baseline.value: Axum local server binds to 127.0.0.1:3001 only; no external port exposure; AutoDock Vina subprocess sandboxed by OS; no network calls from application
security_baseline.rationale: Local-only binding prevents any external access to the Axum API

frontend_stack.value: Tauri 2.0 with React 18 + TypeScript; 3Dmol.js for molecular visualization
frontend_stack.rationale: Tauri provides native desktop packaging with small binary footprint; 3Dmol.js renders PDB/SDF structures in the web view

backend_stack.value: Rust with Axum 0.7 as local IPC server; tokio async runtime for job queue
backend_stack.rationale: Rust provides safe subprocess management for AutoDock Vina execution; Axum minimal HTTP IPC layer

data_platform.value: SQLite via rusqlite for local result persistence; protein and ligand files stored on local filesystem
data_platform.rationale: SQLite suits single-user local persistence without server infrastructure

hosting_target.value: Researcher workstations (macOS 13+ and Windows 11); distributed as Tauri desktop installer
hosting_target.rationale: Cross-platform support for Mac and Windows researcher workstations

privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Local-only data; researcher manages their own workstation data

future_scope.value: Molecular dynamics post-processing, integration with internal compound registry for ligand import, shared result export to team SharePoint
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 network-connected features for team collaboration
