constraints.rationale: Researchers work in network-restricted lab environments

observability_baseline.value: Sentry desktop SDK for crash reporting; local application log file; no cloud telemetry
observability_baseline.rationale: Crash reports help development team diagnose issues without cloud logging

execution_preference.value: GitHub Actions for cross-platform builds (macOS and Windows runners); Tauri bundler for signed installers
execution_preference.rationale: Automated cross-platform builds ensure consistent installer packaging

project.goal: Desktop docking workflow tool for structural biologists
project.target_users: Structural biologists and computational chemists

frontend.state: Zustand for job queue and result state

security.auth: none (local only)

data.files: Local filesystem for PDB and SDF/MOL2 files

constraints.offline: All assets bundled; no external API calls
constraints.vina: AutoDock Vina binary bundled in Tauri resource directory

open_questions.gpu_acceleration: GPU acceleration for GNINA deferred to phase 2

confirmed_decisions.visualization: 3Dmol.js over NGL Viewer for simpler React integration
confirmed_decisions.ipc: Axum HTTP IPC over Tauri commands for easier React fetch calls

reasoner_reviews.product: The scope is well-defined for a researcher productivity tool. Bundling AutoDock Vina with the installer is critical since researchers cannot self-install command-line tools in restricted environments. The multi-ligand side-by-side comparison feature is the highest value item after basic docking, as researchers routinely evaluate series of 20-50 ligands against the same protein target.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Using Axum as a local HTTP IPC server rather than Tauri's native IPC commands simplifies the React side to standard fetch() calls and decouples the frontend from Tauri-specific APIs. The tokio async runtime handles concurrent AutoDock Vina subprocesses cleanly. rusqlite with bundled feature compiles SQLite into the binary, eliminating the system SQLite dependency on researcher machines. The job queue should be an in-memory tokio::sync::mpsc channel backed by a SQLite jobs table for persistence across app restarts.
reasoner_reviews.security: Binding Axum to 127.0.0.1 is the correct isolation approach, but Tauri's allowlist should also restrict the webview from making any fetch() calls to non-localhost origins to prevent supply chain attacks via bundled npm dependencies making external requests.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 3Dmol.js must be bundled as a local asset (not loaded from CDN) to satisfy the offline constraint. AutoDock Vina binary resource path needs platform-specific handling in Rust (macOS: Contents/Resources/vina, Windows: resources/vina.exe). Tauri code signing requires a Developer ID certificate on macOS and EV certificate on Windows.

frontend.app_structure: Tauri 2.0 app with React 18 SPA in src/; Zustand store for docking_jobs and docking_results state; React Router for pages: /proteins, /ligands, /jobs, /results/{job_id}, /compare
frontend.pages_and_flows: Protein Library page: drag-drop PDB file import, protein metadata display, grid view. Ligand Library: SDF/MOL2 import, SMILES display, batch selection for multi-ligand jobs. Job Setup: select protein + ligands, configure Vina search box (center_x/y/z, size_x/y/z, exhaustiveness), submit. Job Monitor: real-time status polling every 2s via fetch() to localhost:3001/jobs/{id}. Result Viewer: 3Dmol.js pose rendering, affinity score table, H-bond annotation overlay. Compare View: side-by-side 3Dmol.js panels for up to 4 ligand poses.
frontend.state_management: Zustand store: proteins[], ligands[], jobs[] (polling live status), selectedJobResult with poses and fingerprints. React Query for localhost API calls with 2-second refetch interval on in-progress jobs.
frontend.frontend_risks: 3Dmol.js canvas performance degrades with >50 poses simultaneously rendered; pagination of poses required. Tauri webview on Windows (WebView2) has subtle CSS differences requiring cross-platform testing.

backend.service_design: Axum router with four handler groups: /proteins (CRUD), /ligands (CRUD), /jobs (submit, status, cancel), /results (poses, fingerprints). Shared AppState with Arc<Mutex<SqlitePool>> and tokio::sync::mpsc::Sender<DockingJob> for job queue.
backend.business_modules: DockingJobRunner: spawns tokio::process::Command for AutoDock Vina binary from Tauri resource directory; parses PDBQT output to extract binding affinity scores and pose coordinates; writes poses to SQLite. InteractionFingerprinter: reads pose PDBQT and protein PDB, computes H-bond donors/acceptors within 3.5A distance cutoff and hydrophobic contacts within 4.5A, stores as JSON in docking_poses.fingerprint_json.
backend.failure_handling: AutoDock Vina exit code non-zero written as job status=failed with stderr captured to jobs.error_log; Axum 500 on SQLite write error with Sentry error capture; job cancellation via tokio::process::Child.kill() on DELETE /jobs/{id}

security.auth_design: No authentication; Axum binds to 127.0.0.1:3001 only; OS-level port not externally accessible
security.secrets_management: No secrets; application stores only local file paths and computation results in SQLite
security.security_risks: Tauri allowlist configured to block all external fetch() origins; 3Dmol.js and React bundled as local assets to prevent CDN-based XSS

data.entities: proteins (id, name, pdb_file_path, resolution_angstrom, chain_count, loaded_at), ligands (id, name, file_path, smiles, mol_weight, loaded_at), docking_jobs (id, protein_id, ligand_ids_json, vina_config_json, status, submitted_at, completed_at, error_log), docking_poses (id, job_id, ligand_id, rank, affinity_kcal_mol, rmsd_lb, rmsd_ub, pdbqt_content, fingerprint_json), binding_scores (job_id, ligand_id, best_affinity, pose_count)
data.storage_design: SQLite database at $APP_DATA/dockinglab.db via rusqlite; PDB and SDF files referenced by absolute path (not stored in DB); PDBQT pose content stored in docking_poses for offline re-visualization without re-running jobs
data.migration_notes: SQLite schema migrations via rusqlite_migration crate on app startup; version tracked in schema_version table

devops.deployment_topology: Tauri 2.0 bundler produces .app (macOS), .msi (Windows); AutoDock Vina binary included as Tauri externalBin resource; GitHub Actions builds on macos-14 and windows-2022 runners with cargo build --release
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: cargo test (unit tests for DockingJobRunner parser and InteractionFingerprinter), cargo clippy (lint), tauri build on both platforms, upload installers as release artifacts
devops.observability_stack: Sentry Rust SDK for panic and error capture (offline buffering, sends on next network connection); application log file at $APP_DATA/logs/dockinglab.log via tracing crate

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a local-only desktop app for protein-ligand docking. The key architecture decisions are: Axum HTTP IPC server on 127.0.0.1:3001 (simpler than Tauri native commands), AutoDock Vina binary bundled as Tauri externalBin resource, rusqlite with bundled feature for zero-dependency SQLite, and 3Dmol.js bundled as local asset for offline use. Interaction fingerprint computation is done in the Rust backend by parsing PDBQT pose coordinates against the protein PDB to identify H-bond and hydrophobic contacts.
target_output.title: DockingLab Desktop - Protein-Ligand Docking Workstation Application Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A cross-platform Tauri 2.0 desktop application for structural biologists that unifies protein-ligand docking job submission, AutoDock Vina execution, 3D pose visualization, and interaction fingerprint analysis in a single offline-capable tool. The React 18 frontend communicates via fetch() to a local Axum Rust server that manages Vina subprocess execution, SQLite persistence, and PDBQT output parsing. No external network calls are made; all assets and the Vina binary are bundled in the installer.
target_output.architecture_overview: A Tauri 2.0 application consists of a React 18 webview frontend communicating over HTTP to an Axum 0.7 local server bound exclusively to 127.0.0.1:3001. The Axum server manages four resource domains: proteins (PDB imports), ligands (SDF/MOL2 imports), jobs (AutoDock Vina subprocess lifecycle), and results (pose retrieval and interaction fingerprints). A tokio async job queue processes docking submissions by spawning AutoDock Vina as a managed subprocess via tokio::process::Command using the bundled externalBin binary. Completed PDBQT outputs are parsed by the Rust DockingJobRunner to extract affinity scores and binding poses, which are stored in a SQLite database via rusqlite. The InteractionFingerprinter module computes H-bond and hydrophobic contact annotations from pose coordinates. 3Dmol.js renders molecular structures entirely from local bundled assets with no CDN dependency.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Tauri 2.0, React 18, TypeScript, Zustand, React Query, 3Dmol.js 2.1 (local bundle), Tailwind CSS. Backend: Rust 1.78, Axum 0.7, tokio 1.37, rusqlite 0.31 (bundled SQLite), rusqlite_migration, serde_json, tracing. Docking: AutoDock Vina 1.2.5 (bundled binary, macOS arm64/x86_64 + Windows x64). Build: Tauri CLI 2.0, GitHub Actions (macos-14 and windows-2022). Observability: Sentry Rust SDK, tracing crate with local log file.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Protein Library: PDB file drag-drop import with automatic chain count and resolution extraction from ATOM records; grid view with search by protein name. Ligand Library: SDF and MOL2 batch import; SMILES display parsed from file metadata; selection checkbox for multi-ligand job setup. Job Setup Wizard: protein selection, ligand multi-select, AutoDock Vina search box configuration (center X/Y/Z, size X/Y/Z, exhaustiveness 1-32), job submission with name annotation. Job Monitor: polling status display with elapsed time; cancel button for running jobs. Result Viewer: 3Dmol.js 3D pose renderer with affinity score ranked list; H-bond annotation overlay toggling; hydrophobic contact surface visualization. Multi-Ligand Compare: side-by-side 3Dmol.js panels (up to 4 ligands), ranked affinity bar chart, fingerprint overlap heatmap.
target_output.system_components: Tauri 2.0 app shell (webview wrapper, externalBin resource loader, allowlist config blocking external origins), React 18 SPA (3Dmol.js bundled, Zustand state, React Query polling), Axum 0.7 local server (127.0.0.1:3001, route handlers for proteins/ligands/jobs/results), tokio MPSC job queue (DockingJobRunner consumer), AutoDock Vina subprocess (platform binary in resources/), SQLite database (rusqlite, $APP_DATA/dockinglab.db), InteractionFingerprinter module (Rust, H-bond and hydrophobic distance calculation), Sentry Rust SDK (offline-buffered crash reports), tracing log file ($APP_DATA/logs/dockinglab.log).
target_output.workflows: Docking Job Submission: Researcher opens Job Setup, selects CDK2 protein (loaded from PDB 1FIN.pdb), selects 12 ligands from Ligand Library, sets search box center x=22.5 y=14.3 z=8.7 size=20x20x20, exhaustiveness=8, clicks Submit. Axum POST /jobs creates job record in SQLite (status=queued), sends DockingJob to tokio MPSC channel. DockingJobRunner consumer dequeues job, constructs Vina config file, spawns Vina binary subprocess, monitors stdout for progress lines, parses completed PDBQT output for each ligand, writes affinity scores and PDBQT content to docking_poses. Job status updated to completed. React Query polls GET /jobs/{id} every 2 seconds, updates Zustand jobs[] state. Interaction Fingerprint Generation: Researcher opens result for top pose of ligand L-003 against CDK2. Axum GET /results/{job_id}/poses/{pose_id}/fingerprint calls InteractionFingerprinter, loads protein PDB and pose PDBQT, iterates all protein residue atoms, identifies H-bond donors (NH, OH) within 3.5 angstroms of pose acceptors (O, N), identifies hydrophobic residues (Leu, Val, Ile, Phe) within 4.5 angstroms of pose carbons, returns fingerprint JSON with per-residue contact list. 3Dmol.js renders contact annotations as dashed line sticks.
target_output.data_model: proteins (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, name TEXT, pdb_file_path TEXT, resolution_angstrom REAL, chain_count INTEGER, loaded_at TEXT). ligands (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, name TEXT, file_path TEXT, smiles TEXT, mol_weight REAL, loaded_at TEXT). docking_jobs (id TEXT PK, protein_id INTEGER FK, ligand_ids_json TEXT, vina_config_json TEXT, status TEXT CHECK(status IN ('queued','running','completed','failed','cancelled')), submitted_at TEXT, completed_at TEXT, error_log TEXT). docking_poses (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, job_id TEXT FK, ligand_id INTEGER FK, rank INTEGER, affinity_kcal_mol REAL, rmsd_lb REAL, rmsd_ub REAL, pdbqt_content TEXT, fingerprint_json TEXT). schema_version (version INTEGER PK, applied_at TEXT) managed by rusqlite_migration.
target_output.api_design: Local Axum REST API on 127.0.0.1:3001: POST /proteins {pdb_file_path, name} -> {id, chain_count, resolution}. GET /proteins -> [{id, name, resolution, chain_count}]. POST /ligands {file_path, name} -> {id, smiles, mol_weight}. POST /jobs {protein_id, ligand_ids, vina_config} -> {job_id, status: 'queued'}. GET /jobs/{id} -> {job_id, status, submitted_at, completed_at, ligand_count}. DELETE /jobs/{id} -> 204 (cancels running subprocess). GET /results/{job_id}/poses -> [{pose_id, ligand_id, rank, affinity_kcal_mol, rmsd_lb}]. GET /results/{job_id}/poses/{pose_id}/pdbqt -> {pdbqt_content}. GET /results/{job_id}/poses/{pose_id}/fingerprint -> {hbonds: [{residue, distance_angstrom}], hydrophobic: [{residue, distance_angstrom}]}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Local-only security model: Axum server binds exclusively to 127.0.0.1:3001 via SocketAddr::from(([127,0,0,1], 3001)); no external port binding possible. Tauri allowlist configured in tauri.conf.json to restrict webview fetch() to localhost origin only; all external origins blocked. 3Dmol.js and React bundle served as local Tauri asset protocol (asset://localhost/) with no CDN requests. AutoDock Vina subprocess sandboxed by OS process isolation; subprocess stdin/stdout/stderr pipes controlled by tokio::process::Command with no shell=true. SQLite database protected by OS filesystem permissions at $APP_DATA path. No secrets stored; no credentials required. Sentry error reports contain no file contents, only stack traces and panic messages.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: cargo test (DockingJobRunner PDBQT parser unit tests with fixture files, InteractionFingerprinter H-bond distance calculation tests), cargo clippy --deny warnings, cargo build --release on macos-14 (universal binary for arm64+x86_64) and windows-2022 (x64). Tauri bundler packages: macOS .dmg with Developer ID code signing (notarized), Windows .msi with EV certificate signature. AutoDock Vina binaries included as Tauri externalBin with platform-specific names: vina-x86_64-apple-darwin, vina-aarch64-apple-darwin, vina-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.exe. GitHub Release created on version tag with both platform installers uploaded as assets. SQLite schema migrations run on app startup via rusqlite_migration; user is notified if migration fails and app exits safely.
target_output.observability: Sentry Rust SDK initialized at startup with offline event buffering (events queued to local file if network unavailable, flushed on next successful connection). Tracing crate writes structured log lines to $APP_DATA/logs/dockinglab.log with daily rotation (7-day retention). Log levels: INFO for job lifecycle events (submitted, completed, failed), DEBUG for Vina subprocess stdout progress lines, ERROR for Axum handler failures and SQLite errors captured by Sentry. No cloud metrics; Sentry releases track panic rate and error frequency per app version across the research team.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero ongoing infrastructure cost; desktop app runs entirely on researcher hardware. GitHub Actions build time approximately 15 minutes per release (macOS universal + Windows builds in parallel). Sentry free tier sufficient for research team of 20 users. AutoDock Vina single-job performance: exhaustiveness=8 with 12 ligands completes in 3-8 minutes on a modern MacBook Pro M3; exhaustiveness=32 for high-accuracy runs takes 15-40 minutes. Multi-ligand jobs processed sequentially in the tokio job queue; future parallel execution possible via multiple Vina subprocess workers.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (weeks 1-4): Axum local server with protein and ligand file import, AutoDock Vina subprocess execution for single protein+ligand pair, PDBQT output parsing to SQLite, job status polling, 3Dmol.js pose viewer for top-5 poses, basic affinity score table, Tauri packaging for macOS. Phase 2 (weeks 5-7): Multi-ligand batch job submission (up to 50 ligands), H-bond and hydrophobic interaction fingerprint computation and visualization, multi-ligand affinity comparison chart, Windows build added to CI. Phase 3 (weeks 8-10): Multi-ligand 3D comparison panels, interaction fingerprint overlap heatmap, SQLite export to CSV for downstream analysis, Sentry crash reporting, GitHub Release packaging for both platforms.
target_output.development_guidelines: Axum handlers must not block the tokio runtime; AutoDock Vina subprocess must be spawned via tokio::process::Command::new() not std::process. SQLite writes in Axum handlers must use rusqlite connection from a connection pool (r2d2_sqlite) to prevent blocking. PDBQT parser unit tests must use fixture files committed to the repo, not live Vina runs, for CI speed. 3Dmol.js must be imported from a local bundled path in the Vite build, never from cdnjs or jsdelivr. Tauri allowlist in tauri.conf.json must be reviewed on every dependency update for new external origin requirements. InteractionFingerprinter distance cutoffs (3.5A H-bond, 4.5A hydrophobic) are scientific parameters; changes require structural biology lead sign-off.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: AutoDock Vina binary bundled in installer may fail macOS Gatekeeper verification if Developer ID code signing lapses. Mitigation: notarization via GitHub Actions using APPLE_DEVELOPER_ID secret; automated signing in CI. Risk: SQLite concurrent write from multiple Axum handler tasks causes SQLITE_BUSY errors. Mitigation: WAL mode enabled at startup (PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL) and connection pool max size=1 for writes. Tradeoff: Axum HTTP IPC over Tauri native commands adds latency (localhost TCP round trip ~0.1ms) but simplifies React to standard fetch() and enables unit testing of handlers without Tauri mock. Tradeoff: PDBQT content stored in SQLite for offline re-visualization increases database size (~50KB per pose x 10 poses x 50 ligands = 25MB per job) but eliminates dependency on retained output files.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: 3Dmol.js chosen over NGL Viewer for React 18 compatibility and simpler JavaScript API for programmatic annotation overlays. Axum HTTP IPC chosen over Tauri native commands for easier unit testing and standard fetch() client-side code. AutoDock Vina 1.2.5 chosen over GNINA for offline constraint; GNINA GPU acceleration deferred to phase 2. rusqlite bundled feature compiles SQLite 3.45 into the binary, eliminating system SQLite version dependency on Windows.

metadata.notes: Structural biology docking desktop app; Tauri + Rust Axum local IPC + AutoDock Vina subprocess management.

project_goal.value: Continuously monitor GxP-validated system change control records, detect unauthorized configuration drift in production analytical instruments, and trigger incident tickets with evidence packages in ServiceNow
project_goal.rationale: Manual periodic audits miss configuration drift between formal qualification cycles, creating regulatory non-compliance risk

target_users.value: GxP compliance team, IT operations, instrument validation engineers
target_users.rationale: Compliance team investigates incidents; IT ops manages baseline updates; validation engineers own qualification records

project_class.rationale: Scheduled polling and incident automation; no interactive UI component

capabilities.rationale: Python backend for drift logic, ServiceNow and instrument API integrations, Celery batch polling jobs

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-instrument API polling, baseline diff computation, ServiceNow integration, 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail

risk_level.rationale: False negatives (missed drift) create regulatory audit exposure; false positives create operational burden

data_sensitivity.rationale: Instrument configuration data and validation records are confidential GxP documentation

external_exposure.rationale: All instrument APIs and ServiceNow are on the corporate network; no external exposure

access_model.value: Azure AD Managed Identity for Azure Container Instances; per-instrument service account credentials in Azure Key Vault; ServiceNow API key in Key Vault
access_model.rationale: Managed Identity eliminates static service principal secrets; per-instrument credentials limit blast radius

feature_scope.value: Scheduled configuration polling per instrument, baseline comparison with deepdiff, drift event classification (critical/high/medium/low), ServiceNow incident ticket creation with evidence package, change control record lookup to classify authorized vs unauthorized drift, audit trail for all drift events
feature_scope.rationale: Full detection-to-ticketing workflow for GxP compliance monitoring

mvp_scope.value: HPLC and mass spectrometer polling, baseline comparison, unauthorized drift ServiceNow tickets with diff evidence, audit trail
mvp_scope.rationale: HPLC and MS are highest GxP criticality instruments in the pilot scope

security_baseline.value: Azure Managed Identity for all Azure resource access, instrument credentials in Key Vault, TLS 1.3 for all API calls, audit log append-only in PostgreSQL
security_baseline.rationale: 21 CFR Part 11 and GAMP 5 require tamper-evident audit trail and controlled access

frontend_stack.value: none - automation tool; visibility via ServiceNow tickets and Datadog alerts
frontend_stack.rationale: No interactive UI; compliance team uses ServiceNow for incident management

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Celery 5.4, Redis 7 as broker
backend_stack.rationale: Celery provides reliable distributed task execution with retry policies; Redis broker for Azure Container Instances deployment

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for validated_systems, configuration_baselines, drift_events, and audit_trail tables
data_platform.rationale: Relational model for baseline versioning and drift event history with foreign key integrity

hosting_target.value: Azure Container Instances with Managed Identity; Redis Cache for Celery broker
hosting_target.rationale: ACI suitable for scheduled batch workload without persistent compute; Azure-native for Managed Identity

privacy_retention_policy.value: Drift events and audit trail retained 10 years per 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records retention; configuration snapshots retained 5 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GxP electronic records must be retained for inspection period

future_scope.value: Automated baseline update workflow with electronic approval, drift trend analytics dashboard, integration with LIMS qualification records
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 baseline management and analytics roadmap

constraints.value: Must not modify instrument configurations; polling only via read-only API credentials; ServiceNow integration via REST API v2
constraints.rationale: Read-only instrument access is a safety constraint; no risk of tool causing configuration changes

observability_baseline.value: Datadog for Celery task metrics, drift detection rate, ServiceNow ticket creation success rate; PagerDuty for critical drift events
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate Datadog standard; PagerDuty for GxP critical system alerts

execution_preference.value: Azure DevOps Pipelines for CI/CD; Celery beat scheduler for periodic polling tasks
execution_preference.rationale: Azure DevOps aligns with corporate CI standard; Celery beat replaces cron for distributed scheduling

compliance_context.value: 21 CFR Part 11 (electronic records and signatures), EU GMP Annex 11 (computerised systems), GAMP 5 Category 4 configured product
compliance_context.rationale: GxP-validated system monitoring tool is itself subject to GxP validation requirements as a Category 4 configured product

project.goal: Automated GxP instrument drift detection and ServiceNow incident automation
project.target_users: GxP compliance team, IT ops, validation engineers

security.auth: Azure Managed Identity, Key Vault for instrument and ServiceNow credentials
security.compliance: 21 CFR Part 11, EU GMP Annex 11, GAMP 5

data.cache: Redis 7 on Azure Cache for Redis

reasoner_reviews.product: By round 3, the core drift detection logic is solid. The remaining gap is ServiceNow incident deduplication - the system must not create duplicate tickets for the same active drift event if the polling cycle fires again before the incident is resolved. The change control record lookup to classify authorized vs unauthorized drift also needs clearer handling for the grace period window when a change is in-flight.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The ServiceNow deduplication issue requires idempotent ticket creation: before POST /api/now/table/incident, query for active incidents with the same system_id and drift_event_id as correlation_id. Celery task idempotency key based on drift_event_id prevents duplicate task execution if Redis delivers a task twice. The change control lookup grace period (authorized drift window) should be a per-system configuration parameter in validated_systems, not a global constant.
reasoner_reviews.security: Rounds 1 and 2 left the instrument credential rotation mechanism unspecified. Key Vault secrets must have a defined rotation schedule (90 days for instrument service accounts); the ACI container must handle credential refresh by re-fetching from Key Vault on 401 responses from instrument APIs rather than caching credentials at startup.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The read-only constraint on instrument APIs must be enforced at the Key Vault secret level by granting only the minimum API permission scope on instrument service accounts. If an instrument API does not support read-only scope, the instrument must be excluded from the polling scope and documented as a monitoring gap.

backend.service_design: Three Celery task types: InstrumentPoller (per-instrument, scheduled by beat), DriftClassifier (triggered by poller on snapshot delta), IncidentCreator (triggered by classifier for unauthorized drift). Shared PostgreSQL connection pool via SQLAlchemy 2.0 async engine.
backend.business_modules: InstrumentPoller: fetches current configuration from instrument API using Key Vault credential, serializes to canonical JSONB format, stores as configuration_snapshots record, publishes delta to DriftClassifier task. DriftClassifier: loads current validated baseline from configuration_baselines, calls deepdiff.DeepDiff(baseline, snapshot), classifies each diff key by GAMP 5 criticality lookup table (CriticalParam, MajorParam, MinorParam), checks change_control_records for authorized changes within grace period window, emits drift_events with severity. IncidentCreator: for unauthorized drift events, queries ServiceNow for existing active incident with matching sys_correlation_id, creates new incident via POST /api/now/table/incident with evidence_package JSON attachment if none found.
backend.background_jobs: Celery beat schedule: InstrumentPoller every 15 minutes per instrument (configurable per validated_systems.poll_interval_minutes); DriftClassifier triggered immediately on snapshot delta; IncidentCreator triggered immediately on unauthorized drift classification
backend.failure_handling: Instrument API 401 triggers Key Vault credential refresh and single retry; 5xx triggers Celery retry with exponential backoff (max 3 retries, 5-minute base); ServiceNow API failure writes drift_event.ticket_status=failed and triggers PagerDuty alert; all task failures captured to Datadog with instrument_id tag

security.auth_design: Azure Managed Identity assigned to ACI container group; Managed Identity fetches instrument credentials and ServiceNow API key from Key Vault at task execution time (not startup cache); 90-day Key Vault secret rotation with automatic refresh on 401
security.authorization_model: Per-instrument Key Vault secrets scoped to read-only API permission on target instrument; ServiceNow API key scoped to incident table create/read only; PostgreSQL connection via Managed Identity password-less authentication
security.secrets_management: Key Vault secrets named by pattern: instrument-{system_id}-api-key; ServiceNow API key at servicenow-api-key; rotation alerts via Key Vault expiry notifications to ops team
security.audit_and_logging_controls: audit_trail table records every drift detection cycle: system_id, snapshot_hash, baseline_version, diff_keys_json, classification_result, ticket_id, operator_id (NULL for automated), timestamp - UPDATE/DELETE blocked by trigger. All Celery task executions logged to Datadog with task_name, instrument_id, duration, result.

data.entities: validated_systems (system_id, system_name, instrument_type, api_endpoint, poll_interval_minutes, auth_key_vault_secret, grace_period_minutes, created_at), configuration_baselines (id, system_id, version, baseline_json JSONB, approved_by, approved_at, active BOOLEAN), configuration_snapshots (id, system_id, snapshot_json JSONB, snapshot_hash SHA256, captured_at), drift_events (id, system_id, snapshot_id, baseline_id, diff_json JSONB, severity, authorized BOOLEAN, change_control_id, ticket_id, ticket_status, detected_at), change_control_records (id, system_id, parameter_path, new_value, authorized_by, effective_from, effective_to), audit_trail (id, system_id, event_type, actor, details_json, timestamp)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server; JSONB for baseline and snapshot storage enables deepdiff-compatible key-path queries; configuration_snapshots partitioned by captured_at (monthly) for retention management
data.retention_and_deletion: drift_events and audit_trail retained 10 years via pg_partman lifecycle; configuration_snapshots retained 5 years; old partitions archived to Azure Blob Storage cold tier before DROP

devops.deployment_topology: Azure Container Instances: one container group with Celery worker container and Celery beat container; Azure Cache for Redis (Premium P1 for persistence); Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server; Azure Container Registry for images
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipeline: unit tests (DriftClassifier deepdiff logic with baseline fixtures, IncidentCreator deduplication logic with ServiceNow mock), integration tests (PostgreSQL test container, Redis test container), docker build + ACR push, ACI deployment via az container create
devops.observability_stack: Datadog agent sidecar in ACI container group; custom Datadog metrics: gxp_drift_events_total (counter by severity and system), gxp_poll_duration_seconds (histogram by instrument), servicenow_ticket_created_total (counter by status). PagerDuty critical alert on gxp_drift_events severity=critical within 5 minutes of detection.
devops.rollback_strategy: ACI redeployment from prior ACR image tag; PostgreSQL point-in-time restore; baseline versioning allows rollback to prior approved baseline without data loss

ISS-001.title: ServiceNow duplicate ticket creation on repeated polling cycle
ISS-001.detail: Resolved in round 2 by adding sys_correlation_id query before POST /api/now/table/incident. Deduplication confirmed by integration test with ServiceNow mock.

history.note: Identified: no deduplication check before ticket creation
Fixed: correlation_id query added before incident POST

ISS-002.title: Instrument credential cached at startup not refreshed on rotation
ISS-002.detail: Instrument API credentials fetched from Key Vault at ACI container startup are cached in memory; when Key Vault rotates the secret (90-day schedule), the cached credential becomes invalid and all instrument polling fails with 401 until container restart.

history.note: Not addressed in round 1
Security reviewer flagged: credential refresh on 401 not implemented

ISS-003.title: Change control grace period is a hardcoded global constant
ISS-003.detail: The authorized drift window (change control grace period) is hardcoded as 4 hours in the DriftClassifier, but different instrument classes (analytical balances vs HPLC systems) have different validation cycle windows requiring per-system configurability.

history.note: Architect reviewer identified: per-system grace period needed

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 resolves two remaining issues: ISS-002 (credential cached at startup) and ISS-003 (hardcoded grace period). For ISS-002, the InstrumentPoller task fetches Key Vault credentials on each task invocation with a 1-hour in-memory cache (using functools.lru_cache with TTL via cachetools), and handles 401 responses by invalidating the cache and retrying once before failing. For ISS-003, grace_period_minutes is moved to the validated_systems table as a per-system field, allowing HPLC systems to have a 2-hour window while analytical balances have an 8-hour window per validation cycle expectations.

fix_report.action_taken: Moved instrument credential fetch from ACI container startup to InstrumentPoller task body; implemented cachetools.TTLCache(maxsize=50, ttl=3600) for 1-hour credential caching per system_id; added 401-response handler that calls cache.pop(system_id) and re-fetches from Key Vault before single retry; added unit test for 401 refresh flow with Key Vault mock
fix_report.expected_outcome: Instrument credentials automatically refresh from Key Vault within 1 hour of rotation without container restart; polling outage on rotation eliminated

fix_report.action_taken: Added grace_period_minutes INTEGER column to validated_systems table; seeded with instrument-class defaults (HPLC: 120, mass_spectrometer: 120, analytical_balance: 480, incubator: 240); DriftClassifier now reads validated_systems.grace_period_minutes per system_id instead of GRACE_PERIOD_HOURS constant; database migration adds column with NOT NULL DEFAULT 240 for existing systems; unit test validates per-system grace period lookup
fix_report.expected_outcome: Authorized drift window correctly reflects per-instrument-class validation cycle expectations; false positive rate reduced for instruments with longer qualification windows

target_output.title: GxP Configuration Drift Monitor - Compliance Automation v3
target_output.executive_summary: A Python Celery automation tool on Azure Container Instances that continuously polls GxP-validated analytical instrument APIs, compares current configurations against approved baselines using deepdiff, classifies drift by GAMP 5 criticality, and automatically creates ServiceNow incident tickets with evidence packages for unauthorized drift. Round 3 resolves Key Vault credential rotation handling and per-system authorized drift window configuration, completing the compliance-grade implementation for 21 CFR Part 11, EU GMP Annex 11, and GAMP 5.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three Celery task types form the core automation pipeline: InstrumentPoller fetches current instrument configurations via read-only REST/SOAP APIs using per-system credentials cached with a 1-hour TTL from Azure Key Vault (with 401-triggered refresh), stores snapshots in PostgreSQL, and publishes deltas to DriftClassifier. DriftClassifier loads the current approved baseline version, runs deepdiff.DeepDiff against the snapshot, classifies each differing parameter against the GAMP 5 criticality lookup table (CriticalParam/MajorParam/MinorParam), checks change_control_records for authorized changes within the per-system grace_period_minutes window, and emits drift_events with severity. IncidentCreator checks ServiceNow for existing active incidents via sys_correlation_id before creating new tickets, attaching a full evidence package including the diff JSON, last approved baseline version, and change control record status. All task executions and drift events are recorded in the append-only audit_trail table for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Python 3.12, Celery 5.4 with Redis 7 broker. Drift comparison: deepdiff 7.0. Key Vault integration: azure-keyvault-secrets 4.8 with cachetools 5.3 TTLCache for credential caching. ORM: SQLAlchemy 2.0 async with asyncpg. ServiceNow: requests 2.31 via REST API v2. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server. Cache/broker: Azure Cache for Redis Premium P1. Hosting: Azure Container Instances with Managed Identity. CI/CD: Azure DevOps Pipelines with Azure Container Registry. Observability: Datadog agent sidecar, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Instrument Polling: configurable per-system polling interval (validated_systems.poll_interval_minutes) via Celery beat; read-only credential from Key Vault with 1-hour cache and 401-triggered refresh. Drift Detection: deepdiff comparison of current snapshot against active baseline_json; per-parameter GAMP 5 criticality classification. Change Control Lookup: authorized drift grace window per system (validated_systems.grace_period_minutes) prevents false positives during in-flight change windows. Incident Creation: idempotent ServiceNow ticket creation via sys_correlation_id pre-flight query; evidence package includes diff_json, baseline version, change control status. Audit Trail: append-only PostgreSQL audit_trail records every detection cycle for 10-year GxP retention. Drift Severity Alerting: critical drift triggers PagerDuty P1 within 5 minutes of detection.
target_output.system_components: Azure Container Instances container group (Celery worker container, Celery beat scheduler container, Datadog agent sidecar), Azure Cache for Redis Premium P1 (Celery broker with AOF persistence), Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server (validated_systems, configuration_baselines, configuration_snapshots, drift_events, change_control_records, audit_trail), Azure Container Registry (instrument-drift-monitor Docker images), Azure Key Vault (per-instrument API credentials with 90-day rotation, ServiceNow API key), Azure Managed Identity (assigned to ACI container group, Key Vault get/list secrets, PostgreSQL passwordless auth), ServiceNow REST API v2 endpoint (incident table), instrument API endpoints (HPLC, mass spectrometer, analytical balance REST/SOAP, read-only).
target_output.workflows: Configuration Drift Detection Cycle: Celery beat fires InstrumentPoller task for HPLC-001 (every 15 minutes). InstrumentPoller calls cachetools.TTLCache to retrieve Key Vault secret instrument-hplc-001-api-key; if cache miss or TTL expired, fetches from Key Vault via azure-keyvault-secrets SecretClient. Fetches /api/configuration from HPLC-001 endpoint; if 401 received, calls cache.pop('hplc-001') and re-fetches Key Vault secret, retries once. Canonicalizes response to JSONB, computes SHA-256 hash, stores as configuration_snapshots record. Loads active configuration_baselines record for HPLC-001. Calls deepdiff.DeepDiff(baseline_json, snapshot_json); if no diff, records audit_trail with event_type=no_drift and exits. Publishes DriftClassifier task with snapshot_id and diff result. DriftClassifier reads GAMP 5 criticality lookup for each diff key (e.g., detector_wavelength_nm -> CriticalParam -> severity=critical). Queries change_control_records for active authorized changes within validated_systems.grace_period_minutes=120 for HPLC-001. Unauthorized critical diff: creates drift_events record (authorized=false, severity=critical), publishes IncidentCreator task. IncidentCreator pre-flight: GET /api/now/table/incident?sysparm_query=correlation_id={drift_event_id}^active=true; if result count > 0, updates existing incident with new evidence and exits. If none found: POST /api/now/table/incident with short_description, description including full diff_json, and file attachment with evidence_package JSON; stores ticket sys_id in drift_events.ticket_id. Writes audit_trail record with event_type=incident_created. Key Vault Credential Rotation: IT ops rotates instrument-hplc-001-api-key in Key Vault. Next InstrumentPoller task for HPLC-001 uses cached credential (still valid for up to 1 hour). On cache TTL expiry, next task re-fetches from Key Vault and gets new credential. If rotation causes 401 before cache TTL expires, 401 handler pops cache entry and immediately re-fetches. No container restart required.
target_output.data_model: validated_systems (system_id VARCHAR PK, system_name VARCHAR, instrument_type ENUM hplc/mass_spec/analytical_balance/incubator, api_endpoint VARCHAR, poll_interval_minutes INTEGER DEFAULT 15, auth_key_vault_secret VARCHAR, grace_period_minutes INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 240, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). configuration_baselines (id UUID PK, system_id VARCHAR FK, version INTEGER, baseline_json JSONB, approved_by VARCHAR, approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ, active BOOLEAN DEFAULT false) - only one active=true per system_id. configuration_snapshots (id UUID PK, system_id VARCHAR FK, snapshot_json JSONB, snapshot_hash VARCHAR(64), captured_at TIMESTAMPTZ) partitioned by captured_at monthly. drift_events (id UUID PK, system_id VARCHAR FK, snapshot_id UUID FK, baseline_id UUID FK, diff_json JSONB, severity ENUM critical/high/medium/low, authorized BOOLEAN, change_control_id UUID, ticket_id VARCHAR, ticket_status ENUM created/failed/deduplicated, detected_at TIMESTAMPTZ). change_control_records (id UUID PK, system_id VARCHAR FK, parameter_path VARCHAR, new_value JSONB, authorized_by VARCHAR, effective_from TIMESTAMPTZ, effective_to TIMESTAMPTZ). audit_trail (id UUID PK, system_id VARCHAR, event_type ENUM no_drift/drift_detected/incident_created/incident_deduplicated/baseline_updated, actor VARCHAR DEFAULT 'system', details_json JSONB, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ) - UPDATE/DELETE blocked by trigger.
target_output.api_design: No external HTTP API exposed by this automation tool. Internal integrations: Instrument APIs (read-only GET /api/configuration per instrument); ServiceNow REST API v2 (GET /api/now/table/incident?sysparm_query=correlation_id={id}^active=true for deduplication check, POST /api/now/table/incident for ticket creation, POST /api/now/attachment/file?table_name=incident&table_sys_id={sys_id} for evidence package attachment); Azure Key Vault SDK (SecretClient.get_secret(name) per instrument); Azure PostgreSQL passwordless auth via Managed Identity token. Celery task interfaces: InstrumentPoller.apply_async(args=[system_id], eta=next_poll_time), DriftClassifier.apply_async(args=[snapshot_id, diff_result_json]), IncidentCreator.apply_async(args=[drift_event_id]).
target_output.security_and_compliance: 21 CFR Part 11: audit_trail table append-only enforced via PostgreSQL trigger (BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE RAISE EXCEPTION); all drift detection cycle events recorded with actor, timestamp, and details_json; electronic records retained 10 years via pg_partman. EU GMP Annex 11: computerised system validation for the tool itself as GAMP 5 Category 4; test protocols include DriftClassifier accuracy tests and IncidentCreator deduplication tests. GAMP 5 criticality matrix encoded in gamp5_criticality_lookup table seeded per instrument type. Azure security: Managed Identity for ACI with no static service principal secrets; per-instrument Key Vault secrets scoped to read-only instrument API permission; credentials fetched on demand with 1-hour TTLCache (cachetools); 401 response triggers cache invalidation and Key Vault re-fetch before single retry; 90-day Key Vault secret rotation with expiry notifications. PostgreSQL: Managed Identity passwordless auth (no DB password in config); SSL required. Instrument access: read-only API scope enforced at Key Vault secret provisioning time.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure DevOps Pipeline stages: unit-test (DriftClassifier with deepdiff fixture baselines, IncidentCreator deduplication logic with requests-mock ServiceNow mock, InstrumentPoller credential refresh flow with azure-keyvault mock, per-system grace_period_minutes lookup test), integration-test (PostgreSQL test container, Redis test container, SQLAlchemy migration test), docker-build (push to ACR with Git SHA tag), deploy (az container create with Managed Identity assignment, Redis and PostgreSQL connection strings from Key Vault, rolling update by stopping beat container first then worker). Environments: dev (ACI single container, Redis Basic, PostgreSQL dev instance, polling disabled for non-test instruments), staging (ACI 2-container group, Redis Standard, PostgreSQL staging, full polling enabled for non-production instruments), production (ACI 2-container group, Redis Premium P1 with AOF, PostgreSQL Flexible Server, full instrument scope). Database migrations: Alembic applied at container startup; migration for ISS-003 adds grace_period_minutes column with DEFAULT 240 for existing rows. Configuration snapshots partitioned monthly with pg_partman; old partitions cold-archived to Azure Blob after 5-year retention threshold.
target_output.observability: Datadog agent sidecar in ACI container group. Custom DogStatsD metrics emitted by Celery tasks: gxp_drift_events_total (tags: severity, system_id, authorized), gxp_poll_duration_seconds (histogram, tag: system_id), gxp_servicenow_ticket_created_total (tags: status=created/failed/deduplicated), gxp_credential_refresh_total (tag: trigger=ttl_expiry/401_response). Datadog dashboards: instrument polling health (success rate per system, poll duration trends), drift event frequency by severity and instrument type, ServiceNow ticket backlog, credential refresh frequency. PagerDuty alerts via Datadog monitor: gxp_drift_events_total severity=critical within last 5 minutes (P1), gxp_poll_duration_seconds p95 >120s for any instrument (P2 polling degradation), ServiceNow ticket creation failure for critical drift (P1). Celery Flower monitoring for task queue depth and worker health.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Azure cost estimate ~$800/month for production: Azure Cache for Redis Premium P1 at $400/month (persistence required for Celery task reliability), Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server Standard_D2s_v3 at $200/month, Azure Container Instances at $50/month for 2-container group, Azure Container Registry Basic at $5/month, Key Vault at $5/month. Scaling to 50 instruments: Redis Premium P1 handles 100+ concurrent Celery tasks without upgrade; PostgreSQL Standard_D4s_v3 upgrade at $400/month if snapshot write throughput exceeds current capacity. Celery worker concurrency=10 per ACI container handles 50 15-minute polling cycles comfortably.
target_output.phased_implementation: Production-readiness completing in round 3: Sprint 3a (current, weeks 1-2) - ISS-002 fix: cachetools TTLCache credential caching with 401 refresh, Key Vault mock unit tests; ISS-003 fix: grace_period_minutes column migration, DriftClassifier per-system lookup, seeded instrument-class defaults. Sprint 3b (weeks 3-4) - End-to-end integration test in staging with live HPLC and mass spec instruments, GAMP 5 validation protocol execution (IQ/OQ/PQ for the tool as Category 4), production deployment with full instrument scope, Datadog dashboard and PagerDuty alert configuration. Sprint 3c (weeks 5-6) - Compliance team sign-off, IT ops runbook documentation, Key Vault rotation procedure tested in staging, GxP validation summary report.
target_output.development_guidelines: InstrumentPoller must fetch Key Vault credentials via cachetools TTLCache(maxsize=50, ttl=3600) inside the task body, never at module import time or container startup. 401 response handling must call TTLCache.__delitem__(system_id) before Key Vault re-fetch - use a helper function get_instrument_credential(system_id, force_refresh=False) for testability. DriftClassifier must read grace_period_minutes from the validated_systems record passed to the task, never from a module-level constant. deepdiff DeepDiff must be called with ignore_order=True for list-valued configuration parameters to prevent false positives on re-ordered lists with identical values. IncidentCreator must perform the ServiceNow correlation_id pre-flight GET before every POST - never skip the deduplication check. All Celery tasks must emit task_name and system_id as DogStatsD tags for per-instrument observability. Alembic migrations must be idempotent (use op.execute with IF NOT EXISTS guards).
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: An instrument API returns non-deterministic parameter ordering in its response JSON, causing deepdiff to flag benign changes as drift on every poll. Mitigation: DriftClassifier normalizes all list-valued parameters with sorted() before deepdiff comparison; per-parameter ignore rules configurable in validated_systems. Risk: grace_period_minutes window set too short for an instrument causes authorized drift during a change window to generate tickets. Mitigation: grace_period_minutes seeded conservatively (instrument-class defaults); compliance team can extend per-system via admin SQL with audit trail record. Tradeoff: 1-hour TTLCache for credentials means up to 1 hour polling delay after Key Vault rotation before new credential is active (if no 401 triggers earlier refresh); acceptable given 90-day rotation schedule.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ServiceNow deduplication resolved in round 2 via sys_correlation_id pre-flight query. Credential rotation resolved in round 3 via TTLCache with 401-triggered refresh. Per-system grace period resolved in round 3 via validated_systems.grace_period_minutes column. Celery chosen over APScheduler for distributed task execution across multiple ACI containers. deepdiff 7 chosen over manual JSON comparison for rich diff output format compatible with GAMP 5 evidence documentation requirements.

metadata.notes: GxP drift detection round 3; resolves Key Vault credential rotation and per-system grace period; 21 CFR Part 11 compliant.

project_goal.value: Replace legacy Oracle Forms PV system with a web-based ICSR case management application supporting MedDRA coding, causality assessment, and E2B(R3) electronic submission to EudraVigilance and FDA FAERS
project_goal.rationale: Oracle Forms end-of-life forces migration; regulatory submission deadlines require modern tooling

target_users.value: Pharmacovigilance scientists, medical reviewers, case intake coordinators, regulatory affairs specialists
target_users.rationale: Primary user groups identified in discovery workshops

project_class.rationale: Browser-based application with complex multi-role workflows; no mobile requirement at MVP

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, integrations, analytics, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: Full-featured PV system requiring ICH gateway integration and MedDRA dictionary service

complexity_level.rationale: 500 ICSRs/day peak with multi-tier review workflows and binding 15-day regulatory submission SLAs

risk_level.rationale: Patient safety data with direct regulatory authority submission obligations; submission errors have immediate compliance consequences

data_sensitivity.rationale: ICSRs contain patient-identifiable adverse event data subject to HIPAA and EU GDPR Article 9

external_exposure.rationale: All access requires Okta SSO with SAML federation; no unauthenticated endpoints

access_model.value: RBAC with roles: case_intake, pv_scientist, medical_reviewer, regulatory_affairs, admin; MFA mandatory for all roles
access_model.rationale: Different user groups have different case access and submission rights under GVP Module VI

feature_scope.value: AE intake and narrative capture, MedDRA term coding, causality assessment workflow, multi-level medical review, E2B(R3) XML generation, ICH gateway submission to EudraVigilance and FAERS, submission receipt tracking, case analytics dashboard, admin panel
feature_scope.rationale: Full ICSR lifecycle from intake to regulatory submission

mvp_scope.value: AE intake form, MedDRA coding UI with SMQ browser, two-tier medical review workflow, E2B(R3) XML export, manual submission to EudraVigilance and FAERS
mvp_scope.rationale: Automates highest-effort manual steps; automated ICH gateway submission in Phase 2

security_baseline.value: AES-256 at rest via Oracle TDE, TLS 1.3 in transit, Okta MFA enforced, field-level audit logging for all ICSR edits with old/new values and change_reason, 15-minute idle session timeout
security_baseline.rationale: Patient safety data and GVP Module VI require comprehensive tamper-evident audit trails

frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; Angular is standard in enterprise pharma applications

backend_stack.value: Java 21 with Spring Boot 3, Spring Security, Hibernate 6 with Envers
backend_stack.rationale: Enterprise Java standard; team proficient; strong Oracle JDBC support; Envers for audit revision tracking

data_platform.value: Oracle Database 19c (migrated from 12c), Redis 7 for session cache and MedDRA dictionary
data_platform.rationale: Oracle retained for schema migration continuity; Redis for distributed session management and MedDRA term caching

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in eu-west-1 (primary) and us-east-1, origin-pinned case routing, Oracle GoldenGate replication for DR
hosting_target.rationale: EU and US data residency for EudraVigilance and FAERS submissions; GoldenGate provides DR failover

privacy_retention_policy.value: ICSRs retained 25 years per ICH E2B guidelines; audit logs retained 15 years; session logs 1 year; GDPR deletion via pseudonymization not hard-delete
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Regulatory pharmacovigilance record retention requirements; hard delete would destroy required case structure

future_scope.value: Automated ICH gateway submission with SQS retry, signal detection analytics, DSUR/PSUR aggregate reporting, SAML federation for partner CROs, mobile companion for field case intake
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2-3 roadmap following MVP validation

constraints.value: 500 ICSRs/day peak throughput; 15-day regulatory submission clock from AE receipt date; EU GDPR Article 9 for patient health data; Oracle licensing budget capped at current spend
constraints.rationale: Submission SLAs are legally binding; GDPR mandates lawful basis for health data processing

observability_baseline.value: Splunk for log aggregation and SIEM, Micrometer/JMX metrics, PagerDuty P1 alerts for submission DLQ drain and DB saturation
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate SIEM standard; submission failures require immediate on-call escalation

execution_preference.value: Scrum with 3-week sprints; release candidate every 6 weeks with PV team UAT gate before production
execution_preference.rationale: Complex regulatory domain requires iterative validation with pharmacovigilance subject matter experts

compliance_context.value: ICH E2B(R3) for ICSR format and submission, EU GVP Module VI for pharmacovigilance procedures, FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32 for IND safety reporting, EMA EudraVigilance Technical Specifications v8.1, GDPR Article 9 for sensitive health data processing
compliance_context.rationale: Multi-jurisdictional PV obligations with direct regulatory authority submission requirements

project.goal: ICSR case management replacing Oracle Forms PV system
project.target_users: PV scientists, medical reviewers, regulatory affairs

frontend.styling: Angular Material + custom PV component theme
frontend.state: NgRx for case workflow state machine, RxJS WebSocket for real-time queue updates

backend.framework: Spring Boot 3 with Spring Security, Hibernate 6 with Envers
backend.api: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.0, versioned at /api/v1, idempotency keys on case and submission creation

security.auth: Okta OIDC + SAML 2.0 federation
security.mfa: Okta MFA enforced for all roles via SAML assertion attribute
security.audit: Field-level Hibernate Envers audit capturing old/new values and change_reason on every ICSR mutation

data.db: Oracle 19c with TDE and Row-Level Security for case ownership
data.cache: Redis 7 for sessions (15-min sliding TTL) and MedDRA 26.1 dictionary (500K terms preloaded)
data.search: Oracle Text for ICSR narrative full-text search

devops.ci: Jenkins declarative pipeline with SonarQube quality gate
devops.hosting: AWS ECS Fargate eu-west-1 primary, us-east-1 DR

constraints.residency: EU cases primary in eu-west-1, US cases primary in us-east-1

reasoner_reviews.product: The 15-day submission clock is the single highest-stakes constraint in the system. The case queue UI must expose days_remaining prominently with automated escalation reminders at day 10 and day 13. MedDRA coding is consistently the bottleneck in PV operations -- the coding workspace must support SMQ search and partial-term autocomplete against the Redis-cached dictionary to keep per-case coding time under 15 minutes.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Origin-pinned case routing is the correct pattern for dual-region active-active: receipt_country at intake determines the primary region for all workflow steps, with Oracle GoldenGate bidirectional replication for DR only -- never for live cross-region reads. The E2B(R3) XML generation module needs its own service boundary with DTD schema validation before any gateway dispatch to prevent rejected transmission errors that consume submission SLA time.
reasoner_reviews.security: Field-level audit logging under GVP Module VI requires capturing old_value, new_value, user_id, and change_reason for every ICSR field mutation -- entity-level save events are insufficient. Hibernate Envers provides this automatically via @Audited annotations. Oracle RLS must restrict SELECT and UPDATE to the assigned reviewer role tier per case. Redis session tokens must store only case IDs and role claims -- no patient data.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Processing 500 ICSRs/day requires the review queue to support parallel workload assignment across PV scientists. The MedDRA dictionary Redis cache (~500K terms at approximately 200MB) must be preloaded at application startup to avoid cold-query latency on the coding screen. The SQS retry window of 72 hours for ICH gateway failures provides adequate buffer for planned gateway maintenance windows.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 1 plan lacked field-level audit granularity (ISS-002) and dual-region case routing definition (ISS-003). The E2B submission retry issue (ISS-001) was resolved. This round must add Hibernate Envers-backed CaseAuditListener with change_reason enforcement on regulatory fields, and explicitly define the origin-region pinning model with GoldenGate replication topology.

frontend.app_structure: Angular 17 lazy-loaded feature modules: CaseIntakeModule, MedDRACodingModule, ReviewWorkflowModule, SubmissionModule, AnalyticsModule, AdminModule. Each module has its own NgRx feature store slice.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Case Intake (AE narrative entry + suspect drug table + patient demographics), MedDRA Coding Workspace (PT/LLT autocomplete with SMQ browser panel), Review Queue (sortable by days_remaining ascending with countdown badges), Medical Review Form (causality assessment matrix per suspect drug), Submission Tracker (E2B XML preview + gateway dispatch + ACK tracking), Analytics Dashboard (case aging heatmap, on-time submission rate by authority), Admin Config (user provisioning, MedDRA version management, gateway credentials).
frontend.state_management: NgRx store with typed actions for case lifecycle state transitions. CaseEffects handles API calls and WebSocket reconnection. RxJS WebSocket observable for real-time queue update push from backend on case assignment or status change.
frontend.ui_modules: CaseAgeIndicator component displaying days remaining with color thresholds (green >7, amber 4-7, red <4). MedDRACoder autocomplete component with PT/LLT hierarchy drill-down. CausalityMatrix grid for per-drug causality assessment. E2BPreviewPanel rendering formatted XML diff before submission dispatch.
frontend.frontend_risks: MedDRA SMQ search latency on large group queries -- mitigated by Redis caching. Concurrent edits to same case causing NgRx state divergence -- mitigated by ETag-based optimistic lock on PATCH /cases/:id.

backend.service_design: Spring Boot 3 domain modules: CaseManagementService (ICSR CRUD + workflow state machine), MedDRAService (dictionary queries against Redis cache with Oracle fallback), E2BGeneratorService (E2B(R3) XML serialization with DTD validation), GatewaySubmissionService (ICH gateway HTTP client with SQS-backed retry), AuditService (Envers revision query API).
backend.api_patterns: REST /api/v1 versioned controllers. POST /cases and POST /cases/:id/submissions require Idempotency-Key header. PATCH /cases/:id requires If-Match ETag for optimistic concurrency. Paginated GET /cases queue endpoint with filter by status, region, and days_remaining.
backend.background_jobs: Quartz scheduler: nightly submission deadline reminder job (fires at day 10 and day 13), daily MedDRA 26.1 dictionary refresh from MSSO distribution, SQS submission retry consumer with exponential backoff (15min, 1h, 6h, 24h, max 72h total). DLQ drain triggers PagerDuty alert.
backend.failure_handling: ICH gateway submission failures enqueued to SQS DLQ after 3 attempts with PagerDuty P1 alert. Oracle connection pool monitored via Micrometer with circuit breaker on pool saturation. Global Spring @ControllerAdvice maps domain exceptions to structured problem+json responses.

security.auth_design: Okta OIDC + SAML 2.0 SP-initiated login. Spring Security ResourceServer validates Okta JWT on every request, checking mfa_authenticated claim. Session tokens stored in Redis with 15-minute sliding TTL; session data contains only user_id, role, and case_region -- no patient data.
security.authorization_model: Spring @PreAuthorize method security per role tier. Oracle RLS policies: pv_scientist rows filtered to own-assigned cases; medical_reviewer sees in_review cases in their origin region; regulatory_affairs sees approved cases for submission; admin sees all. Role elevation requires re-authentication.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Hibernate Envers @Audited on AdverseEvent, CaseSafetyReport, Drug entities. CaseAuditListener validates change_reason is non-null for regulated fields (narrative_text, meddra_pt_code, causality_assessment). Audit records written to append-only Oracle audit_log table -- no UPDATE or DELETE granted to application DB user. Audit events forwarded to Splunk via Fluentd sidecar.
security.security_risks: ICSR data exposure via over-permissive Oracle queries mitigated by RLS policies tested in staging. ICH gateway replay attacks mitigated by idempotency key validation on submission records.

data.entities: adverse_events (id UUID PK, case_number VARCHAR2(20) UNIQUE, receipt_date DATE, receipt_country CHAR(2), report_type ENUM spontaneous/literature/clinical_trial/regulatory, narrative_text CLOB, submission_deadline_date DATE, status ENUM new/coding/in_review/approved/submitted/closed, origin_region ENUM eu/us, assigned_scientist_id FK). case_safety_reports (id UUID PK, ae_id FK, meddra_pt_code CHAR(8), meddra_llt_code CHAR(8), meddra_pt_term, seriousness_criteria VARRAY, causality_assessment ENUM related/unrelated/possible/probable/unknown, is_suspect_flag BOOLEAN). drugs (id UUID PK, ae_id FK, drug_name, who_drug_code, dose, dose_unit, route, indication, suspect_or_concomitant ENUM). patients (id UUID PK, ae_id FK, age INT, age_unit ENUM, sex ENUM, weight_kg DECIMAL, relevant_medical_history CLOB -- pseudonymized per GDPR on deletion request). regulatory_submissions (id UUID PK, ae_id FK, authority ENUM eudravigilance/faers, e2b_xml_s3_key, submitted_at TIMESTAMP, acknowledgement_number, status ENUM pending/accepted/rejected, attempt_count INT). audit_log (id UUID PK, case_id FK, user_id FK, field_name, old_value VARCHAR2(4000), new_value VARCHAR2(4000), change_reason VARCHAR2(500), changed_at TIMESTAMP).
data.storage_design: Oracle 19c RDS Custom primary in eu-west-1 with TDE encryption; Oracle GoldenGate bidirectional replication to us-east-1 replica with origin-wins conflict resolution for DR failover. Redis 7 ElastiCache per region for MedDRA 26.1 dictionary cache and session store. S3 for archived E2B XML submission artifacts with server-side encryption.
data.retention_and_deletion: ICSRs and regulatory_submissions retained 25 years per ICH E2B retention guidelines. audit_log retained 15 years. GDPR deletion requests handled via pseudonymization of patients table identifiers -- hard delete prohibited to preserve ICSR pharmacovigilance record structure required by GVP Module VI.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate clusters in eu-west-1 and us-east-1. ALB with WAF (SQL injection + XSS rules) per region. Route53 latency-based routing with health check failover. Jenkins builds Docker images, pushes to ECR, triggers rolling ECS deployments per region in sequence (eu-west-1 first, 10-minute soak, then us-east-1).
devops.environments: dev (single AZ eu-west-1, shared Oracle instance), staging-eu and staging-us (mirroring prod topology, connected to EudraVigilance and FAERS test gateway environments for E2B submission testing), prod-eu and prod-us (multi-AZ Fargate with Oracle RDS Custom multi-AZ and GoldenGate replication).
devops.ci_cd_design: Jenkins declarative pipeline: Maven compile -> JUnit/Mockito unit tests -> SonarQube quality gate (80% coverage) -> Docker build -> ECR push -> ECS rolling deploy eu-west-1 -> smoke tests -> 10-min soak -> ECS rolling deploy us-east-1 -> smoke tests -> Slack notify. Manual approval gate before prod deployments. Feature branches deploy to dev; main deploys to staging on merge.
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS service maintains prior task definition revision for immediate one-click rollback. Jenkins supports rollback to any ECR image tag. Oracle schema changes via Flyway versioned migrations with undo scripts maintained for every changeset.

ISS-001.title: ICH gateway submission retry logic absent
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan described E2B XML generation but contained no retry mechanism for gateway timeouts or rejection responses. Submissions failing silently would violate GVP Module VI 15-day SLA.

history.note: Gateway client had no retry, backoff, or DLQ design

ISS-002.title: Field-level audit log granularity insufficient
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 audit logging captured entity-level save events but did not record per-field old/new values and reason-for-change, required under GVP Module VI and 21 CFR Part 312.32.

history.note: audit_log table schema missing old_value, new_value, change_reason columns

ISS-003.title: Dual-region case routing strategy undefined
ISS-003.detail: Active-active Fargate deployment described without specifying how cases are pinned to origin region or how cross-region replication is handled for DR.

history.note: No origin-pinning or replication topology defined for EU vs US case ownership

focus_issues.title: Field-level audit log granularity insufficient
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 audit logging captured entity-level save events but did not record per-field old/new values and reason-for-change.

focus_issues.title: Dual-region case routing strategy undefined
focus_issues.detail: Active-active Fargate deployment described without specifying origin-region case pinning or GoldenGate replication topology.

latest_recommendations: Implement Hibernate Envers CaseAuditListener capturing per-field old/new values and mandatory change_reason on regulated ICSR fields
Define origin-region case pinning model with explicit receipt_country routing table and Oracle GoldenGate DR topology

convergence_state.reason: Two issues (1 high, 1 medium) unresolved; score 6.9 below passing threshold of 7.5

previous_audits.summary: Solid foundation for the PV case management system with appropriate MedDRA integration design, but critical gaps in audit trail field granularity and the absence of dual-region routing definition prevent compliance with GVP Module VI.
strengths: MedDRA dictionary Redis caching strategy well-specified
Okta SAML 2.0 federation and MFA design correctly scoped
E2B(R3) XML generation service boundary appropriately isolated
concerns: audit_log schema captures entity-level saves but lacks per-field old/new values and change_reason required by GVP Module VI
No routing logic for EU vs US case origin-pinning in dual-region deployment
Dual-region DR replication mechanism not defined
blocking_issues: audit_log missing old_value, new_value, change_reason columns -- regulatory blocker for GVP Module VI compliance
recommendations: Add Hibernate Envers @Audited annotations on ICSR entities with CaseAuditListener enforcing non-null change_reason on regulated fields
Define case ownership pinning: receipt_country at intake determines origin region; Oracle GoldenGate bidirectional replication for DR with origin-wins conflict resolution
