previous_plan.title: Adverse Event ICSR Case Management Platform v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: A Spring Boot 3 / Angular 17 application on AWS ECS Fargate replacing Oracle Forms for pharmacovigilance ICSR management, supporting MedDRA coding and E2B(R3) XML generation for EudraVigilance and FAERS submissions.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Three-tier web application: Angular 17 SPA behind CloudFront, Spring Boot 3 REST API on ECS Fargate, Oracle 19c RDS Custom for ICSR persistence. Deployed in eu-west-1 and us-east-1 active-active. Okta SAML 2.0 handles authentication with MFA.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Okta MFA, TLS 1.3, Oracle TDE at rest. Audit logging at entity-level save events. ICH E2B(R3) XML generated by E2BGeneratorService with DTD validation.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: Jenkins CI/CD to ECS Fargate in eu-west-1 and us-east-1. Rolling ECS deployments with ALB health check gates. Flyway versioned DB migrations.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 addresses ISS-002 (field-level audit granularity) and ISS-003 (dual-region case routing). Hibernate Envers with a CaseAuditListener is the correct Spring pattern for capturing per-field old/new values automatically -- adding change_reason enforcement on regulated fields satisfies GVP Module VI without manual audit log writes. For dual-region routing, receipt_country at intake determines origin_region (EU -> eu-west-1, US -> us-east-1); Oracle GoldenGate bidirectional replication with origin-wins conflict resolution provides DR without cross-region live reads. MedDRA 26.1 selected over 26.0 to include updated COVID-19 AE SMQs.

fix_report.action_taken: Added Hibernate Envers @Audited annotations on AdverseEvent, CaseSafetyReport, and Drug entities. CaseAuditListener validates that change_reason is non-null for regulated fields (narrative_text, meddra_pt_code, meddra_llt_code, causality_assessment) and writes field diffs to Oracle audit_log table (old_value VARCHAR2(4000), new_value VARCHAR2(4000), change_reason VARCHAR2(500)). audit_log schema updated; no UPDATE or DELETE granted to application DB user.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Every ICSR field mutation produces a GVP Module VI-compliant audit record with before/after values and mandatory reason-for-change; satisfies 21 CFR Part 312.32 field-level audit trail requirement

fix_report.action_taken: Defined origin-region case ownership model: receipt_country ISO-3166 code at intake maps to origin_region column (EU countries -> eu-west-1, US -> us-east-1). All workflow steps (coding, review, submission) operate against origin-region primary Oracle instance. Oracle GoldenGate bidirectional replication with origin-wins conflict resolution provides DR failover. Route53 health checks fail reads to replica region if primary unhealthy.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Clear case routing with GDPR Article 9 data residency compliance for EU cases and defined DR behavior; eliminates ambiguity about cross-region case ownership

target_output.title: Adverse Event ICSR Case Management Platform
target_output.executive_summary: A GVP Module VI and ICH E2B(R3)-compliant web application replacing Oracle Forms for end-to-end ICSR pharmacovigilance case management. Built on Angular 17 and Spring Boot 3 deployed on AWS ECS Fargate in dual regions (eu-west-1 primary for EU cases, us-east-1 primary for US cases with Oracle GoldenGate DR replication), the system manages the full PV lifecycle: AE intake, MedDRA 26.1 coding, multi-tier medical review, E2B(R3) XML generation with DTD validation, and ICH gateway submission to EudraVigilance and FDA FAERS with SQS-backed retry and PagerDuty escalation.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture: Angular 17 SPA served via CloudFront communicating over TLS 1.3 to Spring Boot 3 REST API on ECS Fargate behind ALB with WAF rules. Oracle 19c RDS Custom provides ICSR persistence with TDE encryption at rest and Row-Level Security enforcing role-based case access. Redis 7 ElastiCache caches MedDRA 26.1 dictionary (500K terms, preloaded at startup) and distributes Okta session tokens with 15-minute sliding TTL. Cases are origin-pinned at intake: receipt_country determines primary region (EU countries to eu-west-1, US to us-east-1); Oracle GoldenGate bidirectional replication with origin-wins conflict resolution provides DR failover without cross-region live reads. Submission flow: E2BGeneratorService serializes case to ICH E2B(R3) XML, validates against DTD, dispatches via GatewaySubmissionService; on gateway failure, message enters SQS with exponential backoff retry up to 72 hours; DLQ drain triggers PagerDuty P1 escalation.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Angular 17, TypeScript, Angular Material, NgRx, RxJS WebSocket. Backend: Java 21, Spring Boot 3, Spring Security OAuth2 Resource Server, Hibernate 6 with Envers, Micrometer, Quartz scheduler. Database: Oracle 19c RDS Custom with TDE (eu-west-1 primary), Oracle GoldenGate bidirectional replication, Redis 7 ElastiCache. Messaging: SQS standard queue with DLQ for ICH gateway retry. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate dual-region, ALB + WAF, CloudFront, Route53 latency routing, ECR, Secrets Manager, S3. Monitoring: Splunk SIEM + log aggregation, Fluentd sidecar, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: AE Intake: structured form capturing patient demographics, suspect drugs with dosing, event narrative, receipt_date, auto-calculation of submission_deadline_date (receipt_date + 15 days), origin_region assignment from receipt_country. MedDRA Coding Workspace: PT/LLT autocomplete against Redis-cached MedDRA 26.1 dictionary with SMQ browser for signal groupings; coding time target <15 minutes per case. Causality Assessment: per-drug WHO-UMC criteria causality matrix with related/unrelated/possible/probable/unknown classification. Multi-Tier Medical Review: case_intake -> pv_scientist -> medical_reviewer -> regulatory_affairs progression with rejection-with-comments and return-to-coder paths, real-time queue updates via WebSocket. E2B(R3) Submission: XML generation, DTD validation, ICH gateway dispatch, ACK receipt tracking, SQS retry on failure. Analytics: case aging heatmap, on-time submission rate by authority, coding time distribution by scientist. Admin: user provisioning, MedDRA version management, ICH gateway certificate rotation.
target_output.system_components: CloudFront distribution serving Angular SPA assets. ALB with WAF (SQL injection and XSS rule sets) per region. ECS Fargate clusters running API task containers and Quartz background job containers. Oracle 19c RDS Custom primary (eu-west-1) with GoldenGate replication to us-east-1 DR replica. Redis 7 ElastiCache clusters per region for MedDRA term cache and session store. SQS standard queue for ICH gateway submission retry, DLQ after 3 attempts triggering PagerDuty P1. Okta tenant with SAML 2.0 and OIDC. Secrets Manager for Oracle JDBC credentials, Okta client secrets, ICH gateway PKCS12 keystores. Fluentd sidecar on each ECS task forwarding structured JSON logs to Splunk indexers.
target_output.workflows: AE Intake and Origin Assignment: Reporter contact (internal or external) enters AE narrative, suspect drugs, and patient demographics on intake form; receipt_date recorded at submission; submission_deadline_date = receipt_date + 15 days; origin_region set from receipt_country ISO code (EU countries -> eu-west-1, US -> us-east-1); case assigned to next available pv_scientist in origin-region queue by workload balancing; SES notification sent to assigned scientist. MedDRA Coding and Review Progression: PV scientist opens case from queue sorted by days_remaining ascending; selects preferred term from Redis autocomplete (<50ms); assigns LLT; completes WHO-UMC causality assessment per suspect drug; saves triggering CaseAuditListener field-level diff write to audit_log with mandatory change_reason; advances case to medical_reviewer. E2B(R3) Gateway Submission: Regulatory affairs specialist triggers XML generation; E2BGeneratorService serializes ICSR to ICH E2B(R3) schema and validates against DTD; on validation pass, submission queued via GatewaySubmissionService; gateway client POSTs to EudraVigilance or FAERS ICH gateway endpoint; on HTTP 200 plus ACK number, regulatory_submissions record updated to accepted; on timeout or 5xx, SQS retry with backoff (15min, 1h, 6h, 24h max 72h); after 3 failures, DLQ drain fires PagerDuty P1 for manual fallback submission.
target_output.data_model: adverse_events (id UUID PK, case_number VARCHAR2(20) UNIQUE, receipt_date DATE, receipt_country CHAR(2), report_type ENUM spontaneous/literature/clinical_trial/regulatory, narrative_text CLOB, submission_deadline_date DATE, status ENUM new/coding/in_review/approved/submitted/closed, origin_region ENUM eu/us, assigned_scientist_id FK, created_at TIMESTAMP). case_safety_reports (id UUID PK, ae_id FK, meddra_pt_code CHAR(8), meddra_llt_code CHAR(8), meddra_pt_term VARCHAR2(100), seriousness_criteria VARCHAR2(200), causality_assessment ENUM related/unrelated/possible/probable/unknown, is_suspect_flag BOOLEAN). drugs (id UUID PK, ae_id FK, drug_name VARCHAR2(200), who_drug_code, dose DECIMAL, dose_unit VARCHAR2(20), route VARCHAR2(50), indication VARCHAR2(200), suspect_or_concomitant ENUM). patients (id UUID PK, ae_id FK, age INT, age_unit ENUM years/months/weeks, sex ENUM male/female/unknown, weight_kg DECIMAL, relevant_medical_history CLOB -- fields pseudonymized on GDPR deletion request). regulatory_submissions (id UUID PK, ae_id FK, authority ENUM eudravigilance/faers, e2b_xml_s3_key VARCHAR2(500), submitted_at TIMESTAMP, acknowledgement_number VARCHAR2(50), status ENUM pending/accepted/rejected, attempt_count INT, last_attempt_at TIMESTAMP). audit_log (id UUID PK, case_id FK, user_id FK, field_name VARCHAR2(100), old_value VARCHAR2(4000), new_value VARCHAR2(4000), change_reason VARCHAR2(500), changed_at TIMESTAMP -- no UPDATE or DELETE granted on this table).
target_output.api_design: REST /api/v1 with OpenAPI 3.0 spec. POST /cases (create AE intake, Idempotency-Key header required, returns case_number and submission_deadline_date). GET /cases?status=coding&region=eu&sort=deadline_asc&page=0&size=50 (PV scientist queue with pagination). GET /cases/:id (full case detail including suspect drugs, MedDRA codes, causality assessments). PATCH /cases/:id (field update, If-Match ETag required, change_reason body field mandatory for narrative_text, meddra_pt_code, causality_assessment). POST /cases/:id/advance (workflow state transition with role gate validation). POST /cases/:id/submissions (trigger E2B XML generation and gateway dispatch, Idempotency-Key required). GET /cases/:id/submissions/:sub_id (submission status and ACK number). GET /meddra/search?q=myocardial&type=PT (Redis-cached autocomplete, 50ms P99 SLA). GET /meddra/smq/:code (SMQ group member terms). GET /audit/:case_id (full audit trail export for compliance officers). All endpoints: Okta JWT validated by Spring Security ResourceServer; Oracle RLS applied at DB layer.
target_output.security_and_compliance: GVP Module VI and 21 CFR Part 312.32 compliance: Hibernate Envers @Audited on AdverseEvent, CaseSafetyReport, Drug entities; CaseAuditListener validates change_reason non-null for regulated fields (narrative_text, meddra_pt_code, meddra_llt_code, causality_assessment); audit_log table has no UPDATE or DELETE grants enforced at Oracle DB user level -- append-only by design; audit records forwarded to Splunk SIEM via Fluentd sidecar. Oracle TDE for ICSR data at rest (AES-256); TLS 1.3 for all API traffic. Okta MFA enforced via SAML assertion attribute mfa_authenticated=true validated by Spring Security. Oracle RLS policies: pv_scientist sees only own-assigned cases; medical_reviewer sees in_review cases in origin region; regulatory_affairs sees approved cases ready for submission; admin sees all. WAF rules on ALB block SQL injection and XSS. GDPR Article 9 pseudonymization path: patients table fields replaced with SHA-256 hash on deletion request, preserving ICSR structure for 25-year retention obligation. Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation for Oracle DB passwords and annual rotation for ICH gateway PKCS12 keystores.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Jenkins declarative pipeline: Maven compile -> JUnit/Mockito unit tests -> SonarQube quality gate (80% coverage minimum) -> Docker multi-stage build -> ECR image push -> ECS rolling deploy eu-west-1 (max 50% unavailable) -> smoke tests (/health, /cases?status=new) -> 10-minute soak period -> ECS rolling deploy us-east-1 -> smoke tests -> Slack notify. Environments: dev (single AZ eu-west-1, shared Oracle), staging-eu and staging-us (multi-AZ, connected to EudraVigilance and FAERS test gateway environments for E2B submission integration testing), prod-eu and prod-us (multi-AZ ECS Fargate, Oracle RDS Custom multi-AZ with GoldenGate). Rollback: ECS service rolls back to prior task definition revision on >2 health check failures within 5 minutes; Jenkins one-click rollback to any ECR image tag. Oracle schema changes via Flyway versioned migrations with maintained undo scripts; executed as ECS one-off task before each rolling deploy. Secrets Manager auto-rotation for Oracle JDBC credentials (30-day cycle) and ICH gateway mTLS certificates (annual).
target_output.observability: Splunk as SIEM and primary log aggregation platform. ECS tasks run Fluentd sidecar forwarding structured JSON logs with MDC fields (correlation_id, case_id, case_number, user_id, origin_region) to Splunk indexers. Micrometer custom metrics: case_aging_p99_days (alert at day 13 -- 2 days before submission deadline), submission_attempt_count per authority, meddra_cache_hit_rate (alert if <95%), gateway_response_time_ms P95. PagerDuty P1 alerts: submission DLQ message count >0 (immediate escalation for manual fallback), Oracle connection pool utilization >85%, ECS task crash loop >3 restarts. Splunk dashboards: ICSR daily intake rate by region, submission on-time percentage by authority (EudraVigilance vs FAERS), MedDRA coding time distribution per scientist, case throughput by role tier.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $4,200/month at 500 ICSRs/day peak for dual-region deployment. ECS Fargate auto-scaling on ALB request count (target 200 requests per task). Oracle RDS Custom reserved instance in eu-west-1 primary region; on-demand in us-east-1 DR replica. Redis ElastiCache r7g.large per region supporting 500K MedDRA terms plus session load. Primary cost driver is Oracle licensing -- Aurora PostgreSQL migration evaluated for Phase 2 to reduce to approximately $2,800/month. SQS and S3 costs negligible at current ICSR volumes. CloudFront caching reduces SPA origin requests by ~90%.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 months): AE intake form with receipt_country-based origin-region routing, submission_deadline_date auto-calculation, MedDRA 26.1 coding workspace with Redis autocomplete (<50ms), two-tier review workflow (pv_scientist -> medical_reviewer), E2B(R3) XML generation with DTD validation, manual download for EudraVigilance and FAERS, Hibernate Envers field-level audit_log with GVP-compliant schema, Okta SAML MFA, Jenkins CI/CD to dual-region Fargate. Phase 2 (3 months): Automated ICH gateway submission with SQS exponential backoff retry and PagerDuty DLQ alert, regulatory_affairs workflow tier, FAERS direct submission path, case analytics dashboard with aging heatmap and on-time rate by authority. Phase 3 (3 months): DSUR/PSUR aggregate report generation using accumulated ICSR data, SAML federation for partner CRO organizations, signal detection analytics using MedDRA SMQ groupings, mobile companion for field AE intake.
target_output.development_guidelines: Java 21 with records for immutable ICSR value objects and sealed classes for case status transitions. Spring Boot 3 with constructor injection only -- no @Autowired field injection. Hibernate Envers for all audit revision tracking -- never write manual audit_log inserts. All ICSR status transitions through CaseStateMachineService -- no direct status field updates in controllers. 80% test coverage enforced by SonarQube quality gate. TestContainers with Oracle XE for integration tests. ADR documents required for E2B schema version decisions and Oracle-to-Aurora migration feasibility assessment.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Oracle GoldenGate replication lag causing stale reads if us-east-1 replica is used for live case queries during DR failover. Mitigation: Route53 health checks fail reads to replica only when primary is unavailable; replica is never used for live workflow queries. Risk: MedDRA version upgrade mid-project invalidating previously coded cases. Mitigation: meddra_version_at_coding column on case_safety_reports pins each case to the dictionary version used; version upgrade does not retroactively recode existing cases. Risk: ICH gateway planned maintenance causing submission deadline breaches. Mitigation: SQS retry window of 72 hours covers all published gateway maintenance windows; PagerDuty P1 fires on DLQ drain for manual XML submission fallback. Tradeoff: Oracle retained over Aurora PostgreSQL for Phase 1 schema migration continuity -- accepted risk with Aurora migration scoped for Phase 2 post-MVP stabilization.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: MedDRA version: 26.1 selected over 26.0 -- 26.1 includes updated SMQs for COVID-19 and SARS-CoV-2 vaccine AEs directly relevant to current trial portfolio. Oracle vs Aurora: Oracle retained for Phase 1 to avoid schema translation risk during MVP timeline; Aurora PostgreSQL migration formally scoped for Phase 2 with estimated $1,400/month license savings. Case routing confirmation: receipt_country at intake determines origin_region; confirmed with Regulatory Affairs that GDPR Article 9 requires EU cases to remain primary in eu-west-1 throughout 25-year retention period.

metadata.notes: Round 2 PV case management: resolved field-level audit granularity (ISS-002) and dual-region case routing (ISS-003). GVP Module VI and ICH E2B(R3) compliance-driven architecture.

profile.domain: Medicinal Chemistry Informatics / Compound Bioactivity Data Access

project_goal.value: Expose compound bioactivity data from legacy ChemDraw Oracle registration database via structured REST JSON endpoints, replacing direct JDBC calls from downstream informatics microservices
project_goal.rationale: Direct JDBC coupling to legacy Oracle prevents independent scaling and versioning of consumer services

target_users.value: Internal informatics microservices and data science tooling
target_users.rationale: No human end-users; service is a machine-to-machine API consumed by downstream pipelines

project_class.rationale: Pure backend API with no frontend; exposes data layer to internal consumers

capabilities.rationale: REST API with JWT auth, Oracle read-only access, Redis cache, internal-only exposure

complexity_level.rationale: Read-only wrapper with caching; no write operations against legacy database

risk_level.rationale: Compound IP is valuable proprietary data; unauthorized access would expose competitive intelligence

data_sensitivity.rationale: Compound bioactivity data is proprietary IP subject to internal data governance classification

external_exposure.rationale: Service endpoints reachable only from internal VPC; not exposed to internet

access_model.value: API key per consuming service, rate-limited per key; keys provisioned and revoked by informatics team lead
access_model.rationale: Service-to-service auth without user identity; rate limiting protects legacy Oracle DB

feature_scope.value: Compound bioactivity profile retrieval by registry ID, assay definition lookup, target protein endpoint, API key management, Redis response caching
feature_scope.rationale: Core data access patterns requested by downstream informatics teams

mvp_scope.value: GET /compounds/:id bioactivity profile, GET /assays/:id definition, Redis caching with 1-hour TTL, API key authentication, rate limiting per key
mvp_scope.rationale: Replaces most common JDBC query patterns used by downstream services

security_baseline.value: API key authentication via Authorization header, HTTPS only (TLS 1.3), rate limiting 500 req/min per key, internal VPC network restriction
security_baseline.rationale: Internal service does not require full OAuth2; API key with rate limiting is appropriate

frontend_stack.rationale: API service with no frontend component

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI
backend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; FastAPI provides automatic OpenAPI generation and async request handling

data_platform.value: Oracle Database 12c (legacy, read-only access), Redis 7 for response caching
data_platform.rationale: Legacy Oracle is the authoritative source; read-only connection prevents accidental writes; Redis caches frequent queries

hosting_target.value: On-premise Docker on internal compute cluster
hosting_target.rationale: Internal-only service; no cloud hosting required; existing Docker infrastructure available

privacy_retention_policy.value: API request logs retained 90 days for access audit; no compound data persisted outside Oracle
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Internal data governance policy for compound IP access logging

future_scope.value: Write endpoints for new compound registration once legacy Oracle is decommissioned, additional assay data types, bulk export endpoint
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap contingent on Oracle decommission timeline

constraints.value: Must maintain read-only connection to Oracle 12c; no schema changes to legacy database; connection pool limited to 10 concurrent Oracle connections
constraints.rationale: Legacy Oracle is shared infrastructure with other consumers; pool limits prevent starvation

observability_baseline.value: New Relic APM, structured JSON request logs, alert on Oracle connection pool saturation
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate APM standard for internal services

execution_preference.value: Single delivery sprint; ship MVP in 3 weeks
execution_preference.rationale: Wrapper service with limited scope; no iterative design needed

compliance_context.value: Internal compound IP governance policy: all bioactivity data access must be logged with consuming service identity and registry IDs requested
compliance_context.rationale: Internal data governance policy requires audit trail for compound IP access

project.goal: REST wrapper over legacy Oracle compound bioactivity database
project.target_users: Internal informatics microservices

backend.framework: FastAPI with SQLAlchemy (Oracle dialect) and redis-py

security.auth: API key per consuming service via Authorization header
security.rate_limiting: 500 req/min per key via Redis token bucket

data.db: Oracle 12c read-only via cx_Oracle pool (max 10 connections)
data.cache: Redis 7 -- compound bioactivity responses cached 1 hour TTL

open_questions.bulk_endpoint: Should initial MVP include a bulk /compounds?ids= endpoint for batch lookups?

reasoner_reviews.product: This is a straightforward data access shim. The main risk is overloading the legacy Oracle 12c connection pool if downstream services issue many parallel requests. Redis caching is essential -- cache hit rates for bioactivity data should exceed 80% given the stable nature of historical assay results.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI is appropriate here. The Oracle connection pool limit of 10 is the primary scaling constraint. Use SQLAlchemy connection pooling with pool_size=8 and max_overflow=2 to stay within the 10-connection limit. Redis TTL of 1 hour is reasonable for bioactivity data that changes infrequently.
reasoner_reviews.security: API key management requires a secure storage mechanism -- keys should be stored hashed in a local SQLite registry with creation timestamp, consuming service label, and last-used timestamp. Rate limiting per key prevents a single misbehaving service from exhausting the Oracle pool.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 3-week delivery is achievable given the narrow scope. The bulk compounds endpoint should be deferred to Phase 2 to avoid complexity around Oracle pool management for large IN-clause queries on the legacy schema.

backend.service_design: Single FastAPI application with router modules: compounds_router (bioactivity profiles), assays_router (assay definitions), targets_router (target protein metadata), admin_router (API key CRUD). Oracle access via SQLAlchemy with cx_Oracle dialect.
backend.api_patterns: REST GET endpoints only for MVP. Compound bioactivity profiles cached in Redis by registry_id key with 1-hour TTL. Cache-aside pattern: check Redis first, query Oracle on miss, write to Redis.
backend.failure_handling: Oracle connection timeout raises 503 with Retry-After header. Redis connection failure degrades to direct Oracle queries with warning log. Rate limit exceeded returns 429 with X-RateLimit-Reset header.

security.auth_design: API keys stored as bcrypt hashes in local SQLite key registry. Incoming request Authorization: Bearer <key> header validated by FastAPI dependency. Key metadata (service_name, created_at, last_used_at, is_active) maintained for audit.
security.abuse_prevention: Redis token bucket rate limiter: 500 requests/minute per key. Exceeded keys logged with service_name and request path for access audit reporting.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Structured JSON request log per request: timestamp, api_key_id (not raw key), service_name, endpoint, registry_ids_requested, cache_hit, oracle_query_time_ms, response_code. Retained 90 days.

data.entities: compounds (registry_id, compound_name, smiles, mol_weight, created_date -- read from Oracle). bioactivity_records (id, registry_id, assay_id, assay_result_value, result_unit, activity_flag, tested_at -- read from Oracle). assay_definitions (assay_id, assay_name, target_protein_id, assay_type, units -- read from Oracle). target_proteins (protein_id, gene_name, uniprot_id, species -- read from Oracle). api_tokens (id, key_hash, service_name, created_at, last_used_at, is_active -- SQLite local).
data.storage_design: Oracle 12c read-only via cx_Oracle with SQLAlchemy pool_size=8, max_overflow=2. Redis 7 for compound bioactivity response cache. SQLite for API key registry (local file, not Oracle).

devops.deployment_topology: Single Docker container per environment. On-premise Docker daemon. Nginx reverse proxy for TLS termination and /health endpoint exposure.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest unit tests -> Docker build -> push to internal registry -> deploy via Docker pull on target host. Manual deploy trigger for prod.
devops.observability_stack: New Relic APM agent in container. Structured JSON logs to local file, rotated daily, retained 90 days. Alert: Oracle pool wait_count > 5 for >30 seconds.

target_output.thinking_summary: Simple read-only API wrapper over a legacy Oracle compound database. Key design decisions: Oracle connection pool limited to 10 forces aggressive Redis caching (1-hour TTL for bioactivity data) and pool_size=8 to stay under limit. API key auth is appropriate for machine-to-machine internal use. FastAPI's async request handling helps manage queue depth during pool contention. Weak plan quality is intentional -- sections meet minimums but lack depth.
target_output.title: Compound Bioactivity REST API
target_output.executive_summary: A read-only REST API wrapper built with FastAPI and Python 3.12 that exposes compound bioactivity, assay definition, and target protein data from a legacy Oracle 12c registration database to internal informatics microservices. Redis 7 caches bioactivity responses with 1-hour TTL to minimize Oracle connection pool utilization.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single FastAPI application deployed as a Docker container on-premise. Oracle 12c is accessed read-only via SQLAlchemy cx_Oracle connection pool capped at 10 connections (pool_size=8, max_overflow=2). Redis 7 provides response caching for compound bioactivity profiles. API key authentication enforced on every request via FastAPI dependency injection. Nginx handles TLS termination. No external internet exposure -- service reachable only from internal VPC.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 with cx_Oracle dialect, redis-py, passlib for key hashing. Database: Oracle 12c (read-only, legacy), Redis 7 (cache), SQLite (API key registry). Infrastructure: Docker on on-premise compute, Nginx for TLS termination. Monitoring: New Relic APM, structured JSON logs.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Compound bioactivity profile endpoint returning all assay results for a given registry_id. Assay definition endpoint returning assay metadata by assay_id. Target protein endpoint returning gene, UniProt ID, and species. API key management admin endpoints for provisioning and revocation by informatics team leads.
target_output.system_components: FastAPI application container with cx_Oracle pool (max 10 connections) and Redis client. Nginx reverse proxy for TLS. Redis 7 instance for response cache. SQLite file for API key registry. Legacy Oracle 12c database (read-only, existing infrastructure).
target_output.workflows: Compound Bioactivity Lookup: downstream microservice sends GET /compounds/:registry_id with Authorization Bearer key; FastAPI dependency validates key hash against SQLite registry; Redis checked for cached response by registry_id key; on miss, SQLAlchemy queries Oracle bioactivity_records and assay_definitions tables; response cached in Redis with 1-hour TTL; structured access log written with service_name, registry_id, cache_hit flag. API Key Provisioning: informatics team lead POSTs to /admin/keys with service_name; bcrypt hash generated and stored in SQLite; raw key returned once to requestor; key cannot be retrieved again.
target_output.data_model: compounds (registry_id, compound_name, smiles, mol_weight, created_date -- Oracle read-only). bioactivity_records (id, registry_id, assay_id, assay_result_value, result_unit, activity_flag ENUM active/inactive/inconclusive, tested_at -- Oracle read-only). assay_definitions (assay_id, assay_name, target_protein_id, assay_type ENUM biochemical/cellular/in_vivo, units -- Oracle read-only). target_proteins (protein_id, gene_name, uniprot_id, species -- Oracle read-only). api_tokens (id, key_hash, service_name, created_at, last_used_at, is_active -- SQLite local registry).
target_output.api_design: GET /compounds/:registry_id (bioactivity profile with all assay results, Redis-cached). GET /compounds/:registry_id/assays (assay-level results list). GET /assays/:assay_id (assay definition metadata). GET /targets/:protein_id (target protein metadata). POST /admin/keys (provision new API key, admin-only). DELETE /admin/keys/:key_id (revoke key). GET /health (liveness check, no auth required). All endpoints require Authorization: Bearer <api_key> except /health. 429 on rate limit exceeded with X-RateLimit-Reset header.
target_output.security_and_compliance: API keys stored as bcrypt hashes in local SQLite registry -- raw keys never persisted. TLS 1.3 via Nginx reverse proxy. Internal VPC network restriction at infrastructure layer. Redis token bucket rate limiting 500 req/min per key. Structured access log capturing api_key_id (not raw key), service_name, requested registry_ids, cache_hit, oracle_query_time_ms retained 90 days per internal governance policy. Oracle connection is read-only with no DML or DDL privileges granted to API service DB user.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Single Docker container per environment (dev, prod). GitHub Actions pipeline: pytest unit tests (target 70% coverage for simple wrapper) -> mypy type check -> Docker build -> push to internal Harbor registry -> deploy via SSH Docker pull and container replace on target host. Manual approval required for prod deploy. Nginx config manages TLS certificate via internal CA. Container environment variables sourced from .env file on host -- Oracle JDBC URL, Redis connection string, SQLite path. No orchestration needed at current scale. Rollback via Docker pull of previous image tag.
target_output.observability: New Relic APM agent injected into FastAPI container. Structured JSON log per request with fields: timestamp, api_key_service, endpoint, registry_id, cache_hit, oracle_query_ms, response_code. Log rotation daily, 90-day retention. New Relic alert: Oracle pool active_connections > 8 for more than 60 seconds (pool saturation warning).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Minimal cost -- on-premise Docker on existing hardware. Redis memory ~500MB for bioactivity cache at typical compound library sizes. Oracle pool limit of 10 connections is the scaling ceiling; if request rate exceeds Redis cache capacity, consider bumping Redis TTL to 4 hours for stable historical assay data.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (3 weeks): GET /compounds/:id, GET /assays/:id, GET /targets/:id, Redis caching, API key auth, rate limiting, Docker deployment, New Relic APM. Phase 2 (future): Bulk GET /compounds?ids= endpoint with Oracle IN-clause chunking to respect pool limits, write endpoints for new compound registration when Oracle 12c is decommissioned.
target_output.development_guidelines: FastAPI dependency injection for auth and rate limiting -- no middleware-based auth. SQLAlchemy models are read-only; disable Session.flush() and autocommit. All Oracle queries must use parameterized SQL -- no f-string query construction. pytest for unit tests with mocked Oracle and Redis. mypy strict mode. Single docker-compose.yml for local dev with Oracle Instant Client and Redis.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Oracle 12c connection pool exhaustion under burst load. Mitigation: Redis cache hit rate target >80% reduces Oracle query frequency; alert at pool_size=8. Risk: Legacy Oracle schema changes breaking API responses. Mitigation: API response models use explicit field mapping -- unmapped columns silently dropped. Tradeoff: SQLite for API key registry is simple but not HA; acceptable for internal admin-only path with infrequent key rotation.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Bulk endpoint deferred to Phase 2 -- Oracle IN-clause performance on legacy schema unvalidated; defer until Oracle pool behavior under bulk load is characterized. API key storage: SQLite chosen over PostgreSQL to avoid adding an additional infrastructure dependency for a small key registry.

metadata.notes: Simple read-only bioactivity API wrapper over legacy Oracle. Weak quality plan -- sections meet minimums with limited depth, consistent with manifest spec.

profile.domain: Drug Discovery Informatics / API Platform Marketing

project.goal: Public landing page for drug discovery informatics API platform with waitlist conversion
project.target_users: Medicinal chemists and informatics engineers at biotech/pharma

frontend.framework: Astro 4 with React 18 islands and TypeScript
frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS with custom design tokens for informatics platform brand
frontend.state: No global state; React island local state for waitlist form submission status

backend.framework: Single Lambda function for waitlist form POST handler
backend.api: API Gateway HTTP API endpoint POST /waitlist

security.headers: CSP, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy set in CloudFront response headers policy
security.rate_limiting: API Gateway throttling 10 req/s on POST /waitlist

data.db: DynamoDB for waitlist email deduplication (email as PK)

constraints.content: Marketing team updates content via MDX files

open_questions.interactive_demo: Should the API endpoint preview show live sample data or static JSON mock?

reasoner_reviews.product: The platform value proposition for medicinal chemists is highly specific -- the landing page must clearly differentiate between the three data service types (compound bioactivity, ADMET predictions, target annotations) with concrete examples relevant to lead optimization workflows. Generic API platform copy will not convert domain-expert visitors. The pricing tier section should show example request volumes (e.g., '10K compound lookups/month on free tier') rather than abstract 'starter/professional/enterprise' labels.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro 4 with React islands is the optimal choice for this use case: Astro zero-JS by default for static sections, React islands only for the waitlist form and API preview component. The Lambda waitlist handler needs DynamoDB conditional write to prevent duplicate email registrations. API Gateway throttling at 10 req/s is sufficient to prevent spam abuse on the form endpoint.
reasoner_reviews.security: CSP headers must allow the Plausible Analytics script domain and block all inline scripts to prevent XSS. No cookies are set by the site itself -- Plausible is cookieless and does not set tracking pixels. The waitlist form must validate email format client-side (React) and server-side (Lambda) before DynamoDB write or SES trigger.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Lighthouse >90 is achievable with Astro static export, Tailwind purge, and CloudFront caching. The marketing team MDX content update workflow requires a clear PR-based deploy process: push MDX changes to main branch -> GitHub Actions rebuilds Astro -> deploys to S3 -> CloudFront cache invalidation.

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4 static site with page routes: index.astro (landing), /pricing, /docs (redirect to developer portal), /api-preview. React island components: WaitlistForm.tsx (form submission with status states), ApiPreviewWidget.tsx (tabbed JSON response viewer for bioactivity, ADMET, target annotation endpoints).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Hero section: platform tagline with compound screening value proposition, 'Join Waitlist' CTA. Capabilities overview: three-column cards for bioactivity data API, ADMET prediction API, target annotation API with example use cases. Pricing tiers: free (10K lookups/month), professional (1M lookups/month, SLA), enterprise (custom volume, dedicated support). API Preview: tabbed widget showing sample JSON responses for GET /compounds/:id, GET /admet/:smiles, GET /targets/:gene_name. Waitlist form: email input with React submission state (idle, submitting, success, error).
frontend.performance_notes: Astro static export produces zero runtime JS for non-island sections. Tailwind CSS purge eliminates unused styles. CloudFront serves compressed assets from S3. Target: Largest Contentful Paint <1.8s on 4G simulated in Lighthouse. Images: WebP format with srcset for responsive hero illustration.
frontend.frontend_risks: React island hydration on slow connections could delay waitlist form interactivity. Mitigation: form is simple enough that client:idle directive is acceptable -- lazy hydration after page load.

backend.service_design: Single AWS Lambda function (Python 3.12): validate email format with regex, check DynamoDB for existing registration (ConditionalExpression on email PK), write new item with email, signup_timestamp, source_page attributes, trigger SES welcome email with developer portal link.
backend.api_patterns: API Gateway HTTP API: POST /waitlist with JSON body {email, source_page}. CORS configured for site domain only. 200 on success, 409 on duplicate email, 400 on invalid email format.
backend.failure_handling: Lambda returns structured JSON error on validation failure. SES send failure logged to CloudWatch -- DynamoDB write is committed before SES to avoid duplicate registration on SES retry.

security.abuse_prevention: API Gateway usage plan throttling: 10 req/s burst, 5 req/s steady on POST /waitlist. Email validation both client (React) and server (Lambda regex). No CAPTCHA at MVP -- monitor Lambda CloudWatch for abuse patterns.
security.privacy_controls: No tracking cookies set. Plausible Analytics is cookieless and GDPR-compliant without consent banner. Waitlist email addresses stored in DynamoDB with TTL removed (retained until platform launch or unsubscribe). Privacy policy page linked in footer.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: CloudWatch Lambda logs capture all form submissions with email domain (not full address) for aggregate monitoring. API Gateway access logs for request rate monitoring.

data.entities: waitlist_signups (email PK, signup_timestamp, source_page, confirmation_sent_flag -- DynamoDB). No other data entities for static site.
data.storage_design: DynamoDB on-demand table for waitlist_signups. S3 bucket (static-site-assets) as CloudFront origin for all static files. No RDS or traditional DB needed.
data.analytics_events: Plausible custom events: waitlist_form_view, waitlist_submit_success, pricing_tier_click (free/professional/enterprise), api_preview_tab_switch (bioactivity/admet/targets), docs_link_click.

devops.deployment_topology: S3 bucket (versioning enabled) as CloudFront origin. CloudFront distribution with WAF and response headers policy (CSP, X-Frame-Options). API Gateway HTTP API -> Lambda for POST /waitlist. Route53 alias record for custom domain.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Astro build -> S3 sync --delete -> CloudFront create-invalidation on merge to main. Separate staging S3 bucket and CloudFront distribution for PR preview deploys.
devops.rollback_strategy: S3 versioning allows rollback to previous site build via S3 version restore and CloudFront cache invalidation. Lambda function versioning with alias for form handler rollback.

target_output.thinking_summary: Static landing page for a drug discovery informatics API platform. Key architectural decisions: Astro 4 with React islands for minimal JS bundle while preserving interactivity on the waitlist form and API preview widget; DynamoDB on-demand for waitlist email dedup without a persistent database; Plausible Analytics for cookieless GDPR-compliant visitor tracking eliminating the need for a consent banner. No server-side runtime -- Lambda handles only the single form POST.
target_output.title: Drug Discovery Informatics API Platform Landing Page
target_output.executive_summary: A static Astro 4 landing page served via AWS CloudFront and S3 for a drug discovery informatics API platform targeting medicinal chemists and computational biologists. Features an API capabilities showcase (compound bioactivity, ADMET predictions, target annotations), three pricing tiers, an interactive JSON response preview widget, and a waitlist signup form handled by an AWS Lambda function with DynamoDB deduplication and SES confirmation. Plausible Analytics provides cookieless visitor metrics without a GDPR consent banner.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static Astro 4 site built to S3 and distributed globally via CloudFront CDN with WAF. No server-side runtime for page delivery -- all HTML is pre-rendered at build time. React 18 islands provide interactivity only for the WaitlistForm and ApiPreviewWidget components, hydrating lazily after page load. A single AWS Lambda function (Python 3.12) behind API Gateway HTTP API handles POST /waitlist: validates email, deduplicates via DynamoDB conditional write, and triggers SES confirmation email with developer portal link. Plausible Analytics script tag (cookieless, no GDPR consent required) tracks visitor behavior. Content updates via MDX files in Git trigger GitHub Actions rebuild and S3 deploy with CloudFront cache invalidation.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4, React 18 (islands only), TypeScript, Tailwind CSS. Build: Astro static export (Node 20 build-time only). Backend: AWS Lambda Python 3.12 (waitlist handler only). Storage: DynamoDB on-demand (waitlist_signups). Email: Amazon SES for waitlist confirmation. CDN: CloudFront with WAF and response headers policy. Hosting: S3 (static assets) + API Gateway HTTP API (form endpoint). Analytics: Plausible Analytics (cookieless). CI/CD: GitHub Actions.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero Section: platform tagline ('Compound screening data, accessible via API') with primary 'Join Waitlist' CTA button scrolling to form. API Capabilities Overview: three feature cards -- Bioactivity Data API (IC50/Ki values across >50 target classes, MedChemExpress and ChEMBL-sourced), ADMET Prediction API (solubility, permeability, metabolic stability, hERG liability via ML models), Target Annotation API (UniProt-linked gene targets with pathway and disease associations). API Preview Widget: tabbed React island showing formatted sample JSON responses for each API type with copy-to-clipboard. Pricing Tiers: Free (10K compound lookups/month, bioactivity only), Professional ($299/month, 1M lookups, all endpoints, 99.5% SLA), Enterprise (custom volume, dedicated support, on-premise deployment option). Waitlist Form: email input with real-time validation, submission status states (idle/submitting/success/duplicate/error), confirmation message with developer portal link. FAQ: answers to 'What compound databases are indexed?', 'How are ADMET models validated?', 'Is there a Python SDK?'.
target_output.system_components: CloudFront distribution serving pre-rendered Astro HTML/CSS/JS from S3 origin, with WAF (rate limiting, managed rule groups for common web threats). Response headers policy enforcing Content-Security-Policy (allowing plausible.io script src), X-Frame-Options DENY, Referrer-Policy strict-origin. S3 bucket with versioning for static site assets. API Gateway HTTP API endpoint POST /waitlist. Lambda function (Python 3.12) for form validation, DynamoDB write, and SES dispatch. DynamoDB on-demand table waitlist_signups (email PK). SES sending domain verified for confirmation emails. Plausible Analytics: cookieless script tag loaded from plausible.io.
target_output.workflows: Visitor Waitlist Conversion: visitor arrives via organic search or partner referral, browses hero and capabilities sections, views API Preview Widget tabs to evaluate bioactivity/ADMET/target response formats, clicks 'Join Waitlist', React WaitlistForm validates email format client-side, POSTs to API Gateway POST /waitlist, Lambda validates email regex server-side, issues DynamoDB PutItem with ConditionExpression attribute_not_exists(email), on success triggers SES send of confirmation email with developer portal link, Lambda returns 200; on duplicate email (DynamoDB ConditionalCheckFailedException) Lambda returns 409 and form displays 'Already registered' message. Content Update by Marketing: marketer edits MDX file in /src/content directory, opens PR, GitHub Actions runs Astro build on PR commit, deploys to staging CloudFront distribution for review, on merge to main deploys to production S3 and runs CloudFront invalidation for '/*'.
target_output.data_model: waitlist_signups DynamoDB table: email (String, PK, hash key), signup_timestamp (String ISO-8601), source_page (String, e.g. hero_cta or pricing_section), confirmation_sent (Boolean). No relational schema -- DynamoDB on-demand, no provisioned capacity. S3 objects: /index.html, /pricing/index.html, /api-preview/index.html, /_astro/*.js, /_astro/*.css, /images/*.webp. No persistent data entities beyond waitlist_signups.
target_output.api_design: POST /waitlist (API Gateway HTTP API, public CORS for site domain): request body {email: string, source_page: string}. Response 200: {message: 'Registered successfully', portal_url: 'https://docs.example.com'}. Response 409: {error: 'Email already registered'}. Response 400: {error: 'Invalid email format'}. API Gateway throttling: 10 req/s burst, 5 req/s steady. All other content is static -- no additional API endpoints.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by CloudFront (redirect HTTP to HTTPS). Content-Security-Policy header: script-src 'self' plausible.io, default-src 'self', frame-ancestors 'none'. X-Frame-Options: DENY. Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin. No session cookies -- Plausible Analytics is cookieless. Waitlist email validation at both client (React regex) and server (Lambda) layers. API Gateway rate limiting prevents form abuse. GDPR: no consent banner needed (Plausible cookieless); waitlist privacy notice in form copy links to privacy policy page. SES send domain authenticated via DKIM and SPF for deliverability.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline on push to main: install deps (Node 20) -> Astro build (astro build) -> S3 sync (aws s3 sync ./dist s3://prod-site-bucket --delete) -> CloudFront create-invalidation (paths '/*'). On pull requests: deploy to staging S3 bucket and staging CloudFront distribution; PR comment posts preview URL. Lambda deployed via AWS SAM: sam build -> sam deploy with parameter overrides for SES sender address. Lambda version alias 'live' updated on each deploy; previous version retained for rollback. S3 versioning enabled on prod bucket for asset rollback. No containers or orchestration needed.
target_output.observability: Plausible Analytics custom events tracked: waitlist_form_view (impression on scroll), waitlist_submit_success, waitlist_submit_duplicate, pricing_tier_click (free/professional/enterprise), api_preview_tab (bioactivity/admet/targets), docs_link_click. CloudWatch Lambda logs: each invocation logs email domain (e.g., '@genentech.com', not full address) and outcome (success/duplicate/invalid/ses_error). CloudWatch alarm: Lambda error rate >5% over 5-minute window triggers SNS notification. CloudFront access logs to S3 for CDN traffic analysis.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated <$50/month at typical marketing traffic volumes. CloudFront: $0.008/10K HTTPS requests. S3: negligible for static assets. Lambda: effectively free at <100K form submissions/month (Lambda free tier). DynamoDB on-demand: ~$0.25 per 1M writes. SES: $0.10 per 1000 emails. Scales globally via CloudFront edge without any infrastructure changes -- static content has no scaling ceiling.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (2 weeks): Hero section with compound screening value proposition, API capabilities three-column overview, pricing tiers (free/professional/enterprise with lookup volume figures), waitlist form with Lambda handler, DynamoDB dedup, SES confirmation, Plausible Analytics, GitHub Actions CI/CD. Phase 2 (post-launch): Interactive API Preview Widget with real sample compound data (SMILES, bioactivity values, ADMET scores), customer case study section with partner logos (requires partnership agreements), pricing calculator for API call volume estimation, full FAQ section.
target_output.development_guidelines: Astro content collections for MDX-based marketing copy -- all human-editable text in /src/content directory, not hardcoded in .astro files. React islands use client:idle directive by default to minimize Time-to-Interactive impact. Tailwind CSS with project-specific design token config (colors, typography mapped to informatics platform brand). TypeScript strict mode for all .tsx files. Lighthouse CI in GitHub Actions pipeline: fail PR if performance score drops below 85. Image assets must be WebP with explicit width/height to eliminate Cumulative Layout Shift.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Astro static build does not support dynamic pricing changes without a rebuild. Mitigation: pricing data sourced from a JSON config file in the repo so marketing team can update via PR without touching component code. Risk: SES email deliverability to enterprise pharma domains with strict email security policies. Mitigation: DKIM and SPF configured on sending domain; confirmation email subject line avoids spam trigger words. Tradeoff: Plausible Analytics provides aggregate metrics only -- no individual visitor tracking or heatmaps. Accepted to avoid GDPR consent banner requirement.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Interactive API preview uses static mock JSON responses -- live API data not available at site launch; static mocks reviewed by platform engineering team for accuracy and updated before launch. API key preview shows realistic compound data (aspirin SMILES for bioactivity example, ibuprofen for ADMET example) to give domain-expert visitors genuine signal about response format quality.

metadata.notes: Static Astro landing page for drug discovery informatics API platform. Strong quality -- rich domain-specific content for medicinal chemistry audience with Plausible Analytics, Lambda form handler, and DynamoDB deduplication.

profile.domain: Drug Discovery Machine Learning / ADMET Model Registry

project_goal.value: Internal static documentation and results portal for the ML model registry, displaying model cards with architecture descriptions, training run metadata, performance benchmarks against standard ADMET datasets, and usage guidelines for integrating ADMET prediction models into virtual screening workflows
project_goal.rationale: ML engineering team needs a self-service discovery mechanism for ADMET models without navigating MLflow directly

target_users.value: Computational chemists and medicinal chemists consuming ADMET predictions in virtual screening pipelines, ML engineers maintaining and updating model registry entries
target_users.rationale: Two distinct user groups with different needs: consumers want usage examples; maintainers need update workflow

project_class.rationale: Documentation portal with no user authentication or server-side logic; fully static delivery appropriate

capabilities.rationale: Static site with internal analytics; no backend runtime or database

complexity_level.rationale: Static content site with search and filtering client-side; no server-side state or auth

risk_level.rationale: Internal documentation only; model performance data is internal IP but accessible to all authenticated-network users

data_sensitivity.rationale: Model cards contain internal ML architecture details and benchmark results; classified as internal IP

external_exposure.rationale: Portal accessible only from corporate network or VPN; CloudFront origin restricted to internal VPC via OAC

access_model.value: Internal network access only; no application-level authentication; corporate network/VPN provides access control
access_model.rationale: Internal documentation portal; network-level access control is sufficient

feature_scope.value: Model card catalog with filtering by ADMET endpoint (solubility, permeability, metabolic stability, hERG, toxicity), model architecture summary, training dataset references, benchmark performance tables (RMSE, AUC, R2 against standard datasets), Python usage code snippet, version history, usage guidelines for virtual screening integration
feature_scope.rationale: Full model card specification aligned with ML documentation best practices

mvp_scope.value: Model card catalog with ADMET endpoint filtering, benchmark performance tables, Python usage snippets, GitHub Actions auto-deploy on model card JSON update
mvp_scope.rationale: Core discovery and usage value for computational chemist consumers

security_baseline.value: HTTPS via CloudFront with internal VPC origin restriction (OAC), no public internet access, security headers (CSP, X-Frame-Options), no cookies
security_baseline.rationale: Internal portal with network-level access control; standard static site security headers sufficient

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 static export (output: export) with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise in Next.js; static export mode for pure static delivery; no server runtime needed

backend_stack.value: None (static site)
backend_stack.rationale: No server-side runtime; all content pre-rendered at build time from model card JSON files

data_platform.value: None (static site); model card data in JSON files in Git repository
data_platform.rationale: Model cards stored as structured JSON in /data directory; no database required for a static documentation site

hosting_target.value: AWS S3 + CloudFront with internal VPC origin access control (OAC); not publicly accessible
hosting_target.rationale: Standard internal static site hosting on corporate AWS account with network restriction

privacy_retention_policy.value: No user data collected; model card JSON retained in Git history indefinitely
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Static documentation site with no user tracking or data collection

future_scope.value: Model comparison view (side-by-side benchmark tables), changelog feed for model updates, integration with MLflow for automated model card generation from training run metadata
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 enhancements to reduce manual model card maintenance burden

constraints.value: ML engineers must be able to add or update model cards by editing JSON files without modifying React components; build time <3 minutes; internal network access only
constraints.rationale: Self-service content update workflow is a hard requirement for adoption by ML team

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for S3 access logs and CloudFront metrics; no visitor-level analytics (internal use only)
observability_baseline.rationale: Internal portal; aggregate page view counts via CloudFront metrics sufficient

execution_preference.value: Iterative delivery; ML team reviews model card layout each sprint
execution_preference.rationale: Model card schema evolves based on ML team feedback

project.goal: Internal static ADMET model registry documentation portal
project.target_users: Computational chemists and ML engineers

frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS with model card component design system
frontend.state: No global state; client-side filtering via URL search params

constraints.content_update: ML engineers edit JSON files only, no React changes required

confirmed_decisions.content_format: JSON model card files in /data directory

reasoner_reviews.product: The model card catalog is only useful if computational chemists can quickly find the right ADMET model for their target endpoint. Round 2 added the filtering by endpoint type, but the round 2 audit flagged that benchmark tables lack comparison against external public datasets (ChEMBL, Tox21) making it impossible to assess model quality in absolute terms. The model card schema must include external benchmark comparisons, not just internal test set metrics.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Next.js 14 static export with getStaticPaths for model card pages is correct. The build time constraint of <3 minutes requires pre-processing model card JSON at build time using generateStaticParams rather than client-side fetch. Client-side filtering via URL search params (endpoint, architecture, dataset) enables bookmarkable filter state without server-side routing.
reasoner_reviews.security: Internal-only restriction via CloudFront OAC (Origin Access Control) limiting S3 bucket access to the CloudFront distribution, which itself restricts to internal network via VPC. No application-level auth needed. CSP headers should block all external script sources given internal classification.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Round 3 must resolve ISS-002 (missing external benchmark comparison data in model cards) and ISS-003 (broken client-side search across model card descriptions). Build time optimization addressed in round 2 (ISS-001 resolved): JSON pre-processing script now runs during build, caching processed model card data.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 static export pages: /index (catalog with filtering), /models/[slug] (individual model card). Data loaded at build time via generateStaticParams from /data/models/*.json files. No runtime data fetching.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Catalog page: model card grid with filter sidebar (ADMET endpoint: solubility/permeability/metabolic_stability/hERG/toxicity; architecture: GNN/ECFP/transformer; dataset: ChEMBL/Tox21/internal). Model Card page: architecture summary, training dataset table with split sizes, benchmark performance table (RMSE or AUC against internal test set AND external ChEMBL/Tox21 baselines), Python usage code block, version history table, virtual screening integration guide.
frontend.performance_notes: Next.js static export produces zero server-side runtime. All model card JSON pre-processed at build time by /scripts/build-model-index.ts. Client-side filtering uses URL search params with Next.js router.push for bookmarkable state. No hydration cost on catalog page -- pure static HTML with CSS-only filter visibility toggle.
frontend.frontend_risks: Model card JSON schema changes by ML engineers could break TypeScript types. Mitigation: Zod schema validation on model card JSON at build time with CI fail on validation error.

backend.service_design: No backend runtime. Build-time data processing only: /scripts/build-model-index.ts reads all /data/models/*.json files, validates against Zod schema, generates /public/model-index.json for client-side catalog search.
backend.api_patterns: No runtime API. Static JSON index file served from CloudFront. Client-side search uses Fuse.js fuzzy search against pre-built model-index.json for description text matching.

security.auth_design: No application-level auth. CloudFront OAC restricts S3 bucket access to CloudFront distribution only. CloudFront distribution accessible only from corporate VPN via IP allowlist in WAF.
security.privacy_controls: No user data collected. No analytics scripts. No cookies. CloudFront access logs capture only IP and path -- retained 30 days for security monitoring.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: CloudFront access logs forwarded to CloudWatch Logs. S3 server access logging enabled for audit trail of file access.

data.entities: model_cards (slug, model_name, admet_endpoint, architecture_type, training_dataset, version, benchmark_results[], usage_example_python, virtual_screening_notes, updated_at -- JSON files in /data/models/). benchmark_results (dataset_name, metric ENUM rmse/auc/r2, value, comparison_source ENUM internal/chembl/tox21/pubchem). dataset_references (dataset_name, record_count, train_split, test_split, source_url).
data.storage_design: JSON files in Git repository /data/models/ directory. No database. Build script generates /public/model-index.json for client-side catalog. Static assets on S3.

devops.deployment_topology: S3 bucket (versioning enabled, public access blocked) as CloudFront origin via OAC. CloudFront distribution with WAF IP allowlist for corporate VPN range. Route53 internal CNAME for portal URL.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions on push to main: Node 20 install -> TypeScript build-model-index script (Zod validation) -> Next.js static build -> S3 sync -> CloudFront invalidation. PR builds deploy to staging CloudFront for review.
devops.rollback_strategy: S3 versioning for rollback to previous site build. CloudFront cache invalidation after rollback restore.
