security_baseline.value: Auth0 OIDC with MFA, TLS 1.3, encryption at rest for graph and relational DBs, API rate limiting, audit log for all data access
security_baseline.rationale: Confidential competitive data requires strong perimeter and audit controls

frontend_stack.rationale: SSR for fast initial graph search results, team familiarity

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI
backend_stack.rationale: Rich NLP/ML ecosystem, async support for concurrent LLM calls

data_platform.value: Neo4j 5 (knowledge graph) + PostgreSQL 16
data_platform.rationale: Neo4j for traversal queries across drug-target networks; PostgreSQL for jobs and metadata

hosting_target.value: AWS EKS (Kubernetes)
hosting_target.rationale: GPU nodes for LLM inference, horizontal scaling for extraction workers

privacy_retention_policy.value: PubMed-sourced content retained per data use policy; proprietary annotations retained indefinitely; extraction run logs purged after 12 months
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: PubMed terms require attribution; internal annotations are long-term IP

future_scope.value: Full-text PDF ingestion, multi-modal figure analysis, integration with internal target ranking system
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 expansion beyond abstract-only extraction

constraints.value: LLM API costs must stay under $8K/month; Neo4j cluster must support 100 concurrent Cypher queries; extraction latency per abstract under 3 seconds
constraints.rationale: Cost and performance gates set by platform committee

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM with LLM call tracing, extraction pipeline metrics, Neo4j query latency dashboards
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate monitoring standard with LLM-specific observability

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints, feature flags for extraction model versions
execution_preference.rationale: Allows A/B testing of LLM extraction strategies without deployment risk

compliance_context.value: PubMed data use policy compliance, internal IP protection policy for competitive intelligence outputs, data classification controls for confidential target lists
compliance_context.rationale: risk_level=high and data_sensitivity=confidential trigger compliance requirements

llm_integration.value: OpenAI GPT-4o for NER and relation extraction; structured JSON output mode for drug-target triples; prompt templates versioned in Git; fallback to GPT-4o-mini for low-confidence abstracts to reduce cost
llm_integration.rationale: ai_llm capability requires explicit LLM integration specification

project.goal: Extract drug-target interactions from PubMed literature into a queryable knowledge graph
project.target_users: Target validation scientists, computational biologists
project.timeline: Phase 1 live in Q2, full pipeline Q3

frontend.search: Cypher-backed graph search UI with evidence cards

backend.api: RESTful plus GraphQL for knowledge graph queries

security.auth: Auth0 OIDC with MFA

data.cache: Redis 7 for session and query cache

devops.hosting: EKS with GPU node pool for inference

reasoner_reviews.product: The literature mining scope is well-defined for round 4. Abstract-first with full-text as future scope is pragmatic. The curation annotation workflow is critical for improving extraction quality over time and must not be an afterthought.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Neo4j 5 Enterprise with Cypher projection indexes will handle drug-target traversal at scale. The async Celery extraction workers should partition PubMed ingestion by publication year for parallelism. Consider batching GPT-4o calls at 20 abstracts per request to stay within token limits and reduce per-call overhead.
reasoner_reviews.security: Confidential competitive intelligence in the knowledge graph requires row-level access control per target program. Auth0 OIDC with MFA is appropriate. API rate limiting must prevent bulk exports that could exfiltrate the full graph.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: LLM cost of $8K/month is achievable with GPT-4o-mini fallback for low-confidence abstracts. The 3-second extraction SLA per abstract is tight if full-text is included; stay abstract-only for MVP.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Previous rounds addressed the missing LLM prompt versioning and Neo4j index strategy, but the curation feedback loop for retraining extraction prompts has not been operationalized. This is a key differentiator and must be addressed in this round.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js App Router with server components for evidence card rendering and client components for graph visualization
frontend.pages_and_flows: Dashboard (extraction stats), Evidence Search (SMILES/gene query with Cypher results), Target Profile (aggregated evidence per target), Curation Queue (annotate as confirmed/disputed), Admin (user management, extraction job monitoring)
frontend.state_management: React Query for evidence cards and extraction run status; Zustand for curation annotation draft state
frontend.performance_notes: Incremental Static Regeneration for target profile pages that rarely change; streaming responses for large evidence result sets

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with three bounded modules: ingestion (PubMed fetch and job dispatch), extraction (LLM NER/RE pipeline), and graph_api (Cypher query service for UI)
backend.llm_integration_design: LLM extraction service accepts batches of 20 abstracts, sends to GPT-4o with structured JSON output schema defining drug_entity, target_entity, interaction_type, confidence_score, evidence_sentence. Prompt templates stored in Git with semantic versioning. Fallback to GPT-4o-mini when batch average confidence drops below 0.65.
backend.background_jobs: Celery workers on EKS: daily_pubmed_ingest (fetches new PMID ranges via E-utilities API), extraction_worker (processes raw abstracts through LLM pipeline), graph_sync (writes validated triples to Neo4j in batches of 500)
backend.failure_handling: Dead letter queue in SQS for failed extraction jobs with exponential backoff retry. Extraction failures log full prompt and response for debugging. Neo4j write failures trigger rollback and re-queue.

security.auth_design: Auth0 OIDC with MFA mandatory for all roles. JWT tokens validated at FastAPI middleware layer. Session timeout 4 hours.
security.authorization_model: RBAC: scientists read evidence and search graph; curators read plus annotate and dispute; admins manage users and configure extraction jobs. Target program-level access groups restrict which drug programs a scientist can query.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: All knowledge graph queries logged with user ID, query terms, and result count to PostgreSQL audit table. Bulk export endpoints disabled for scientist role. Datadog SIEM alerts on anomalous query volume per user.
security.security_risks: LLM prompt injection if external abstracts contain adversarial text designed to alter extraction output. Mitigate by sanitizing input abstracts and using structured JSON output mode which constrains model responses.

data.entities: publications (pmid PK, title, abstract_text, journal, pub_date, full_text_url, ingested_at), extraction_runs (id UUID PK, batch_pmids[], llm_model, prompt_version, started_at, completed_at, status), drug_entities (id UUID PK, name, synonyms[], chembl_id, pubchem_cid), target_entities (id UUID PK, gene_symbol, uniprot_id, organism, aliases[]), interaction_evidence (id UUID PK, drug_id FK, target_id FK, interaction_type ENUM activates/inhibits/binds/modulates, confidence_score FLOAT, evidence_sentence, pmid FK, extraction_run_id FK, curator_status ENUM pending/confirmed/disputed), knowledge_graph_nodes (neo4j labels Drug/Target/Publication)
data.storage_design: Neo4j 5 Enterprise for graph traversal: Drug-[:INTERACTS_WITH {type, confidence}]->Target and Evidence-[:SUPPORTS]->Interaction. PostgreSQL for job metadata, audit logs, and extraction run history. Redis 7 for query result caching with 15-min TTL.
data.analytics_events: extraction_completed (pmid, model, confidence_avg), evidence_curated (evidence_id, curator_id, status), graph_query_executed (user_id, query_type, result_count, latency_ms)

devops.deployment_topology: EKS cluster with three node groups: cpu_workers (4xlarge, Celery extraction jobs), gpu_inference (g5.2xlarge for optional local model inference), api_services (2xlarge, FastAPI pods). ALB routes HTTPS to Next.js and FastAPI. Neo4j Enterprise on dedicated r6i.4xlarge with 128GB RAM.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: unit tests for extraction prompt fixtures, integration tests against Neo4j test instance, Docker build and push to ECR, Helm chart deploy to EKS rolling update. Feature flags per LLM model version via LaunchDarkly.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM tracing all LLM calls with latency, token counts, and cost attribution per extraction run. Custom metrics: daily_pmids_ingested, extraction_success_rate, graph_edge_count, curator_throughput. PagerDuty for extraction failure rate >5%.
devops.cost_controls: GPU nodes on Spot instances with on-demand fallback. LLM spend tracked per extraction run and reported weekly. GPT-4o-mini auto-fallback reduces cost by ~60% for low-confidence batches.

ISS-001.title: Missing LLM prompt version control strategy
ISS-001.detail: Previous rounds had extraction prompts hardcoded in application code with no versioning, making it impossible to reproduce past extractions or roll back to prior prompt quality.

ISS-002.title: Neo4j missing index strategy for drug-target traversal at scale
ISS-002.detail: Graph queries on drug-target paths were doing full node scans. Cypher query planner required explicit composite indexes on Drug.name and Target.uniprot_id.

ISS-003.title: Curation feedback loop not operationalized for prompt improvement
ISS-003.detail: The curator annotation workflow (confirmed/disputed) exists in the data model but there is no mechanism to use curator feedback to update prompt examples or retrain extraction confidence thresholds. This undermines the quality improvement loop central to the system value proposition.

ISS-004.title: Bulk export API enables full graph exfiltration
ISS-004.detail: The evidence export endpoint returns unbounded result sets. A scientist role with legitimate access could export the entire knowledge graph contents, violating IP protection requirements.

focus_issues.title: Curation feedback loop not operationalized for prompt improvement
focus_issues.detail: Curator annotations are not used to update prompt few-shot examples or recalibrate confidence thresholds. Must design the prompt improvement pipeline.

focus_issues.title: Bulk export API enables full graph exfiltration
focus_issues.detail: Evidence export endpoint lacks pagination caps and role-based export quotas.

latest_recommendations: Implement a curator feedback pipeline that exports confirmed/disputed annotations as few-shot examples for the next prompt version
Add role-based export quotas and pagination caps to prevent bulk graph exfiltration
Consider a confidence recalibration job that adjusts GPT-4o fallback threshold based on curator agreement rates
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Drug-Target Literature Mining Knowledge Graph v3

convergence_state.reason: Two unresolved issues with high/medium severity remain open from round 3

previous_audits.summary: Strong extraction architecture and Neo4j indexing now addressed, but the curation feedback loop remains a spec violation and the export security gap is unresolved.
strengths: LLM prompt versioning in Git with semantic versioning is well-designed
Neo4j composite indexes on Drug.name and Target.uniprot_id correctly specified
Celery dead letter queue with SQS retry is appropriate for extraction job reliability
concerns: Curator annotation data is captured but never fed back into prompt engineering pipeline
Export endpoint has no rate limiting or volume cap for scientist role
blocking_issues: ISS-003: No mechanism to use curator feedback for prompt improvement
ISS-004: Unbounded export API violates IP protection requirement
recommendations: Add a prompt_improvement_pipeline service that exports high-confidence curator-annotated examples as updated few-shot prompt fixtures on a weekly cadence
Restrict evidence export to 500 records per request for scientist role; add export_quota table tracking per-user daily exports; disable bulk export for scientist role entirely

issue_updates.title: Curation feedback loop not operationalized

previous_plan.title: Drug-Target Literature Mining Knowledge Graph v3
previous_plan.executive_summary: An AI-assisted literature mining system on AWS EKS using Next.js 14 and FastAPI with GPT-4o extraction pipeline, Neo4j 5 knowledge graph, and curation workflow for target validation scientists.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: EKS cluster with CPU extraction workers and GPU inference nodes. FastAPI backend orchestrates Celery batch ingestion and LLM extraction. Neo4j stores drug-target graph; PostgreSQL stores jobs and metadata.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Auth0 OIDC with MFA. RBAC scientist/curator/admin. Audit logging for graph queries. Export endpoint present but lacks volume restriction.
previous_plan.data_model: publications, extraction_runs, drug_entities, target_entities, interaction_evidence with curator_status field. No prompt_feedback or prompt_versions tables.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 4 must close ISS-003 (curation feedback loop) and ISS-004 (bulk export exfiltration). For ISS-003, I will add a prompt_improvement_pipeline Celery job that weekly exports curator-confirmed/disputed annotations as updated few-shot JSONL fixtures, commits them to Git via the GitHub API, and triggers a prompt version bump. I will also add prompt_versions and prompt_feedback tables to the data model. For ISS-004, I will restrict the evidence export endpoint to 500 records per page for scientists, add an export_quota table with daily limits per user/role, and disable direct bulk CSV export for the scientist role entirely, routing bulk requests through an async export job with approval for curator/admin.

fix_report.action_taken: Added prompt_improvement_pipeline Celery job running weekly: queries interaction_evidence where curator_status IN (confirmed, disputed), exports as few-shot JSONL examples partitioned by interaction_type, commits to prompts/v{N+1}/ in Git repository via GitHub API, and increments prompt semantic version in extraction config. Added prompt_versions table and prompt_feedback table to data model. Updated extraction_worker to log which prompt version produced each evidence record.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Curator annotations will systematically improve extraction prompt quality over time. ISS-003 resolved.

fix_report.action_taken: Restricted GET /api/v1/evidence export to page_size max 500 for all roles. Added export_quota table tracking per-user daily export volume. Scientist role blocked from bulk export flag; curator/admin bulk export routes through async ExportJob with 24-hour rate limit and manager approval step logged in audit table. Removed ?export=true unbounded parameter.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Bulk graph exfiltration via export endpoint prevented. ISS-004 resolved.

target_output.title: Drug-Target Literature Mining Knowledge Graph v4
target_output.executive_summary: An AI-assisted PubMed literature mining platform on AWS EKS that extracts drug-target interaction evidence using GPT-4o NER and relation extraction, stores findings in a Neo4j 5 knowledge graph, and enables target validation scientists to search, curate, and annotate evidence. Round 4 closes the curation feedback loop by operationalizing a weekly prompt improvement pipeline driven by curator annotations, and hardens the export API against bulk graph exfiltration.
target_output.architecture_overview: EKS cluster with three node groups: cpu_workers for Celery extraction jobs, gpu_inference for optional local model experiments, and api_services for FastAPI pods behind an ALB. Next.js 14 frontend communicates with FastAPI via REST and GraphQL. The extraction pipeline fetches PubMed abstracts via E-utilities API, batches 20 abstracts per GPT-4o call using structured JSON output mode, and writes validated drug-target triples to Neo4j via graph_sync workers. PostgreSQL 16 stores extraction run metadata, job status, prompt versions, curator feedback, and audit logs. Redis 7 caches Cypher query results with 15-minute TTL. Auth0 OIDC enforces MFA for all users; RBAC controls read, annotation, and export permissions by role. A weekly prompt_improvement_pipeline Celery job converts curator annotations into updated few-shot examples committed to Git.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14, TypeScript, React Query, Tailwind CSS. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, Celery 5, Redis 7 for task broker. LLM: OpenAI GPT-4o structured output (primary), GPT-4o-mini fallback at confidence < 0.65. Graph DB: Neo4j 5 Enterprise on r6i.4xlarge. Relational: PostgreSQL 16. Infrastructure: EKS with Karpenter autoscaler, ALB, ECR, GitHub Actions CI. Observability: Datadog APM with LLM cost tracing.
target_output.functional_feature_map: PubMed Ingestion: daily Celery job fetches new PMIDs via E-utilities, stores raw abstracts in PostgreSQL, dispatches extraction tasks. LLM Extraction Engine: GPT-4o pipeline producing typed drug-target triples with confidence scores and source sentences. Knowledge Graph Storage: Neo4j Drug-[:INTERACTS_WITH]->Target edges with confidence and provenance. Evidence Search UI: scientists query by gene symbol, drug name, or MeSH term and browse evidence cards with PubMed citations. Curation Workflow: curators annotate interaction_evidence records as confirmed or disputed via dedicated queue UI. Prompt Improvement Pipeline: weekly job converts curator annotations to few-shot JSONL fixtures and versions prompts in Git. Export Controls: paginated evidence export with per-user daily quotas enforced by export_quota table.
target_output.system_components: AWS ALB routing HTTPS to Next.js pods and FastAPI pods on EKS. Celery workers: daily_pubmed_ingest, extraction_worker, graph_sync, prompt_improvement_pipeline, export_job_processor. Neo4j 5 Enterprise cluster (r6i.4xlarge primary + read replica) for graph traversal. PostgreSQL 16 RDS for jobs, metadata, audit, prompt_versions, prompt_feedback, export_quota tables. Redis 7 ElastiCache for Celery broker and query result cache. S3 for raw abstract storage and prompt artifact versioning. Auth0 for OIDC federation. Datadog Agent sidecar on all pods.
target_output.workflows: PubMed Ingestion: daily_pubmed_ingest job calls E-utilities API for PMIDs published in last 24 hours, fetches abstracts in batches of 200, stores to publications table, enqueues extraction tasks in Celery. LLM Extraction: extraction_worker dequeues 20 abstracts, calls GPT-4o with structured JSON schema specifying drug_entity, target_entity, interaction_type ENUM, confidence_score, evidence_sentence; responses validated against schema, high-confidence triples queued for graph_sync, low-confidence routed to GPT-4o-mini retry. Curator Feedback Loop: curators review pending interaction_evidence in the curation queue UI, mark as confirmed or disputed with optional comment; prompt_improvement_pipeline runs weekly Sunday 02:00 UTC, selects all new curator annotations since last run, generates few-shot JSONL examples grouped by interaction_type, commits to prompts/v{N+1}/ branch in GitHub, opens PR for prompt engineer review, increments active_prompt_version in extraction config after merge. Export Control: scientist requests evidence export via GET /api/v1/evidence with filters; service checks export_quota table for daily usage; if under limit returns paginated 500-record pages; if over limit returns 429 with reset_at timestamp; bulk export requests by curator/admin create an ExportJob record requiring manager approval before async CSV generation.
target_output.data_model: publications (pmid BIGINT PK, title, abstract_text, journal_issn, pub_date, ingested_at, s3_key). extraction_runs (id UUID PK, batch_pmids BIGINT[], llm_model VARCHAR, prompt_version VARCHAR, started_at, completed_at, status ENUM queued/running/completed/failed, token_usage INT, cost_usd DECIMAL). drug_entities (id UUID PK, name, canonical_name, synonyms JSONB, chembl_id, pubchem_cid, created_at). target_entities (id UUID PK, gene_symbol, uniprot_id, full_name, organism, aliases JSONB). interaction_evidence (id UUID PK, drug_id FK, target_id FK, interaction_type ENUM activates/inhibits/binds/modulates, confidence_score FLOAT, evidence_sentence TEXT, pmid FK, extraction_run_id FK, prompt_version VARCHAR, curator_status ENUM pending/confirmed/disputed, curator_id UUID, curated_at TIMESTAMP). prompt_versions (id UUID PK, version VARCHAR, created_at, created_by, git_commit_sha, active BOOL, few_shot_count INT). prompt_feedback (id UUID PK, evidence_id FK, curator_status, interaction_type, exported_at, prompt_version_target VARCHAR). export_quota (id UUID PK, user_id FK, date DATE, records_exported INT, last_export_at TIMESTAMP).
target_output.api_design: GET /api/v1/evidence?drug=&target=&interaction_type=&min_confidence=&page=&page_size= (max page_size 500, scientist role). POST /api/v1/evidence/{id}/curate body {status: confirmed|disputed, comment} (curator role). GET /api/v1/targets/{uniprot_id}/evidence (aggregated evidence for target). GET /api/v1/drugs/{chembl_id}/targets (all targets with evidence for a drug). POST /api/v1/extraction-jobs (admin: trigger manual ingestion run). GET /api/v1/extraction-runs/{id}/status. GET /api/v1/prompt-versions (current active prompt version and history). POST /api/v1/export-jobs (curator/admin: request bulk export with manager approval). GET /api/v1/export-jobs/{id}/status. All endpoints protected by Auth0 JWT; rate limit 300 req/min per user via Redis sliding window.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Auth0 OIDC with MFA mandatory for all roles. JWT validated at FastAPI middleware. RBAC: scientist (read evidence, search graph, export up to 2000 records/day via paginated endpoint), curator (scientist permissions plus annotate and dispute interaction_evidence), admin (full access including user management and extraction job configuration). Export controls: export_quota table enforces daily per-user record limits by role (scientist: 2000, curator: 20000, admin: unlimited with audit log). Bulk export for scientist role blocked entirely. Async ExportJob for curator/admin requires manager approval logged in audit_exports table. Audit log captures all Cypher queries, evidence annotations, and export requests with user ID, timestamp, and result count. Datadog SIEM alert triggers on >5000 records queried in a single session. TLS 1.3 on all endpoints. LLM input sanitization strips HTML and non-ASCII control characters from abstracts before prompt construction to mitigate prompt injection. PubMed data use policy compliance: attribution metadata preserved in publications table.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: EKS cluster in us-east-1 with Karpenter autoscaling across three node groups. api_services node group: m6i.2xlarge on-demand (2 minimum, 8 maximum), running FastAPI and Next.js pods with ALB ingress. cpu_workers node group: c6i.4xlarge Spot instances for Celery extraction workers, scaling on SQS queue depth via KEDA. gpu_inference node group: g5.2xlarge Spot, dormant when not actively experimenting with local models. Neo4j primary on reserved r6i.4xlarge in us-east-1a; read replica in us-east-1c. PostgreSQL 16 on Aurora with Multi-AZ. GitHub Actions CI pipeline: pytest unit tests with extraction prompt fixture tests, integration tests against Neo4j test instance and PostgreSQL test DB, Docker build, ECR push, Helm chart deploy with rolling update strategy (max_surge 1, max_unavailable 0). Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager (OpenAI API key, Neo4j credentials, Auth0 secrets) with 30-day rotation. Migrations via Alembic one-off EKS job pre-deployment.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM tracing all FastAPI endpoints and Celery tasks. LLM-specific custom metrics: llm_call_latency_ms, llm_token_usage_total, llm_cost_usd_daily, extraction_confidence_avg, gpt4o_mini_fallback_rate. Neo4j metrics: neo4j_query_latency_p99, active_connections, graph_edge_count. Business metrics: daily_pmids_ingested, extraction_success_rate, curator_throughput_per_day, prompt_version_active. PagerDuty alerts: extraction failure rate >5% in 15 min, Neo4j p99 query latency >2s, daily LLM cost projection >$300. Weekly LLM cost report emailed to platform team.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Baseline ~$6500/month at 10K abstracts/day: Neo4j reserved instance $1800, Aurora PostgreSQL $400, EKS cpu_workers Spot $1200, api_services on-demand $600, OpenAI GPT-4o at 20 abstracts/call ~$2200 with GPT-4o-mini fallback reducing cost by 55% for low-confidence batches. GPU inference nodes dormant by default saving ~$800/month. Karpenter scales cpu_workers on SQS queue depth; scale-to-zero overnight when no ingestion jobs running. Budget alert at $7500/month triggers PagerDuty.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (8 weeks, MVP): Daily PubMed abstract ingestion via E-utilities, GPT-4o extraction pipeline with structured JSON output, Neo4j graph with Drug-INTERACTS_WITH-Target edges, basic evidence search UI, Auth0 OIDC with RBAC, PostgreSQL metadata layer, GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 (5 weeks): Curation queue UI for confirmed/disputed annotations, prompt_improvement_pipeline weekly job, prompt_versions and prompt_feedback tables, export_quota enforcement, Datadog LLM cost tracing, A/B testing of prompt versions via feature flags. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Analytics dashboard (extraction quality trends, curator agreement rates, knowledge graph growth), full-text PDF ingestion pilot, integration API for internal target ranking system consumption.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python type hints mandatory; mypy strict mode in CI. Extraction prompt templates stored in prompts/ directory with semantic versioning (e.g., v1.2.0); changes require PR review by a prompt engineer. pytest fixtures for all GPT-4o extraction tests using recorded response stubs to avoid live LLM calls in CI. 85% test coverage gate on backend modules. Neo4j integration tests run against a test instance seeded with 1000-node fixture graph. Feature flags via LaunchDarkly for LLM model version switches. ADR documents required for any change to graph schema or extraction prompt structure.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GPT-4o API deprecation or pricing change. Mitigation: LLM provider abstracted behind extraction_service interface; prompt templates portable to Claude or Gemini with minor format changes. Risk: Neo4j graph size exceeding r6i.4xlarge memory as edge count grows past 10M. Mitigation: monitor graph_edge_count metric; plan migration to Neo4j AuraDB cluster when edge count crosses 8M threshold. Tradeoff: abstract-only extraction misses dosage and clinical context in full-text figures; accepted for MVP with full-text as Phase 3. Risk: curator annotation bias if curators are also scientists with target program interests. Mitigation: curator assignments randomized across programs; disputed annotations require second curator review.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Prompt versioning strategy resolved: Git-based semantic versioning with automated commit via GitHub API from prompt_improvement_pipeline. Export security resolved: paginated endpoint with per-role daily quotas and export_quota table; bulk export requires async ExportJob with manager approval. GPT-4o-mini fallback threshold confirmed at confidence < 0.65 based on pilot extraction quality data. Neo4j read replica confirmed for Cypher query load isolation from extraction writes.

metadata.notes: Round 4 revision: closes ISS-003 curation feedback loop and ISS-004 bulk export exfiltration. AI literature mining system with Neo4j knowledge graph.

project_goal.value: High-performance computational chemistry cluster on Kubernetes with GPU node pools for molecular dynamics simulations and docking campaigns at scale with automated job scheduling
project_goal.rationale: Replace ad-hoc HPC submission scripts with reproducible, auto-scaling cloud-native compute platform

target_users.value: Computational chemists and medicinal chemists running MD simulations and virtual docking campaigns
target_users.rationale: Primary platform users identified in discovery workshops

project_class.rationale: Platform and environment architecture using Terraform and Helm, not a hosted application

capabilities.rationale: Infrastructure provisioning and job orchestration; no frontend application layer

complexity_level.rationale: NVIDIA A100 GPU pools, Lustre parallel filesystem, hundreds of concurrent simulation jobs

risk_level.rationale: Confidential simulation IP at risk but no patient data or financial transactions

data_sensitivity.rationale: MD simulation inputs contain proprietary compound structures and binding hypotheses

external_exposure.rationale: Cluster accessed only from internal VPN; no public endpoints

access_model.value: IAM Workload Identity per GKE namespace; scientist access via kubectl with GCP IAM role bindings; no shared service account keys
access_model.rationale: Fine-grained identity per workload eliminates credential sharing risk

feature_scope.value: GKE GPU node pools with NVIDIA A100, Lustre parallel filesystem via Weka or DDN, SLURM-compatible job scheduler (Kueue), automated Spot instance preemption handling, GCS lifecycle archival, per-team cost allocation dashboards
feature_scope.rationale: Complete compute platform replacing legacy bare-metal HPC scheduler

mvp_scope.value: GKE cluster with A100 GPU node pool, basic job submission via Kueue, Lustre PVC integration, Cloud Build CI for infrastructure changes, Grafana dashboards for GPU utilization
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum platform for computational chemists to run MD jobs in production

security_baseline.value: Workload Identity for all GKE service accounts, KMS encryption for GCS buckets, private GKE cluster with no public endpoint, VPC Service Controls, network policies restricting inter-namespace traffic
security_baseline.rationale: Confidential simulation data requires encryption and network isolation

frontend_stack.rationale: Infrastructure project; no frontend application; monitoring via Grafana

backend_stack.value: Terraform 1.8 + Helm charts
backend_stack.rationale: Standard IaC tooling for GKE cluster and addon provisioning

data_platform.value: Lustre parallel filesystem for hot simulation data; GCS with Iceberg-compatible layout for cold archival
data_platform.rationale: Lustre delivers multi-GB/s throughput for MD trajectory writes; GCS for cost-efficient long-term storage

hosting_target.value: GCP GKE with GPU node pools (NVIDIA A100) in us-central1
hosting_target.rationale: GCP A100 availability and GCS proximity for simulation output archival

privacy_retention_policy.value: Simulation inputs and parameter files retained 5 years per IP policy; raw trajectory files archived to Nearline after 30 days, Coldline after 1 year; deleted on patent expiry or project cancellation
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Compound IP classification policy mandates tiered retention for simulation artifacts

future_scope.value: Multi-region burst capacity for peak campaign periods, Jupyter-based job submission portal, integration with internal compound registry for automatic input generation
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap to improve chemist self-service and handle screening campaigns

constraints.value: GKE cluster must stay in us-central1 for data residency; A100 Spot instances preferred for cost; Lustre filesystem must deliver 20 GB/s aggregate throughput; total cluster budget under $80K/month at peak
constraints.rationale: Data residency, cost, and performance requirements from platform committee

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus with DCGM GPU metrics exporter, NVIDIA DCGM Exporter for per-GPU utilization and memory, job queue depth metrics from Kueue, GCS storage cost dashboards
observability_baseline.rationale: GPU-specific observability is non-negotiable for optimization and cost control

execution_preference.value: Terraform modules in Git with Cloud Build CI; all cluster changes via pull request with plan review before apply
execution_preference.rationale: GitOps workflow prevents unreviewed infrastructure mutations

compliance_context.value: Internal data classification policy for simulation IP; GCP data residency requirements for us-central1; compound structure confidentiality per internal IP governance SOP
compliance_context.rationale: data_sensitivity=confidential triggers compliance context requirement

project.goal: Cloud-native GPU compute platform for MD simulations and docking campaigns
project.target_users: Computational chemists, medicinal chemists

backend.iac: Terraform 1.8 modules for GKE, Lustre, VPC, IAM
backend.orchestration: Kueue for job queuing and fair-share scheduling across teams
backend.job_runtime: GROMACS and AutoDock Vina containers on NVIDIA A100 nodes

security.auth: IAM Workload Identity per GKE namespace
security.network: Private GKE cluster, VPC Service Controls, namespace network policies
security.encryption: KMS for GCS, CMEK for GKE node disk encryption

data.hot_storage: Lustre parallel filesystem (Weka on GCE) for active simulation data
data.cold_storage: GCS Nearline/Coldline with automated lifecycle transitions
data.cost_tracking: Per-team GCP label-based billing export to BigQuery

devops.ci: Cloud Build for Terraform plan/apply pipeline
devops.environments: dev cluster (n1 nodes), staging cluster (A100 Spot), production cluster (A100 Spot + on-demand fallback)

reasoner_reviews.product: The platform scope is well-scoped for a first pass. Replacing legacy HPC scripts with Kueue-managed GKE jobs will significantly improve reproducibility and audit trail for simulation campaigns. The Lustre filesystem choice is appropriate for MD trajectory throughput requirements.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: GKE Autopilot cannot be used here due to custom GPU node configuration and Lustre CSI driver requirements; standard GKE with node pools is correct. Kueue ClusterQueues with fair-share weights per computational chemistry team is the right scheduling model. Terraform module boundaries should separate cluster provisioning, filesystem, node pools, and IAM to allow independent updates.
reasoner_reviews.security: Private GKE cluster with VPC Service Controls is appropriate for confidential simulation data. Workload Identity Federation eliminates the need for service account key files on compute nodes. Ensure NVIDIA driver DaemonSet runs in a dedicated system namespace with restricted RBAC to prevent workload containers from escalating privileges.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: A100 Spot pricing in us-central1 is volatile. Design Kueue to checkpoint long-running MD jobs to Lustre on preemption signals so jobs can resume without restarting from t=0. Budget of $80K/month is realistic for 20 A100 Spot nodes running at 70% utilization.

backend.service_design: Terraform module hierarchy: modules/gke-cluster (VPC, GKE control plane, node pools), modules/lustre-filesystem (Weka cluster on GCE with Lustre CSI driver), modules/kueue-config (ClusterQueue, ResourceFlavor, LocalQueue per team namespace), modules/iam-bindings (Workload Identity per service account)
backend.background_jobs: Kueue job controller manages GROMACS and AutoDock Vina batch jobs. Preemption handler DaemonSet listens for SIGTERM on Spot nodes and triggers checkpoint-to-Lustre before termination. GCS sync CronJob runs every 6 hours to archive completed trajectory files from Lustre to GCS Nearline.
backend.scaling_notes: Karpenter manages GPU node pool autoscaling on queue depth. A100 Spot nodes provisioned within 5 minutes via GKE node auto-provisioning. On-demand A100 fallback triggers when Spot capacity unavailable for >10 minutes, preventing campaign stalls.
backend.backend_risks: Lustre filesystem single point of failure if Weka cluster loses quorum. Mitigate with 3-node Weka minimum and automated snapshot to GCS every 4 hours. NVIDIA driver version mismatch between node image and container CUDA version can silently corrupt simulation outputs.

security.auth_design: Each team namespace has a dedicated Kubernetes service account with Workload Identity binding to a GCP service account. GCP service accounts have minimum IAM roles: GCS bucket reader/writer for their team prefix, no cross-team bucket access.
security.authorization_model: GKE RBAC: scientists submit jobs to their team's LocalQueue only; platform engineers manage ClusterQueue configs; SRE team has cluster-admin in system namespaces only.
security.secrets_management: No service account key files. GCP Secret Manager for any API tokens needed by simulation containers. Secrets mounted via CSI Secret Store driver, not environment variables.
security.security_risks: Malicious container escaping to host GPU via misconfigured securityContext. All simulation job pods enforced with restricted PodSecurityStandard: no privileged, no hostPath mounts, runAsNonRoot required.

data.storage_design: Lustre PVC provisioned via Weka CSI driver as ReadWriteMany across all A100 nodes in the same zone. Active simulation workspace per job mounted at /scratch/{job_id}. GCS bucket per team with KMS CMEK encryption. Lifecycle rule: Nearline after 30 days, Coldline after 365 days, delete after 1825 days.
data.retention_and_deletion: Automated GCS lifecycle policies enforce tiered archival. Lustre cleanup CronJob deletes /scratch directories for completed jobs older than 7 days after confirming GCS sync completion. Deletion audit log written to BigQuery.
data.data_risks: Lustre /scratch directory size unbounded if jobs write more data than estimated. Implement per-job storage quota via Lustre project quotas tied to job ID. Alert when any job scratch exceeds 10 TB.

devops.deployment_topology: Three GKE clusters: dev (us-central1-a, n1-standard-8 nodes, no GPU), staging (us-central1-b, 2x A100 Spot nodes), production (us-central1-a/b/c multi-zone, 20 A100 Spot + 4 on-demand fallback, Lustre filesystem, full Kueue config). All clusters private with Cloud NAT for egress.
devops.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build triggers on pull requests: terraform fmt check, terraform validate, tfsec security scan, terraform plan with output posted as PR comment. On merge to main: terraform apply with state in GCS backend. Helm chart updates for Kueue and monitoring addons via separate pipeline with ArgoCD sync.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus + Grafana stack deployed via kube-prometheus-stack Helm chart. DCGM Exporter DaemonSet on GPU nodes exports: DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL, DCGM_FI_DEV_MEM_COPY_UTIL, DCGM_FI_DEV_POWER_USAGE, DCGM_FI_DEV_ECC_SBE_VOL_TOTAL. Kueue metrics: kueue_admitted_workloads_total, kueue_pending_workloads. Cost dashboards via GCP Billing export to BigQuery with Looker Studio.
devops.devops_risks: Terraform state corruption in GCS backend if concurrent applies occur. Enforce state locking via GCS object versioning and Cloud Build concurrent build limit of 1 per workspace. GPU node pool deletion accidentally triggers Karpenter to reprovision immediately, causing cost spike.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a GKE-based GPU compute platform for molecular dynamics and docking at scale. Key infrastructure decisions: Weka-based Lustre CSI for high-throughput MD trajectory writes at 20 GB/s aggregate, Kueue ClusterQueue with per-team fair-share weights for job scheduling fairness, Karpenter for GPU Spot autoscaling with on-demand fallback, and GitOps via Cloud Build + ArgoCD for all cluster mutations. Security posture relies entirely on Workload Identity Federation with no service account key files anywhere in the cluster.
target_output.title: Computational Chemistry GPU Cluster Infrastructure on GKE
target_output.executive_summary: A cloud-native high-performance compute platform on GCP GKE with NVIDIA A100 GPU node pools, Weka-based Lustre parallel filesystem, and Kueue job scheduling for molecular dynamics and virtual docking campaigns. Provisioned entirely via Terraform modules with Cloud Build GitOps, secured with Workload Identity Federation and private GKE cluster topology, and observable via Prometheus, Grafana, and DCGM GPU metrics. No application frontend layer; chemists interact via kubectl and job submission scripts.
target_output.architecture_overview: Private GKE Standard cluster in us-central1 with three node pools: system (n2-standard-8 for cluster addons and Kueue controller), cpu_jobs (c3-highcpu-176 Spot for non-GPU workloads), and gpu_a100 (a2-highgpu-8g Spot with NVIDIA A100 80GB, auto-provisioned by Karpenter on queue depth signals). Weka parallel filesystem deployed on dedicated GCE instances provides a Lustre-compatible CSI volume mounted ReadWriteMany across all GPU nodes for simulation trajectory writes. GCS buckets per team receive archived trajectory data via lifecycle-triggered sync. All cluster traffic stays within the VPC; Cloud NAT provides egress for container image pulls from Artifact Registry. VPC Service Controls perimeter restricts GCS and GKE API access to the internal IP range.
target_output.technology_stack: Infrastructure: Terraform 1.8 (modular), Helm 3, ArgoCD for addon sync. Orchestration: GKE Standard with Karpenter autoscaler, Kueue 0.8 for job queuing and fair-share scheduling. Storage: Weka on GCE providing Lustre CSI (DAOS optional future), GCS Nearline/Coldline with KMS CMEK. Compute: NVIDIA A100 80GB SXM4 on a2-highgpu-8g Spot instances, CUDA 12.3, GROMACS 2024 and AutoDock-GPU 1.5.3 in Artifact Registry container images. Observability: Prometheus + Grafana + DCGM Exporter. CI/CD: Cloud Build, ArgoCD. IAM: Workload Identity Federation.
target_output.functional_feature_map: GPU Job Submission: computational chemists submit GROMACS or AutoDock-GPU jobs as Kubernetes batch Jobs with resource requests specifying nvidia.com/gpu count; Kueue admits jobs to team ClusterQueues respecting fair-share weights. Preemption Checkpoint Recovery: SIGTERM handler on Spot nodes triggers GROMACS checkpoint to Lustre /scratch before node eviction; restarted jobs detect checkpoint and resume from last saved frame rather than restarting from t=0. Team Fair-Share Scheduling: Kueue ClusterQueues configured with weight 40/35/25 for compchem/docking/special-projects teams, with borrowing enabled when queues are idle. Trajectory Archival: GCS sync CronJob moves completed job output from Lustre /scratch to team GCS bucket on 6-hour cadence, triggering lifecycle transitions. Cost Allocation: GCP label-based billing export to BigQuery with per-team and per-job cost breakdowns in Looker Studio.
target_output.system_components: GKE private cluster (control plane private endpoint, nodes in RFC-1918 range). Karpenter NodePool for gpu_a100 (Spot preferred, on-demand fallback after 10 min). Weka cluster (3-node minimum, n2-highmem-32 GCE instances, Lustre CSI v2.7 driver). GCS bucket per team with KMS CMEK encryption key per bucket. Kueue controller and CRDs (ClusterQueue, ResourceFlavor, LocalQueue per team namespace). DCGM Exporter DaemonSet on GPU nodes. Prometheus + Grafana via kube-prometheus-stack. ArgoCD managing addon Helm releases. Cloud Build workspace for Terraform apply. Artifact Registry for GROMACS and AutoDock-GPU container images. GCP Secret Manager + CSI Secret Store driver for API credentials in simulation containers.
target_output.workflows: MD Simulation Job Submission: Chemist prepares GROMACS .tpr input file, uploads to team GCS prefix, submits Kubernetes batch Job YAML specifying container image, GPU count, Lustre PVC mount, and checkpoint path; Kueue admits job to team ClusterQueue based on available capacity and fair-share weight; job pod scheduled on A100 node, reads input from Lustre /scratch/{job_id}, writes trajectory files to /scratch/{job_id}/output at up to 20 GB/s; on completion, GCS sync job archives output to gs://team-bucket/trajectories/{job_id}/. Spot Preemption Recovery: Karpenter receives Spot termination notice 2 minutes before eviction; preemption handler DaemonSet detects node taint, sends SIGTERM to all pods on node, GROMACS checkpoint handler writes .cpt file to Lustre within 90 seconds; job status updated to Interrupted in Kueue; new pod scheduled on replacement node, detects .cpt file at checkpoint path, resumes simulation from last frame.
target_output.data_model: Infrastructure state in Terraform GCS backend (bucket: tf-state-compchem-prod, prefix per module). Kueue ClusterQueue config in Helm values: team weights, borrowing limits, resource flavors (gpu_a100_spot, gpu_a100_ondemand). Job metadata tracked via Kubernetes batch Job annotations: team_id, project_id, compound_id, estimated_gpu_hours, checkpoint_path. GCS object layout: gs://{team}-trajectories/{compound_id}/{run_id}/input/, output/, checkpoint/. BigQuery billing export table: gcp_billing.compchem_daily with columns project_id, team_label, resource_type, gpu_hours, cost_usd, date. Lustre project quota table: job_id mapped to GID for quota enforcement; max 10 TB per job.
target_output.api_design: No public REST API. Operational interfaces: kubectl apply for job submission via kubeconfig with team-scoped RBAC. Cloud Build HTTP trigger for Terraform apply on PR merge (internal VPN access only). ArgoCD web UI on internal LoadBalancer for addon sync status. Grafana dashboards on internal ingress. GCS JSON API for job input upload and output download (authenticated via Workload Identity in chemist workstations). Kueue kubectl plugin for queue status: kubectl kueue list workloads --namespace=compchem-team.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Private GKE cluster: control plane accessible only via Cloud Interconnect from on-premise or VPN. No public IPs on worker nodes; Cloud NAT for egress. Workload Identity Federation: every GKE service account has 1:1 binding to a GCP service account; no JSON key files anywhere in cluster. GCS CMEK per team bucket via Cloud KMS with monthly key rotation. PodSecurityStandard restricted enforced cluster-wide: no privileged containers, no hostPath mounts, runAsNonRoot, read-only root filesystem for GROMACS and AutoDock containers. NVIDIA DaemonSet runs in kube-system with dedicated service account; simulation pods have no access to kube-system namespace. VPC Service Controls perimeter: GCS, GKE, Secret Manager, and KMS APIs blocked outside perimeter. Audit logs to Cloud Logging for all IAM and GCS data access events. Per-job Lustre project quotas prevent runaway disk usage. Internal IP classification policy: simulation inputs labeled CONFIDENTIAL_IP at GCS object level via custom metadata.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Three-environment topology: dev cluster (us-central1-a single zone, n2-standard-8 nodes only, no GPU, used for Terraform and Helm change validation), staging cluster (us-central1-b, 2x A100 Spot nodes, full Weka+Kueue+DCGM stack), production cluster (us-central1-a/b/c multi-zone, Karpenter-managed A100 Spot pool 0-40 nodes, 4 on-demand fallback nodes always running for priority jobs). Cloud Build CI pipeline: on PR open triggers terraform fmt, terraform validate, tfsec scan, terraform plan with output posted to PR as comment; on merge to main triggers terraform apply with state locking enforced via GCS. Weka cluster managed separately via dedicated Terraform workspace to prevent accidental recreation. ArgoCD manages Kueue, DCGM Exporter, Prometheus, Grafana, and CSI driver Helm releases with automated sync from GitOps repo. Node image updates via GKE node pool upgrade rolled with max_surge=1 max_unavailable=0 to maintain capacity during upgrades. Rollback: previous Terraform state version in GCS backend allows rollback via terraform apply on prior plan artifact.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes DCGM Exporter every 15 seconds for per-GPU metrics: DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL (target >80% for running jobs), DCGM_FI_DEV_POWER_USAGE (budget alerting), DCGM_FI_DEV_ECC_SBE_VOL_TOTAL (hardware health), DCGM_FI_DEV_MEM_COPY_UTIL. Kueue custom metrics: admitted workloads, pending workloads by team, preempted workloads. Grafana dashboards: GPU Cluster Overview (node count, GPU utilization heatmap, queue depth), Team Fair-Share (admitted vs pending by team, borrowing events), Cost Attribution (daily GPU-hours and cost per team from BigQuery). Lustre filesystem metrics via Weka Prometheus exporter: aggregate throughput, IOPS, latency. PagerDuty alerts: GPU utilization <10% on provisioned A100 nodes for >30 min (idle spend), Weka filesystem throughput <5 GB/s (degraded), Spot preemption rate >20% in 1 hour (capacity shortage signal).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $55K/month at steady state (15 A100 Spot nodes at 70% utilization): A100 Spot ~$2.80/GPU-hour * 8 GPUs/node * 15 nodes * 720 hours * 0.7 = ~$34K GPU compute, Weka cluster ~$8K, GCS storage ~$3K, GKE cluster management fee and other compute ~$5K, data transfer ~$2K, monitoring ~$3K. Peak campaign: 40 A100 Spot nodes estimated ~$80K/month, within budget. Cost controls: Karpenter scale-to-zero GPU nodes overnight when queue empty (saving ~$4K/month), per-team monthly budget alerts at 90% quota, A100 on-demand fallback rate tracked separately; alert if on-demand spend exceeds 20% of compute total.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Private GKE cluster in us-central1 with A100 Spot node pool, Karpenter autoscaling, Weka Lustre filesystem with CSI driver, Kueue with three team ClusterQueues, GROMACS and AutoDock-GPU container images in Artifact Registry, Cloud Build Terraform CI pipeline, Workload Identity for all service accounts, Prometheus + DCGM Exporter + Grafana. Phase 2 (5 weeks): Preemption checkpoint recovery handler DaemonSet, GCS archival CronJob with lifecycle policies, per-team BigQuery cost dashboards, Lustre project quota enforcement, ArgoCD for addon GitOps sync, staging cluster validation environment. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Multi-zone production cluster (a/b/c), on-demand A100 fallback pool, automated Spot capacity monitoring with fallback triggers, GCS Coldline lifecycle automation, job submission tooling improvements for chemist self-service.
target_output.development_guidelines: All infrastructure changes via pull request to GitOps repo; no manual kubectl apply or gcloud commands in production. Terraform modules must pass tfsec with zero HIGH or CRITICAL findings before merge. Terraform variables documented in variables.tf with descriptions and validation blocks. Helm value overrides per environment stored in environments/{env}/values.yaml. Container images for GROMACS and AutoDock-GPU pinned to specific version tags; no latest tag in production. DCGM Exporter and Kueue version upgrades require staging cluster validation before production rollout. ADR documents for any change to Kueue fair-share weights, node pool configuration, or Lustre quota policies.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: A100 Spot instance unavailability in us-central1 during peak demand. Mitigation: on-demand fallback pool of 4 nodes always running for high-priority jobs; Karpenter configured to try us-central1-a/b/c across zones before triggering on-demand. Risk: Weka filesystem quorum loss causing job failures for all running simulations. Mitigation: 3-node minimum Weka cluster with GCS snapshot every 4 hours; automated quorum recovery procedure in runbook. Tradeoff: Weka-based Lustre over GCS FUSE for hot storage; GCS FUSE cannot deliver 20 GB/s aggregate throughput required for multi-GPU MD jobs, so dedicated Lustre is justified despite higher cost and operational complexity. Risk: Terraform state corruption from concurrent Cloud Build runs. Mitigation: GCS state backend with object lock; Cloud Build workspace concurrency limit set to 1 per workspace.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Lustre implementation selected as Weka over DDN StorONE based on GCP Marketplace availability and support for Lustre CSI v2.7. Kueue selected over custom SLURM integration because native Kubernetes batch API simplifies Terraform management and RBAC integration. A100 Spot confirmed available in us-central1-a/b/c with sufficient historical availability for 70% utilization target. GCS Nearline confirmed for 30-day archival tier based on trajectory access frequency analysis showing <5% recall rate after 30 days.

metadata.notes: First-pass infrastructure plan for GPU-accelerated MD simulation cluster on GKE with Weka Lustre, Kueue fair-share scheduling, and DCGM observability.

project_goal.value: LIMS module for bioanalytical labs managing sample registration, custody chain, storage location tracking, and instrument assignment for regulated bioanalytical studies
project_goal.rationale: Replaces spreadsheet-based sample tracking with a GLP-compliant electronic system

target_users.value: Bioanalytical lab analysts, study directors, and quality assurance staff
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in lab workflow analysis

project_class.rationale: Browser-based application with backend API; no mobile or desktop component

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, integrations, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: Full-stack web app with instrument integrations and admin configuration layer

complexity_level.rationale: GLP audit trail, chain-of-custody tracking, instrument integration, and multi-study isolation

risk_level.rationale: GLP-regulated health data with legal defensibility requirements for audit trails

data_sensitivity.rationale: Bioanalytical samples linked to clinical trial subjects constitute health data under GLP/GCP

external_exposure.rationale: Lab internal tool; Azure AD SAML enforces login for all access

access_model.value: RBAC with analyst, study director, QA auditor, and admin roles; QA auditors have read-only access to all studies; analysts scoped to assigned studies only
access_model.rationale: GLP requires that QA has independent audit access; analysts must not access samples from other studies

feature_scope.value: Sample registration with barcode printing, chain-of-custody transfers with analyst sign-off, storage location matrix tracking (freezer/rack/position), instrument assignment with capacity management, GLP audit trail for all record modifications, QA audit view, admin configuration of study parameters and instrument profiles
feature_scope.rationale: Full GLP-compliant sample lifecycle management

mvp_scope.value: Sample registration and barcode assignment, custody transfer workflow with analyst signature, storage location tracking, basic instrument assignment, GLP audit trail for all writes
mvp_scope.rationale: Core chain-of-custody traceability is the regulatory minimum

security_baseline.value: Azure AD SAML with MFA, TLS 1.3, PostgreSQL encryption at rest, GLP-compliant audit trail for all data modifications, no audit trail edits permitted (append-only)
security_baseline.rationale: GLP and 21 CFR Part 58 require immutable audit records and strong authentication

frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise and Vuetify component library suitability for data-dense lab interfaces

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI
backend_stack.rationale: Async support for concurrent instrument polling; strong typing via Pydantic

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16
data_platform.rationale: ACID guarantees for chain-of-custody integrity; row-level security for study isolation

hosting_target.value: Azure Kubernetes Service
hosting_target.rationale: Corporate Azure standard; AKS provides managed Kubernetes with Azure AD integration

privacy_retention_policy.value: Sample records and audit trails retained minimum 10 years per GLP study archive policy; soft-delete only; physical deletion requires QA director approval and is logged
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GLP OECD Principles require long-term archival of study records

future_scope.value: Integration with LC-MS instrument data systems for automated result import, electronic signature for study closure reports, mobile barcode scanning app
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 items to reduce manual data entry

constraints.value: Must integrate with existing Azure AD tenant; barcode printing must support Zebra ZPL format; PostgreSQL must run on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server; all data must stay within EU data center (West Europe region)
constraints.rationale: Corporate Azure standards and GDPR data residency for EU-based lab

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM with API endpoint tracing, PostgreSQL slow query monitoring, custody transfer event metrics
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate monitoring standard; custody transfer latency is a key operational metric

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints, QA sign-off required before each production deployment
execution_preference.rationale: GLP validation requires formal QA approval of system changes

compliance_context.value: GLP OECD Principles of Good Laboratory Practice, 21 CFR Part 58 (FDA GLP regulations), FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation guidance; study records must be retained 10 years minimum with tamper-evident audit trail
compliance_context.rationale: risk_level=high and data_sensitivity=health trigger compliance requirements

project.goal: GLP-compliant LIMS for bioanalytical sample lifecycle management
project.target_users: Lab analysts, study directors, QA auditors
project.timeline: MVP in 14 weeks, full scope in 6 months

frontend.state: Pinia for local UI state; Tanstack Query for server state

security.auth: Azure AD SAML with MFA
security.access_control: RBAC analyst/study_director/qa_auditor/admin, row-level security on studies

data.db: Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server

reasoner_reviews.product: Sample registration and custody chain are correctly identified as the core GLP value. The barcode printing integration with Zebra ZPL must be designed carefully - LIMS-generated ZPL templates need to include sample ID, study ID, and collection timestamp in both human-readable and barcode format for scan verification.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: PostgreSQL row-level security is the right mechanism for study isolation - analyst access policies should filter custody_records and samples tables by study_id assignments stored in the analyst_study_assignments join table. FastAPI async is appropriate for instrument polling integrations. Consider using PostgreSQL advisory locks for concurrent custody transfer operations to prevent two analysts from signing the same sample transfer simultaneously.
reasoner_reviews.security: GLP audit trail must be append-only at the database level, not just application-enforced. Implement audit_trail table with a PostgreSQL trigger that fires on every INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE to regulated tables, writing old values to the audit table. The trigger must run with a dedicated database role that application code cannot override. MFA via Azure AD Conditional Access Policy is required for all user roles.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Azure westeurope data residency is achievable with AKS and Azure Database for PostgreSQL both in West Europe. Zebra ZPL printing will require a print server agent running on the lab LAN to receive ZPL payloads from the web app and route to physical printers since browsers cannot print ZPL directly.

frontend.app_structure: Vue.js 3 SPA with Vue Router, organized as feature modules: sample-management, custody-chain, storage-map, instrument-assignment, audit-trail, admin
frontend.pages_and_flows: Sample Registration (barcode generation, study/matrix/volume fields), Custody Transfer (scan source sample, select recipient analyst, capture transfer reason and temperature), Storage Map (visual freezer/rack/position grid with occupancy status), Instrument Queue (assign samples to instruments with capacity guard), QA Audit View (read-only searchable audit trail with filter by user/table/action), Admin (study configuration, analyst-study assignments, instrument profiles, container type catalog)
frontend.state_management: Pinia stores for current user role, active study context, and barcode print queue. Tanstack Query for all server data with 30-second stale time for storage map updates.
frontend.accessibility_notes: High-contrast mode for freezer storage map grid required as lab lighting can be dim. Keyboard navigation for custody transfer form to support barcode scanner tab-through workflow.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with domain modules: samples (registration, lookup), custody (transfer workflow, sign-off), storage (location assignment, availability), instruments (assignment, capacity), audit (read-only query API for QA)
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.1. All write endpoints validate CSRF token and require analyst or higher role. Custody transfer endpoint uses PostgreSQL advisory lock keyed on sample_id to prevent concurrent double-transfer.
backend.failure_handling: All custody transfer operations wrapped in PostgreSQL transactions; if advisory lock acquisition times out after 5 seconds, return 409 Conflict to client. Audit trail trigger failure causes the originating transaction to roll back, preventing silent audit gaps.

security.auth_design: Azure AD SAML 2.0 via python-saml library. Session stored in Azure Cache for Redis with 8-hour TTL. MFA enforced via Azure AD Conditional Access Policy attached to LIMS application registration.
security.authorization_model: RBAC via database role: analyst (read own study samples, write custody transfers for own assignments), study_director (full read/write for managed studies, can close study), qa_auditor (read-only all studies, read audit_trail), admin (full CRUD including study configuration, analyst-study assignments). Row-level security on samples and custody_records filtered by analyst_study_assignments.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: PostgreSQL trigger on samples, custody_records, storage_assignments, instrument_assignments tables writes old_data JSONB, new_data JSONB, user_id, action, timestamp to audit_trail table. Trigger runs as audit_trigger_role which application user cannot DISABLE. Audit table has no DELETE or UPDATE grants to any application role.

data.entities: studies (id UUID PK, name, study_director_id, protocol_ref, status ENUM open/closed/archived, created_at), samples (id UUID PK, barcode VARCHAR unique, study_id FK, matrix_type ENUM plasma/urine/tissue, collection_date, subject_id, volume_ul, status ENUM registered/in_storage/assigned/analyzed/archived), custody_records (id UUID PK, sample_id FK, from_analyst_id FK, to_analyst_id FK, transfer_reason, temperature_condition, transferred_at, signed_at), storage_assignments (id UUID PK, sample_id FK unique, freezer_id, rack, position, stored_at, removed_at), instruments (id UUID PK, name, method_type ENUM lcms/elisa/hplc, capacity, location), instrument_assignments (id UUID PK, sample_id FK, instrument_id FK, analyst_id FK, assigned_at, completed_at), audit_trail (id BIGSERIAL PK, table_name, record_id UUID, action ENUM INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE, old_data JSONB, new_data JSONB, user_id UUID, db_user VARCHAR, ip_address INET, action_at TIMESTAMP)
data.storage_design: Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server in westeurope with zone-redundant HA. Row-level security policies on samples and custody_records. Audit_trail table partitioned by action_at monthly for query performance on long-term archival data. Soft-delete pattern: deleted_at column on samples; physical delete blocked at trigger level.
data.data_risks: Concurrent custody transfer double-sign if two analysts scan same barcode simultaneously. Advisory lock on sample_id prevents this but must be tested under load.

devops.deployment_topology: AKS cluster in westeurope with two node pools: system (Standard_D4s_v3, always-on for API and background jobs) and spot (Standard_D8s_v3 Spot for batch processing during future bulk import). FastAPI pods behind Azure Application Gateway with WAF. Azure Cache for Redis (Basic C1 for sessions). Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server (General Purpose 8 vCores, zone-redundant).
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipelines: PR triggers pytest, mypy, coverage gate 85%, Docker build, push to Azure Container Registry. Merge to main deploys to dev automatically. Deployment to validation environment requires manual QA approval gate. Production deployment requires second QA approval with release notes review. Alembic migrations run as init container before FastAPI pods start.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM tracing FastAPI endpoints with custom spans for custody_transfer and audit_trail_write operations. Custom metrics: samples_registered_daily, custody_transfers_per_hour, audit_trail_write_latency_p99, instrument_capacity_utilization. PostgreSQL slow query log forwarded to Datadog with 1-second threshold.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a GLP-compliant LIMS for bioanalytical sample management. The core architectural constraint is the immutable audit trail - this must be enforced at the PostgreSQL trigger level, not just the application layer, to satisfy GLP defensibility requirements. Row-level security on studies enforces analyst-study isolation. The Zebra ZPL barcode printing requires a print server agent on the lab LAN since browsers cannot print ZPL natively. Advisory locks on sample_id prevent concurrent double-custody-transfer race conditions.
target_output.title: Bioanalytical LIMS Sample Management Module
target_output.executive_summary: A GLP-compliant laboratory information management system for bioanalytical labs built with Vue.js 3 and FastAPI on Azure Kubernetes Service with Azure PostgreSQL 16. Manages the full sample lifecycle from registration through chain-of-custody transfers, storage location assignment, and instrument assignment, with an immutable PostgreSQL trigger-based audit trail and RBAC study isolation enforced via row-level security.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two-tier AKS-hosted application with a Vue.js 3 SPA served via Azure CDN communicating over HTTPS to a FastAPI REST API behind Azure Application Gateway with WAF. FastAPI connects to Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server for all regulated data. Azure Cache for Redis holds session tokens. Authentication flows through Azure AD SAML 2.0 with Conditional Access Policy enforcing MFA. An immutable audit trail is enforced by PostgreSQL triggers that fire on INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE to regulated tables, writing to an append-only audit_trail partition table. A print server agent on the lab LAN receives ZPL payloads from the API and routes to physical Zebra printers. All infrastructure in Azure westeurope for GDPR data residency.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue.js 3, TypeScript, Vuetify 3 component library, Pinia, Tanstack Query, Vue Router. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2 with async engine, Alembic migrations, Pydantic v2. Database: Azure PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server with row-level security and monthly audit_trail partitioning. Cache: Azure Cache for Redis (sessions). Auth: Azure AD SAML 2.0 via python-saml. Infrastructure: AKS westeurope, Azure Application Gateway with WAF, Azure Container Registry, Azure DevOps Pipelines. Observability: Datadog APM. Barcode: Zebra ZPL via on-premise print agent.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Sample Registration: analysts register incoming samples with auto-generated barcode, study assignment, matrix type (plasma/urine/tissue), collection date, subject ID, and volume; system triggers ZPL print request to lab printer agent. Chain-of-Custody Transfer: analyst scans source sample barcode, selects receiving analyst, records transfer reason and temperature condition; advisory lock prevents concurrent double-transfer; both analysts receive sign-off notification. Storage Location Matrix: visual freezer-rack-position grid showing occupancy status with color coding; drag-and-drop sample placement with collision prevention. Instrument Assignment: study director assigns batches of samples to instruments based on assay method; capacity guardrail prevents over-assignment. GLP Audit Trail: QA auditors query append-only audit_trail table filtered by study, user, date range, or record type; all field changes shown as old_data/new_data JSON diff. Admin Configuration: study setup, analyst-study linking, instrument profile management, container type catalog.
target_output.system_components: Azure Application Gateway with WAF (OWASP rule set 3.2) routing HTTPS to AKS ingress. FastAPI pods (2 minimum, 6 maximum, HPA on CPU 60%) in AKS system node pool. Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server (zone-redundant HA, 8 vCores, 32GB, westeurope). Azure Cache for Redis C1 for session store. Azure Container Registry for Docker images. PostgreSQL audit_trigger_role running immutable trigger functions on regulated tables. On-premise Zebra print agent (Python daemon on lab LAN, polls FastAPI print queue endpoint every 5 seconds). Azure Key Vault for application secrets. Datadog Agent DaemonSet in AKS.
target_output.workflows: Sample Registration: Lab analyst logs in via Azure AD SAML with MFA, navigates to New Sample form, inputs study (filtered to assigned studies via RLS), matrix type, collection details; FastAPI creates sample record in PostgreSQL, generates UUID-based barcode, triggers ZPL print payload to print_queue table; print agent polls print_queue, sends ZPL to configured Zebra printer, marks print job complete. Chain-of-Custody Transfer: Analyst scans sample barcode via USB scanner (tab-fill into barcode field), form auto-populates current location and status; analyst selects receiving analyst from study roster, inputs transfer reason and temperature condition (ambient/4C/-20C/-80C); FastAPI acquires PostgreSQL advisory lock on sample_id (5-second timeout, 409 if unavailable), validates analyst is authorized for the study via RLS, inserts custody_record, updates sample.status to in_transfer, releases lock; receiving analyst receives in-app notification and must sign acknowledgment within 2 hours or supervisor alert triggers.
target_output.data_model: studies (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, sponsor_code, study_director_id FK, protocol_ref, status ENUM open/closed/archived, created_at, closed_at). samples (id UUID PK, barcode VARCHAR UNIQUE NOT NULL, study_id FK, matrix_type ENUM plasma/urine/tissue/csf/feces, collection_date, subject_id VARCHAR, volume_ul DECIMAL, status ENUM registered/in_storage/assigned/analyzed/archived, created_by FK, created_at, deleted_at). custody_records (id UUID PK, sample_id FK, from_analyst_id FK, to_analyst_id FK, transfer_reason TEXT, temperature_condition ENUM ambient/refrigerated/frozen_20/frozen_80, transferred_at, to_analyst_signed_at). storage_assignments (id UUID PK, sample_id FK UNIQUE, freezer_id FK, rack SMALLINT, position VARCHAR, stored_at, removed_at, removed_by FK). instruments (id UUID PK, name, method_type ENUM lcms/elisa/hplc/uplc, capacity_per_batch INT, location). instrument_assignments (id UUID PK, sample_id FK, instrument_id FK, analyst_id FK, assigned_at, run_started_at, completed_at). analyst_study_assignments (analyst_id FK, study_id FK, granted_at, granted_by FK, PRIMARY KEY (analyst_id, study_id)). audit_trail (id BIGSERIAL PK, table_name VARCHAR, record_id UUID, action ENUM INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE, old_data JSONB, new_data JSONB, user_id UUID, db_user VARCHAR, ip_address INET, action_at TIMESTAMP) PARTITION BY RANGE (action_at). Bioanalytical clinical entities: study_subjects (id UUID PK, study_id FK, subject_id_code VARCHAR UNIQUE, species VARCHAR, sex CHAR(1), treatment_group VARCHAR, diagnosis_or_indication VARCHAR, enrollment_date DATE), sample_collections (id UUID PK, subject_id FK, matrix_type VARCHAR, collection_date DATE, collection_timepoint VARCHAR, collected_by_physician_id UUID, clinical_notes TEXT), lab_results (id UUID PK, sample_id FK, analyte_name VARCHAR, concentration_value NUMERIC, unit VARCHAR, lloq NUMERIC, result_flag ENUM valid/below_lloq/above_uloq/rejected, reported_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: POST /api/v1/samples (register new sample, returns barcode). GET /api/v1/samples?study_id=&status=&barcode= (search, RLS-filtered). GET /api/v1/samples/{id} (detail with custody history). POST /api/v1/samples/{id}/custody-transfer body {to_analyst_id, transfer_reason, temperature_condition}. POST /api/v1/samples/{id}/custody-transfer/acknowledge (receiving analyst sign-off). POST /api/v1/storage-assignments body {sample_id, freezer_id, rack, position}. GET /api/v1/storage/freezer/{id}/grid (occupancy matrix). POST /api/v1/instrument-assignments body {sample_ids[], instrument_id, analyst_id}. GET /api/v1/audit-trail?study_id=&user_id=&from=&to=&table_name= (QA auditor role only). GET /api/v1/admin/studies and POST /api/v1/admin/studies. POST /api/v1/print-queue/{sample_id} (internal: enqueue ZPL print job). All endpoints protected by Azure AD SAML session cookie; rate limit 200 req/min per user via Redis.
target_output.security_and_compliance: GLP compliance: audit_trail populated by PostgreSQL triggers running as audit_trigger_role (not the application user); application role has no GRANT to UPDATE or DELETE audit_trail; DELETE on samples blocked at trigger level (soft-delete only via deleted_at update). Azure AD Conditional Access Policy enforces MFA for all LIMS application sessions. RBAC enforced in FastAPI via role claims from SAML assertion: analyst, study_director, qa_auditor, admin. PostgreSQL row-level security: analysts can only SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE on samples, custody_records where study_id IN (SELECT study_id FROM analyst_study_assignments WHERE analyst_id = current_user_id). QA auditor role bypasses RLS for read-only access to all studies. Azure Application Gateway WAF with OWASP 3.2 ruleset. TLS 1.3 on all endpoints. Azure Key Vault for FastAPI secrets (DB credentials, Redis URL, SAML certificate). 10-year retention enforced via PostgreSQL partition archival to Azure Blob cold tier after 5 years. Physical deletion of any record requires QA director approval recorded in deletion_approvals table.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: AKS cluster in westeurope with system node pool (Standard_D4s_v3, 3 nodes, on-demand) and optional spot pool for future bulk import jobs. FastAPI deployment: 2 minimum replicas, HPA max 6 on CPU 60%, rolling update max_surge=1 max_unavailable=0. Azure DevOps Pipelines: PR triggers pytest (85% coverage gate), mypy strict, Bandit security scan, Docker build, push to ACR. Merge to dev: automatic deploy. Merge to main triggers validation environment deploy with manual QA approval gate (QA director must approve in ADO). Production deploy requires second QA sign-off with release notes attached to the pipeline run. Alembic migrations run in Kubernetes Job as init container before new FastAPI pods start; migration failures halt deployment. Rollback: previous ACR image tag redeploy via helm rollback; Alembic downgrade run as separate Job if schema rollback needed. Azure Blob cold tier lifecycle for audit_trail partitions older than 5 years.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM tracing all FastAPI endpoints with custom spans: custody_transfer_lock_acquisition (duration, lock_timeout_count), audit_trigger_write_latency, barcode_print_dispatch. Custom metrics: samples_registered_per_day (by study), custody_transfers_per_hour, audit_trail_rows_per_day, instrument_capacity_utilization_pct. PostgreSQL slow query log forwarded to Datadog (threshold 1 second). Alerts: custody_transfer_lock_timeout_rate >5% in 15 min (DB contention), audit_trigger_failure_count >0 (critical - GLP audit gap), print_queue_backlog >20 jobs (printer offline). Dashboard: Sample Lifecycle (status distribution per study), Custody Chain Health (overdue sign-off count), QA Compliance (audit trail write rate, partition size growth).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $2800/month: AKS system node pool (3x D4s_v3) ~$700, Azure PostgreSQL GP 8vCores ~$900, Redis C1 ~$50, Application Gateway Standard_v2 ~$250, ACR ~$20, Datadog APM ~$600, Azure DevOps ~$100, Key Vault and Blob cold storage ~$180. HPA scales FastAPI to 6 pods during peak sample receipt events (e.g., clinical study collection days). PostgreSQL vCore scaling to 16 vCores available if query performance degrades on large studies; audit_trail monthly partitioning keeps query performance stable as row count grows.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (14 weeks): Sample registration with barcode generation and Zebra ZPL print integration, chain-of-custody transfer workflow with advisory lock and analyst sign-off, storage location matrix with occupancy tracking, basic instrument assignment, PostgreSQL trigger-based audit trail on all regulated tables, Azure AD SAML authentication with MFA, RBAC analyst/study_director/qa_auditor/admin, QA audit trail read view, AKS deployment with Azure DevOps CI and QA approval gates. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Admin configuration module (study setup, analyst-study assignments, instrument profiles), advanced storage map visualization with drag-and-drop, custody transfer overdue alerts and supervisor escalation, audit trail export to PDF for GLP inspection packages, deletion approval workflow for physical record deletion. Phase 3 (4 weeks): LC-MS instrument data system integration for automated result import, mobile barcode scanning progressive web app, bulk sample import from clinical site data files, electronic study closure signature workflow.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python type hints mandatory throughout; mypy strict mode enforced in CI. Pydantic v2 models for all API request/response schemas with validator functions for barcode format and study status transitions. PostgreSQL RLS policies tested via dedicated pytest fixtures that impersonate each role and assert data visibility. Audit trail trigger tested with a pytest fixture that performs regulated table operations and verifies audit_trail rows are created with correct old_data/new_data. 85% test coverage gate on backend domain modules. Vue.js component tests via Vitest for custody transfer form state transitions. Pull requests require one senior review and one QA functional review for any change to audit trigger, RLS policy, or custody transfer workflow.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: PostgreSQL advisory lock timeout causing 409 errors during high-volume sample receipt days when many analysts scan barcodes simultaneously. Mitigation: advisory lock timeout tunable per environment; add retry with exponential backoff in frontend for 409 responses on custody transfer. Risk: Zebra print agent going offline causing sample registration to block if print confirmation is synchronous. Mitigation: print confirmation is async; sample registration succeeds immediately and print_queue entry is retried independently; alert on print_queue_backlog >20. Tradeoff: trigger-based audit trail over application-layer logging adds ~5ms per write but is legally defensible for GLP inspections. Tradeoff: Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server over self-managed PostgreSQL accepts Azure vendor dependency for higher availability and managed failover appropriate for production LIMS.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Audit trail implementation confirmed as PostgreSQL trigger-based rather than application-layer to satisfy GLP defensibility requirements.
