Zebra ZPL printing confirmed to require on-premise print agent polling a FastAPI queue endpoint; direct browser ZPL printing is not feasible. Row-level security confirmed as the study isolation mechanism for analyst data scoping. Custody transfer double-sign prevented via PostgreSQL advisory lock on sample_id. Physical deletion confirmed blocked at trigger level with approval workflow for exceptional cases.

metadata.notes: First-pass moderate-quality plan for GLP-compliant bioanalytical LIMS on AKS. Immutable audit trail via PostgreSQL triggers is the key architectural decision.

project_goal.value: Streaming data infrastructure for real-time ingestion of high-throughput screening plate reader results, enabling live hit identification dashboards during active screening campaigns
project_goal.rationale: Current batch-upload workflow delays hit identification by hours; real-time streaming cuts campaign cycle time

target_users.value: HTS scientists and screening campaign managers
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers of live hit dashboards during screening runs

project_class.rationale: Kafka MSK cluster, ClickHouse, ECS stream processors - platform infrastructure, not an application

capabilities.rationale: Streaming platform and stream processing; no frontend application in this project

complexity_level.rationale: MSK Kafka cluster, Kafka Streams processing, ClickHouse OLAP, mTLS auth across all producers

risk_level.rationale: Confidential compound screening data but no patient records or financial transactions

data_sensitivity.rationale: HTS plate data contains proprietary compound identities and screening hit candidates

external_exposure.rationale: Streaming platform accessible only from HTS lab network via internal VPC

access_model.value: IAM roles for ECS task producers; mTLS client certificates for plate reader instrument producers; internal API gateway for downstream dashboard consumers
access_model.rationale: Instrument producers cannot use IAM; mTLS is the appropriate auth for hardware producers

feature_scope.value: MSK Kafka cluster with plate-reader-raw and hit-candidates topics, Kafka Streams normalization and Z-score jobs on ECS, ClickHouse OLAP cluster for fast aggregation queries, Redis for latest-state caching, Terraform IaC for all components, Grafana dashboards for stream health and hit rates
feature_scope.rationale: End-to-end streaming platform from instrument to hit dashboard

mvp_scope.value: MSK cluster with plate-reader-raw topic, single Kafka Streams ECS job for Z-score normalization, ClickHouse receiving processed hits, basic Grafana dashboard for queue depth and hit rate
mvp_scope.rationale: Core streaming pipeline sufficient to demonstrate real-time hit identification

security_baseline.value: mTLS for all Kafka producers, IAM auth for ECS consumer applications, TLS 1.3 in transit, ClickHouse data encrypted at rest via EBS KMS, VPC-internal MSK cluster with no public endpoint
security_baseline.rationale: Confidential compound data requires transport encryption and producer authentication

frontend_stack.rationale: Infrastructure project; hit dashboards are a separate frontend application consuming ClickHouse

backend_stack.value: Apache Kafka + Kafka Streams
backend_stack.rationale: MSK managed Kafka for broker operations; Kafka Streams for stateful stream processing with changelog topics

data_platform.value: ClickHouse for OLAP hit aggregation; Redis 7 for latest plate state caching
data_platform.rationale: ClickHouse sub-second aggregation queries on billions of well readings; Redis for real-time single-plate lookups

hosting_target.value: AWS MSK (Managed Streaming for Kafka) + ECS Fargate
hosting_target.rationale: MSK removes broker operations burden; ECS Fargate for Kafka Streams processor containerization

privacy_retention_policy.value: Raw plate reader data retained 3 years in Kafka log compaction or S3 archival; processed hit candidates retained 5 years per compound IP policy; deletion on project cancellation with IP team sign-off
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Compound IP classification policy governs HTS result retention

future_scope.value: ML-based hit prioritization model consuming Kafka Streams output, integration with compound registration system for automatic hit annotation with structural data
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 items requiring the streaming platform to be in place first

constraints.value: MSK must stay within existing AWS account VPC; ClickHouse cluster must handle 50 concurrent dashboard queries; Kafka Streams processing lag must stay under 10 seconds from plate completion to ClickHouse availability
constraints.rationale: Platform committee performance and architecture requirements

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for Kafka consumer lag, ClickHouse query latency, and hit rate metrics
observability_baseline.rationale: Kafka consumer lag is the primary operational health signal

execution_preference.value: Terraform modules with GitHub Actions CI
execution_preference.rationale: GitOps infrastructure changes with plan review before apply

compliance_context.value: Internal compound IP classification policy for HTS hit data; data retention and deletion procedures per IP governance SOP
compliance_context.rationale: data_sensitivity=confidential triggers compliance context

project.goal: Real-time streaming platform for HTS plate reader hit identification
project.target_users: HTS scientists, screening campaign managers

backend.streaming: Apache Kafka on MSK with plate-reader-raw (64 partitions) and hit-candidates topics
backend.processing: Kafka Streams jobs on ECS Fargate with stateful Z-score normalization
backend.olap: ClickHouse on EC2 for fast hit aggregation queries

security.producers: mTLS client certs for plate reader instruments
security.consumers: IAM roles for ECS Kafka Streams processors
security.transport: TLS 1.3 in transit, EBS KMS at rest for ClickHouse

devops.hosting: MSK in us-east-1 VPC + ECS Fargate

constraints.lag_sla: Under 10 seconds plate completion to ClickHouse

reasoner_reviews.product: The real-time hit identification use case is clear. The 10-second SLA from plate completion to ClickHouse availability is achievable with Kafka Streams if partitioning matches the plate reader count. Z-prime calculation must be included in the Streams job since it is the standard HTS assay quality metric that screening managers check first.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: 64 Kafka partitions on the plate-reader-raw topic allows parallelism up to 64 Streams processor instances. ClickHouse MergeTree table engine with partition by toYYYYMM(plate_read_at) and ORDER BY (campaign_id, plate_id, well_position) will deliver sub-second aggregation for the hit dashboard queries. Consider a ReplacingMergeTree for normalized_activities to handle late-arriving re-processed well readings.
reasoner_reviews.security: mTLS for plate reader producers is critical - without it any process on the lab network could inject fake screening data. Certificate issuance for lab instruments requires a private CA; consider AWS Private CA with automated certificate rotation. MSK cluster must be in a private subnet with no public broker endpoints.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 10-second lag SLA requires Kafka Streams flush interval tuned to 1 second (commit.interval.ms=1000) and ClickHouse async_insert mode enabled to avoid batching delays on low-volume plates.

backend.service_design: Three Kafka Streams ECS services: normalization_processor (reads plate-reader-raw, applies percent inhibition calculation and Z-score against plate controls), hit_classifier (reads normalized_activities, applies threshold rules per campaign config, emits hit_candidates topic), clickhouse_sink (reads hit_candidates and normalized_activities, batches inserts to ClickHouse every 2 seconds via async_insert)
backend.scaling_notes: ECS Fargate normalization_processor scales horizontally up to 64 tasks matching Kafka partition count; each task owns a subset of partitions. ClickHouse cluster on r6i.4xlarge EC2 (3 nodes, ReplicatedMergeTree for HA) handles 50 concurrent dashboard queries at p99 < 200ms for typical campaign-level aggregations.
backend.failure_handling: Kafka consumer group offset committed only after successful ClickHouse insert to prevent data loss on crash. Dead letter topic plate-reader-raw-dlq captures records that fail normalization after 3 retries (e.g., malformed plate schema).
backend.backend_risks: ClickHouse schema migration on a live cluster requires careful coordination with insert traffic. Normalization config (control well positions) per campaign must be pre-loaded before plates arrive or Z-score will be incorrect.

security.auth_design: AWS Private CA issues mTLS certificates for each plate reader instrument. Certificates have 1-year validity with automated renewal via a renewal Lambda checking expiry 30 days in advance. MSK configured with SASL/SCRAM for ECS consumers; mTLS for instrument producers.
security.secrets_management: Instrument mTLS private keys stored in AWS Secrets Manager and injected into instrument configuration via a provisioning script run by the lab IT team. ECS tasks use IAM role with MSK:Connect and MSK:DescribeCluster permissions; no static credentials.
security.security_risks: Certificate distribution to physical lab instruments is a manual process. Establish a certificate rotation runbook and lab IT SOP. Expired instrument certificate causes producer failure which halts data for that instrument without alerting.

data.entities: screening_campaigns (id UUID, name, target_id, assay_type ENUM fluorescence/luminescence/absorbance, hit_threshold_pct_inhibition, z_prime_threshold, started_at), screening_plates (id UUID, campaign_id FK, plate_barcode, plate_type ENUM 384well/1536well, reader_instrument_id, read_at, control_wells_config JSONB, status ENUM pending/normalized/classified), wells (plate_id FK, well_position VARCHAR, raw_signal FLOAT, pct_inhibition FLOAT, z_score FLOAT, is_hit BOOL), hit_candidates (id UUID, plate_id FK, well_position, compound_id VARCHAR, pct_inhibition, confidence_score, campaign_id FK, detected_at), processing_configs (campaign_id FK, normalization_method ENUM percent_inhibition/b_score, positive_control_wells TEXT[], negative_control_wells TEXT[])
data.storage_design: Kafka: plate-reader-raw (64 partitions, 7-day retention, LZ4 compression), normalized-activities (64 partitions, 3-day retention), hit-candidates (32 partitions, 14-day retention). ClickHouse: wells table as ReplicatedMergeTree partitioned by toYYYYMM(read_at); normalized_activities as ReplicatedReplacingMergeTree; hit_candidates as ReplicatedMergeTree. Redis 7: latest plate state keyed by plate_barcode with 4-hour TTL.
data.data_risks: Late plate re-processing can duplicate wells rows if ReplacingMergeTree deduplication has not yet merged. Query results during a merge window may include both old and new versions. Mitigate by querying with FINAL modifier on normalized_activities.

devops.deployment_topology: MSK cluster: 3 brokers, kafka.m5.2xlarge, in private subnets across us-east-1a/b/c. ECS Fargate cluster: normalization_processor (1-64 tasks, scaling on Kafka consumer lag via KEDA MSK scaler), hit_classifier (2-16 tasks), clickhouse_sink (2-8 tasks). ClickHouse: 3-node EC2 r6i.4xlarge cluster with ZooKeeper on r6i.large. Redis 7 on ElastiCache cluster mode.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Terraform fmt, tfsec, terraform plan on PR. Terraform apply on merge. Kafka Streams Docker images built and pushed to ECR. ECS service update via rolling deployment. Kafka topic configuration changes via a separate Terraform workspace with explicit plan review.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus JMX exporter on MSK brokers via CloudWatch MSK metrics. Kafka consumer lag exported via kafka-consumer-lag-exporter sidecar on each ECS task. ClickHouse system.metrics and system.query_log scraped by Prometheus. Grafana dashboards: Kafka Lag (per topic, per consumer group), ClickHouse Query Rate, Hit Rate per Campaign (hits/plate over time), Z-prime trend per campaign.

target_output.thinking_summary: Infrastructure platform for real-time HTS screening data. Key decisions: MSK with 64 partitions for plate-reader-raw topic to allow full parallelism of normalization processors matching the maximum instrument count; ClickHouse ReplicatedMergeTree for sub-second hit aggregation queries; mTLS via AWS Private CA for plate reader instrument authentication since instruments cannot use IAM; 10-second SLA met by tuning Kafka Streams commit interval and ClickHouse async_insert. Plan quality is intentionally weak - several sections are thinner than ideal.
target_output.title: HTS Plate Reader Streaming Infrastructure on AWS MSK and ClickHouse
target_output.executive_summary: A real-time streaming platform for high-throughput screening hit identification using Amazon MSK (Kafka), Kafka Streams ECS processors, and ClickHouse OLAP. Plate reader instruments publish raw well signals to Kafka; Kafka Streams jobs normalize, score, and classify hits; ClickHouse enables sub-second aggregation for live hit dashboards. All components provisioned via Terraform in the existing AWS VPC.
target_output.architecture_overview: MSK Kafka cluster in three private subnets receives plate reader well signals via mTLS-authenticated producers. Three Kafka Streams ECS Fargate services process the stream: normalization_processor computes percent inhibition and Z-scores per plate, hit_classifier applies campaign-specific thresholds to emit hit candidates, and clickhouse_sink batches writes to ClickHouse for dashboard queries. Redis 7 caches the latest plate state for immediate single-plate lookups. No public endpoints; all components within the existing us-east-1 VPC.
target_output.technology_stack: Streaming: Apache Kafka 3.7 on Amazon MSK (m5.2xlarge brokers, 3-zone). Processing: Kafka Streams 3.7 in Java 21 containers on ECS Fargate. OLAP: ClickHouse 24.3 on EC2 r6i.4xlarge (3-node ReplicatedMergeTree cluster). Cache: Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster mode. Auth: mTLS via AWS Private CA for instruments; IAM roles for ECS. IaC: Terraform 1.8. Observability: Grafana + Prometheus. CI: GitHub Actions.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Plate Reader Ingestion: instruments publish raw well signals per plate to plate-reader-raw topic as Protobuf messages keyed by plate_barcode. Z-Score Normalization: normalization_processor reads control well positions from processing_configs, computes percent inhibition per well, emits to normalized-activities topic. Hit Classification: hit_classifier reads normalized-activities, applies campaign hit_threshold_pct_inhibition, emits qualifying wells to hit-candidates topic with compound_id lookup. ClickHouse Sink: batched inserts every 2 seconds via async_insert. Redis Latest-Plate Cache: normalization_processor writes latest plate summary to Redis after processing.
target_output.system_components: MSK cluster (3 brokers, kafka.m5.2xlarge, private subnets). Topics: plate-reader-raw (64 partitions), normalized-activities (64 partitions), hit-candidates (32 partitions), plate-reader-raw-dlq (8 partitions). ECS Fargate cluster with three Kafka Streams service families. ClickHouse 3-node EC2 cluster with ZooKeeper ensemble. Redis 7 ElastiCache. AWS Private CA for instrument mTLS certificates. ECR for Kafka Streams container images. S3 for Kafka Streams RocksDB state store backups.
target_output.workflows: Plate Ingestion: plate reader instrument completes a 384-well read, serializes well positions and raw fluorescence signals to Protobuf, publishes to plate-reader-raw Kafka topic authenticated via mTLS client certificate; message key is plate_barcode ensuring all wells from a plate land in the same partition. Normalization: normalization_processor ECS task consumes partition, loads processing_config for the campaign from an in-memory cache refreshed every 60 seconds, computes percent inhibition as ((negative_control_mean - sample_signal) / negative_control_mean) * 100 and Z-score per plate, produces records to normalized-activities, commits offset, writes latest plate summary to Redis with plate_barcode key.
target_output.data_model: ClickHouse tables: screening_campaigns (campaign_id UUID, target_id, assay_type, hit_threshold FLOAT, started_at DateTime). screening_plates (plate_id UUID, campaign_id UUID, plate_barcode String, reader_instrument String, read_at DateTime, z_prime FLOAT, status String). wells (plate_id UUID, well_position String, compound_id String, raw_signal Float32, pct_inhibition Float32, z_score Float32, is_hit UInt8, read_at DateTime) ENGINE=ReplicatedMergeTree PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(read_at) ORDER BY (campaign_id, plate_id, well_position). hit_candidates (candidate_id UUID, campaign_id UUID, plate_id UUID, well_position String, compound_id String, pct_inhibition Float32, detected_at DateTime) ENGINE=ReplicatedMergeTree ORDER BY (campaign_id, detected_at).
target_output.api_design: No REST API in this infrastructure project. Downstream consumers interact via direct ClickHouse SQL queries authenticated with ClickHouse user credentials. Kafka Streams management via ECS service update API (scale task count). Redis access via internal VPC endpoint. Grafana as the primary operational interface for queue depth and hit rate dashboards. Future: a thin query API wrapping ClickHouse may be added in a follow-on project for dashboard backend.
target_output.security_and_compliance: mTLS for all plate reader instrument Kafka producers using AWS Private CA certificates with 1-year validity and 30-day renewal Lambda. MSK cluster in private subnets, no public broker endpoints. ECS Fargate tasks use IAM execution roles with MSK:Connect and ECR:GetAuthorizationToken only; no static credentials. ClickHouse nodes in private subnets, accessible only from ECS task security group and Grafana instance security group. EBS volumes on ClickHouse EC2 instances encrypted with KMS CMK. VPC security groups restrict inter-component traffic to minimum required ports. S3 bucket for state store backups with server-side encryption and versioning enabled. Compound IP classification label applied to all S3 objects via bucket tag policy.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Terraform modules: modules/msk-cluster, modules/kafka-topics, modules/clickhouse-cluster, modules/ecs-streams, modules/private-ca. Dev environment uses a single-broker MSK cluster with smaller EC2 instances. Production uses 3-broker MSK with m5.2xlarge and 3-node ClickHouse with r6i.4xlarge. GitHub Actions CI: tfsec scan, terraform plan on PR, terraform apply on merge to main. ECS Kafka Streams services updated via rolling deployment on new image push. ClickHouse schema changes run as SQL migration scripts in a dedicated CI job against a staging ClickHouse instance before production apply. MSK broker upgrades via MSK rolling upgrade API with producer/consumer monitoring during the window.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes kafka-consumer-lag-exporter sidecar on each ECS Streams task; Grafana dashboard shows lag per topic per consumer group. CloudWatch MSK metrics exported to Prometheus via MSK CloudWatch exporter. ClickHouse Prometheus endpoint exposes query_count, query_latency_p99, merge_rows_per_second, insert_rows_per_second. Business metrics: hit_detection_latency_seconds (plate read_at to ClickHouse first insert), hits_per_plate_avg per campaign, z_prime_below_threshold_count. Alerts: consumer lag >30K messages on plate-reader-raw (processing bottleneck), ClickHouse insert errors >0 (data loss risk), instrument certificate expiry <14 days.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $4200/month for 10 active screening campaigns at 500 plates/day: MSK 3x m5.2xlarge ~$800, ClickHouse 3x r6i.4xlarge reserved ~$2200, ElastiCache Redis ~$150, ECS Fargate normalization tasks at average 4 tasks ~$400, data transfer ~$200, Private CA ~$400, S3 ~$50. Scale headroom: ECS normalization_processor auto-scales to 64 tasks on consumer lag. ClickHouse horizontal scaling limited to 3 nodes with current ZooKeeper config; ClickHouse Keeper migration would enable adding nodes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): MSK cluster with plate-reader-raw topic, single normalization_processor ECS Kafka Streams job, ClickHouse receiving normalized well data, basic Grafana lag and hit rate dashboard, mTLS via Private CA for first 5 instruments, Terraform CI pipeline. Phase 2 (4 weeks): hit_classifier Kafka Streams job, hit-candidates topic, Redis latest-plate cache, dead letter queue handling, ClickHouse hit aggregation views for dashboard queries, instrument certificate rotation Lambda. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Additional instrument onboarding, S3 Kafka Streams state store backup, Z-prime per-plate metric pipeline, ClickHouse query API for dashboard frontend decoupling.
target_output.development_guidelines: Kafka Streams Java services require unit tests using TopologyTestDriver to validate normalization logic without a live Kafka cluster. Protobuf schema changes to plate-reader-raw topic require schema compatibility check via AWS Glue Schema Registry before deployment. ClickHouse schema changes must be tested against a staging instance with production-scale data before applying to production. Terraform modules tested via terratest in CI against a dev AWS account. mTLS certificate rotation procedures documented in lab IT runbook.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: plate reader instrument certificate expiry halts data from that instrument. Mitigation: 30-day renewal Lambda with PagerDuty alert at 14 days remaining. Risk: normalization_processor state store growing unbounded for long-running campaigns. Mitigation: RocksDB state store with 24-hour retention window; state backed up to S3 hourly. Tradeoff: ClickHouse over Redshift for OLAP; ClickHouse delivers sub-200ms aggregation on billions of well rows which Redshift cannot match at this latency requirement, justified despite higher operational complexity.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ClickHouse confirmed over Redshift based on 200ms p99 latency requirement for live dashboards which Redshift cannot meet on unindexed well-level data. MSK confirmed over self-managed Kafka to eliminate broker operations burden. 64 partitions on plate-reader-raw confirmed based on maximum concurrent instrument count of 50 with headroom. mTLS via AWS Private CA confirmed as the instrument authentication mechanism since instruments cannot run IAM credential providers.

metadata.notes: Weak first-pass plan for HTS streaming infrastructure. Several sections thinner than ideal. MSK Kafka + ClickHouse for real-time hit identification.

project_goal.value: High-performance cheminformatics API serving substructure search, similarity search, and chemical property queries over a 50 million compound library, designed to serve concurrent requests from virtual screening pipelines
project_goal.rationale: Current direct PostgreSQL JDBC calls from screening pipelines are unscalable and expose raw DB credentials to client teams

target_users.value: Computational chemistry teams and virtual screening automation pipelines
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers of substructure and similarity search at scale

project_class.rationale: Stateless REST API with no frontend; pure service consumed by programmatic clients

capabilities.rationale: Backend API with compound database, JWT auth, and versioned API surface

complexity_level.rationale: 50M compound library with Morgan fingerprint indexing, concurrent substructure queries, and sub-second latency requirements

risk_level.rationale: Proprietary compound library is valuable IP but no patient data or financial transactions

data_sensitivity.rationale: 50M compound library includes proprietary synthesized and virtual compounds representing significant IP value

external_exposure.rationale: API consumed by internal computational chemistry pipelines only; no partner or public access

access_model.value: JWT-based service-to-service authentication; each pipeline team issued a service token scoped to allowed query types (similarity, substructure, properties); rate limits enforced per token
access_model.rationale: Token scoping prevents a lower-priority pipeline from consuming full capacity and starving high-priority virtual screening runs

feature_scope.value: Tanimoto similarity search with configurable threshold and Morgan fingerprint radius, substructure search using RDKit PostgreSQL cartridge, chemical property queries (logP, MW, TPSA, HBA, HBD, rotatable bonds), compound batch lookup by registry ID or InChIKey, compound library statistics endpoint
feature_scope.rationale: Core cheminformatics query types needed by virtual screening and hit identification workflows

mvp_scope.value: Similarity search by SMILES with Tanimoto threshold, substructure search by SMARTS, compound property lookup by registry ID; JWT auth with rate limiting
mvp_scope.rationale: Three core query types sufficient to replace direct DB access for most pipeline use cases

security_baseline.value: JWT service tokens with 90-day expiry, TLS 1.3 in transit, PostgreSQL compound data encrypted at rest via EBS KMS, per-token rate limiting via Redis sliding window, no bulk compound export endpoint
security_baseline.rationale: Confidential compound library requires export controls; rate limits protect DB from capacity exhaustion

frontend_stack.rationale: API service consumed by programmatic clients; no frontend application

backend_stack.value: C++/Python hybrid with FastAPI (RDKit cartridge layer)
backend_stack.rationale: Python FastAPI for API layer; PostgreSQL RDKit cartridge for indexing; C++ via RDKit Python bindings for in-process fingerprint computation

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with RDKit cartridge
data_platform.rationale: RDKit mol column with GiST index enables set-based substructure queries; PostgreSQL morganbv_fp column with ivfflat index for similarity

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate with Application Load Balancer
hosting_target.rationale: Stateless API containers with ALB for load distribution; RDS PostgreSQL for managed DB operations

privacy_retention_policy.value: Compound library retained indefinitely while active in discovery programs; deprecated compound records soft-deleted and archived to S3 Parquet after 2 years; query logs purged after 90 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Compound IP has indefinite value; query logs are operational only

future_scope.value: 3D conformer-based shape similarity search, integration with ADMET prediction service for enriched hit profiles, GraphQL endpoint for complex multi-property queries
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 capabilities requiring the base API service to be stable

constraints.value: Similarity search p99 latency under 800ms for top-100 results on 50M compounds; substructure search p99 under 5 seconds for most SMARTS queries; no more than 500K compounds returned in any single query; ECS Fargate containers must not store compound data locally
constraints.rationale: Latency SLAs from virtual screening pipeline requirements; compound data must only reside in RDS

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM with per-query-type latency tracking, Redis hit/miss rates, RDS slow query monitoring
observability_baseline.rationale: Similarity search latency per query type is the primary SLA metric

execution_preference.value: Trunk-based development with GitHub Actions CI; semantic versioning for API versions
execution_preference.rationale: API versioning is critical for pipeline clients that cannot be updated immediately

compliance_context.value: Internal compound IP protection policy; query log retention policy per data governance SOP; export controls preventing bulk compound structure download
compliance_context.rationale: data_sensitivity=confidential triggers compliance context requirement

project.goal: High-performance cheminformatics API for 50M compound library queries
project.target_users: Computational chemistry pipelines, virtual screening automation
project.timeline: MVP in 10 weeks with 50M compound library loaded

backend.framework: FastAPI with async endpoints; synchronous RDKit fingerprint operations in thread pool

security.auth: JWT service tokens with HS256 signing; Redis token registry for immediate revocation
security.rate_limiting: Redis sliding window per JWT token ID

data.cache: ElastiCache Redis 7 (query result caching and rate limiting)
data.indexes: GiST index on mol column for substructure; ivfflat index on morganbv_fp for similarity

devops.hosting: ECS Fargate in us-east-1 with ALB
devops.environments: dev, staging, production with blue-green for API version releases

constraints.data_locality: RDS only, no local compound storage in containers

reasoner_reviews.product: The query type scope is well-defined and matches the actual needs of virtual screening pipelines. The similarity search with configurable Tanimoto threshold (typically 0.7-0.85 for lead hopping) and SMARTS substructure search for pharmacophore queries cover 90% of pipeline use cases. The batch registry ID lookup is important for enriching hit lists from other systems.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The 800ms p99 SLA for similarity search on 50M compounds is achievable but requires careful index tuning. Pre-computed Morgan fingerprints stored as bit vectors in a morganbv_fp column with ivfflat index (pgvector-style) will outperform on-the-fly calculation. For substructure search, the RDKit GiST index prunes the candidate set effectively for drug-like SMARTS patterns but can degrade on very broad queries; add a query complexity heuristic that warns clients before executing expensive substructure queries. Redis caching of frequent similarity queries (same SMILES, same threshold) with SMILES canonical form as cache key will significantly reduce DB load for repeated pipeline queries.
reasoner_reviews.security: JWT service tokens scoped by query type prevent a batch property lookup job from consuming similarity search capacity. Implement token claims: allowed_query_types[] and rate_limit_tier (standard/premium). No bulk structure export must be enforced both in API logic and at the RDS level via a separate read-only user that cannot run queries returning >500K rows. Query logs must not contain full SMILES strings from client queries to avoid logging proprietary client compound structures.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 5-second substructure SLA requires query timeout enforcement at the PostgreSQL level (statement_timeout per session) to prevent runaway SMARTS queries from holding connections. ECS containers must not cache compound data to disk; configure ephemeral storage to minimum and mount no persistent volumes.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with three route modules: /similarity (Tanimoto-based fingerprint search), /substructure (SMARTS-based RDKit cartridge query), /compounds (batch lookup, property queries, library stats). Shared middleware: JWT validation, rate limit check via Redis, request logging with query_type but not SMILES content.
backend.api_patterns: REST with OpenAPI 3.1. Similarity and substructure endpoints accept POST with JSON body (SMILES/SMARTS, threshold, max_results). Property queries accept GET with registry_id or inchikey parameter. All responses include query_id UUID for Datadog correlation. 500K result cap enforced at SQL LIMIT clause.
backend.failure_handling: PostgreSQL statement_timeout set to 8 seconds per session for substructure queries; FastAPI returns 504 with suggested query simplification. Redis connection failure falls back to uncached DB query with Datadog alert. Circuit breaker on RDS connection pool: if >80% connections in use, return 503 with Retry-After header.
backend.scaling_notes: ECS auto-scaling on ALB RequestCountPerTarget metric: scale out at 1000 req/min, scale in at 300 req/min. RDS read replicas for similarity and property queries (read-only user); substructure queries routed to primary only due to RDKit index write amplification on replicas.
backend.backend_risks: RDKit cartridge version mismatch between RDS custom parameter group and container RDKit version can cause fingerprint encoding incompatibility. Pin RDKit version in Dockerfile to match RDS cartridge version explicitly.

security.auth_design: JWT tokens issued by internal token service (separate microservice). Tokens contain: sub (pipeline team ID), jti (unique token ID for Redis revocation registry), allowed_query_types [], rate_limit_tier, exp (90 days). FastAPI middleware validates JWT signature, checks jti not in Redis revoked set, enforces allowed_query_types claim against requested endpoint.
security.authorization_model: Service tokens are team-scoped: virtual_screening team has similarity+substructure+properties, property_enrichment team has properties only, admin token has all types plus library stats. No compound-level access control; all authenticated teams see the full 50M library.
security.secrets_management: JWT signing secret in AWS Secrets Manager, loaded at FastAPI startup via boto3. RDS credentials via AWS Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation. Redis auth token via Secrets Manager. No secrets in Dockerfile or environment variables in ECS task definition plaintext.
security.security_risks: SMARTS complexity attack: a malicious client submits an unbounded SMARTS pattern causing a full table scan that exhausts DB connections. Mitigate via statement_timeout and a SMARTS complexity score heuristic that rejects queries above a ring count or atom count threshold.

data.entities: compounds (registry_id VARCHAR PK, inchikey VARCHAR UNIQUE, smiles TEXT, mol mol, morganbv_fp bit(2048), mw FLOAT, logp FLOAT, tpsa FLOAT, hba SMALLINT, hbd SMALLINT, rotatable_bonds SMALLINT, status ENUM active/deprecated, created_at, deprecated_at). query_logs (id UUID PK, token_jti VARCHAR, query_type ENUM similarity/substructure/property/batch, query_fingerprint_hash VARCHAR, result_count INT, latency_ms INT, executed_at TIMESTAMP). search_sessions (id UUID PK, token_jti VARCHAR, query_type, smiles_hash VARCHAR, threshold FLOAT, result_count INT, cache_hit BOOL, executed_at TIMESTAMP).
data.storage_design: RDS PostgreSQL 16 on r6g.8xlarge (32 vCores, 256GB RAM) to hold 50M mol objects and 2048-bit fingerprint vectors in memory. GiST index on mol column (rdkit_mol_ops) for substructure. ivfflat index on morganbv_fp (100 lists, probes=10) for similarity ANN search. ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster for query result cache (SMILES canonical form hash as key, 30-min TTL for similarity results, 5-min TTL for substructure) and rate limiting. S3 Parquet for deprecated compound archive.
data.analytics_events: api_query_completed (query_type, token_team, latency_ms, result_count, cache_hit), rate_limit_triggered (token_jti, token_team, endpoint), slow_query_detected (query_type, latency_ms, smarts_complexity_score).
data.data_risks: ivfflat index recall accuracy degrades below 0.95 at high similarity thresholds (>0.9 Tanimoto). Add exact-match fallback for threshold >0.9 using sequential bit vector comparison on candidates returned by ANN index.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate service with ALB in us-east-1a/b/c. Minimum 3 tasks, maximum 20. RDS PostgreSQL r6g.8xlarge Multi-AZ with 2 read replicas for similarity and property queries. ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster mode (3 shards). ALB target group with connection draining 30 seconds. Blue-green deployment for API version releases via weighted ALB listener rules.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest with RDKit substructure and fingerprint unit tests using a 10K compound test fixture, mypy, coverage 90% gate, Docker build, ECR push. Deploy to staging via ECS rolling update. Production blue-green: deploy new task definition as green target group, shift 10% traffic, monitor error rate for 10 minutes, shift 100% or rollback. Migrations: Alembic one-off ECS task pre-deployment; RDKit index rebuild runs as a separate Job after schema migration.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM tracing FastAPI endpoints with custom spans: rds_fingerprint_query, redis_cache_check, smarts_complexity_evaluation. Custom metrics: similarity_query_latency_p99 (target <800ms), substructure_query_latency_p99 (target <5s), cache_hit_rate_pct (target >40% for repeat pipeline queries), rds_connection_pool_utilization_pct. PagerDuty alerts: similarity_p99 >1s for 5 minutes, substructure_p99 >8s, connection_pool_utilization >85%.
devops.cost_controls: RDS r6g.8xlarge reserved instance ~$3500/month. Read replicas ~$2200 total. ElastiCache Redis ~$400. ECS Fargate at 3-20 tasks ~$600-$2000 depending on campaign load. Datadog ~$800. Total ~$7500-9500/month. Cost reduction: Redis cache target 40%+ hit rate reduces RDS query load; ECS scale-to-zero during non-campaign periods saves ~$800/month.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a high-performance cheminformatics API for 50M compound substructure and similarity search. Core performance decisions: pre-computed 2048-bit Morgan fingerprints in a PostgreSQL bit vector column with ivfflat ANN index for similarity; RDKit GiST mol index for substructure; RDS r6g.8xlarge with 256GB RAM to hold the full fingerprint index in memory; Redis caching keyed on canonical SMILES hash for repeat pipeline queries. Security requires query logs to omit client SMILES to prevent logging proprietary structures from client pipelines. JWT token claims include allowed_query_types to enforce tier-based access.
target_output.title: Cheminformatics Compound Library API v1
target_output.executive_summary: A high-performance REST API service for querying a 50 million compound library via Tanimoto similarity search, SMARTS substructure search, and chemical property retrieval. Built with Python FastAPI and PostgreSQL 16 with RDKit cartridge on an RDS r6g.8xlarge instance, deployed on ECS Fargate with ALB, and secured with JWT service tokens scoped by query type and team. Redis caches repeated similarity queries. Designed to replace direct JDBC connections from virtual screening pipelines with a governed, rate-limited API surface.
target_output.architecture_overview: Stateless FastAPI containers on ECS Fargate sit behind an ALB in three availability zones. Containers route query types to either the RDS primary (substructure, writes) or one of two read replicas (similarity, property lookups). PostgreSQL 16 with the RDKit cartridge indexes 50M compounds via a GiST mol index for substructure set operations and an ivfflat index on 2048-bit Morgan fingerprint bit vectors for approximate nearest neighbor similarity search. Redis 7 caches similarity and property query results keyed by canonical SMILES SHA-256 hash with 30-minute TTL, targeting 40%+ cache hit rate for repeated screening pipeline queries. JWT middleware validates service tokens, enforces allowed_query_types claims, and applies per-token rate limiting via Redis sliding window. No compound data is persisted in ECS containers; all compound structures reside exclusively in RDS.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, RDKit 2024.03 (Python bindings for in-process fingerprint computation), SQLAlchemy 2 with asyncpg driver. Database: PostgreSQL 16 RDS r6g.8xlarge (256GB RAM, Multi-AZ) with RDKit cartridge extension; 2 read replicas for non-write queries. Index: GiST on mol column (rdkit_mol_ops), ivfflat on morganbv_fp bit(2048) with 100 lists. Cache: ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster mode (3 shards, query cache + rate limiting). Auth: HS256 JWT with Redis revocation registry. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB, ECR, Secrets Manager. Observability: Datadog APM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Similarity Search: POST /api/v1/similarity with SMILES and Tanimoto threshold (0.4-1.0), returns top-N ranked hits with registry_id, smiles, similarity_score, and calculated properties; ANN search on ivfflat index with exact-match fallback at threshold >0.9. Substructure Search: POST /api/v1/substructure with SMARTS pattern, returns matching compound registry_ids with RDKit GiST index; SMARTS complexity guard rejects patterns above atom count threshold. Property Queries: GET /api/v1/compounds/{registry_id} returns mw, logP, tpsa, hba, hbd, rotatable_bonds for a single compound. Batch Lookup: POST /api/v1/compounds/batch with up to 1000 registry_ids or inchikeys for bulk property enrichment of hit lists. Library Stats: GET /api/v1/library/stats returns total compound count, active count, property distribution histograms (read-only, cached 1 hour).
target_output.system_components: ALB with HTTPS listener (TLS 1.3), forward rules to ECS Fargate target group. ECS Fargate service (3-20 tasks, auto-scaling on ALB request count). FastAPI application container with RDKit Python bindings (1 vCPU, 4GB, no persistent volume). RDS PostgreSQL 16 r6g.8xlarge Multi-AZ primary with rdkit cartridge extension. Two RDS read replicas (r6g.4xlarge) for similarity and property query routing. ElastiCache Redis 7 (3 shards, 2 replicas per shard) for result cache and rate limiting. AWS Secrets Manager for DB credentials, JWT signing key, Redis auth. ECR for FastAPI container images. Datadog Agent sidecar on each ECS task.
target_output.workflows: Similarity Query Pipeline: pipeline client POSTs to /api/v1/similarity with SMILES string and threshold; FastAPI middleware validates JWT, checks revocation in Redis, enforces allowed_query_types similarity claim, increments rate limit counter; query handler canonicalizes SMILES via RDKit, computes SHA-256 hash, checks Redis cache; on cache miss, computes Morgan fingerprint (radius 2, 2048 bits) in-process via RDKit Python bindings, queries read replica with SELECT registry_id, smiles, tanimoto_sml(morganbv_fp, query_fp::bfp) AS score FROM compounds WHERE morganbv_fp % query_fp ORDER BY score DESC LIMIT 100; caches result in Redis with 30-min TTL; returns ranked list. Substructure Query Pipeline: client POSTs SMARTS; FastAPI validates JWT and query type claim; SMARTS complexity checker counts atoms and rings, returns 400 if above threshold; FastAPI queries read replica (or primary for certain complex patterns) with SELECT registry_id, smiles FROM compounds WHERE mol @> query_mol; statement_timeout 8 seconds enforced per session; result capped at 500K via LIMIT; returns compound list with result count.
target_output.data_model: compounds (registry_id VARCHAR(20) PK, inchikey VARCHAR(27) UNIQUE NOT NULL, smiles TEXT NOT NULL, mol mol GENERATED ALWAYS AS (mol_from_smiles(smiles)) STORED, morganbv_fp bit(2048) GENERATED ALWAYS AS (morganbv_fp(mol_from_smiles(smiles), 2)) STORED, mw FLOAT, logp FLOAT, tpsa FLOAT, hba SMALLINT, hbd SMALLINT, rotatable_bonds SMALLINT, status ENUM active/deprecated, created_at TIMESTAMP, deprecated_at TIMESTAMP). Indexes: CREATE INDEX compounds_mol_gist ON compounds USING GIST (mol rdkit_mol_ops); CREATE INDEX compounds_fp_ivfflat ON compounds USING ivfflat (morganbv_fp bit_hamming_ops) WITH (lists=100). query_logs (id UUID PK, token_jti VARCHAR, token_team VARCHAR, query_type ENUM similarity/substructure/property/batch/stats, smiles_hash VARCHAR, result_count INT, cache_hit BOOL, latency_ms INT, executed_at TIMESTAMP). api_tokens (jti UUID PK, team_id VARCHAR, allowed_query_types TEXT[], rate_limit_tier ENUM standard/premium, issued_at, expires_at, revoked_at).
target_output.api_design: POST /api/v1/similarity body {smiles: str, threshold: float [0.4-1.0], radius: int [1-3] default 2, max_results: int [1-1000] default 100}. Response: {query_id, results: [{registry_id, smiles, similarity_score, mw, logp}], cached, latency_ms}. POST /api/v1/substructure body {smarts: str, max_results: int [1-500000] default 10000, include_properties: bool default false}. Response: {query_id, results: [{registry_id, smiles, ...properties if requested}], result_count, truncated: bool}. GET /api/v1/compounds/{registry_id} Response: {registry_id, inchikey, smiles, mw, logp, tpsa, hba, hbd, rotatable_bonds}. POST /api/v1/compounds/batch body {identifiers: str[], id_type: registry_id|inchikey, max_batch: 1000}. GET /api/v1/library/stats (admin token or read-only scope). Auth: Bearer JWT on all endpoints. Rate limits: standard tier 60 req/min similarity, 20 req/min substructure; premium tier 300 req/min similarity, 100 req/min substructure.
target_output.security_and_compliance: JWT service tokens with HS256 signing, 90-day expiry, jti claim checked against Redis revocation set on every request (O(1) Redis GET). Token claims include allowed_query_types[] and rate_limit_tier; FastAPI enforces query type against claim before executing. Per-token sliding window rate limiting in Redis using token jti as key. RDS connection: FastAPI ECS tasks connect with a read-only PostgreSQL user for similarity and property endpoints; substructure search routed through a separate connection string to primary with statement_timeout=8000ms per session. No bulk compound export: max_results cap 500K enforced at SQL LIMIT; no endpoint returns full compound structures in bulk. Query logs store smiles_hash (SHA-256 of canonicalized SMILES) not the raw SMILES string to avoid logging client proprietary compound structures. EBS KMS encryption on RDS and ElastiCache. TLS 1.3 at ALB with HTTP/2. Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager; no plaintext credentials in ECS task definitions. Compound export firewall: RDS IAM policy denies SELECT on compounds table without LIMIT clause (enforced via PostgreSQL row security policy requiring LIMIT < 500001).
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate service in us-east-1 (a/b/c) with 3 minimum tasks, scaling to 20 on ALB request count (scale out threshold: 1000 req/min, scale in: 300 req/min). Rolling update strategy for minor releases; blue-green weighted ALB for API version releases (v1 vs v2). RDS r6g.8xlarge Multi-AZ primary with automated failover. Two r6g.4xlarge read replicas with read routing in FastAPI SQLAlchemy engine (read-only user, replica endpoint). GitHub Actions CI: pytest with 10K compound fixture (includes substructure and similarity unit tests), mypy, 90% coverage gate, Bandit security scan, Docker build, ECR push. Staging deploy on merge to main; production blue-green deploy on tag. Database migrations: Alembic one-off ECS task; RDKit GiST and ivfflat index rebuild as separate CONCURRENTLY Job after schema migration to avoid table locks. Rollback: repoint ALB to previous task definition; prior ECR image retained 30 days. Secrets Manager rotation: 30-day rotation for RDS master password; JWT signing key rotation triggers token reissuance workflow for all pipeline teams.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM tracing all FastAPI endpoints with custom spans: rds_query_execution (query_type, replica/primary, rows_returned), redis_cache_lookup (hit/miss, key_pattern), smarts_complexity_check (atom_count, ring_count, rejected). Custom metrics: similarity_search_latency_p99_ms (SLA target 800ms), substructure_search_latency_p99_ms (SLA target 5000ms), query_cache_hit_rate_pct (target >40%), rds_read_replica_lag_seconds, rds_connection_pool_utilization_pct. Business metrics: queries_per_hour_by_type_and_team, rate_limit_triggers_per_team_per_day, slow_substructure_queries_count (latency >4s). PagerDuty: similarity_p99 >1s for 5 minutes (SLA breach risk), substructure_p99 >8s, connection_pool_utilization >85%, RDS read replica lag >30s.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $8200/month at steady state: RDS r6g.8xlarge reserved ($3500) + 2 read replicas r6g.4xlarge (~$2200 total) + ElastiCache Redis 3-shard cluster ($420) + ECS Fargate 3-8 avg tasks ($700) + ALB ($150) + Datadog APM ($800) + Secrets Manager and ECR ($100) + data transfer ($300). During peak virtual screening campaigns (20 tasks): ECS Fargate cost increases ~$1200. Redis cache hit rate >40% reduces RDS read replica query load by equivalent fraction. Scale ceiling: RDS r6g.8xlarge supports 50M compound fingerprint index in memory; scaling beyond 100M compounds would require partitioned tables or a dedicated vector similarity service.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): PostgreSQL 16 RDS with RDKit cartridge, 50M compound library loaded and indexed (GiST mol + ivfflat morganbv_fp), FastAPI with similarity, substructure, and compound property endpoints, JWT auth middleware with Redis revocation, per-token rate limiting, ECS Fargate with ALB, GitHub Actions CI with compound fixture tests, Datadog APM with p99 latency dashboards, Secrets Manager integration. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Read replica routing for similarity and property queries, Redis query result caching with canonical SMILES hash, batch compound lookup endpoint, SMARTS complexity guard, blue-green deployment pipeline for API version releases, library stats endpoint, query log analysis dashboards per team. Phase 3 (4 weeks): ivfflat index tuning based on production query patterns, exact-match fallback for high-threshold similarity, token management self-service for pipeline teams, SLA reporting dashboard for stakeholders.
target_output.development_guidelines: RDKit version pinned in Dockerfile to match PostgreSQL RDKit cartridge version exactly - version mismatch causes silent fingerprint encoding incompatibility. pytest fixtures use a 10K compound subset loaded into a test RDS instance via Alembic migrations; similarity and substructure tests assert result counts and top-hit registry_ids against known ground truth. mypy strict mode with RDKit type stubs. 90% coverage gate on API route handlers and query service layer. No raw SMILES strings in application logs; log smiles_hash only. API versioning via URL prefix /api/v1; breaking changes require /api/v2 with deprecation notice to pipeline teams 60 days in advance. Statement timeout and max_results cap verified via integration test that submits a deliberately broad SMARTS and asserts 504 response at 8-second mark.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: ivfflat ANN index recall below 0.95 at Tanimoto threshold >0.9. Mitigation: exact-match sequential scan on top-1000 ANN candidates when threshold >0.9; tested in Phase 2 validation suite. Risk: SMARTS complexity attack causing DB connection exhaustion. Mitigation: SMARTS complexity score heuristic (atom count >50 or ring count >6 rejected with 400) plus statement_timeout. Risk: RDKit cartridge version pinning creates friction for PostgreSQL major version upgrades. Mitigation: document cartridge-PostgreSQL version matrix in runbook; test upgrade path in staging before each RDS engine upgrade. Tradeoff: ivfflat over HNSW for similarity ANN; ivfflat supported natively by pgvector on PostgreSQL 16 without additional extensions; HNSW would give better recall-speed tradeoff at the cost of an additional extension dependency.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: RDS r6g.8xlarge (256GB RAM) confirmed sufficient to hold 50M * 2048-bit fingerprints (12.8GB) plus mol column and property indexes in memory with headroom. ivfflat with 100 lists confirmed as the similarity index; probes=10 delivers 0.97 recall at p99 <600ms on benchmark queries against a 50M compound test dataset. Query log design confirmed to store smiles_hash not raw SMILES to protect client compound IP. Rate limit tiers confirmed as standard (60 similarity/min) and premium (300 similarity/min) based on pipeline team SLA requirements.

metadata.notes: Strong first-pass plan for high-performance cheminformatics API. Key decisions: RDKit cartridge ivfflat index on 50M Morgan fingerprints, query log SMILES hashing for IP protection, JWT scoped by query type.

project_goal.value: Replace a monolithic Perl-based compound registration system with a modular Go REST API providing versioned endpoints for structure validation, salt stripping, and registry ID assignment
project_goal.rationale: Eliminates decades of Perl technical debt while preserving all registration business logic in discrete testable units

target_users.value: Chemistry informaticians and downstream microservices consuming registration events via REST
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers are automated synthesis pipelines and chemistry design tooling

project_class.rationale: Pure service exposing compound registration logic with no UI component

capabilities.rationale: REST API with structured data access, JWT auth, and versioned public endpoints

complexity_level.rationale: Chemical structure normalization logic (salt stripping, InChIKey deduplication) is domain-complex; parallel traffic shadowing during cutover adds operational complexity

risk_level.rationale: Incorrect registry ID assignment or duplicate compound registration errors would corrupt the compound master database used across all research programs

data_sensitivity.rationale: Compound structures represent core pharmaceutical IP; registry IDs link to proprietary drug discovery programs

external_exposure.rationale: Service is consumed exclusively by internal chemistry informatics systems over corporate VPN

access_model.value: JWT bearer tokens issued per service client with compound registration and read-only scopes; no interactive user login
access_model.rationale: Machine-to-machine API with scoped token access per consuming service

feature_scope.value: Structure validation endpoint, salt stripping and parent structure normalization, InChIKey-based duplicate detection, registry ID assignment, registration event log, shadow mode for parallel migration testing
feature_scope.rationale: Full scope required to achieve parity with legacy Perl system before cutover

mvp_scope.value: Structure validation, salt stripping, InChIKey deduplication, and registry ID assignment endpoints with shadow mode producing side-by-side comparison logs
mvp_scope.rationale: Shadow mode is critical MVP requirement to validate parity before decommissioning Perl system

security_baseline.value: JWT authentication for all endpoints, TLS 1.3 in transit, no compound structure logging to stdout, registry DB accessed via IAM role-restricted credentials
security_baseline.rationale: Compound IP must not leak through logs or misconfigured access paths

frontend_stack.rationale: No UI component; consumed programmatically by downstream services

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Chi router, RDKit via CGo bindings for cheminformatics operations
backend_stack.rationale: Go provides low-latency handling for concurrent registration requests; RDKit CGo bindings give access to production-grade cheminformatics algorithms

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with RDKit cartridge for mol column storage; Redis 7 for duplicate-check result caching
data_platform.rationale: RDKit cartridge enables native SMILES indexing; Redis caches InChIKey lookups to reduce DB load during bulk registrations

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate with ALB; Buildkite CI/CD; New Relic APM
hosting_target.rationale: Managed container runtime matching existing informatics platform standard

privacy_retention_policy.value: Registration event logs retained 10 years per compound IP governance SOP; Redis cache TTL 24 hours
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Long-term retention required for patent defense and regulatory submission traceability

future_scope.value: Batch registration endpoint for bulk CSV uploads, webhook notifications for downstream system integration, compound structure correction workflows
future_scope.rationale: Batch mode needed for legacy data migration; webhooks enable event-driven downstream processing

constraints.value: Must maintain sub-200ms p95 latency for single-compound registration; shadow mode must run without degrading Perl system performance; Go service must reproduce Perl salt stripping rules exactly
constraints.rationale: Latency SLA required for real-time chemistry design tool integration; exact parity needed for safe migration

observability_baseline.value: New Relic APM with distributed tracing; structured JSON logs with compound registry ID (never raw SMILES); custom metrics: registration throughput, duplicate detection rate, shadow discrepancy rate
observability_baseline.rationale: Shadow discrepancy rate is the primary migration confidence metric

execution_preference.value: Incremental migration: shadow mode for 4 weeks, traffic split 10/90 then 50/50 then full cutover
execution_preference.rationale: Staged migration with discrepancy monitoring before committing to full cutover

compliance_context.value: Internal compound IP governance SOP, structure registration SOP mandating audit trail for all registry ID assignments
compliance_context.rationale: Risk level and confidential IP require documented audit trail for all registration events

project.goal: Modular Go REST API replacing Perl compound registration monolith
project.target_users: Chemistry informatics services and synthesis pipeline automation
project.timeline: 4 weeks shadow mode, 8 weeks to full cutover

backend.framework: Chi router with custom middleware chain
backend.api: REST with versioned prefixes /v1/ and /v2/ during migration

security.auth: JWT bearer tokens with registration and read-only scopes
security.secrets: AWS Secrets Manager for DB credentials

data.cache: Redis 7 for InChIKey duplicate cache TTL 24h

devops.ci: Buildkite with Go test and golangci-lint gates
devops.environments: dev, staging, production with shadow mode flag

constraints.parity: Go and Perl must produce identical registry IDs for identical inputs

reasoner_reviews.product: The shadow mode requirement is the correct migration strategy for a compound registration system where any discrepancy would corrupt the master registry. Parity validation logs should be stored for at least the full shadow period to allow forensic comparison.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: RDKit CGo bindings in Go are production-tested at several pharma informatics shops. The salt stripping rules from the Perl system must be reverse-engineered and encoded as unit-testable pure functions before coding the Go equivalent. InChIKey-based dedup is the right approach for canonical structure matching across different SMILES representations of the same compound.
reasoner_reviews.security: JWT scopes should distinguish read-only (informatics dashboards) from registration (synthesis pipelines) to prevent unauthorized registry ID assignment. SMILES must never appear in logs or New Relic traces - use registry IDs and InChIKey fragments only.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Sub-200ms p95 for a single-compound registration including salt stripping and InChIKey lookup is achievable if duplicate checks hit Redis before PostgreSQL. The CGo RDKit call for standardization typically takes 5-20ms, leaving budget for DB round-trip.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The plan must address what happens when Go and Perl produce different registry IDs during shadow mode: define a discrepancy threshold (e.g., >0.1% triggers halt) and an escalation path before cutover proceeds.

backend.service_design: Three handler groups: /v1/compounds/validate (SMILES syntax and valence check), /v1/compounds/normalize (salt strip, standardize, return parent InChIKey), /v1/compounds/register (full pipeline: validate, normalize, dedup, assign ID, log event). Shadow mode middleware intercepts all register calls, fans out to both Go handler and Perl proxy, logs discrepancies asynchronously.
backend.api_patterns: Versioned routing under /v1/ with /v2/ stubs for future batch endpoints. All endpoints return structured JSON with field: compound_id, inchikey, action (registered/duplicate), registry_id, salt_stripped_smiles. Error payloads include error_code and detail fields.
backend.failure_handling: Perl shadow proxy failures are logged but do not fail Go requests. PostgreSQL write failures trigger rollback and return 503. Duplicate check Redis failures fall through to PostgreSQL to preserve correctness at cost of latency.
backend.backend_risks: CGo build complexity increases CI time and complicates cross-compilation. Mitigation: pre-built RDKit shared libraries in Docker image.

security.auth_design: JWT HS256 tokens with iss, sub (service name), scope (registration or read_only), exp. Tokens issued by internal secrets service with 1-hour TTL and refresh via client credentials flow.
security.secrets_management: PostgreSQL credentials and JWT signing key in AWS Secrets Manager, rotated every 30 days. CGo RDKit library path is baked into Docker image, not runtime config.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every successful registry ID assignment writes to registration_events table with timestamp, client_id, input_inchikey, assigned_registry_id, salt_stripped_flag. Table is append-only with no UPDATE or DELETE grants on the application role.

data.entities: compounds (registry_id SERIAL PK, inchikey VARCHAR(27) UNIQUE, parent_smiles TEXT, mol mol, registered_at TIMESTAMPTZ, registered_by VARCHAR client_id). registration_events (id BIGSERIAL PK, registry_id INT FK, client_id VARCHAR, action ENUM registered/duplicate, input_inchikey VARCHAR, salt_stripped BOOL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). salts (id SERIAL PK, salt_smiles TEXT, salt_inchikey VARCHAR, name VARCHAR, active BOOL). shadow_discrepancies (id BIGSERIAL PK, input_smiles TEXT, go_registry_id INT, perl_registry_id INT, discrepancy_type VARCHAR, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL mol column with GiST index on RDKit cartridge for substructure queries. Separate schema compound_registry with application role compound_api granted SELECT/INSERT on compounds and registration_events only.
data.schema_notes: InChIKey uniqueness constraint on compounds table is the deduplication enforcement mechanism. salt_stripped flag in registration_events enables audit of how often salt stripping changed the parent structure.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate service behind internal ALB. Two task variants: go-api (serves all traffic) and perl-proxy (receives shadow requests from go-api middleware). Perl proxy is a lightweight HTTP wrapper around the existing Perl registration scripts.
devops.ci_cd_design: Buildkite pipeline: go test ./... with -race flag, golangci-lint, Docker build with RDKit shared libs, push to ECR, deploy to ECS with blue-green and health check gate. Shadow discrepancy Grafana dashboard reviewed before each traffic percentage increase.
devops.rollback_strategy: Traffic split managed via ALB weighted target groups. Rollback is weight reversal to 0% Go / 100% Perl, executable in under 2 minutes without code change.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a migration of a critical compound registration system - correctness is paramount over performance. Key architectural decision: shadow mode middleware must be the first thing built to enable safe incremental cutover. RDKit CGo bindings are the right call since the cheminformatics logic (salt stripping, InChIKey generation) must exactly replicate the Perl behavior. The registration_events table must be append-only to satisfy the compound IP governance SOP audit trail requirement.
target_output.title: Compound Registration API - Perl-to-Go Migration Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A modular Go 1.22 REST API that replaces a monolithic Perl compound registration system, exposing versioned endpoints for SMILES validation, salt stripping, InChIKey-based deduplication, and registry ID assignment. A traffic shadowing architecture runs both systems in parallel during a 4-week validation period, with discrepancy monitoring as the primary migration gate. The service runs on AWS ECS Fargate behind an internal ALB with PostgreSQL 16 (RDKit cartridge) and Redis 7 for duplicate caching.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-purpose API service with three layered processing stages: (1) Validation - SMILES syntax and valence check via RDKit CGo binding; (2) Normalization - salt stripping using the internal salt table and parent structure standardization to canonical SMILES with InChIKey generation; (3) Registration - dedup check against compounds table InChIKey unique index (Redis L1 cache then PostgreSQL L2), followed by registry ID assignment and append-only event log write. A shadow mode middleware layer intercepts all /register requests during migration, fans each out to both the Go handler and a Perl proxy container, and logs any output discrepancies to the shadow_discrepancies table. Discrepancy rate is exposed as a New Relic metric and must stay below 0.1% before traffic split advances.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22 with Chi router v5, RDKit 2024.03 via CGo bindings (pre-compiled shared library in Docker image), golang-migrate for schema management, pgx/v5 for PostgreSQL driver. Database: PostgreSQL 16 with RDKit cartridge extension, compound_registry schema with least-privilege application role. Cache: Redis 7 (ElastiCache) for InChIKey-to-registry-ID lookups with 24h TTL. Infrastructure: AWS ECS Fargate (go-api and perl-proxy task definitions), internal Application Load Balancer, ECR for images, Secrets Manager for credentials. CI/CD: Buildkite with race-condition testing and golangci-lint. Observability: New Relic APM with custom metrics for shadow discrepancy rate.
target_output.functional_feature_map: SMILES Validation (/v1/compounds/validate): Accepts SMILES string, returns valence check result, atom count, and detected charge states. Salt Stripping (/v1/compounds/normalize): Identifies and removes known salts by matching fragment InChIKeys against the salts table, returns parent_smiles and parent_inchikey. Compound Registration (/v1/compounds/register): Full pipeline including validation, normalization, dedup (Redis then DB), and registry ID assignment or duplicate record return. Registry ID Lookup (/v1/compounds/:registry_id): Returns compound metadata without SMILES in response body. Shadow Mode Control (internal /admin/shadow): Enables or disables shadow fanout and sets discrepancy halt threshold.
target_output.system_components: Go API service (ECS Fargate, 2-4 tasks, auto-scaling on request count), Perl Proxy service (ECS Fargate, 1-2 tasks, receives shadow requests only), Internal ALB (weighted target groups for traffic splitting), PostgreSQL 16 RDKit cartridge (RDS Multi-AZ, compound_registry schema), Redis 7 (ElastiCache, InChIKey dedup cache), Secrets Manager (DB credentials and JWT signing key), New Relic APM (distributed traces, custom metrics), Buildkite CI (test, lint, build, deploy pipeline).
target_output.workflows: Compound Registration Flow: Synthesis pipeline POSTs {smiles, client_id} to /v1/compounds/register with JWT bearer token. Chi middleware validates JWT scope=registration. Handler calls RDKit CGo validate(), then normalize() to produce parent InChIKey. Redis GET inchikey:{inchikey} checked first; cache hit returns existing registry_id with action=duplicate. Cache miss queries compounds table by inchikey; if found, populates Redis and returns duplicate. If novel, INSERT into compounds with parent_smiles and mol column, assigns registry_id, INSERTs registration_events record, sets Redis key, returns {registry_id, action=registered, inchikey}. Shadow Discrepancy Flow: Shadow middleware clones request to Perl proxy via internal HTTP call with 500ms timeout. Perl response and Go response are compared field-by-field; any mismatch is written to shadow_discrepancies with input SMILES, both output registry_ids, and discrepancy_type. Discrepancy rate metric is emitted to New Relic every 60 seconds. If rate exceeds 0.1%, a PagerDuty alert fires and traffic split advancement is blocked.
target_output.data_model: compounds (registry_id SERIAL PK, inchikey VARCHAR(27) UNIQUE NOT NULL, parent_smiles TEXT NOT NULL, mol mol NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), registered_by VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL). registration_events (id BIGSERIAL PK, registry_id INT NOT NULL REFERENCES compounds, client_id VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL, action VARCHAR(16) CHECK action IN ('registered','duplicate'), input_inchikey VARCHAR(27), salt_stripped BOOL DEFAULT false, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()). salts (id SERIAL PK, salt_smiles TEXT NOT NULL, salt_inchikey VARCHAR(27) UNIQUE NOT NULL, common_name VARCHAR, active BOOL DEFAULT true). shadow_discrepancies (id BIGSERIAL PK, input_smiles TEXT NOT NULL, go_registry_id INT, perl_registry_id INT, discrepancy_type VARCHAR(64), recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()). GiST index on compounds.mol for cartridge substructure queries. registration_events and shadow_discrepancies are append-only; application role lacks UPDATE/DELETE grants.
target_output.api_design: POST /v1/compounds/validate - body: {smiles}; returns {valid:bool, error?:string, atom_count:int}. POST /v1/compounds/normalize - body: {smiles}; returns {parent_smiles, parent_inchikey, salt_stripped:bool, salts_removed:[]}. POST /v1/compounds/register - body: {smiles, source_system?}; returns {registry_id:int, inchikey:string, action:'registered'|'duplicate', parent_smiles, salt_stripped:bool}. GET /v1/compounds/:registry_id - returns {registry_id, inchikey, registered_at, registered_by} (no SMILES in GET response per IP policy). GET /v1/compounds?inchikey={inchikey} - lookup by InChIKey for downstream dedup checks. All endpoints require Authorization: Bearer {jwt}. 429 returned when per-client rate limit (1000 req/min) exceeded.
target_output.security_and_compliance: JWT HS256 tokens with scope claim (registration or read_only) validated on every request. Tokens issued by internal auth service with 1-hour TTL; Chi middleware rejects expired or wrong-scope tokens with 401/403. SMILES strings are never written to logs, New Relic traces, or error messages - only registry_id and InChIKey fragments appear in observability data. PostgreSQL application role compound_api has SELECT and INSERT on compounds and registration_events; no UPDATE, DELETE, or DDL grants. Secrets Manager credentials rotated every 30 days. TLS 1.3 enforced on ALB listener. IP governance SOP satisfied by append-only registration_events table covering all registry ID assignments with client attribution.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate task definitions: go-api (1 vCPU, 2GB, min 2 max 4 tasks, scale on ALB RequestCountPerTarget > 500), perl-proxy (0.5 vCPU, 1GB, min 1 max 2 tasks, shadow traffic only). Buildkite pipeline: go test ./... -race, golangci-lint, docker build (RDKit shared libs pre-staged in base image), docker push ECR, ECS rolling update with health check on GET /health. Migration traffic schedule: Week 1-4 shadow mode (100% Perl + shadow Go), Week 5 10% Go / 90% Perl, Week 7 50/50, Week 9 full Go if discrepancy rate <0.1%. Environments: dev (single Fargate task, no shadow), staging (shadow enabled, 1 week soak), production (shadow then graduated cutover). Rollback: ALB weighted target group weight reversal to 0/100 in under 2 minutes via CLI script.
target_output.observability: New Relic APM with Go agent for distributed tracing. Custom metrics: compound_registration_throughput (registrations/min), shadow_discrepancy_rate (%), redis_dedup_hit_rate (%), rdk_normalization_latency_ms (p50/p95/p99). Structured JSON logs with fields: request_id, client_id, registry_id, action, latency_ms - SMILES excluded. PagerDuty alerts: shadow_discrepancy_rate > 0.1% (blocks cutover), p95_registration_latency > 200ms, PostgreSQL connection pool exhaustion.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate: ~$180/month for go-api (2 tasks baseline), ~$60/month for perl-proxy during shadow period. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ: ~$400/month (db.r6g.large). ElastiCache Redis: ~$80/month (cache.r6g.small). Total: ~$720/month in shadow phase, ~$580/month after Perl decommission. Auto-scaling keeps go-api tasks between 2 and 4 based on request rate; RDKit CGo operations are CPU-bound so scaling target set at 70% CPU.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (Weeks 1-3): Implement salt stripping logic with unit tests against Perl system output, InChIKey dedup, registry ID assignment, append-only registration_events table, JWT middleware. Phase 2 (Weeks 4-5): Build shadow mode middleware, Perl proxy container, discrepancy logging to shadow_discrepancies, New Relic custom metrics for discrepancy rate. Deploy to staging with shadow enabled. Phase 3 (Weeks 6-9): Production shadow deployment for 4 weeks with weekly discrepancy reports reviewed by informatics team. Phase 4 (Weeks 10-12): Graduated traffic cutover (10%, 50%, 100%) with rollback plan active. Perl system decommission after 4 weeks of clean production operation at 100%.
target_output.development_guidelines: All cheminformatics logic (salt stripping, InChIKey generation) in pure functions with table-driven unit tests using known Perl system input/output pairs. No SMILES in any log.Printf or error string. golangci-lint with errcheck, gosec, and staticcheck enabled. Pull requests require 2 approvals including one from the cheminformatics SME who validated the salt stripping parity. Integration tests run against a PostgreSQL+RDKit Docker container in Buildkite. Go race detector enabled in all CI test runs.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: CGo RDKit bindings complicate Docker image builds and cross-compilation. Mitigation: pre-built RDKit shared libraries in a versioned base image maintained by DevOps. Risk: Salt stripping rule discrepancies between Perl and Go cause shadow mismatches. Mitigation: unit test every salt in the salts table against Perl output before shadow mode begins. Risk: Redis cache stale InChIKey entries allowing duplicate registrations after compound record correction. Mitigation: Redis TTL set to 24h and cache invalidation endpoint added for admin use. Tradeoff: append-only registration_events prevents retroactive correction of erroneous registrations - corrections require new registration events with explicit superseded_id reference.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Chi over Gin: Chi's net/http compatibility simplifies middleware composition for shadow fanout. Confirmed RDKit CGo over python subprocess: CGo eliminates IPC overhead keeping p95 under 200ms. Shadow discrepancy halt threshold set at 0.1% based on historical Perl system error rate analysis. Registry ID sequence starting point set to current Perl max + 1,000,000 to avoid collisions during shadow period.

metadata.notes: Perl-to-Go compound registration migration with shadow mode architecture and cheminformatics-specific dedup logic.

profile.domain: Pharmaceutical Informatics - Open Bioactivity Data SDK

project_goal.value: Python SDK providing a unified query interface for ChEMBL, BindingDB, and PubChem BioAssay with consistent BioactivityRecord data models, async bulk download, and pandas DataFrame output
project_goal.rationale: Informatics scientists waste time writing bespoke client code for each public bioactivity database with incompatible response formats

target_users.value: Pharmaceutical informatics engineers and computational biologists querying public bioactivity databases for target validation and ADMET model training
target_users.rationale: Primary users are Python-literate scientists integrating bioactivity data into ML pipelines

project_class.rationale: Pure Python package with no hosted service component; distributed via PyPI

capabilities.rationale: Library provides data access logic, source integration adapters, query/transform functions, and DataFrame output

complexity_level.rationale: Three distinct API source adapters with different pagination models; async bulk download adds moderate design complexity

risk_level.rationale: Wraps public read-only APIs; no sensitive data stored or processed by the library itself

data_sensitivity.rationale: All data comes from public databases; no proprietary compound data handled by the library

external_exposure.rationale: SDK is published to PyPI and calls public database REST APIs on behalf of the user

access_model.value: No authentication in the SDK; users provide optional API keys for ChEMBL/PubChem premium tiers via environment variables or SDK config object
access_model.rationale: Public APIs do not require auth at standard rate limits; key injection allows power users to avoid throttling

feature_scope.value: ChEMBL adapter, BindingDB adapter, PubChem BioAssay adapter, unified BioactivityRecord pydantic model, target query by UniProt ID, bulk download with async pagination, optional local diskcache, pandas DataFrame output, CLI convenience commands
feature_scope.rationale: Full adapter coverage and consistent output format are the core value proposition

mvp_scope.value: ChEMBL and PubChem BioAssay adapters with UniProt-based target query, BioactivityRecord model, pandas DataFrame output, and async bulk download as v0.1
mvp_scope.rationale: ChEMBL and PubChem cover the highest-priority use cases; BindingDB adapter deferred to v0.2

security_baseline.value: No credential storage in the library; API keys passed at runtime only; HTTPS enforced for all upstream calls; no telemetry or usage data collection
security_baseline.rationale: SDK must not introduce credential leakage risk or unexpected network calls

frontend_stack.rationale: library_sdk project class with no UI component

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 (compatible 3.10+), httpx 0.27 for async HTTP, pydantic v2 for data models and validation, pandas 2.x for DataFrame output, tenacity for retry logic
backend_stack.rationale: httpx provides sync and async HTTP with identical API; pydantic v2 gives fast validation with JSON schema export for documentation

data_platform.value: No persistent database; optional local diskcache (SQLite-backed) for repeated queries; pandas DataFrames for in-memory result handling
data_platform.rationale: Library must work without any server infrastructure; diskcache is opt-in with lazy import

hosting_target.value: PyPI distribution via GitHub Actions trusted publisher; Read the Docs for API documentation
hosting_target.rationale: Standard open-source Python library distribution channel

privacy_retention_policy.value: No user data retained by the library; local diskcache stores only public bioactivity data and is entirely user-controlled
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: All queried data is public domain; no retention obligations on the library

future_scope.value: BindingDB adapter, async streaming for >1M record result sets, compound structure fetching alongside activity data, polars DataFrame output option
future_scope.rationale: Growing demand for larger bulk downloads and structure-activity paired exports for ML training

constraints.value: Python 3.10+ compatibility; zero mandatory runtime dependencies outside httpx, pydantic, pandas; wheel size under 500KB; standard queries return within 10 seconds
constraints.rationale: Lightweight dependency footprint is essential for adoption in constrained ML pipeline environments

observability_baseline.value: Optional debug logging via Python standard logging module; no third-party monitoring library; GitHub Actions test matrix for Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12
observability_baseline.rationale: Library observability is user-controlled via standard logging; CI matrix validates cross-version compatibility

execution_preference.value: Iterative: ChEMBL+PubChem adapters first, publish to PyPI as v0.1, BindingDB adapter in v0.2 after community feedback
execution_preference.rationale: Early PyPI release enables community feedback before completing all three adapters

project.goal: Unified Python SDK for public bioactivity database queries with consistent data model
project.target_users: Pharma informatics engineers and computational biologists
