project.timeline: v0.1 with ChEMBL+PubChem in 6 weeks; v0.2 with BindingDB in 10 weeks

security.api_keys: environment variable injection only, never stored in SDK

constraints.no_heavy_deps: no rdkit or scipy in core

reasoner_reviews.product: The unified BioactivityRecord data model is the SDK's core value. It must capture the superset of fields across all three source databases and use None for fields unavailable from a given source, so scientists get consistent DataFrame column names when merging results from ChEMBL and PubChem for ML training dataset assembly.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The adapter pattern is correct for three databases with different pagination schemes: ChEMBL uses offset/limit, PubChem BioAssay uses stateful listkeys with sequential fetch, BindingDB uses cursor-based pagination. Each adapter should implement a common AbstractAdapter ABC. httpx AsyncClient should be instantiated once per query session and reused across pagination calls to avoid TCP connection overhead on large bulk downloads.
reasoner_reviews.security: API keys must only flow in at initialization time, stored only in private adapter attributes, and never serialized in __repr__ output. The __repr__ of any config or client object must redact key values to prevent accidental logging.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The zero-mandatory-dependency constraint is achievable since httpx, pydantic, and pandas are all lightweight. The optional diskcache dependency must be behind a try/except import so the library works without it installed.

backend.service_design: Package layout: bioactivity_sdk/ with adapters/ (chembl.py, pubchem.py, bindingdb.py each implementing AbstractAdapter), models/ (bioactivity_record.py, query_filters.py), client.py (BioactivityClient facade routing queries and merging results), cache.py (optional diskcache wrapper returning no-op CacheLayer if diskcache not installed).
backend.api_patterns: Fluent query builder: BioactivityQuery().target('P00533').activity_type('IC50').potency_max_nm(1000).sources(['chembl','pubchem']). Sync execution via client.query(q) wrapping asyncio.run(). Async execution via await client.aquery(q). Bulk streaming via async for batch in client.abulk_download(q, batch_size=5000). All adapters yield BioactivityRecord instances via async generators; pagination is fully internal.
backend.failure_handling: tenacity retry with exponential backoff (max 5 attempts, 1s initial wait, jitter) on HTTP 429 and 5xx responses from upstream APIs. Each adapter raises SourceUnavailableError on persistent failure; BulkDownloadResult collects adapter errors alongside successful records so partial results are usable.
backend.backend_risks: PubChem BioAssay listkey pagination is stateful server-side and expires after 10 minutes, breaking long-running bulk downloads. Mitigation: checkpoint current page index to diskcache if available to allow resume on failure.

data.entities: BioactivityRecord (pydantic BaseModel): source Literal['chembl','pubchem','bindingdb'], target_uniprot str, compound_chembl_id Optional[str], compound_pubchem_cid Optional[int], smiles Optional[str], activity_type str, activity_value_nm Optional[float] (all coerced to nM by adapter), activity_unit_original Optional[str], assay_id str, assay_description Optional[str], confidence_score Optional[int], relation Optional[str] for inequality operators, data_quality_flag Optional[str] from ChEMBL data_validity_comment, published_at Optional[date]. QueryFilters: target_uniprot str, activity_types list[str], potency_max_nm Optional[float], potency_min_nm Optional[float], sources list[Literal[...]].
data.schema_notes: BioactivityRecord uses pydantic field_validator to normalize all activity values from reported units (uM, nM, pIC50) to nM before storage. ChEMBL standard_value and PubChem ActivityValue map to the same activity_value_nm field via adapter-level transformation.
data.data_risks: Public databases have inconsistent data quality; erroneous IC50 values (e.g., reported as 0 or missing units) should not silently produce NaN activity_value_nm rows. Mitigation: validator logs a warning at debug level and sets data_quality_flag='coercion_failed' rather than raising.

devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions workflow: matrix test on Python 3.10/3.11/3.12 on ubuntu-latest, pytest with pytest-asyncio and responses HTTP mocking (no live API calls in CI), mypy strict type checking, 90% coverage gate via pytest-cov + codecov. PyPI publish job on tag push via trusted publisher (no stored API token).
devops.rollback_strategy: Semantic versioning with no-break promise for MINOR/PATCH releases. Breaking adapter API changes (e.g., renamed BioactivityRecord fields) require MAJOR version bump and CHANGELOG entry. PyPI does not allow re-publishing the same version; broken releases are yanked and a patch version published.
devops.devops_risks: ChEMBL or PubChem API schema changes can break adapters silently if pydantic strict mode rejects unknown fields. Mitigation: pydantic model_config extra='ignore' with debug-level log for unexpected fields; weekly scheduled CI run against live APIs.

target_output.thinking_summary: Library SDK means no hosted infrastructure - all design decisions are about the package API surface, adapter pattern, and data model. The BioactivityRecord unified model must be the superset of all three database schemas with field-level unit coercion to nM. Async-first with sync wrapper serves both Jupyter (sync convenience) and pipeline (async throughput) use cases. The zero-mandatory-dep constraint must hold: httpx, pydantic, pandas are the only required imports.
target_output.title: BioactivitySDK - Unified Public Bioactivity Database Python Library
target_output.executive_summary: A pure Python 3.12 library (compatible with Python 3.10+) providing a unified interface for querying ChEMBL, BindingDB, and PubChem BioAssay with a consistent BioactivityRecord pydantic data model and pandas DataFrame output. An adapter pattern with AbstractAdapter ABC handles source-specific pagination differences; a BioactivityClient facade exposes sync and async query paths with transparent pagination. Optional diskcache integration avoids redundant API calls during iterative analysis sessions. Distributed via PyPI with no required dependencies beyond httpx, pydantic v2, and pandas.
target_output.architecture_overview: Package library architecture with no hosted service. BioactivityClient facade accepts BioactivityQuery objects and dispatches to source-specific adapters (ChEMBLAdapter using offset/limit pagination, PubChemBioAssayAdapter using stateful listkeys, BindingDBAdapter using cursor-based pagination), all implementing AbstractAdapter ABC with paginate(), fetch_by_target(), and fetch_bulk() abstract methods. Adapters share a single httpx.AsyncClient instance per query session to avoid TCP connection overhead. All adapters normalize source-specific field names and coerce activity values to nanomolar units before yielding BioactivityRecord instances. An optional CacheLayer using diskcache (SQLite-backed) transparently caches adapter output keyed by query hash; if diskcache is not installed, CacheLayer degrades to a no-op stub without raising errors.
target_output.technology_stack: Language: Python 3.12, compatible with 3.10+. HTTP: httpx 0.27 with AsyncClient reuse per session. Validation: pydantic v2 with field_validator for nM coercion. DataFrame output: pandas 2.x with model_dump() conversion. Retry: tenacity 9.x, exponential backoff with jitter, max 5 attempts on 429/5xx. Optional cache: diskcache 5.x with lazy import guard. Packaging: pyproject.toml with hatch build backend, semantic versioning. Publishing: GitHub Actions PyPI trusted publisher (no stored credentials). Documentation: Sphinx with autodoc and Jupyter notebook examples on Read the Docs.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Target-Based Query: query ChEMBL and/or PubChem BioAssay by UniProt accession ID, returning all measured bioactivity records for compounds tested against that target. Activity Filtering: filter results by activity type (IC50, Ki, EC50), nanomolar potency range, and ChEMBL assay confidence score. Bulk Async Download: async generator interface yielding configurable batch sizes for complete target bioactivity sets, with progress callback support and partial result return on source errors. DataFrame Export: client.to_dataframe(records) returns pandas DataFrame with normalized columns (source, target_uniprot, activity_value_nm, assay_id) regardless of source database. Local Cache: opt-in per-query caching with configurable TTL via .with_cache(ttl_seconds=3600) on the query builder. Multi-Source Parallel Dispatch: queries multiple sources concurrently via asyncio.gather and merges deduplicated records.
target_output.system_components: BioactivityClient (public facade, async query dispatch, result collection and deduplication), BioactivityQuery (fluent builder with pydantic validation of filter parameters), AbstractAdapter (ABC enforcing paginate/fetch_by_target/fetch_bulk interface), ChEMBLAdapter (ChEMBL REST API v6, offset/limit pagination, confidence score filtering), PubChemBioAssayAdapter (PubChem BioAssay REST API, listkey pagination with server-side expiry handling), BindingDBAdapter (BindingDB REST API, cursor pagination - v0.2), BioactivityRecord (pydantic BaseModel with nM coercion validator), CacheLayer (diskcache wrapper, no-op if library absent), RetryTransport (httpx transport subclass integrating tenacity retry for upstream 429/5xx).
target_output.workflows: Standard Target Query: scientist calls client.query(BioactivityQuery().target('P00533').activity_type('IC50').sources(['chembl'])). Client instantiates ChEMBLAdapter with shared httpx.AsyncClient. Adapter calls /activity.json?target_chembl_id=CHEMBL1824&standard_type=IC50&limit=1000, paginates via offset increment until no next page URL, coerces each standard_value from reported unit to nM, yields BioactivityRecord. Client collects into list and returns. CacheLayer checks cache key before adapter dispatch and stores result after. Bulk Download: scientist calls async for batch in client.abulk_download(query, batch_size=5000, on_progress=lambda n: print(f'Fetched {n} records')). Adapter yields batches via async generator. PubChem listkey expiry is handled by re-initiating the listkey request if server returns 404 on sequential fetch after 10 minutes. tenacity retries 429 responses with Retry-After header parsed for wait duration. BulkDownloadResult accumulates all records and any SourceUnavailableError instances.
target_output.data_model: BioactivityRecord (pydantic BaseModel): source: Literal['chembl','pubchem','bindingdb'], target_uniprot: str, compound_chembl_id: Optional[str]=None, compound_pubchem_cid: Optional[int]=None, smiles: Optional[str]=None, activity_type: str, activity_value_nm: Optional[float]=None, activity_unit_original: Optional[str]=None, assay_id: str, assay_description: Optional[str]=None, assay_type: Optional[str]=None, confidence_score: Optional[int]=None, relation: Optional[str]=None (operator string '>','<','='), data_quality_flag: Optional[str]=None, published_at: Optional[date]=None. @field_validator('activity_value_nm') coerces uM to nM (multiply by 1000), pIC50 to nM (10^(9-pIC50)), logs warning and sets data_quality_flag='coercion_failed' if unit unrecognized. QueryFilters: target_uniprot str, activity_types list[str] default=['IC50'], potency_max_nm Optional[float], potency_min_nm Optional[float], sources list default=['chembl']. BulkDownloadResult: records list[BioactivityRecord], errors list[SourceError], total_fetched int, sources_queried list[str].
target_output.api_design: BioactivityQuery fluent builder: .target(uniprot_id: str) -> self, .activity_type(t: str) -> self, .potency_max_nm(v: float) -> self, .potency_min_nm(v: float) -> self, .sources(s: list[str]) -> self, .with_cache(ttl_seconds: int) -> self. BioactivityClient: client.query(q) -> list[BioactivityRecord] (sync, wraps asyncio.run), await client.aquery(q) -> list[BioactivityRecord] (async parallel dispatch), async for batch in client.abulk_download(q, batch_size=5000) -> AsyncGenerator[list[BioactivityRecord]] (streaming), client.to_dataframe(records) -> pd.DataFrame. Exceptions: SourceUnavailableError(source, message, status_code), RateLimitError(source, retry_after_seconds), InvalidQueryError(field, message). No authenticated endpoints in the library; downstream API keys injected via BioactivityClient(chembl_api_key=os.getenv('CHEMBL_API_KEY')).
target_output.security_and_compliance: API keys accepted only at BioactivityClient instantiation via constructor parameter or environment variable; stored as private instance attributes with name-mangling. __repr__ of BioactivityClient returns '<BioactivityClient sources=[...] cached=False>' with no key values. httpx transport configured with allowed_urls restricted to https:// scheme; any http:// call raises InsecureRequestError. No telemetry, crash reporting, or usage analytics calls in the library. diskcache path must be explicitly specified by the caller; the library never defaults to a shared or system-writable path.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: test matrix on Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12 with ubuntu-latest and macos-latest. pytest with pytest-asyncio for async adapter tests, responses library for HTTP mocking (all CI tests use recorded responses, no live API calls). mypy in strict mode. 90% line coverage gate enforced via codecov PR comment. PyPI publish job triggered on semver tag push using OIDC trusted publisher (no API token stored in repo secrets). hatch build produces wheel (target under 200KB) and sdist. Dependabot monitors httpx and pydantic for updates weekly. Weekly scheduled GitHub Actions job runs integration tests against live ChEMBL and PubChem APIs to detect upstream schema changes.
target_output.observability: Python standard logging using logger = logging.getLogger('bioactivity_sdk'). DEBUG level logs each adapter pagination URL, response size, and record count per page. INFO level logs total records fetched per source and cache hit/miss. WARNING level logs data_quality_flag events (unit coercion failures). Users configure log level via standard logging.basicConfig() or getLogger. No third-party observability dependency. Benchmark test suite in tests/benchmarks/ using pytest-benchmark measuring ChEMBL adapter throughput for 10K IC50 records as a performance regression guard.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost for the library itself. Upstream rate limits: ChEMBL 30 requests/minute unauthenticated (authenticated users get higher limits via CHEMBL_API_KEY), PubChem 5 requests/second. asyncio.Semaphore per adapter enforces these limits automatically. Bulk downloads of 100K+ records per target are expected; async generator with configurable batch_size prevents memory exhaustion. Package wheel under 200KB (well within 500KB limit).
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - Core Framework (Weeks 1-2): BioactivityRecord pydantic model with nM coercion validator, AbstractAdapter ABC, BioactivityQuery fluent builder, BioactivityClient facade with sync/async paths, CacheLayer no-op stub, pytest suite skeleton with HTTP mocking fixtures. Phase 2 - ChEMBL Adapter (Weeks 3-4): ChEMBLAdapter with offset/limit pagination, target-by-UniProt mapping (UniProt to ChEMBL target ID via /target.json?target_components__accession= endpoint), activity filtering, tenacity retry. Phase 3 - PubChem Adapter + v0.1 Release (Weeks 5-6): PubChemBioAssayAdapter with listkey pagination and expiry handling, diskcache optional integration, CLI command bioactivity query, PyPI v0.1.0 publish. Phase 4 - BindingDB + v0.2 (Weeks 7-10): BindingDBAdapter, multi-source parallel dispatch with result merging, polars output option as optional extra, v0.2.0 release.
target_output.development_guidelines: All adapter HTTP calls mocked with responses library in unit tests; zero live API calls permitted in CI. AbstractAdapter enforced with @abstractmethod; mypy will catch non-conforming adapters at type-check time. BioactivityRecord fields documented with Field(description=...) for auto-generated API docs. No print() anywhere in library code - only logging.debug/info/warning. All public functions fully type-annotated. Adapter-specific data quirks handled in adapter layer only, never bleeding into pydantic model validators. Integration test suite isolated in tests/integration/ with @pytest.mark.integration marker skipped by default in CI.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Upstream API schema changes break adapters silently between weekly integration test runs. Mitigation: pydantic model_config extra='ignore' with debug-level log of unexpected fields, and Dependabot-style alerts from weekly scheduled CI. Risk: PubChem listkey server-side state expires during slow bulk downloads. Mitigation: abulk_download checkpoints page index to diskcache if available, restarting listkey if expiry 404 is received. Risk: asyncio.run() sync wrapper adds overhead in environments that already have a running event loop (e.g., Jupyter). Mitigation: document nest_asyncio pattern in Jupyter usage guide. Tradeoff: nM normalization in the SDK means users cannot recover the exact originally-reported unit without inspecting activity_unit_original field.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed httpx over aiohttp: httpx provides identical sync and async API reducing code duplication to zero. Confirmed pydantic v2 over dataclasses: v2 field_validator provides the unit coercion logic needed. BindingDB deferred to v0.2 based on usage analysis showing ChEMBL and PubChem BioAssay cover the primary target query use cases. Optional diskcache chosen over functools.lru_cache: diskcache supports TTL and persistence across Python processes.

metadata.notes: Pure Python SDK with adapter pattern for unified public bioactivity database access; no hosted infrastructure; adapter-specific pagination complexity is the main design challenge.

profile.domain: Pharmaceutical Drug Discovery - Lead Optimization Tracker

project_goal.value: Fullstack compound progression tracker giving medicinal chemistry project teams a unified view of SAR data, ADMET profiles, selectivity panels, and project decision history to accelerate lead optimization cycles
project_goal.rationale: Teams currently manage SAR data in disconnected spreadsheets and ELN exports; a unified view reduces data assembly time from days to minutes

target_users.value: Medicinal chemists, computational chemists, project team leads, and research directors managing lead optimization programs
target_users.rationale: Primary users synthesize and evaluate compounds; secondary users consume aggregated SAR trends for program decisions

project_class.rationale: Requires interactive React UI for SAR visualization and a FastAPI backend integrating with informatics data sources

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, integrations, analytics, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: Full capability set: interactive chemistry UI, REST API, PostgreSQL data layer, Okta auth, integrations with informatics pipeline, analytics charts, and admin for user/project management

complexity_level.rationale: Complex chemistry data relationships (SAR series, ADMET multidimensional profiles, selectivity matrices), integration with upstream informatics pipeline, and role-based data access controls

risk_level.rationale: Compound structures and SAR data constitute core pharmaceutical IP; unauthorized access or data integrity failures could compromise drug discovery programs and patent positions

data_sensitivity.rationale: Compound structures, ADMET profiles, and project decision records are highly sensitive IP; external disclosure would be commercially damaging

external_exposure.rationale: Access restricted to authenticated users in chemistry and computational biology teams; no public endpoints

access_model.value: Okta OIDC with project-level RBAC: chemist (read/write own project), team_lead (manage project team roster and decision records), admin (create projects, manage users, access audit log)
access_model.rationale: Project-level scoping prevents cross-program IP exposure between competing programs

feature_scope.value: SAR series management with analog comparison tables, ADMET profile display with radar charts, selectivity panel heatmaps, progression milestone tracking with go/no-go decision records and rationale, informatics pipeline integration for automated data sync, admin panel for project and user management
feature_scope.rationale: Covers the full lead optimization workflow from compound registration through program milestone decisions

mvp_scope.value: SAR series view with analog table, ADMET profile display, basic progression milestones with decision record capture, and manual CSV import for initial data population
mvp_scope.rationale: Manual CSV import unblocks early adoption while informatics pipeline integration is developed in parallel

security_baseline.value: Okta OIDC with JWT validation, project-level RBAC enforced server-side, TLS 1.3, all compound structure data encrypted at rest with AWS KMS, audit log for all write operations
security_baseline.rationale: IP sensitivity requires encryption at rest and full write audit trail for GxP data integrity

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript, TanStack Query for server state, Recharts for SAR trend and ADMET radar charts, React Table for analog comparison grids
frontend_stack.rationale: TanStack Query provides stale-while-revalidate caching for expensive SAR queries; Recharts gives composable chemistry-specific chart components

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0, Alembic for migrations, httpx for informatics pipeline polling
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI async handlers match the I/O-bound nature of informatics pipeline polling; SQLAlchemy 2.0 async ORM for non-blocking DB access

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ; Redis 7 (ElastiCache) for session cache and SAR query result cache
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL relational model handles complex compound-to-series-to-project relationships; Redis caches expensive cross-series ADMET aggregation queries

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1 with ALB, GitHub Actions CI/CD, Datadog APM
hosting_target.rationale: ECS Fargate matches existing informatics platform infrastructure standard; Datadog provides integrated APM and infra monitoring

privacy_retention_policy.value: Compound data retained indefinitely per IP governance policy; audit logs retained 7 years; Redis cache TTL 4 hours for SAR queries
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Indefinite compound retention required for patent defense; 7-year audit log for regulatory data integrity requirements

future_scope.value: Real-time informatics pipeline push integration via webhooks, 3D molecular visualization via NGL Viewer, machine learning-driven ADMET flag recommendations, partner diligence read-only export package
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 features add automated data freshness and structural visualization capabilities

constraints.value: SAR table for 500 analogs must render in under 2 seconds; must integrate with existing informatics REST pipeline within 6 months; compound structures must never appear in application logs
constraints.rationale: Performance target based on typical series size in lead optimization; log sanitization required for IP protection

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM with distributed tracing, structured JSON logs with compound registry IDs (not SMILES), custom metrics for SAR query latency, informatics sync lag, and decision record submission rate
observability_baseline.rationale: Informatics sync lag is critical operational metric for data freshness SLA

execution_preference.value: Agile 2-week sprints; MVP with manual CSV import in 10 weeks; informatics pipeline integration in weeks 12-18
execution_preference.rationale: Manual import unblocks chemistry team adoption while integration is built

compliance_context.value: Internal IP governance SOP for compound data; GxP data integrity principles for data used in IND filing packages
compliance_context.rationale: High risk and confidential data sensitivity require audit trail and data integrity controls for regulatory-traceable records

project.goal: Unified compound progression tracker for medicinal chemistry lead optimization
project.target_users: Medicinal chemists, computational chemists, project leads
project.timeline: MVP in 10 weeks; informatics integration by week 18

frontend.state: TanStack Query for server state, Zustand for UI state
frontend.charts: Recharts for SAR trends and ADMET radar
frontend.tables: React Table v8 for analog comparison grids

security.auth: Okta OIDC with JWT validation middleware
security.authz: project-level RBAC enforced in FastAPI dependency
security.encryption: RDS KMS at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit

data.cache: ElastiCache Redis 7 for SAR query results

constraints.no_smiles_in_logs: sanitize compound structures from all log outputs

reasoner_reviews.product: The analog comparison table is the highest-value feature for medicinal chemists - they need to see potency, selectivity, and ADMET flags side by side for 50-500 compounds in a series. The 2-second render target for 500 rows requires server-side pagination with virtual scrolling on the frontend, not client-side data loading. Decision records with structured rationale (not free-text) will be critical for the regulatory traceability use case.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: PostgreSQL with a compounds-to-sar_series junction design handles the many-to-many relationship where a compound can appear in multiple SAR series across projects. The ADMET profile storage as JSONB enables flexible property addition without schema migrations as new assay types are added. Redis caching for cross-series ADMET aggregations is essential given the join complexity. FastAPI async is the right choice given the blocking informatics pipeline polling.
reasoner_reviews.security: Project-level RBAC must be enforced as a FastAPI dependency injected into every route, not just middleware, to prevent project ID spoofing in request bodies. Compound registry IDs must replace SMILES strings in all Datadog traces and log payloads - add a log sanitizer that redacts SMILES patterns.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 2-second SAR table target for 500 analogs is achievable with: server-side pagination (50 rows per page), Redis caching of the per-series compound list, and React Table virtual scrolling. Full 500-row fetch for CSV export can be async with a progress indicator.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The plan must address data freshness between the informatics pipeline and the tracker. A polling interval of 15 minutes is not sufficient for active synthesis campaigns where new data arrives hourly. Recommend webhook-based push integration for v1.1 while polling covers MVP.

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with route-based code splitting: /projects (project list), /projects/:id/sar (SAR series view), /projects/:id/admet (ADMET profile view), /projects/:id/milestones (progression tracker), /admin (admin panel). TanStack Query QueryClient with 5-minute stale time and background refetch.
frontend.pages_and_flows: SAR Series Page: tabbed view with Analog Comparison Table (React Table v8 with virtualization, sortable columns for potency, selectivity, cLogP, PSA), SAR Trend Chart (Recharts LineChart of potency vs. modification position), and Structure Image column using RDKit.js for client-side SMILES-to-SVG rendering. ADMET Profile Page: radar chart (Recharts RadarChart) comparing up to 6 compounds across ADMET endpoints, with traffic-light flags for Lipinski violations. Milestone Tracker: timeline component showing project phases with decision record cards showing go/no-go outcome, date, decision makers, and structured rationale fields.
frontend.state_management: TanStack Query for all server state (SAR data, ADMET profiles, decisions). Zustand store for UI-only state: active series selection, comparison compound set (max 6 for ADMET radar), table column visibility preferences stored to localStorage.
frontend.frontend_risks: RDKit.js WASM bundle is 8MB; lazy-load only on SAR pages. Recharts RadarChart with 6 compounds x 12 ADMET endpoints may have render performance issues; memoize data transformation with useMemo.

backend.service_design: FastAPI app with router modules: /compounds (CRUD + search), /series (SAR series management), /admet (ADMET profile read/aggregation), /milestones (progression tracking), /decisions (go/no-go records), /admin (user/project management), /sync (informatics pipeline polling trigger and status). Dependency injection for Okta JWT validation and project-level RBAC check.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.0 docs at /docs. Project-scoped URLs: /v1/projects/{project_id}/compounds, /v1/projects/{project_id}/series/{series_id}/analogs. Pagination via cursor-based next_cursor token for analog tables. Async background task for informatics pipeline polling via FastAPI BackgroundTasks.
backend.background_jobs: Informatics sync job polls the upstream REST pipeline every 15 minutes for new compound registrations and updated bioactivity data. New records are upserted into compounds table and admet_profiles JSONB column. Sync lag metric emitted to Datadog after each run.
backend.failure_handling: Informatics pipeline unavailability is non-fatal: last sync timestamp displayed in UI, staleness warning shown after 2 hours. All write endpoints use database transactions; partial writes rolled back on exception.

security.auth_design: Okta OIDC: frontend obtains ID token and access token via Auth Code + PKCE flow. Backend FastAPI dependency validates JWT signature using Okta JWKS endpoint with 1-hour key cache. Project membership checked against project_members table on every project-scoped request.
security.authorization_model: Project-level RBAC: chemist role allows read all project data and write own compound annotations; team_lead adds decision record write and project team management; admin adds project create/delete and audit log access. Role stored in project_members.role column, enforced in FastAPI Depends(require_project_role) dependency.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: All write operations (compound import, decision record creation, ADMET update, user role change) appended to audit_events table with user_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, old_value_hash, new_value_hash, timestamp. Compound SMILES sanitized from all log payloads using regex filter on FastAPI logging middleware.

data.entities: projects (id UUID PK, name, therapeutic_area, target_name, status ENUM active/archived, created_by, created_at). sar_series (id UUID PK, project_id FK, series_name, core_smiles, rationale, created_at). compounds (id UUID PK, registry_id INT UNIQUE, inchikey VARCHAR(27), project_id FK, series_id FK, synthesized_at). admet_profiles (id UUID PK, compound_id FK, profile_data JSONB with assay_name->value mapping, updated_at, sync_source). progression_milestones (id UUID PK, project_id FK, milestone_name, target_date, status ENUM pending/passed/failed/deferred). decision_records (id UUID PK, milestone_id FK, outcome ENUM go/no_go/hold, decision_date, rationale_structured JSONB, decision_makers text[], recorded_by). project_members (project_id FK, user_id, role ENUM chemist/team_lead/admin, added_at).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ with KMS encryption at rest. admet_profiles.profile_data JSONB with GIN index for property name queries. Redis 7 caches GET /projects/{id}/series/{sid}/analogs responses keyed by series_id + page cursor with 4-hour TTL, invalidated on compound import or ADMET update for that series.
data.retention_and_deletion: Compound records never deleted per IP governance SOP; soft-delete via status=archived on projects. Audit events append-only with no DELETE grant on application role. Decision records immutable after 30-day correction window.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate: api service (2-4 tasks auto-scaling on CPU 70%), frontend service (static files via CloudFront + S3). RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ with 1 read replica for analytics queries. ElastiCache Redis single-node (failover via cluster mode in prod). ALB with WAF rule set for OWASP Top 10.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: frontend build (TypeScript typecheck, ESLint, Vite build, S3 deploy), backend pipeline (pytest 85% coverage gate, mypy, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling update). Alembic migration runs as ECS one-off task before API service update.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM with Python tracer on FastAPI. Custom metrics: sar_query_latency_ms (p95 target <2000ms for 500-analog queries), informatics_sync_lag_minutes, decision_record_submissions_per_day. Structured JSON logs with request_id and project_id; SMILES filtered by logging middleware.

target_output.thinking_summary: The core design challenges are: (1) project-level RBAC must be watertight to prevent cross-program IP exposure, (2) the SAR analog table performance target (500 analogs under 2 seconds) requires server-side pagination + Redis caching + React Table virtualization in combination, and (3) compound SMILES must be actively sanitized from all observability outputs given IP sensitivity. ADMET profile stored as JSONB enables flexible property addition as assay panels evolve without schema migrations.
target_output.title: Compound Progression Tracker - Lead Optimization Fullstack Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A fullstack React 18 + FastAPI application that gives medicinal chemistry project teams a unified view of SAR analog data, ADMET profiles, selectivity panels, and project decision history to accelerate lead optimization decisions. Built on PostgreSQL 16 with JSONB ADMET storage for flexible property extension, Redis 7 for SAR query caching, and project-level Okta OIDC RBAC to prevent cross-program IP exposure. Deployed on AWS ECS Fargate with a CloudFront-delivered React SPA. Designed for sub-2-second SAR table renders for 500-analog series and complete audit trails for GxP data integrity compliance.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture with React 18 SPA delivered via CloudFront communicating over HTTPS to a FastAPI backend on ECS Fargate behind an ALB with WAF. FastAPI routes are project-scoped (/v1/projects/{project_id}/...) with a Depends(require_project_role) FastAPI dependency that validates Okta JWT and checks project_members table membership on every request, preventing cross-program data access via ID spoofing. Backend connects to PostgreSQL 16 (RDS Multi-AZ) for transactional compound and decision data, with a read replica for analytics aggregate queries. Redis 7 caches expensive SAR series analog list responses keyed by series_id with 4-hour TTL, invalidated on informatics sync updates for that series. A background polling job syncs new compound registrations and ADMET data from the upstream informatics pipeline every 15 minutes, with sync lag exposed as a Datadog metric and staleness warning shown in the UI after 2 hours. Compound SMILES are sanitized from all log payloads and Datadog traces by a FastAPI logging middleware.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, TanStack Query v5 (5-minute stale time, background refetch), Zustand for UI state, Recharts for SAR trend lines and ADMET radar charts, React Table v8 with virtualization for 500-row analog grids, RDKit.js (WASM, lazy-loaded) for client-side SMILES-to-SVG rendering. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async ORM, Alembic, httpx for informatics pipeline polling, python-jose for JWT validation. Database: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ with KMS encryption, GIN index on admet_profiles.profile_data JSONB. Cache: ElastiCache Redis 7. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB with WAF, CloudFront + S3 for static assets, Okta OIDC, Datadog APM. CI/CD: GitHub Actions.
target_output.functional_feature_map: SAR Series View: analog comparison table with sortable columns (potency IC50/Ki, selectivity ratio, cLogP, PSA, synthesis date), per-analog structure thumbnail via RDKit.js SVG, and SAR trend chart plotting potency shift vs. R-group modification position. ADMET Profile View: radar chart comparing up to 6 compounds across ADMET endpoints (solubility, permeability, metabolic stability, hERG, plasma protein binding) with Lipinski flag overlays. Selectivity Panel: heatmap of compound activity across off-target panel assays. Progression Milestones: project timeline with milestone cards, each linked to a structured decision record capturing go/no-go outcome, date, decision makers (referenced to user IDs), and structured rationale with mandatory fields (primary_reason, supporting_data_refs). Admin Panel: project creation, team member invite with role assignment, audit log viewer filtered by project and action type.
target_output.system_components: CloudFront + S3 (React SPA static delivery), ALB with WAF OWASP rules (API traffic), ECS Fargate API service (2-4 tasks, CPU-based auto-scaling), ECS Fargate informatics sync worker (1 task, 15-minute polling schedule via ECS scheduled task), RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ (primary + read replica for analytics), ElastiCache Redis 7 (SAR query result cache), Okta (OIDC identity provider), Datadog (APM + custom metrics for SAR latency and sync lag), AWS KMS (RDS encryption key), GitHub Actions (CI/CD pipeline).
target_output.workflows: Analog Comparison Workflow: chemist navigates to /projects/{id}/series/{sid}/analogs. TanStack Query fetches GET /v1/projects/{id}/series/{sid}/analogs?page_size=50&cursor=null with Okta JWT. FastAPI Depends validates JWT, confirms project membership. Backend checks Redis for series_id cache hit; on miss, queries compounds JOIN admet_profiles WHERE series_id={sid} with LIMIT 50 and cursor encoding, stores result in Redis. React Table renders 50 rows with virtualization; chemist scrolls to load next page via cursor. Decision Record Workflow: team_lead opens a milestone, selects go/no-go outcome, completes structured rationale form (primary_reason dropdown, supporting_data_refs text array, dissenting_opinions text). Frontend POSTs to /v1/projects/{id}/milestones/{mid}/decisions. Backend validates team_lead role via Depends, inserts decision_records row within transaction, appends to audit_events. Decision record becomes immutable to chemist role; correction requires team_lead within 30-day window. Informatics Sync Workflow: ECS scheduled task fires every 15 minutes, calling GET /informatics-pipeline/v1/compounds?updated_since={last_sync_ts} with service JWT. New records upserted into compounds table; updated ADMET values merged into admet_profiles.profile_data JSONB. Redis cache keys for affected series are invalidated. Sync lag metric emitted to Datadog.
target_output.data_model: projects (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, therapeutic_area VARCHAR, target_gene VARCHAR, status VARCHAR DEFAULT 'active', created_by UUID FK users, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). sar_series (id UUID PK, project_id UUID FK projects, series_name VARCHAR, core_smiles TEXT, design_rationale TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). compounds (id UUID PK, registry_id INT UNIQUE NOT NULL, inchikey VARCHAR(27) NOT NULL, project_id UUID FK, series_id UUID FK sar_series, synthesized_by UUID FK users, synthesized_at TIMESTAMPTZ, import_source VARCHAR). admet_profiles (id UUID PK, compound_id UUID FK compounds UNIQUE, profile_data JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}', last_updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, sync_source VARCHAR). progression_milestones (id UUID PK, project_id UUID FK, milestone_name VARCHAR, target_date DATE, status VARCHAR CHECK IN ('pending','passed','failed','deferred')). decision_records (id UUID PK, milestone_id UUID FK, outcome VARCHAR CHECK IN ('go','no_go','hold'), decision_date DATE, rationale_structured JSONB, decision_makers UUID[], recorded_by UUID FK, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ). project_members (project_id UUID FK, user_id UUID, role VARCHAR CHECK IN ('chemist','team_lead','admin'), PRIMARY KEY(project_id, user_id)). audit_events (id BIGSERIAL PK, user_id UUID, action VARCHAR, resource_type VARCHAR, resource_id UUID, old_value_hash VARCHAR, new_value_hash VARCHAR, project_id UUID, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ). GIN index on admet_profiles.profile_data for assay property queries.
target_output.api_design: All endpoints project-scoped under /v1/projects/{project_id}/. GET /v1/projects/{project_id}/series/{series_id}/analogs?page_size=50&cursor={encoded_cursor} - returns {analogs: [{registry_id, inchikey, admet_summary, potency_ic50_nm, series_position}], next_cursor, total_count}. POST /v1/projects/{project_id}/compounds/import (multipart CSV upload for MVP manual import). GET /v1/projects/{project_id}/admet?compound_ids[]={ids} - returns ADMET profile JSONB for up to 6 compounds for radar chart. POST /v1/projects/{project_id}/milestones/{milestone_id}/decisions - decision record submission. GET /v1/projects/{project_id}/audit-events?action_type={type}&from_date={date} - admin only. GET /v1/projects/{project_id}/series/{series_id}/analytics/sar-trend - returns potency shift data for Recharts. DELETE forbidden on compounds, decision_records, and audit_events endpoints (404 returned). OpenAPI 3.0 docs at /docs (dev/staging only, blocked in production by middleware).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Okta OIDC with Auth Code + PKCE flow on frontend; backend validates JWT using Okta JWKS with 1-hour key rotation cache. Project-level RBAC enforced via FastAPI Depends(require_project_role(minimum_role)) injected into every project-scoped route - validates both token and project_members table membership to prevent ID substitution attacks. Compound SMILES never written to logs: FastAPI logging middleware applies regex substitution removing any string matching SMILES patterns from request bodies before logging. RDS KMS encryption at rest with annual key rotation. WAF blocks OWASP Top 10 patterns on ALB. Audit events table has append-only grant on application role (no UPDATE/DELETE on audit_events). GxP data integrity: decision records immutable to chemist role after creation; 30-day correction window for team_leads only. IP governance SOP satisfied by indefinite compound retention and 7-year audit log retention.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions frontend pipeline: TypeScript tsc --noEmit, ESLint, Vite build, S3 sync, CloudFront invalidation. Backend pipeline: pytest (85% coverage gate), mypy --strict, Docker build push ECR, Alembic migration via ECS one-off task, ECS rolling update with health check gate. Environments: dev (single AZ, no WAF, manual Okta app), staging (multi-AZ mirror with WAF, production Okta app, data subset), production (multi-AZ us-east-1, RDS Multi-AZ, Redis with cluster mode, auto-scaling 2-6 tasks). ECS scheduled task for informatics sync (1 vCPU, 2GB, 15-minute schedule via EventBridge rule). Rollback: previous ECS task definition reactivation via CLI script; database rollback via Alembic downgrade script tested in staging. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager for DB credentials, Redis URL, and Okta client secret with automatic rotation.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM with ddtrace Python library, FastAPI middleware for trace injection and SMILES sanitization. Custom Datadog metrics: sar_query_latency_ms histogram (p50/p95/p99, target p95 under 2000ms for 500-analog queries), informatics_sync_lag_minutes gauge (alert threshold 120 minutes), decision_record_submissions_per_day counter, redis_cache_hit_rate_percent gauge. Structured JSON logs with fields: request_id, project_id, user_id (from JWT sub), route, latency_ms - no SMILES. PagerDuty escalation for: p95 SAR query latency over 3000ms, sync lag over 4 hours, ECS task count below minimum, RDS connections over 80 percent.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate API service: ~$240/month (2 tasks baseline, scale to 4). Informatics sync task: ~$30/month (15-min schedule). RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ (db.r6g.large): ~$400/month with read replica ~$200/month. ElastiCache Redis (cache.r6g.small): ~$80/month. CloudFront + S3 frontend: ~$20/month. Total: ~$970/month. Auto-scaling triggers at 70% CPU to handle concurrent SAR query spikes during project review cycles. Read replica handles analytics aggregate queries (SAR trend charts) to isolate from transactional write load.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (Weeks 1-5): PostgreSQL schema with all core tables, FastAPI with Okta JWT validation and project RBAC, SAR series and analog endpoints, manual CSV import, React SPA with analog comparison table (React Table, no virtualization), basic ADMET display, GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 (Weeks 6-10): ADMET radar chart (Recharts), RDKit.js structure thumbnails (lazy-loaded), progression milestone tracker, decision record form with structured rationale, Redis caching for analog lists, React Table virtualization for 500-row performance target. Phase 3 (Weeks 11-18): Informatics pipeline polling integration, ECS scheduled sync worker, Datadog custom metrics for sync lag, selectivity heatmap, admin panel with audit log viewer, production hardening with WAF and RDS read replica for analytics.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode on all frontend code; mypy --strict on all backend modules. No compound SMILES in any logger.info, logger.error, or exception message - enforce via pre-commit hook that greps for SMILES-like patterns in log statements. React component tests with React Testing Library for SAR table render and decision form validation. FastAPI route tests with pytest and httpx AsyncClient, mocking Okta JWT validation with a test-mode bypass Depends. 85% backend coverage gate. Alembic migrations reviewed by senior engineer before staging merge. All decision_records writes use explicit PostgreSQL transactions with savepoints.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: RDKit.js 8MB WASM bundle increases initial page load significantly. Mitigation: lazy-load on SAR pages only via React.lazy(); cache in browser local storage across sessions. Risk: informatics pipeline 15-minute polling creates stale data periods during active synthesis campaigns. Mitigation: staleness warning banner in UI after 2 hours; webhook push integration planned for v1.1. Risk: admet_profiles JSONB schema drift if informatics pipeline adds new assay properties without notification. Mitigation: JSONB schema validation on write using pydantic AdmetProfile model that allows extra fields but validates known properties. Tradeoff: server-side cursor pagination over offset pagination avoids large-offset PostgreSQL performance degradation for 500+ analog series but requires stateless cursor encoding (compound registry_id as cursor value).
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed TanStack Query over SWR: TanStack Query's query key invalidation granularity matches the series-level cache invalidation pattern needed. Confirmed JSONB for ADMET profiles over fixed columns: assay panel properties change frequently enough that schema migrations would be disruptive. Confirmed ECS scheduled task over Lambda for informatics sync: 15-minute intervals with stateful DB connections are better suited to a persistent container. Manual CSV import selected for MVP over waiting for informatics integration to avoid blocking chemistry team adoption.

metadata.notes: Lead optimization tracker with project-level RBAC, ADMET JSONB design, SAR table performance, and informatics pipeline sync; compound IP protection throughout observability layer.

profile.domain: Pharmaceutical Clinical Research - Secure Multi-Party Computation Prototype

project_goal.value: Research prototype evaluating secure multi-party computation protocols that allow competing pharmaceutical companies to jointly compute aggregate clinical outcome statistics without revealing individual patient-level trial data
project_goal.rationale: Collaborative trial analysis has value but raw patient data sharing is legally and commercially prohibited between competitors

target_users.value: Clinical biostatisticians and research architects at 2-4 participating pharmaceutical partner companies evaluating protocol feasibility
target_users.rationale: Technical evaluators assessing whether SMPC is viable for regulatory-grade aggregate data sharing before committing to production

project_class.rationale: Prototype to evaluate feasibility; production architecture decisions deferred pending prototype benchmark results

capabilities.rationale: Computation engine, encrypted data storage, and benchmark analytics; no frontend UI for prototype

complexity_level.rationale: Garbled circuit computation with multi-party network coordination; cryptographic protocol orchestration is inherently complex

risk_level.rationale: Patient-level clinical outcome data is involved even in encrypted form; protocol implementation errors could leak individual data

data_sensitivity.rationale: Encrypted inputs contain patient clinical outcome data from clinical trials; HIPAA and GDPR apply to patient identifiers even in encrypted form

external_exposure.rationale: Multi-party computation nodes span multiple pharmaceutical partner organizations; mTLS certificates per party

access_model.value: mTLS certificates per participating party; no user-facing authentication; node identity established by certificate CN matching registered party_id
access_model.rationale: Machine-to-machine multi-party protocol; party identity must be cryptographically verified, not password-based

feature_scope.value: MP-SPDZ garbled circuit computation for composite endpoint rate calculation, multi-party input encryption and submission, aggregate output release, per-party privacy budget tracking, feasibility benchmarking across 2-4 parties, computation audit records
feature_scope.rationale: Prototype scope covers the core SMPC workflow and benchmarking needed to make a production go/no-go decision

mvp_scope.value: 2-party SMPC computation for a single composite endpoint (e.g., overall survival rate), privacy budget tracking, and benchmark report generation
mvp_scope.rationale: 2-party case is simpler to validate cryptographically; extend to 4 parties after baseline benchmarks

security_baseline.value: mTLS 1.3 between all party nodes, MP-SPDZ protocol validation before each computation run, encrypted_inputs never persisted to disk after computation, audit records for all protocol runs
security_baseline.rationale: Patient data even in encrypted form requires strong transport security and ephemeral input handling

frontend_stack.value: none - research prototype with CLI and REST API only
frontend_stack.rationale: No UI needed for research prototype evaluation; biostatisticians interact via Python client scripts

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with MP-SPDZ (SMPC framework) and FastAPI for coordination REST endpoints
backend_stack.rationale: MP-SPDZ is the leading open-source SMPC framework with garbled circuit support; FastAPI wraps coordination logic for inter-party API calls

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for computation audit records and party registration; no persistent storage of encrypted patient inputs
data_platform.rationale: Encrypted inputs are ephemeral (in-memory only during computation); only audit metadata persisted

hosting_target.value: Azure Virtual Machines per participating party (one VM per party); Azure DevOps Pipelines; Azure Monitor
hosting_target.rationale: One VM per party maps to real deployment topology; Azure VMs allow direct network configuration needed for MP-SPDZ socket communication

privacy_retention_policy.value: Encrypted patient inputs destroyed after each computation run (not persisted); aggregate outputs retained per HIPAA de-identification safe harbor for 6 years; audit records retained 10 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: HIPAA safe harbor applies to properly aggregated outputs; input data ephemerality is a core privacy guarantee of the SMPC design

future_scope.value: Production-grade deployment with HSM-backed key management, regulatory agency observer node, 4-party computation for multi-arm trials, homomorphic encryption alternative evaluation
future_scope.rationale: Production path requires hardware security module integration and possible regulator participation as observer

constraints.value: Computation must complete within 4 hours for a 10,000-subject trial arm; no raw patient identifiers in any network communication; prototype budget limited to 3 months of researcher time
constraints.rationale: 4-hour limit is practical for overnight batch runs in biostatistics workflows; no-identifier constraint is non-negotiable legal requirement

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor with computation run duration metrics; structured protocol run logs per party; no patient-linkable data in any log output
observability_baseline.rationale: Benchmark run duration is the primary prototype evaluation metric; log sanitization required by HIPAA

execution_preference.value: Research sprint: 2-party baseline in month 1, 4-party extension in month 2, feasibility report with benchmark data in month 3
execution_preference.rationale: Fixed 3-month prototype timeline with clear go/no-go deliverable at end

compliance_context.value: HIPAA de-identification requirements for aggregate output, GDPR data minimization for EU trial participants, ICH E9 statistical principles for aggregate data sharing validity in collaborative trials
compliance_context.rationale: High risk level and health data sensitivity require compliance context covering both US and EU patient data regulations

project.goal: SMPC prototype for aggregate clinical outcome statistics without patient data sharing
project.target_users: Clinical biostatisticians at 2-4 partner pharma companies

backend.api: FastAPI coordination endpoints for party registration and computation triggers

security.input_handling: encrypted inputs ephemeral, never persisted to disk
security.audit: protocol run audit records in PostgreSQL

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 for audit records and party registry only
data.patient_inputs: in-memory only during computation, destroyed after run

constraints.no_identifiers: no patient identifiers in any network message

reasoner_reviews.product: Three prior rounds have clarified the benchmark scope: focus on 2-party and 4-party garbled circuit computation for composite endpoint rate (binary outcome percentage). The feasibility report must include wall-clock computation time, communication bandwidth per party, and a comparison against the MP-SPDZ theoretical complexity bounds. ICH E9 compliance requires the output statistic to be interpretable under the pre-specified statistical analysis plan.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The MP-SPDZ garbled circuit protocol for binary outcome rate computation is well-understood and the 10K-subject case should complete well within the 4-hour constraint based on published benchmarks. The remaining architectural gap from round 3 is the circuit_definitions table - the audit record must include a hash of the circuit definition used so that post-hoc verification can confirm the correct statistical function was evaluated.
reasoner_reviews.security: The ISS-002 issue from round 3 about encrypted_inputs memory residency is partially addressed but the audit record still does not capture whether the secure memory wipe completed successfully. The fix_report for this round must address the memory wipe confirmation gap.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 3-month timeline is tight for 4-party extension if 2-party baseline benchmarks reveal unexpected performance issues. Prototype scope should explicitly state that the round 4 plan addresses ISS-002 and ISS-003 and does not introduce new scope.

backend.service_design: Coordinator node (one per computation run, elected from participating parties by round-robin) runs FastAPI on port 8443 (mTLS). Party nodes expose /v1/party/register, /v1/computation/submit-input, /v1/computation/run, /v1/computation/result. MP-SPDZ subprocess launched by coordinator with party count, circuit definition, and input socket configuration.
backend.api_patterns: Party registration: POST /v1/party/register with party certificate CN and endpoint URL. Computation initiation: POST /v1/computation/run with circuit_id, party_ids[], input_hash per party. Result retrieval: GET /v1/computation/{run_id}/result (available only after all parties confirmed completion).
backend.failure_handling: If any party node drops connection during computation, the run is aborted and audit_records.status set to 'aborted'. Encrypted inputs are wiped from all party memory on abort. Run can be retried with a new run_id; no partial results ever released.
backend.backend_risks: MP-SPDZ subprocess management is fragile - OS-level process crashes may leave network sockets in TIME_WAIT blocking retry. Mitigation: socket reuse option on coordinator; 60-second wait before retry.

data.entities: computation_parties (id UUID PK, party_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, cert_cn VARCHAR, endpoint_url VARCHAR, registered_at TIMESTAMPTZ). circuit_definitions (id UUID PK, circuit_name VARCHAR, statistical_function VARCHAR, input_schema JSONB, circuit_hash VARCHAR UNIQUE, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). protocol_runs (id UUID PK, circuit_id UUID FK, party_ids UUID[], status VARCHAR CHECK IN ('initiated','running','completed','aborted'), started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, computation_time_seconds INT, communication_bytes_total BIGINT, memory_wipe_confirmed BOOL DEFAULT false). aggregate_outputs (id UUID PK, run_id UUID FK protocol_runs UNIQUE, statistic_name VARCHAR, statistic_value NUMERIC, confidence_interval_json JSONB, released_at TIMESTAMPTZ). audit_records (id BIGSERIAL PK, run_id UUID FK, party_id UUID FK, event_type VARCHAR, event_detail JSONB, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
data.schema_notes: memory_wipe_confirmed column added in round 4 to address ISS-002: each party posts a memory wipe confirmation event to audit_records after encrypted input destruction; protocol_runs.memory_wipe_confirmed is set TRUE only when all participating parties have confirmed.
data.data_risks: aggregate_outputs.statistic_value must only be released after memory_wipe_confirmed=TRUE for the run; application logic must enforce this gate. Mitigation: CHECK constraint or application-layer gate in result retrieval endpoint.

devops.deployment_topology: One Azure VM per party (Standard_D4s_v3, 4 vCPU, 16GB RAM). VMs communicate via VNet peering within the prototype network; mTLS certificates provisioned per party via Azure Key Vault. MP-SPDZ binaries compiled into Docker image on each VM.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipelines: Python unit tests for coordinator logic, MP-SPDZ circuit integration test with 2-party local simulation, Docker build and push to Azure Container Registry, VM deployment via Ansible playbook.
devops.devops_risks: MP-SPDZ compilation from source takes 45+ minutes in CI. Mitigation: pre-built Docker base image with MP-SPDZ binaries cached in Azure Container Registry, rebuilt only on MP-SPDZ version bump.

security.auth_design: mTLS with client certificate validation on all FastAPI endpoints. Azure Key Vault stores per-party private keys; certificates provisioned and rotated by the Azure Certificate Authority integration. Party identity resolved from client certificate CN field.
security.secrets_management: Per-party private keys stored in Azure Key Vault, accessed via Azure Managed Identity at VM startup. Keys never written to disk outside Key Vault-managed volumes.
security.security_risks: Garbled circuit implementation bugs in MP-SPDZ could leak individual inputs. Mitigation: use only reviewed MP-SPDZ protocol versions; add input-output differential privacy layer on aggregate outputs before release as defense in depth.

ISS-001.title: Aggregate output released before all party memory wipes confirmed
ISS-001.detail: In round 1, aggregate_outputs had no dependency on memory wipe confirmation status, meaning results could theoretically be retrieved while encrypted inputs were still in memory on party nodes.

history.note: Output endpoint had no wipe gate
Application-layer gate added requiring all party wipe confirmations before result endpoint returns 200

ISS-002.title: Memory wipe completion not captured in audit records
ISS-002.detail: Rounds 2 and 3 added the application-layer gate for ISS-001 but the protocol_runs table had no column to record whether memory wipe actually completed on each party node, making post-hoc audit verification impossible.

history.note: Identified during round 2 audit: wipe gate added but not auditable
memory_wipe_confirmed flag discussed but not implemented in schema

ISS-003.title: circuit_definitions table missing circuit hash for post-hoc verification
ISS-003.detail: Audit records reference circuit_id but the circuit_definitions table in rounds 2-3 did not store a hash of the circuit definition binary, making it impossible to verify post-hoc that the correct statistical function was evaluated.

history.note: Auditor flagged: circuit integrity not verifiable from audit records alone

focus_issues.title: Memory wipe completion not captured in audit records
focus_issues.detail: protocol_runs table lacks memory_wipe_confirmed column; party nodes have no mechanism to report wipe completion to the coordinator; audit verification of HIPAA-required input data destruction is impossible.

focus_issues.title: circuit_definitions table missing circuit hash
focus_issues.detail: circuit_definitions stores circuit_name and statistical_function but not a cryptographic hash of the circuit binary, preventing verifiable audit trail of which statistical function was actually executed.

latest_recommendations: Add memory_wipe_confirmed boolean column to protocol_runs; require each party to POST a wipe confirmation event to audit_records before result endpoint returns
Add circuit_hash (SHA-256 of circuit definition binary) to circuit_definitions table and include in protocol_run audit records for post-hoc statistical function verification

previous_plan.title: SMPC Clinical Outcome Prototype - Round 3 Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: Multi-party computation prototype using MP-SPDZ garbled circuits on Azure VMs with mTLS per-party authentication. Round 3 added benchmark metrics design but left memory wipe audit and circuit hash issues unresolved.
previous_plan.data_model: protocol_runs table lacked memory_wipe_confirmed column. circuit_definitions lacked circuit_hash. Audit records tracked run start/end but not per-party wipe events.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: mTLS between party nodes specified. Encrypted inputs ephemeral. Memory wipe described in prose but not captured in schema or API flow.
previous_plan.api_design: POST /v1/computation/run and GET /v1/computation/{run_id}/result defined. No POST /v1/computation/{run_id}/wipe-confirm endpoint existed.

best_plan.title: SMPC Clinical Outcome Prototype - Round 3 Architecture
best_plan.executive_summary: Same as previous_plan in round 3; no better plan exists in prior rounds.
best_plan.security_and_compliance: Best prior version had ISS-001 resolved but ISS-002 and ISS-003 open.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 4 must close ISS-002 and ISS-003. The fixes are concrete schema additions: memory_wipe_confirmed column on protocol_runs, per-party wipe confirmation API endpoint, and circuit_hash on circuit_definitions. These are localized changes but must propagate consistently through data_model, api_design, security_and_compliance, and deployment sections. This is a research prototype so some plan sections will remain thin - the benchmark design and feasibility report structure are the primary deliverables beyond the issue fixes.

fix_report.action_taken: Added memory_wipe_confirmed BOOL DEFAULT false column to protocol_runs. Added per-party wipe confirmation API endpoint POST /v1/computation/{run_id}/wipe-confirm. Party nodes call this endpoint after in-memory encrypted input destruction; coordinator sets memory_wipe_confirmed=TRUE only when all parties have posted. Result endpoint returns 403 until memory_wipe_confirmed=TRUE for the run.
fix_report.expected_outcome: HIPAA-required input data destruction is now auditable: audit_records captures per-party wipe confirmation events with timestamps, and protocol_runs.memory_wipe_confirmed provides a queryable boolean gate on the run record.

fix_report.action_taken: Added circuit_hash VARCHAR(64) UNIQUE NOT NULL column to circuit_definitions storing SHA-256 of the circuit definition binary. Protocol run audit records now include circuit_hash in event_detail JSONB for post-hoc verification.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Post-hoc audit can verify which circuit binary was executed by comparing the stored circuit_hash against the circuit definition file, confirming the correct statistical function ran on the clinical data.

target_output.title: SMPC Clinical Outcome Prototype - Round 4 Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A research prototype using MP-SPDZ garbled circuit computation on Azure VMs to evaluate secure multi-party computation for aggregate clinical outcome statistics across 2-4 competing pharmaceutical partners. Round 4 closes the two outstanding audit trail issues: memory wipe confirmation is now captured via a per-party API confirmation endpoint and protocol_runs.memory_wipe_confirmed flag, and circuit_definitions now stores a SHA-256 hash of the circuit binary for post-hoc statistical function verification.
target_output.architecture_overview: One Azure VM per party running a Python 3.12 FastAPI coordination service and MP-SPDZ subprocess. Parties communicate via mTLS 1.3 over Azure VNet peering. The coordinator (round-robin elected) orchestrates computation: registers parties, triggers MP-SPDZ garbled circuit execution, collects per-party wipe confirmations, and releases aggregate outputs only after memory_wipe_confirmed=TRUE. PostgreSQL 16 stores computation audit metadata only; encrypted patient inputs are in-memory during computation and destroyed immediately after.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI on port 8443 with mTLS client certificate validation, MP-SPDZ 0.3.x garbled circuit protocol (garbled_sha.x binary), SQLAlchemy 2.0 for PostgreSQL, httpx for inter-party coordination calls. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database (audit and party registry only). Infrastructure: Azure VMs (Standard_D4s_v3 per party), Azure Key Vault for private keys, Azure VNet peering, Azure Container Registry, Azure DevOps Pipelines, Azure Monitor.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Party Registration: each party registers its endpoint and certificate CN with the coordinator node before computation begins. Input Submission: each party encrypts its patient outcome data locally and submits encrypted share to coordinator. Computation Execution: coordinator launches MP-SPDZ subprocess with circuit definition, party endpoints, and input shares; parties execute garbled circuit protocol. Wipe Confirmation: each party destroys in-memory encrypted inputs and POSTs wipe confirmation to coordinator. Result Release: coordinator releases aggregate output only after all parties confirm wipe. Benchmark Collection: protocol_runs records wall-clock time and communication bytes for feasibility report.
target_output.system_components: FastAPI Coordination Service (one per party VM, mTLS port 8443), MP-SPDZ subprocess (garbled circuit executor, launched by coordinator), PostgreSQL 16 (audit records, party registry, circuit definitions with circuit_hash), Azure Key Vault (per-party private keys), Azure VNet peering (inter-party communication), Azure Monitor (computation run duration metrics), Azure DevOps Pipelines (CI with 2-party local simulation test).
target_output.workflows: Computation Run Workflow: coordinator POSTs /v1/computation/run to each party with circuit_id and party_ids[]. Each party retrieves circuit_hash from circuit_definitions, verifies it matches local circuit binary. Coordinator launches MP-SPDZ subprocess; all parties execute garbled circuit protocol over mTLS sockets. On completion, each party destroys encrypted input shares from memory and calls POST /v1/computation/{run_id}/wipe-confirm on coordinator with party_id and timestamp. Coordinator inserts wipe_confirm audit_record for each party; when all parties confirmed, sets protocol_runs.memory_wipe_confirmed=TRUE. GET /v1/computation/{run_id}/result returns 403 until memory_wipe_confirmed=TRUE, then returns aggregate_outputs.statistic_value. Abort Workflow: if any party disconnects mid-computation, coordinator aborts run, all parties wipe inputs, abort wipe confirmations posted, run status set to 'aborted', no result ever released.
target_output.data_model: computation_parties (id UUID PK, party_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, cert_cn VARCHAR, endpoint_url VARCHAR, registered_at TIMESTAMPTZ). circuit_definitions (id UUID PK, circuit_name VARCHAR, statistical_function VARCHAR CHECK IN ('composite_endpoint_rate','arm_comparison_rate'), input_schema JSONB, circuit_hash VARCHAR(64) UNIQUE NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). protocol_runs (id UUID PK, circuit_id UUID FK circuit_definitions, party_ids UUID[], status VARCHAR DEFAULT 'initiated', started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, computation_time_seconds INT, communication_bytes_total BIGINT, memory_wipe_confirmed BOOL DEFAULT false). aggregate_outputs (id UUID PK, run_id UUID FK protocol_runs UNIQUE, statistic_name VARCHAR, statistic_value NUMERIC, confidence_interval_json JSONB, released_at TIMESTAMPTZ). audit_records (id BIGSERIAL PK, run_id UUID FK, party_id UUID FK, event_type VARCHAR CHECK IN ('run_initiated','input_submitted','computation_started','computation_completed','wipe_confirmed','result_released','run_aborted'), event_detail JSONB, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ). Result release gate: GET /result endpoint queries protocol_runs.memory_wipe_confirmed before returning aggregate_outputs. Clinical research data references: federated_patient_cohorts (id UUID PK, party_id FK, cohort_name VARCHAR, diagnosis_icd10_list TEXT[], patient_count_encrypted INT, min_age INT, max_age INT, treatment_exposure VARCHAR), computation_clinical_outputs (id UUID PK, computation_id FK, output_type ENUM survival_curve/hazard_ratio/prevalence, clinical_metric_name VARCHAR, aggregated_value_encrypted BYTEA, patient_count_suppressed BOOLEAN, released_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: POST /v1/party/register - body: {party_id, cert_cn, endpoint_url}. POST /v1/computation/run - body: {circuit_id, party_ids[], input_hashes{}}; returns {run_id}. POST /v1/computation/{run_id}/wipe-confirm - called by each party after local memory wipe; body: {party_id}; coordinator checks all parties confirmed before setting memory_wipe_confirmed=TRUE. GET /v1/computation/{run_id}/result - returns 403 if memory_wipe_confirmed=FALSE; returns {run_id, statistic_name, statistic_value, confidence_interval} if TRUE. GET /v1/computation/{run_id}/benchmark - returns {computation_time_seconds, communication_bytes_total, party_count} for feasibility report. All endpoints require valid mTLS client certificate matching a registered party_id.
target_output.security_and_compliance: mTLS 1.3 client certificate authentication on all FastAPI endpoints; certificate CN validated against computation_parties.cert_cn on every request. Private keys stored in Azure Key Vault accessed via Azure Managed Identity; never written to VM disk. Encrypted patient inputs held only in MP-SPDZ subprocess memory during computation; Python process memory is zeroed after subprocess exit via ctypes.memset on input buffers before deallocation. Memory wipe completion is now auditable: each party calls POST /wipe-confirm and audit_records captures the event with timestamp; protocol_runs.memory_wipe_confirmed gates result release. circuit_hash in circuit_definitions enables post-hoc verification that the correct statistical function binary executed, satisfying ICH E9 statistical analysis plan traceability. HIPAA de-identification safe harbor: aggregate_outputs contain only population-level statistics with no cell sizes below 5.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: One Azure Standard_D4s_v3 VM per party in the same Azure region. Azure VNet peering for inter-party network with NSG rules allowing only mTLS port 8443 between party VM private IPs. Docker image with MP-SPDZ binaries pre-compiled deployed via Azure Container Registry and Ansible playbook. Azure DevOps CI runs Python unit tests and a 2-party local MP-SPDZ simulation test using localhost sockets before any deployment. Azure Monitor alerts on computation_time_seconds exceeding 14400 (4-hour SLA breach).
target_output.observability: Azure Monitor custom metrics: computation_time_seconds per run, communication_bytes_total per run, party_count per run. Structured logs per party node: run_id, event_type, party_id - no patient identifiers or statistical values in logs. Feasibility report generated from protocol_runs table after benchmark runs: tabular comparison of computation_time and communication_bytes across 2-party and 4-party configurations for each supported statistical function.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Azure Standard_D4s_v3 per party: ~$140/month per VM (4 parties = ~$560/month prototype cost). PostgreSQL Azure Database (audit only, smallest tier): ~$50/month. Total prototype: ~$610/month for 3-month evaluation period. Computation is batch-oriented; no auto-scaling needed for prototype.
target_output.phased_implementation: Month 1 (complete): 2-party baseline with ISS-001 resolved, basic protocol run flow. Month 2 (rounds 2-3): 4-party extension design, benchmark metrics collection framework; ISS-002 and ISS-003 identified and noted. Month 3 (round 4, current): ISS-002 resolved via memory_wipe_confirmed column and wipe-confirm endpoint; ISS-003 resolved via circuit_hash. Feasibility report generation from protocol_runs benchmark data. Final deliverable: go/no-go recommendation for production SMPC deployment.
target_output.development_guidelines: All coordinator logic unit-tested with mock party nodes using pytest and pytest-asyncio. MP-SPDZ circuit integration tests run with 2-party localhost simulation in CI (no real VMs). mTLS test fixtures generate self-signed party certificates for local testing. No patient outcome values (even simulated) in any test fixture; use synthetic random binary outcomes. circuit_hash computed at circuit definition registration time using hashlib.sha256 on the circuit binary file; stored and verified before each computation run.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: MP-SPDZ garbled circuit protocol implementation has known theoretical security bounds that may not hold under adversarial party behavior (semi-honest vs. malicious adversary model). For prototype, semi-honest model is acceptable; production would require malicious-secure protocol variant with higher computational cost. Risk: Azure VNet peering latency between parties in different Azure regions could exceed computation time bounds for 4-party case. Mitigation: prototype runs all parties in same Azure region; cross-region evaluation deferred to production feasibility report addendum. Tradeoff: memory_wipe_confirmed flag is set by coordinator based on party-reported confirmations, not cryptographic proof of wipe; a compromised party could lie. Accepted for prototype; HSM-backed attestation required for production.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ISS-002: memory_wipe_confirmed column and per-party wipe confirmation API endpoint added to protocol_runs and audit_records schema respectively. ISS-003: circuit_hash VARCHAR(64) added to circuit_definitions using SHA-256 of circuit binary. Result endpoint now returns 403 until all parties confirm wipe, closing the HIPAA audit gap. Feasibility report format confirmed: tabular benchmark data from protocol_runs table comparing 2-party vs 4-party configurations.

metadata.notes: Round 4 revision prototype; ISS-002 and ISS-003 closed via schema additions and wipe confirmation API; some sections thin as expected for a research prototype in late revision.
